samples.question: Calculate the mean fare paid by the passengers.
samples.constraints: Calculate the mean fare using Python's built-in statistics module or appropriate statistical method in pandas. Rounding off the answer to two decimal places.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS mean_fare FROM test_ave;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS mean_fare FROM (SELECT Fare FROM test_ave);

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT ROUND(AVG(Fare) OVER (), 2) AS mean_fare
    FROM test_ave
)
SELECT mean_fare
FROM fare_stats
LIMIT 1;

WITH fare_data AS (
    SELECT COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS fare
    FROM test_ave
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT ROUND(CAST(SUM(fare) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(COUNT(fare), 0), 2) AS mean_fare
FROM fare_data;

samples.question: Generate a new feature called "FamilySize" by summing the "SibSp" and "Parch" columns. Then, calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) between the "FamilySize" and "Fare" columns.
samples.constraints:
Create a new column 'FamilySize' that is the sum of 'SibSp' and 'Parch' for each row.
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient between 'FamilySize' and 'Fare'
Do not perform any further data cleaning or preprocessing steps before calculating the correlation.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(CORR(SibSp + Parch, Fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient FROM test_ave;

WITH FamilySizeCTE AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        (SibSp + Parch) AS FamilySize,
        Fare
    FROM 
        test_ave
),
StatsCTE AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(FamilySize) AS avg_family_size,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(FamilySize * Fare) AS sum_product,
        SUM(FamilySize * FamilySize) AS sum_family_size_sq,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq
    FROM 
        FamilySizeCTE
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((n * sum_product - AVG(FamilySize) * AVG(Fare) * n) / 
           (SQRT(n * sum_family_size_sq - AVG(FamilySize) * AVG(FamilySize) * n) * 
            SQRT(n * sum_fare_sq - AVG(Fare) * AVG(Fare) * n)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    StatsCTE;

SELECT ROUND((SELECT SUM((fs.FamilySize - avg_fs) * (f.Fare - avg_fare)) / 
                (SQRT(SUM((fs.FamilySize - avg_fs) * (fs.FamilySize - avg_fs)) * 
                SUM((f.Fare - avg_fare) * (f.Fare - avg_fare)))) 
                FROM (SELECT (SibSp + Parch) AS FamilySize FROM test_ave) fs, 
                     (SELECT Fare, AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare FROM test_ave) f, 
                     (SELECT AVG(SibSp + Parch) AS avg_fs FROM test_ave) avg_fs) 
                , (SELECT AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare FROM test_ave)) 
                , 2) AS correlation_coefficient;

WITH FamilyData AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        (SibSp + Parch) AS FamilySize,
        Fare
    FROM 
        test_ave
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(FamilySize) AS sum_family_size,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        SUM(FamilySize * Fare) AS sum_product,
        SUM(FamilySize * FamilySize) AS sum_family_size_sq,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq
    FROM 
        FamilyData
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((n * sum_product - sum_family_size * sum_fare) / 
           (SQRT((n * sum_family_size_sq - sum_family_size * sum_family_size) * 
                  (n * sum_fare_sq - sum_fare * sum_fare))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    Stats;

WITH FamilyData AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(SibSp, 0) + COALESCE(Parch, 0) AS FamilySize,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare
    FROM 
        test_ave
    WHERE 
        SibSp IS NOT NULL 
        AND Parch IS NOT NULL 
        AND Fare IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((SUM(FamilySize * Fare) - SUM(FamilySize) * SUM(Fare) / COUNT(*)) / 
    (SQRT((SUM(FamilySize * FamilySize) - SUM(FamilySize) * SUM(FamilySize) / COUNT(*)) * 
    (SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare) / COUNT(*)))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    FamilyData;

samples.question: Create a new column called "AgeGroup" that categorizes the passengers into four age groups: 'Child' (0-12 years old), 'Teenager' (13-19 years old), 'Adult' (20-59 years old), and 'Elderly' (60 years old and above). Then, calculate the mean fare for each age group.
samples.constraints: Make sure to round the mean fare of each group to 2 decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN Age BETWEEN 0 AND 12 THEN 'Child'
        WHEN Age BETWEEN 13 AND 19 THEN 'Teenager'
        WHEN Age BETWEEN 20 AND 59 THEN 'Adult'
        ELSE 'Elderly'
    END AS AgeGroup,
    ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS mean_fare
FROM test_ave
GROUP BY AgeGroup;

WITH AgeCategorization AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Age BETWEEN 0 AND 12 THEN 'Child'
            WHEN Age BETWEEN 13 AND 19 THEN 'Teenager'
            WHEN Age BETWEEN 20 AND 59 THEN 'Adult'
            ELSE 'Elderly'
        END AS AgeGroup,
        Fare
    FROM test_ave
),
MeanFare AS (
    SELECT 
        AgeGroup,
        ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS mean_fare
    FROM AgeCategorization
    GROUP BY AgeGroup
)
SELECT 
    MAX(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Elderly' THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_elderly,
    MAX(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Teenager' THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_teenager,
    MAX(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Child' THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_child,
    MAX(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Adult' THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_adult
FROM MeanFare;

SELECT 
    AVG(CASE WHEN Age < 13 THEN Fare END) AS mean_fare_child,
    AVG(CASE WHEN Age >= 13 AND Age < 20 THEN Fare END) AS mean_fare_teenager,
    AVG(CASE WHEN Age >= 20 AND Age < 60 THEN Fare END) AS mean_fare_adult,
    AVG(CASE WHEN Age >= 60 THEN Fare END) AS mean_fare_elderly
FROM 
    (SELECT *,
        CASE 
            WHEN Age < 13 THEN 'Child'
            WHEN Age >= 13 AND Age < 20 THEN 'Teenager'
            WHEN Age >= 20 AND Age < 60 THEN 'Adult'
            WHEN Age >= 60 THEN 'Elderly'
        END AS AgeGroup
    FROM test_ave) AS categorized_passengers;

WITH AgeCategorization AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Age <= 12 THEN 'Child'
            WHEN Age BETWEEN 13 AND 19 THEN 'Teenager'
            WHEN Age BETWEEN 20 AND 59 THEN 'Adult'
            ELSE 'Elderly'
        END AS AgeGroup,
        Fare
    FROM test_ave
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Elderly' THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_elderly,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Teenager' THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_teenager,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Child' THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_child,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Adult' THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_adult
FROM AgeCategorization;

WITH AgeCategorized AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN COALESCE(Age, 0) <= 12 THEN 'Child'
            WHEN COALESCE(Age, 0) BETWEEN 13 AND 19 THEN 'Teenager'
            WHEN COALESCE(Age, 0) BETWEEN 20 AND 59 THEN 'Adult'
            WHEN COALESCE(Age, 0) >= 60 THEN 'Elderly'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS AgeGroup,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare
    FROM test_ave
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
)

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Elderly' THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_elderly,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Teenager' THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_teenager,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Child' THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_child,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Adult' THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_adult
FROM AgeCategorized;

samples.question: Apply the linear regression algorithm from the sklearn library to predict whether a passenger survived or not based on the features 'Pclass', 'Sex', 'Age', 'SibSp', 'Parch', 'Fare', and 'Embarked'. Encode 'Sex' and 'Embarked' to numerical values before applying the model. Split the dataset into a training set (80%) and a testing set (20%), train the model on the training set, and evaluate its performance on the testing set using the accuracy score. Ensure that the train_test_split function's random_state parameter is set to 42 for consistency.
samples.constraints: Use one-hot encoding for the 'Sex' and 'Embarked' features. Use the "linear regression" model provided by the sklearn library in Python.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(Survived), 2) AS prediction_accuracy
FROM test_ave;

WITH encoded_data AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Sex_encoded,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Fare,
        CASE 
            WHEN Embarked = 'C' THEN 1 
            WHEN Embarked = 'Q' THEN 2 
            WHEN Embarked = 'S' THEN 3 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS Embarked_encoded
    FROM test_ave
),
train_test_split AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY PassengerId) AS row_num
    FROM encoded_data
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM train_test_split
    WHERE row_num <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_test_split)
),
test_set AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM train_test_split
    WHERE row_num > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_test_split)
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN t.Survived = p.Survived THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 2) AS prediction_accuracy
FROM test_set t
JOIN train_set p ON t.PassengerId = p.PassengerId;

SELECT ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN Survived = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 2) AS prediction_accuracy
FROM (
    SELECT 
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Sex_male,
        CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Sex_female,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Fare,
        CASE WHEN Embarked = 'C' THEN 1 
             WHEN Embarked = 'Q' THEN 2 
             WHEN Embarked = 'S' THEN 3 
             ELSE 0 END AS Embarked_encoded
    FROM test_ave
    ORDER BY RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_ave) * 0.8
) AS training_set;

WITH encoded_data AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Sex_encoded,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Fare,
        CASE 
            WHEN Embarked = 'C' THEN 1 
            WHEN Embarked = 'Q' THEN 2 
            WHEN Embarked = 'S' THEN 3 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS Embarked_encoded
    FROM test_ave
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT *, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY PassengerId) AS rn
    FROM encoded_data
),
train AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM train_set
    WHERE rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_set)
),
test AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM train_set
    WHERE rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_set)
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Survived = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS prediction_accuracy
FROM test t
JOIN train tr ON t.PassengerId = tr.PassengerId;

WITH encoded_data AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        CASE 
            WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 
            WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN 0 
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS Sex_encoded,
        COALESCE(Age, 0) AS Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare,
        CASE 
            WHEN Embarked = 'C' THEN 1 
            WHEN Embarked = 'Q' THEN 2 
            WHEN Embarked = 'S' THEN 3 
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS Embarked_encoded
    FROM test_ave
    WHERE Survived IS NOT NULL AND Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Sex IS NOT NULL AND Age IS NOT NULL AND SibSp IS NOT NULL AND Parch IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL AND Embarked IS NOT NULL
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT * FROM encoded_data 
    ORDER BY RANDOM() 
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM encoded_data)
),
test_set AS (
    SELECT * FROM encoded_data 
    WHERE PassengerId NOT IN (SELECT PassengerId FROM train_set)
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Survived = 1 AND t.Pclass IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(t.PassengerId), 0), 0), 2) AS prediction_accuracy
FROM test_set t;

samples.question: Perform a distribution analysis on the 'Fare' column for each passenger class ('Pclass') separately. Calculate the mean, median, and standard deviation of the fare for each class. Interpret the results in terms of the different passenger classes.
samples.constraints: Keep all numerical values rounded to 2 decimal points. The population standard deviation should be calculated.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    Pclass,
    ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS mean_fare,
    ROUND((SELECT Fare FROM test_ave WHERE Pclass = t.Pclass ORDER BY Fare LIMIT 1 OFFSET (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2), 2) AS median_fare,
    ROUND(STDDEV_POP(Fare), 2) AS std_dev_fare
FROM 
    test_ave t
GROUP BY 
    Pclass;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS mean_fare,
        ROUND(MEDIAN(Fare), 2) AS median_fare,
        ROUND(STDDEV_POP(Fare), 2) AS std_dev_fare
    FROM 
        test_ave
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass
)
SELECT 
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN median_fare END) AS median_fare_class1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN median_fare END) AS median_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class1
FROM 
    fare_stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_class1,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_class2,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN Fare END), 2) AS mean_fare_class3,
    ROUND((SELECT Fare FROM test_ave WHERE Pclass = 1 ORDER BY Fare LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_ave WHERE Pclass = 1) / 2), 2) AS median_fare_class1,
    ROUND((SELECT Fare FROM test_ave WHERE Pclass = 2 ORDER BY Fare LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_ave WHERE Pclass = 2) / 2), 2) AS median_fare_class2,
    ROUND((SELECT Fare FROM test_ave WHERE Pclass = 3 ORDER BY Fare LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_ave WHERE Pclass = 3) / 2), 2) AS median_fare_class3,
    ROUND(STDEV(Fare) OVER (PARTITION BY Pclass), 2) AS std_dev_fare_class1,
    ROUND(STDEV(Fare) OVER (PARTITION BY Pclass), 2) AS std_dev_fare_class2,
    ROUND(STDEV(Fare) OVER (PARTITION BY Pclass), 2) AS std_dev_fare_class3
FROM test_ave
GROUP BY Pclass;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS mean_fare,
        ROUND(STDDEV_POP(Fare), 2) AS std_dev_fare,
        ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare), 2) AS median_fare
    FROM 
        test_ave
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass
)
SELECT 
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN median_fare END) AS median_fare_class1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN median_fare END) AS median_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class1
FROM 
    fare_stats;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2) AS mean_fare,
        ROUND((SELECT Fare FROM test_ave WHERE Pclass = t.Pclass ORDER BY Fare LIMIT 1 OFFSET (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2), 2) AS median_fare,
        ROUND(SQRT(COALESCE(NULLIF(SUM(POW(COALESCE(Fare, 0) - AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2)), 0) / COUNT(*), 0)), 2) AS std_dev_fare
    FROM 
        test_ave t
    WHERE 
        Pclass IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass
)
SELECT 
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN median_fare END) AS median_fare_class1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN median_fare END) AS median_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN mean_fare END) AS mean_fare_class3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN std_dev_fare END) AS std_dev_fare_class3
FROM 
    fare_stats;

samples.question: Calculate the mean value of the "Close Price" column.
samples.constraints: Use the built-in Python (numpy or pandas) to calculate the mean. Do not use any pre-built packages or libraries for mean calculation other than numpy or pandas. The calculation should be done on the whole "Close Price" column. Values in this column should not be rounded or changed in any way before the calculation.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG("Close Price"), 2) AS mean_close_price FROM GODREJIND;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(Close Price), 2) AS mean_close_price FROM (SELECT "Close Price" FROM GODREJIND);
SELECT ROUND(AVG(Close_Price) OVER (), 2) AS mean_close_price FROM GODREJIND;

samples.question: Check if the "Total Traded Quantity" column adheres to a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Use Shapiro-Wilk test from scipy.stats module to check for normality. In this test, the null hypothesis is that the data was drawn from a normal distribution. An alpha level of 0.05 (5%) should be taken as the significance level. If the p-value is less than the alpha level, the null hypothesis is rejected and the data does not follow a normal distribution. If the p-value is greater than the alpha level, the null hypothesis is not rejected and the data may follow a normal distribution.

variants.sql:
SELECT CASE 
           WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'no' 
           ELSE 'yes' 
       END AS is_normal 
FROM GODREJIND 
WHERE Total_Traded_Quantity IS NOT NULL;

WITH total_traded AS (
    SELECT "Total Traded Quantity"
    FROM GODREJIND
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG("Total Traded Quantity") AS mean,
        ROUND(STDEV("Total Traded Quantity"), 2) AS stddev
    FROM total_traded
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN (n < 3) THEN 'insufficient data'
            ELSE 
                CASE 
                    WHEN (ROUND((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM total_traded) * (SELECT AVG("Total Traded Quantity") FROM total_traded) / (SELECT STDEV("Total Traded Quantity") FROM total_traded), 2) < 0.05) 
                    THEN 'no' 
                    ELSE 'yes' 
                END 
        END AS is_normal
    FROM stats
)
SELECT is_normal FROM shapiro_test;

SELECT CASE 
           WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM GODREJIND) < 3 THEN 'no'
           ELSE 'yes' 
       END AS is_normal;

WITH total_traded AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Total Traded Quantity"), 2) AS avg_qty,
        ROUND(STDEV("Total Traded Quantity"), 2) AS stddev_qty,
        COUNT(*) AS count_qty
    FROM GODREJIND
),
normality_test AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN (count_qty - 1) * (stddev_qty / avg_qty) < 0.05 THEN 'no' 
            ELSE 'yes' 
        END AS is_normal
    FROM total_traded
)
SELECT is_normal FROM normality_test;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(*) IS NULL OR COUNT(*) = 0 THEN 'unknown'
        ELSE 
            CASE 
                WHEN AVG(Total_Traded_Quantity) IS NOT NULL AND 
                     STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Total_Traded_Quantity, 0)) IS NOT NULL THEN 'yes'
                ELSE 'no'
            END 
    END AS is_normal
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        Total_Traded_Quantity 
     FROM 
        GODREJIND 
     WHERE 
        Total_Traded_Quantity IS NOT NULL) AS valid_data;

samples.question: Calculate the correlation coefficient between the "High Price" column and the "Low Price" column.
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between "High Price" and "Low Price". Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05. Report the p-value associated with the correlation test. Consider the relationship to be linear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.5. Consider the relationship to be nonlinear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is less than 0.5. If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr >= 0.5 AND p_value < 0.05) THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(corr < 0.5 AND p_value < 0.05) THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(corr, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(High_Price * Low_Price) - SUM(High_Price) * SUM(Low_Price)) / 
        (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(High_Price * High_Price) - SUM(High_Price) * SUM(High_Price)) * 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Low_Price * Low_Price) - SUM(Low_Price) * SUM(Low_Price)))) AS corr,
        2 * (1 - ABS(corr)) AS p_value
    FROM GODREJIND
);

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM("High Price") AS sum_x,
        SUM("Low Price") AS sum_y,
        SUM("High Price" * "High Price") AS sum_xx,
        SUM("Low Price" * "Low Price") AS sum_yy,
        SUM("High Price" * "Low Price") AS sum_xy
    FROM GODREJIND
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS r
    FROM stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        r,
        2 * (1 - ABS(r)) AS p_val
    FROM correlation
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_val < 0.05 AND ABS(r) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_val < 0.05 AND ABS(r) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(r, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM p_value;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr.r) >= 0.5 AND corr.p_value < 0.05 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(corr.r) < 0.5 AND corr.p_value < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(corr.r, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(high.low) - SUM(high.high) * SUM(low.low)) / 
        (SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(high.high * high.high) - SUM(high.high) * SUM(high.high)) * 
         SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(low.low * low.low) - SUM(low.low) * SUM(low.low))) AS r,
        2 * (1 - (SELECT POWER(1 - (r * r), (COUNT(*) - 2)) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM GODREJIND))) AS p_value
    FROM 
        (SELECT "High Price" AS high, "Low Price" AS low FROM GODREJIND) AS high
) AS corr;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM("High Price") AS sum_x,
        SUM("Low Price") AS sum_y,
        SUM("High Price" * "High Price") AS sum_xx,
        SUM("Low Price" * "Low Price") AS sum_yy,
        SUM("High Price" * "Low Price") AS sum_xy
    FROM GODREJIND
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_x,
        sum_y,
        sum_xx,
        sum_yy,
        sum_xy,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM correlation
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM p_value;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(COALESCE("High Price", 0)) AS sum_x,
        SUM(COALESCE("Low Price", 0)) AS sum_y,
        SUM(COALESCE("High Price", 0) * COALESCE("Low Price", 0)) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(COALESCE("High Price", 0) * COALESCE("High Price", 0)) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(COALESCE("Low Price", 0) * COALESCE("Low Price", 0)) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        GODREJIND
    WHERE 
        "High Price" IS NOT NULL 
        AND "Low Price" IS NOT NULL
), correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_x,
        sum_y,
        sum_xy,
        sum_xx,
        sum_yy,
        CAST((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) AS FLOAT) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
), p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - (SELECT 
            COUNT(*) 
            FROM 
                SQLITE_MASTER 
            WHERE 
                type='table' 
                AND name='GODREJIND')) / CAST(n AS FLOAT) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(COALESCE(p_value, 1), 2) AS p_value
FROM 
    correlation, p_value;

samples.question: Create a new feature called "Price Range" which represents the difference between the "High Price" and "Low Price" for each row. Calculate the mean, median, and standard deviation of this new feature.
samples.constraints: Make sure to use the correct columns for calculating the "Price Range". All calculations should be performed up to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(High Price - Low Price), 2) AS price_range_mean,
    ROUND((SELECT AVG(price_range) FROM (SELECT High Price - Low Price AS price_range FROM GODREJIND)), 2) AS price_range_median,
    ROUND(STDDEV(High Price - Low Price), 2) AS price_range_std_dev
FROM GODREJIND;

WITH PriceRange AS (
    SELECT 
        High Price - Low Price AS Price_Range
    FROM 
        GODREJIND
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Price_Range), 2) AS price_range_mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV(Price_Range), 2) AS price_range_std_dev,
        ROUND(MEDIAN(Price_Range), 2) AS price_range_median
    FROM 
        PriceRange
)
SELECT 
    price_range_mean, 
    price_range_std_dev, 
    price_range_median 
FROM 
    Stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(price_range), 2) AS price_range_mean,
    ROUND(STDDEV(price_range), 2) AS price_range_std_dev,
    ROUND((SELECT price_range FROM (SELECT 
        (High_Price - Low_Price) AS price_range 
        FROM GODREJIND 
        ORDER BY price_range 
        LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM GODREJIND) / 2), 
        (SELECT price_range FROM (SELECT 
        (High_Price - Low_Price) AS price_range 
        FROM GODREJIND 
        ORDER BY price_range 
        LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM GODREJIND) / 2 - 1) 
        UNION ALL 
        SELECT price_range FROM (SELECT 
        (High_Price - Low_Price) AS price_range 
        FROM GODREJIND 
        ORDER BY price_range 
        LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM GODREJIND) / 2) 
        ) 
        ) / 2), 2) AS price_range_median
FROM 
    (SELECT (High_Price - Low_Price) AS price_range FROM GODREJIND) AS price_ranges;

WITH PriceRange AS (
    SELECT 
        High_Price - Low_Price AS Price_Range
    FROM 
        GODREJIND
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Price_Range), 2) AS price_range_mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV(Price_Range), 2) AS price_range_std_dev,
        ROUND((SELECT Price_Range FROM PriceRange ORDER BY Price_Range LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM PriceRange) / 2), 2) AS price_range_median
    FROM 
        PriceRange
)
SELECT 
    price_range_mean, 
    price_range_std_dev, 
    price_range_median
FROM 
    Stats;

WITH PriceRange AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(COALESCE(NULLIF(High Price, 0), 0) - COALESCE(NULLIF(Low Price, 0), 0), 2) AS Price_Range
    FROM 
        GODREJIND
    WHERE 
        High Price IS NOT NULL AND Low Price IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Price_Range), 2) AS price_range_mean,
    ROUND((SELECT Price_Range FROM PriceRange ORDER BY Price_Range OFFSET (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2 LIMIT 1), 2) AS price_range_median,
    ROUND(STDDEV(Price_Range), 2) AS price_range_std_dev
FROM 
    PriceRange;

samples.question: Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the "Mar.2019" column.
samples.constraints: Outliers are to be pruned via the interquartile range method before calculating the mean and standard deviation. Handle missing values by using listwise deletion method. Report the measures rounded to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG("Mar.2019"), 2) AS mean_mar_2019, 
    ROUND(STDDEV("Mar.2019"), 2) AS sd_mar_2019 
FROM 
    unemployement_industry 
WHERE 
    "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL 
    AND "Mar.2019" BETWEEN 
        (SELECT 
            PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "Mar.2019") 
         FROM 
            unemployement_industry 
         WHERE 
            "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL) 
        AND 
        (SELECT 
            PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "Mar.2019") 
         FROM 
            unemployement_industry 
         WHERE 
            "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL);

WITH Q1_Q3 AS (
    SELECT 
        percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "Mar.2019") AS Q1,
        percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "Mar.2019") AS Q3
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL
),
IQR AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM Q1_Q3
),
Filtered_Data AS (
    SELECT "Mar.2019"
    FROM unemployement_industry, IQR
    WHERE "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL
    AND "Mar.2019" >= (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) 
    AND "Mar.2019" <= (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Mar.2019"), 2) AS mean_mar_2019,
        ROUND(STDEV("Mar.2019"), 2) AS sd_mar_2019
    FROM Filtered_Data
)
SELECT mean_mar_2019, sd_mar_2019 FROM Stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Mar_2019), 2) AS mean_mar_2019,
    ROUND(STDDEV(Mar_2019), 2) AS sd_mar_2019
FROM (
    SELECT Mar_2019
    FROM (
        SELECT Mar_2019,
               PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) OVER () AS Q1,
               PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) OVER () AS Q3
        FROM unemployement_industry
        WHERE Mar_2019 IS NOT NULL
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE Mar_2019 >= Q1 - 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1) AND Mar_2019 <= Q3 + 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1)
);

WITH IQR AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "Mar.2019") AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "Mar.2019") AS Q3
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL
),
FilteredData AS (
    SELECT "Mar.2019"
    FROM unemployement_industry, IQR
    WHERE "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL
    AND "Mar.2019" >= (Q1 - 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1))
    AND "Mar.2019" <= (Q3 + 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1))
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG("Mar.2019"), 2) AS mean_mar_2019,
    ROUND(STDEV("Mar.2019"), 2) AS sd_mar_2019
FROM FilteredData;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Mar_2019,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Mar_2019) OVER () AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Mar_2019) OVER () AS Q3
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE Mar_2019 IS NOT NULL
),
IQR AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM stats
),
filtered AS (
    SELECT Mar_2019
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE Mar_2019 IS NOT NULL
    AND Mar_2019 >= (SELECT Q1 FROM IQR) - 1.5 * (SELECT IQR FROM IQR)
    AND Mar_2019 <= (SELECT Q3 FROM IQR) + 1.5 * (SELECT IQR FROM IQR)
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Mar_2019, 0)), 2) AS mean_mar_2019,
    ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POW(COALESCE(Mar_2019, 0) - AVG(COALESCE(Mar_2019, 0)) OVER (), 2)), 0), 2) AS sd_mar_2019
FROM filtered;

samples.question: Check if the distribution of the "Mar.2020" column adheres to a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Assume a normal distribution if skewness is between -0.5 and 0.5. Use the Fisher-Pearson coefficient of skewness. Round results to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND((SUM((Mar.2020 - AVG(Mar.2020)) * (Mar.2020 - AVG(Mar.2020)) * (Mar.2020 - AVG(Mar.2020))) / COUNT(*)) / 
        POWER(SQRT(SUM((Mar.2020 - AVG(Mar.2020)) * (Mar.2020 - AVG(Mar.2020))) / COUNT(*)), 3), 2) BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 
        THEN 'Yes' 
        ELSE 'No' 
    END AS is_normal 
FROM unemployement_industry;

WITH skewness_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(POWER("Mar.2020" - AVG("Mar.2020"), 3)) / 
        (SUM(POWER("Mar.2020" - AVG("Mar.2020"), 2)) * POWER(COUNT(*), 1.5))) AS skewness
    FROM unemployement_industry
),
normal_distribution_check AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN ROUND(skewness, 2) BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 THEN 'Yes' 
            ELSE 'No' 
        END AS is_normal
    FROM skewness_calculation
)
SELECT is_normal FROM normal_distribution_check;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND((SELECT (COUNT(*) * SUM((Mar.2020 - avg) * (Mar.2020 - avg)) / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM unemployement_industry) 
        FROM (SELECT Mar.2020, AVG(Mar.2020) AS avg FROM unemployement_industry) AS subquery) / 
        (SELECT (COUNT(*) * SUM((Mar.2020 - avg) * (Mar.2020 - avg)) / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM unemployement_industry) 
        FROM (SELECT Mar.2020, AVG(Mar.2020) AS avg FROM unemployement_industry) AS subquery) 
        AS skewness) BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 
        THEN 'Yes' 
        ELSE 'No' 
    END AS is_normal 
FROM unemployement_industry 
LIMIT 1;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Mar.2020"), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(STDEV("Mar.2020"), 2) AS stddev,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM unemployement_industry
),
skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SUM(POWER("Mar.2020" - mean, 3)) / (n * POWER(stddev, 3)), 2) AS skew
    FROM unemployement_industry, stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN skew BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 THEN 'Yes' 
        ELSE 'No' 
    END AS is_normal
FROM skewness;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(CASE WHEN Mar.2020 IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS n,
        AVG(COALESCE(Mar.2020, 0)) AS mean,
        SUM(COALESCE(Mar.2020, 0) * COALESCE(Mar.2020, 0)) AS sum_squares,
        SUM(COALESCE(Mar.2020, 0) * COALESCE(Mar.2020, 0) * COALESCE(Mar.2020, 0)) AS sum_cubes
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE Mar.2020 IS NOT NULL
),
calc AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        mean,
        ROUND(SQRT((sum_squares - (n * mean * mean)) / NULLIF(n, 0)), 2) AS stddev,
        ROUND((n * sum_cubes - 3 * n * n * mean * stddev * stddev) / NULLIF(n * (n - 1) * (n - 2), 0), 2) AS skewness
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(skewness) <= 0.5 THEN 'Yes' 
        ELSE 'No' 
    END AS is_normal
FROM calc;

samples.question: Apply machine learning techniques to predict the employment level in March 2020 based on the data from March 2019. Split the dataset into a 70-30 split for training and testing sets, train a simple linear regression model on the training set, and evaluate its performance on the testing set using Mean Squared Error as the evaluation metric.
samples.constraints: Split the dataset with a 70-30 split for training and testing sets with a random seed of 42. Use a simple linear regression model for training and evaluate the model's performance by calculating the Mean Squared Error.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG((Mar.2020 - Mar.2019) * (Mar.2020 - Mar.2019)), 2) AS Mean_Squared_Error
FROM unemployement_industry;

WITH training_data AS (
    SELECT 
        "Mar.2019" AS employment_2019,
        "Mar.2020" AS employment_2020
    FROM unemployement_industry
    ORDER BY RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM unemployement_industry) * 0.7
),
testing_data AS (
    SELECT 
        "Mar.2019" AS employment_2019,
        "Mar.2020" AS employment_2020
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE Serial NOT IN (SELECT Serial FROM training_data)
),
predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        employment_2019,
        employment_2020,
        (employment_2019 * (SELECT AVG(employment_2020) / AVG(employment_2019) FROM training_data)) AS predicted_employment_2020
    FROM testing_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(SUM(POWER(employment_2020 - predicted_employment_2020, 2)) / COUNT(*), 2) AS Mean_Squared_Error
FROM predictions;

SELECT ROUND(AVG((Mar.2020 - Predicted_Mar_2020) * (Mar.2020 - Predicted_Mar_2020)), 2) AS Mean_Squared_Error
FROM (
    SELECT Mar.2020, 
           (SELECT AVG(Mar.2020) FROM unemployement_industry) + 
           (SELECT (Mar.2019 - AVG(Mar.2019)) * (Mar.2020 - AVG(Mar.2020)) / (SELECT (Mar.2019 - AVG(Mar.2019)) * (Mar.2019 - AVG(Mar.2019)) FROM unemployement_industry) 
            FROM unemployement_industry) AS Predicted_Mar_2020
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE Serial IN (SELECT Serial FROM unemployement_industry ORDER BY RANDOM() LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.3 FROM unemployement_industry))
) AS Predictions;

WITH training_set AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM unemployement_industry
),
train AS (
    SELECT Mar.2019, Mar.2020
    FROM training_set
    WHERE rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM training_set)
),
test AS (
    SELECT Mar.2019, Mar.2020
    FROM training_set
    WHERE rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM training_set)
)
SELECT ROUND(SUM((t.Mar.2020 - (0.5 * t.Mar.2019 + 0.5)) * (t.Mar.2020 - (0.5 * t.Mar.2019 + 0.5))) / COUNT(*), 2) AS Mean_Squared_Error
FROM test t;

WITH Training_Set AS (
    SELECT
        COALESCE(CAST("Mar.2019" AS FLOAT), 0) AS Employment_2019,
        COALESCE(CAST("Mar.2020" AS FLOAT), 0) AS Employment_2020
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL AND "Mar.2020" IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY RANDOM() LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM unemployement_industry) * 0.7
),
Testing_Set AS (
    SELECT
        COALESCE(CAST("Mar.2019" AS FLOAT), 0) AS Employment_2019,
        COALESCE(CAST("Mar.2020" AS FLOAT), 0) AS Employment_2020
    FROM unemployement_industry
    WHERE "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL AND "Mar.2020" IS NOT NULL
    AND (COALESCE(CAST("Mar.2019" AS FLOAT), 0), COALESCE(CAST("Mar.2020" AS FLOAT), 0)) NOT IN (
        SELECT
            COALESCE(CAST("Mar.2019" AS FLOAT), 0), COALESCE(CAST("Mar.2020" AS FLOAT), 0)
        FROM unemployement_industry
        WHERE "Mar.2019" IS NOT NULL AND "Mar.2020" IS NOT NULL
        ORDER BY RANDOM() LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM unemployement_industry) * 0.7
    ))
),
MSE_Calculation AS (
    SELECT
        AVG(POWER(Employment_2020 - Employment_2019, 2)) AS Mean_Squared_Error
    FROM Testing_Set
)
SELECT ROUND(COALESCE((SELECT Mean_Squared_Error FROM MSE_Calculation), 0), 2) AS Mean_Squared_Error;

samples.question: Calculate the mean age of the individuals in the dataset.
samples.constraints: Ignore rows with missing values in the age column. Use Python's built-in function to calculate the mean.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(age), 2) AS mean_age FROM insurance WHERE age IS NOT NULL;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT age
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(age), 2) AS mean_age
FROM valid_data;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(age), 2) AS mean_age FROM (SELECT age FROM insurance WHERE age IS NOT NULL);

WITH age_data AS (
    SELECT age
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(age) OVER (), 2) AS mean_age
FROM age_data;

WITH valid_ages AS (
    SELECT age

    WHERE age IS NOT NULL

SELECT ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(age, 0)), 2) AS mean_age

samples.question: Check if the distribution of BMI values in the dataset follows a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Consider the distribution as normal if the absolute value of skewness is less than 0.5. Calculate skewness using Python's built-in function.

variants.sql:
SELECT CASE 
           WHEN ABS(ROUND((SUM(POWER(bmi - AVG(bmi), 3) / COUNT(bmi)) / POWER(STDDEV(bmi), 3), 2)) < 0.5 THEN 'normal' 
           ELSE 'not normal' 
       END AS bmi_distribution 
FROM insurance;

WITH bmi_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(bmi) AS mean_bmi,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM((bmi - AVG(bmi)) * (bmi - AVG(bmi))) AS variance_sum
    FROM insurance
),
bmi_skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        (SUM((bmi - mean_bmi) * (bmi - mean_bmi) * (bmi - mean_bmi)) / n) / 
        (POWER(SQRT(variance_sum / n), 3)) AS skewness
    FROM insurance, bmi_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(skewness) < 0.5 THEN 'normal' 
        ELSE 'not normal' 
    END AS bmi_distribution
FROM bmi_skewness;

SELECT CASE 
           WHEN ABS((SELECT SUM((bmi - avg_bmi) * (bmi - avg_bmi) * (bmi_count) / (bmi_count * (bmi_count - 1))) 
                     FROM (SELECT bmi, AVG(bmi) AS avg_bmi, COUNT(*) AS bmi_count 
                           FROM insurance 
                           GROUP BY bmi) AS subquery) / 
                     (SELECT (SUM((bmi - avg_bmi) * (bmi - avg_bmi) / bmi_count) 
                               FROM (SELECT bmi, AVG(bmi) AS avg_bmi, COUNT(*) AS bmi_count 
                                     FROM insurance 
                                     GROUP BY bmi) AS subquery) 
                     ) - 3) < 0.5 
           THEN 'normal' 
           ELSE 'not normal' 
       END AS bmi_distribution;

WITH bmi_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        bmi,
        AVG(bmi) OVER () AS mean_bmi,
        COUNT(bmi) OVER () AS count_bmi,
        SUM((bmi - AVG(bmi) OVER ()) * (bmi - AVG(bmi) OVER ())) OVER () AS variance_bmi
    FROM insurance
),
bmi_skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM((bmi - mean_bmi) * (bmi - mean_bmi) * (bmi - mean_bmi)) AS skew_num,
        SUM((bmi - mean_bmi) * (bmi - mean_bmi)) AS skew_den
    FROM bmi_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(skew_num / (n * POWER(skew_den / n, 1.5))) < 0.5 THEN 'normal' 
        ELSE 'not normal' 
    END AS bmi_distribution
FROM bmi_skewness;

WITH bmi_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(bmi) AS mean_bmi,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(CAST(bmi AS FLOAT) * CAST(bmi AS FLOAT)) AS sum_bmi_squared,
        SUM(CAST(bmi AS FLOAT)) AS sum_bmi
    FROM 
        insurance
    WHERE 
        bmi IS NOT NULL
),
skewness_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * SUM(CAST(bmi AS FLOAT) - mean_bmi) * (CAST(bmi AS FLOAT) - mean_bmi) * (CAST(bmi AS FLOAT) - mean_bmi) / NULLIF(n - 1, 0) / NULLIF(n, 0) / NULLIF(n, 0)) AS skewness
    FROM 
        insurance, bmi_stats
    WHERE 
        bmi IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(COALESCE((SELECT skewness FROM skewness_calculation), 0)) < 0.5 THEN 'bmi_distribution=normal'
        ELSE 'bmi_distribution=not_normal'
    END AS bmi_distribution;

samples.question: Calculate the correlation coefficient between the charges incurred by individuals and the number of children they have.
samples.constraints: Ignore rows with missing values in charges and children columns. Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(CORR(charges, children), 2) AS correlation_coefficient FROM insurance WHERE charges IS NOT NULL AND children IS NOT NULL;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT charges, children
    FROM insurance
    WHERE charges IS NOT NULL AND children IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(charges) AS sum_x,
        SUM(children) AS sum_y,
        SUM(charges * children) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(charges * charges) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(children * children) AS sum_yy
    FROM valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(charges) AS avg_charges,
        AVG(children) AS avg_children,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(charges * children) AS sum_product,
        SUM(charges) AS sum_charges,
        SUM(children) AS sum_children,
        SUM(charges * charges) AS sum_charges_squared,
        SUM(children * children) AS sum_children_squared
    FROM insurance
    WHERE charges IS NOT NULL AND children IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((n * sum_product - sum_charges * sum_children) / 
    (SQRT(n * sum_charges_squared - sum_charges * sum_charges) * 
     SQRT(n * sum_children_squared - sum_children * sum_children)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM stats;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(charges AS FLOAT) AS charges,
        CAST(children AS FLOAT) AS children
    FROM 
        insurance
    WHERE 
        charges IS NOT NULL 
        AND children IS NOT NULL
),
n AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS count, 
           SUM(charges) AS sum_x, 
           SUM(children) AS sum_y, 
           SUM(charges * children) AS sum_xy,
           SUM(charges * charges) AS sum_xx,
           SUM(children * children) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (count * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((count * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (count * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        n
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

samples.question: Identify the outliers in the charges incurred by individuals using the Z-score method.
samples.constraints: Apply the Z-score method for outlier detection using the 1.5xIQR rule. Consider any value that falls below Q1 - 1.5 * IQR or above Q3 + 1.5 * IQR as an outlier. Report the total number of outliers, and the mean and median charges of these identified outliers.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_outliers, 
    ROUND(AVG(charges), 2) AS mean_charges_outliers, 
    ROUND(MEDIAN(charges), 2) AS median_charges_outliers 
FROM insurance 
WHERE charges < (SELECT percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) FROM insurance) - 1.5 * (SELECT (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) FROM insurance) - percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) FROM insurance) 
   OR charges > (SELECT percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) FROM insurance) + 1.5 * (SELECT (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) FROM insurance) - percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) FROM insurance);

WITH charge_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q3
    FROM insurance
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM charge_stats
),
outlier_bounds AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1 - 1.5 * IQR AS lower_bound,
        Q3 + 1.5 * IQR AS upper_bound
    FROM iqr
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        charges
    FROM insurance, outlier_bounds
    WHERE charges < lower_bound OR charges > upper_bound
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_outliers,
    ROUND(AVG(charges), 2) AS mean_charges_outliers,
    ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges), 2) AS median_charges_outliers
FROM outliers;

SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_outliers, 
    ROUND(AVG(charges), 2) AS mean_charges_outliers, 
    ROUND((SELECT charges 
           FROM insurance 
           ORDER BY charges 
           LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM insurance WHERE charges < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR FROM 
               (SELECT 
                   PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q1, 
                   PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q3, 
                   (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges)) AS IQR 
               FROM insurance) 
           )))) AS median_charges_outliers
FROM insurance 
WHERE charges < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR FROM 
                  (SELECT 
                      PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q1, 
                      PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q3, 
                      (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges)) AS IQR 
                  FROM insurance) 
              ) OR charges > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR FROM 
                  (SELECT 
                      PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q1, 
                      PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q3, 
                      (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges)) AS IQR 
                  FROM insurance));

WITH charge_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        charges,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) OVER () AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) OVER () AS Q3
    FROM insurance
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM charge_stats
    LIMIT 1
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        charges
    FROM insurance, iqr
    WHERE charges < Q1 - 1.5 * IQR OR charges > Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_outliers,
    ROUND(AVG(charges), 2) AS mean_charges_outliers,
    ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges), 2) AS median_charges_outliers
FROM outliers;

WITH charge_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(charges), 2) AS mean_charges,
        ROUND(MEDIAN(charges), 2) AS median_charges,
        MIN(charges) AS min_charges,
        MAX(charges) AS max_charges,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY charges) AS Q3
    FROM insurance
    WHERE charges IS NOT NULL
),
IQR AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS iqr
    FROM charge_stats
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        charges
    FROM insurance
    WHERE charges IS NOT NULL 
      AND (charges < (SELECT Q1 FROM IQR) - 1.5 * (SELECT iqr FROM IQR) 
           OR charges > (SELECT Q3 FROM IQR) + 1.5 * (SELECT iqr FROM IQR))
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_outliers,
    ROUND(AVG(charges), 2) AS mean_charges_outliers,
    ROUND(MEDIAN(charges), 2) AS median_charges_outliers
FROM outliers;

samples.question: Perform comprehensive data preprocessing on the dataset, including cleaning, transformation, and handling of missing values.
samples.constraints: Handle the missing values in the 'age', 'sex', and 'region' columns by removing the corresponding rows. Transform the 'sex' and 'smoker' columns to binary format (0 and 1). Normalize 'age', 'bmi', 'children', and 'charges' columns. Report the mean of each column after the preprocessing.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN sex = 'female' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 4) AS mean_sex,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN smoker = 'yes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 4) AS mean_smoker,
    ROUND(AVG((age - MIN(age)) / (MAX(age) - MIN(age))), 4) AS mean_age,
    ROUND(AVG((bmi - MIN(bmi)) / (MAX(bmi) - MIN(bmi))), 4) AS mean_bmi,
    ROUND(AVG((children - MIN(children)) / (MAX(children) - MIN(children))), 4) AS mean_children,
    ROUND(AVG((charges - MIN(charges)) / (MAX(charges) - MIN(charges))), 4) AS mean_charges
FROM insurance
WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND sex IS NOT NULL AND region IS NOT NULL;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT 
        age,
        CASE WHEN sex = 'female' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS sex,
        bmi,
        children,
        CASE WHEN smoker = 'yes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS smoker,
        region,
        charges
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND sex IS NOT NULL AND region IS NOT NULL
),
normalized_data AS (
    SELECT 
        (age - MIN(age) OVER()) / (MAX(age) OVER() - MIN(age) OVER()) AS age,
        (sex - MIN(sex) OVER()) / (MAX(sex) OVER() - MIN(sex) OVER()) AS sex,
        (bmi - MIN(bmi) OVER()) / (MAX(bmi) OVER() - MIN(bmi) OVER()) AS bmi,
        (children - MIN(children) OVER()) / (MAX(children) OVER() - MIN(children) OVER()) AS children,
        (smoker - MIN(smoker) OVER()) / (MAX(smoker) OVER() - MIN(smoker) OVER()) AS smoker,
        (charges - MIN(charges) OVER()) / (MAX(charges) OVER() - MIN(charges) OVER()) AS charges
    FROM cleaned_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(smoker), 4) AS mean_smoker,
    ROUND(AVG(children), 4) AS mean_children,
    ROUND(AVG(sex), 4) AS mean_sex,
    ROUND(AVG(age), 4) AS mean_age,
    ROUND(AVG(bmi), 4) AS mean_bmi,
    ROUND(AVG(charges), 4) AS mean_charges
FROM normalized_data;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(sex_binary), 4) AS mean_sex,
    ROUND(AVG(smoker_binary), 4) AS mean_smoker,
    ROUND(AVG(normalized_children), 4) AS mean_children,
    ROUND(AVG(normalized_age), 4) AS mean_age,
    ROUND(AVG(normalized_bmi), 4) AS mean_bmi,
    ROUND(AVG(normalized_charges), 4) AS mean_charges
FROM (
    SELECT 
        age,
        CASE WHEN sex = 'female' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS sex_binary,
        CASE WHEN smoker = 'yes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS smoker_binary,
        children,
        (age - (SELECT MIN(age) FROM insurance WHERE age IS NOT NULL)) / 
        (SELECT (MAX(age) - MIN(age)) FROM insurance WHERE age IS NOT NULL) AS normalized_age,
        (bmi - (SELECT MIN(bmi) FROM insurance WHERE bmi IS NOT NULL)) / 
        (SELECT (MAX(bmi) - MIN(bmi)) FROM insurance WHERE bmi IS NOT NULL) AS normalized_bmi,
        (children - (SELECT MIN(children) FROM insurance WHERE children IS NOT NULL)) / 
        (SELECT (MAX(children) - MIN(children)) FROM insurance WHERE children IS NOT NULL) AS normalized_children,
        (charges - (SELECT MIN(charges) FROM insurance WHERE charges IS NOT NULL)) / 
        (SELECT (MAX(charges) - MIN(charges)) FROM insurance WHERE charges IS NOT NULL) AS normalized_charges
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND sex IS NOT NULL AND region IS NOT NULL
) AS preprocessed_data;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT 
        age, 
        CASE WHEN sex = 'female' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS sex,
        bmi,
        children,
        CASE WHEN smoker = 'yes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS smoker,
        region,
        charges
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND sex IS NOT NULL AND region IS NOT NULL
),
normalized_data AS (
    SELECT 
        (age - MIN(age) OVER()) / (MAX(age) OVER() - MIN(age) OVER()) AS age,
        (sex - MIN(sex) OVER()) / (MAX(sex) OVER() - MIN(sex) OVER()) AS sex,
        (bmi - MIN(bmi) OVER()) / (MAX(bmi) OVER() - MIN(bmi) OVER()) AS bmi,
        (children - MIN(children) OVER()) / (MAX(children) OVER() - MIN(children) OVER()) AS children,
        (smoker - MIN(smoker) OVER()) / (MAX(smoker) OVER() - MIN(smoker) OVER()) AS smoker,
        (charges - MIN(charges) OVER()) / (MAX(charges) OVER() - MIN(charges) OVER()) AS charges
    FROM cleaned_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(smoker), 4) AS mean_smoker,
    ROUND(AVG(children), 4) AS mean_children,
    ROUND(AVG(sex), 4) AS mean_sex,
    ROUND(AVG(age), 4) AS mean_age,
    ROUND(AVG(bmi), 4) AS mean_bmi,
    ROUND(AVG(charges), 4) AS mean_charges
FROM normalized_data;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(age AS FLOAT) AS age,
        CASE WHEN sex = 'female' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS sex,
        CAST(bmi AS FLOAT) AS bmi,
        CAST(children AS FLOAT) AS children,
        CASE WHEN smoker = 'yes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS smoker,
        region,
        CAST(charges AS FLOAT) AS charges
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL 
      AND sex IS NOT NULL 
      AND region IS NOT NULL
), normalized_data AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((age - (SELECT MIN(age) FROM cleaned_data)) / NULLIF((SELECT MAX(age) FROM cleaned_data) - (SELECT MIN(age) FROM cleaned_data), 0), 2), 2) AS age,
        ROUND((sex - (SELECT MIN(sex) FROM cleaned_data)) / NULLIF((SELECT MAX(sex) FROM cleaned_data) - (SELECT MIN(sex) FROM cleaned_data), 0), 2), 2) AS sex,
        ROUND((bmi - (SELECT MIN(bmi) FROM cleaned_data)) / NULLIF((SELECT MAX(bmi) FROM cleaned_data) - (SELECT MIN(bmi) FROM cleaned_data), 0), 2), 2) AS bmi,
        ROUND((children - (SELECT MIN(children) FROM cleaned_data)) / NULLIF((SELECT MAX(children) FROM cleaned_data) - (SELECT MIN(children) FROM cleaned_data), 0), 2), 2) AS children,
        ROUND((smoker - (SELECT MIN(smoker) FROM cleaned_data)) / NULLIF((SELECT MAX(smoker) FROM cleaned_data) - (SELECT MIN(smoker) FROM cleaned_data), 0), 2), 2) AS smoker,
        ROUND((charges - (SELECT MIN(charges) FROM cleaned_data)) / NULLIF((SELECT MAX(charges) FROM cleaned_data) - (SELECT MIN(charges) FROM cleaned_data), 0), 2), 2) AS charges
    FROM cleaned_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(smoker), 4) AS mean_smoker,
    ROUND(AVG(children), 4) AS mean_children,
    ROUND(AVG(sex), 4) AS mean_sex,
    ROUND(AVG(age), 4) AS mean_age,
    ROUND(AVG(bmi), 4) AS mean_bmi,
    ROUND(AVG(charges), 4) AS mean_charges
FROM normalized_data;

samples.question: Create a linear regression machine learning model using the Scikit-learn library to predict the medical charges based on the age and BMI of individuals. Evaluate the performance of the model using the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) evaluation metric only.
samples.constraints: Use the linear regression model available in the Scikit-Learn library. Split the data into training and testing sets with 80% of the data used for training and 20% used for testing. Use a random state of 42 for the split. The predictor variables are 'age' and 'bmi', and the target variable is 'charges'. Implement RMSE for the model evaluation. Ignore any row with missing values present in these three columns for this analysis.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(SQRT(AVG((charges - predicted_charges) * (charges - predicted_charges))), 2) AS model_rmse
FROM (
    SELECT charges, 
           (0.5 * age + 0.5 * bmi) AS predicted_charges
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL AND charges IS NOT NULL
) AS predictions;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL AND charges IS NOT NULL
),
train_data AS (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges
    FROM filtered_data
    ORDER BY RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM filtered_data) * 0.8
),
test_data AS (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges
    FROM filtered_data
    WHERE (age, bmi, charges) NOT IN (SELECT age, bmi, charges FROM train_data)
),
predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        age, 
        bmi, 
        charges, 
        (0.5 * age + 500 * bmi) AS predicted_charges  -- Placeholder for linear regression prediction
    FROM test_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(charges - predicted_charges, 2))), 2) AS model_rmse
FROM predictions;

SELECT ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(predicted.charges - actual.charges, 2))), 2) AS model_rmse
FROM (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges,
           (SELECT AVG(charges) FROM insurance WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL) AS predicted
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM insurance WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL) * 0.2
) AS actual
JOIN (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges,
           (SELECT AVG(charges) FROM insurance WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL) AS predicted
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM insurance WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL) * 0.8
) AS predicted ON actual.age = predicted.age AND actual.bmi = predicted.bmi;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges
    FROM insurance
    WHERE age IS NOT NULL AND bmi IS NOT NULL AND charges IS NOT NULL
),
training_data AS (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM filtered_data
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges
    FROM training_data
    WHERE rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM training_data),
    test_set AS (
    SELECT age, bmi, charges
    FROM training_data
    WHERE rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM training_data)
)
SELECT ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(t.charges - p.predicted_charges, 2))), 2) AS model_rmse
FROM test_set t
JOIN (
    SELECT age, bmi,
           (SELECT AVG(charges) FROM train_set) + 
           (SELECT (AVG(charges) - AVG(bmi) * (SELECT (SUM(charges) - SUM(bmi) * AVG(bmi)) / (COUNT(*) - 1) FROM train_set)) * (bmi - AVG(bmi)) FROM train_set) * (age - AVG(age)) / (SELECT VARIANCE(age) FROM train_set) FROM train_set) AS predicted_charges
    FROM train_set
) p ON t.age = p.age AND t.bmi = p.bmi;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT 
        age, 
        bmi, 
        charges
    FROM 
        insurance
    WHERE 
        age IS NOT NULL 
        AND bmi IS NOT NULL 
        AND charges IS NOT NULL
),
train_data AS (
    SELECT 
        age, 
        bmi, 
        charges
    FROM 
        filtered_data
    ORDER BY 
        RANDOM() 
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM filtered_data) * 0.8
),
test_data AS (
    SELECT 
        age, 
        bmi, 
        charges
    FROM 
        filtered_data
    WHERE 
        (age, bmi, charges) NOT IN (SELECT age, bmi, charges FROM train_data)
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(SQRT(SUM(POWER((charges - predicted_charges), 2)) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)), 2) AS model_rmse
FROM (
    SELECT 
        age, 
        bmi, 
        charges,
        (0.5 * age + 0.5 * bmi) AS predicted_charges -- Placeholder for linear regression prediction
    FROM 
        test_data
) AS predictions;

samples.question: Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the "importance.score" column.
samples.constraints: Calculate the mean and standard deviation to two decimal places for the "importance.score" column. Ignore any null or missing values in the calculations. The calculations are to be done using standard statistical methods without applying any transformations or filters to the data.

variants.sql:
WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(importance.score), 2) AS importance_score_mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV(importance.score), 2) AS importance_score_std
    FROM 
        imp.score.ldlr.metabolome
    WHERE 
        importance.score IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    importance_score_mean, 
    importance_score_std 
FROM 
    stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(importance_score), 2) AS importance_score_mean,
    ROUND(SQRT(AVG((importance_score - (SELECT AVG(importance_score) FROM imp.score.ldlr.metabolome)) * (importance_score - (SELECT AVG(importance_score) FROM imp.score.ldlr.metabolome)))), 2) AS importance_score_std
FROM 
    imp.score.ldlr.metabolome
WHERE 
    importance_score IS NOT NULL;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(importance.score), 2) AS importance_score_mean,
        ROUND(STDEV(importance.score), 2) AS importance_score_std
    FROM 
        imp.score.ldlr.metabolome
    WHERE 
        importance.score IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    importance_score_mean, 
    importance_score_std 
FROM 
    stats;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(importance.score, 0)), 2) AS importance_score_mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV(COALESCE(importance.score, 0)), 2) AS importance_score_std
    FROM 
        imp.score.ldlr.metabolome
    WHERE

)
SELECT
    importance_score_std,
    importance_score_mean
