    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(age_1st) > AVG(age_3rd) THEN 'Yes' 
        ELSE 'No' 
    END AS significance
FROM (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Age) AS age_1st 
    FROM titanic 
    WHERE Pclass = 1 AND Age IS NOT NULL
) AS first_class,
(
    SELECT 
        AVG(Age) AS age_3rd 
    FROM titanic 
    WHERE Pclass = 3 AND Age IS NOT NULL
) AS third_class;

WITH age_distribution AS (
    SELECT Age, Pclass
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Pclass IN (1, 3) AND Age IS NOT NULL
),
ranked_ages AS (
    SELECT Age, Pclass,
           RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Pclass ORDER BY Age) AS rank,
           COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY Pclass) AS total_count
    FROM age_distribution
),
u_statistic AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(rank) AS U,
        total_count
    FROM ranked_ages
    GROUP BY Pclass
),
mann_whitney_test AS (
    SELECT 
        U,
        total_count,
        (U - (total_count * (total_count + 1) / 4.0)) / 
        SQRT((total_count * (total_count + 1) * (2 * total_count + 1) / 24.0)) AS z_score
    FROM u_statistic
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(z_score) > 1.96 THEN 'Yes' 
        ELSE 'No' 
    END AS significance
FROM mann_whitney_test;

SELECT CASE 
           WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT Age FROM titanic WHERE Pclass = 1 AND Age IS NOT NULL)) > 0 
                AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT Age FROM titanic WHERE Pclass = 3 AND Age IS NOT NULL)) > 0 
                THEN 'Yes' 
           ELSE 'No' 
       END AS significance
WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT Age FROM titanic WHERE Pclass = 1 AND Age IS NOT NULL)) 
      + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT Age FROM titanic WHERE Pclass = 3 AND Age IS NOT NULL)) > 0;

WITH age_distribution AS (
    SELECT Age, Pclass
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Pclass IN (1, 3) AND Age IS NOT NULL
),
ranked_ages AS (
    SELECT Age, Pclass, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Pclass ORDER BY Age) AS rank
    FROM age_distribution
),
count_ages AS (
    SELECT Pclass, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(rank) AS sum_rank
    FROM ranked_ages
    GROUP BY Pclass
),
u_statistic AS (
    SELECT 
        (SUM(rank) - (n * (n + 1) / 2)) AS U
    FROM ranked_ages
    JOIN count_ages ON ranked_ages.Pclass = count_ages.Pclass
    GROUP BY ranked_ages.Pclass
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN MIN(U) < (0.5 * (count_ages.n[1] * count_ages.n[2])) THEN 'Yes' 
        ELSE 'No' 
    END AS significance
FROM u_statistic
JOIN count_ages ON count_ages.Pclass IN (1, 3);

WITH first_class_ages AS (
    SELECT Age
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Pclass = 1 AND Age IS NOT NULL
), 
third_class_ages AS (
    SELECT Age
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Pclass = 3 AND Age IS NOT NULL
) 
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM first_class_ages) > 0 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM third_class_ages) > 0 THEN 
            CASE 
                WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM first_class_ages) < (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM third_class_ages) THEN 'Yes'
                ELSE 'No'
            END
        ELSE 'No'
    END AS significance;

samples.question: Perform comprehensive data preprocessing on the dataset. Handle missing values in the "Embarked" column by imputing them with the mode value. Normalize the "Fare" column using Min-Max scaling. Encode the categorical variable "Sex" using Label Encoding, where "male" is coded as 1 and "female" as 0. Calculate the number of each label after processing "Sex" and the minimum, maximum and mean of "Fare" after scaling.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS male_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS female_count,
    ROUND((Fare - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)) / ((SELECT MAX(Fare) FROM titanic) - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)), 4) AS normalized_fare,
    ROUND(MIN(normalized_fare), 2) AS min_fare,
    ROUND(MAX(normalized_fare), 2) AS max_fare,
    ROUND(AVG(normalized_fare), 4) AS avg_fare
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Embarked IS NULL THEN (SELECT Embarked FROM titanic GROUP BY Embarked ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) 
            ELSE Embarked 
        END AS Embarked,
        CASE 
            WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS Sex,
        Fare
    FROM titanic) AS processed_data;

WITH mode_embarked AS (
    SELECT Embarked, COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM titanic
    GROUP BY Embarked
    ORDER BY count DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
filled_embarked AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        Name,
        Sex,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Ticket,
        Fare,
        Cabin,
        COALESCE(Embarked, (SELECT Embarked FROM mode_embarked)) AS Embarked
    FROM titanic
),
fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        MIN(Fare) AS min_fare,
        MAX(Fare) AS max_fare,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare
    FROM filled_embarked
),
normalized_fare AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        Name,
        CASE 
            WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS Sex,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Ticket,
        (Fare - (SELECT min_fare FROM fare_stats)) / 
        ((SELECT max_fare FROM fare_stats) - (SELECT min_fare FROM fare_stats)) AS normalized_fare,
        Cabin,
        Embarked
    FROM filled_embarked
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Sex = 1 THEN 1 END) AS male_count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Sex = 0 THEN 1 END) AS female_count,
    ROUND(MIN(normalized_fare), 2) AS min_fare,
    ROUND(MAX(normalized_fare), 2) AS max_fare,
    ROUND(AVG(normalized_fare), 4) AS avg_fare
FROM normalized_fare;

SELECT 
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS sex_encoded FROM titanic)) AS sex_encoded_count,
    (SELECT ROUND(MIN(normalized_fare), 2) FROM (SELECT (Fare - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)) / ((SELECT MAX(Fare) FROM titanic) - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)) AS normalized_fare FROM titanic)) AS fare_stats) AS min_fare,
    (SELECT ROUND(MAX(normalized_fare), 2) FROM (SELECT (Fare - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)) / ((SELECT MAX(Fare) FROM titanic) - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)) AS normalized_fare FROM titanic)) AS fare_stats) AS max_fare,
    (SELECT ROUND(AVG(normalized_fare), 2) FROM (SELECT (Fare - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)) / ((SELECT MAX(Fare) FROM titanic) - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)) AS normalized_fare FROM titanic)) AS fare_stats) AS avg_fare
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        (SELECT Embarked FROM titanic GROUP BY Embarked ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) AS mode_embarked, 
        CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Sex, 
        Fare 
    FROM titanic) AS processed_data;

WITH mode_embarked AS (
    SELECT Embarked, COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM titanic
    GROUP BY Embarked
    ORDER BY count DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
normalized_fare AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        (Fare - MIN(Fare) OVER ()) / (MAX(Fare) OVER () - MIN(Fare) OVER ()) AS Fare_Scaled,
        CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Sex_Encoded
    FROM titanic
),
fare_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        MIN(Fare_Scaled) AS Min_Fare,
        MAX(Fare_Scaled) AS Max_Fare,
        ROUND(AVG(Fare_Scaled), 4) AS Mean_Fare
    FROM normalized_fare
),
sex_count AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex_Encoded, 
        COUNT(*) AS Count
    FROM normalized_fare
    GROUP BY Sex_Encoded
)

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN Sex_Encoded = 1 THEN Count ELSE 0 END) AS male_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN Sex_Encoded = 0 THEN Count ELSE 0 END) AS female_count,
    (SELECT Min_Fare FROM fare_summary) AS Min_Fare,
    (SELECT Max_Fare FROM fare_summary) AS Max_Fare,
    (SELECT Mean_Fare FROM fare_summary) AS Mean_Fare
FROM sex_count;

WITH mode_embarked AS (
    SELECT Embarked, COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM titanic
    GROUP BY Embarked
    ORDER BY count DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
normalized_fare AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        Name,
        CASE 
            WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 
            WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN 0 
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS Sex_encoded,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Ticket,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare,
        Cabin,
        COALESCE(Embarked, (SELECT Embarked FROM mode_embarked)) AS Embarked
    FROM titanic
),
fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        MIN(Fare) AS min_fare,
        MAX(Fare) AS max_fare,
        ROUND(AVG(Fare), 4) AS mean_fare
    FROM normalized_fare
),
scaled_fare AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        Name,
        Sex_encoded,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Ticket,
        (Fare - (SELECT min_fare FROM fare_stats)) / 
        NULLIF((SELECT max_fare FROM fare_stats) - (SELECT min_fare FROM fare_stats), 0) AS scaled_fare,
        Cabin,
        Embarked
    FROM normalized_fare
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Sex_encoded = 1 THEN 1 END) AS male_count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Sex_encoded = 0 THEN 1 END) AS female_count,
    ROUND(MIN(scaled_fare), 2) AS min_scaled_fare,
    ROUND(MAX(scaled_fare), 2) AS max_scaled_fare,
    ROUND(AVG(scaled_fare), 4) AS mean_scaled_fare
FROM scaled_fare
WHERE Sex_encoded IS NOT NULL;

samples.question: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient between the age and fare variables for passengers who survived and were in first class.
samples.constraints: Use only passengers that survived and were in the first class. Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between age and fare.

variants.sql:
WITH filtered_passengers AS (
    SELECT Age, Fare
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Survived = 1 AND Pclass = 1
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Age) AS avg_age,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) AS covariance,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age))) AS variance_age,
        SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) AS variance_fare
    FROM filtered_passengers
)
SELECT ROUND(covariance / SQRT(variance_age * variance_fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM stats;

SELECT ROUND(COALESCE((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(Age) AS sum_age, SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare, SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_sq, SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq FROM (SELECT Age, Fare FROM titanic WHERE Survived = 1 AND Pclass = 1) AS first_class_survivors) AS stats WHERE n > 1) * (n * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / (SQRT((n * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * (n * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))), 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient;

WITH filtered_passengers AS (
    SELECT Age, Fare
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Survived = 1 AND Pclass = 1
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Age) AS avg_age,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age) OVER()) * (Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER())) AS cov,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age) OVER()) * (Age - AVG(Age) OVER())) AS var_age,
        SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER()) * (Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER())) AS var_fare
    FROM filtered_passengers
)
SELECT ROUND(cov / SQRT(var_age * var_fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM stats;

WITH filtered_passengers AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Age, 0) AS Age,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare
    FROM 
        titanic
    WHERE 
        Survived = 1 
        AND Pclass = 1
        AND Age IS NOT NULL 
        AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
n AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS count,
           SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
           SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
           SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_sq,
           SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq,
           SUM(Age * Fare) AS sum_age_fare
    FROM filtered_passengers
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (count * sum_age_fare - sum_age * sum_fare) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((count * sum_age_sq - sum_age * sum_age) * (count * sum_fare_sq - sum_fare * sum_fare)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM n
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

samples.question: Perform outlier detection on the fare variable for each passenger class separately. Use the Z-score method and determine the number of outliers in each class.
samples.constraints: Validate outliers using the Z-score method with a threshold of 3. Use separate calculations for each passenger class (1, 2, and 3).

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    Pclass,
    COUNT(*) AS outliers
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        Pclass,
        Fare,
        (Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER (PARTITION BY Pclass)) / NULLIF(STDDEV(Fare) OVER (PARTITION BY Pclass), 0) AS z_score
    FROM 
        titanic) AS fare_z_scores
WHERE 
    ABS(z_score) > 3
GROUP BY 
    Pclass;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        AVG(Fare) AS mean_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev_fare
    FROM 
        titanic
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass
),
outlier_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        t.Pclass,
        COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
    FROM 
        titanic t
    JOIN 
        fare_stats fs ON t.Pclass = fs.Pclass
    WHERE 
        ABS(t.Fare - fs.mean_fare) > 3 * fs.stddev_fare
    GROUP BY 
        t.Pclass
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN outlier_count END), 0) AS class1_outliers,
    COALESCE(MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN outlier_count END), 0) AS class2_outliers,
    COALESCE(MAX(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN outlier_count END), 0) AS class3_outliers
FROM 
    outlier_counts;

SELECT 
    Pclass,
    COUNT(*) AS outliers
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        Pclass,
        Fare,
        (Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic AS t2 WHERE t2.Pclass = t1.Pclass)) / 
        (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic AS t3 WHERE t3.Pclass = t1.Pclass) AS z_score
     FROM 
        titanic AS t1) AS z_scores
WHERE 
    ABS(z_score) > 3
GROUP BY 
    Pclass;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev_fare
    FROM 
        titanic
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass
),
fare_with_zscore AS (
    SELECT 
        t.PassengerId,
        t.Fare,
        t.Pclass,
        (t.Fare - fs.avg_fare) / fs.stddev_fare AS z_score
    FROM 
        titanic t
    JOIN 
        fare_stats fs ON t.Pclass = fs.Pclass
)
SELECT 
    Pclass,
    COUNT(*) AS outliers
FROM 
    fare_with_zscore
WHERE 
    ABS(z_score) > 3
GROUP BY 
    Pclass;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev_fare
    FROM 
        titanic
    WHERE 
        Fare IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        t.Pclass,
        COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
    FROM 
        titanic t
    JOIN 
        fare_stats fs ON t.Pclass = fs.Pclass
    WHERE 
        t.Fare IS NOT NULL 
        AND (ABS(t.Fare - fs.avg_fare) / NULLIF(fs.stddev_fare, 0)) > 3
    GROUP BY 
        t.Pclass
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN outlier_count END), 0) AS class1_outliers,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN outlier_count END), 0) AS class2_outliers,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN outlier_count END), 0) AS class3_outliers
FROM 
    outliers;

samples.question: 1. Which column(s) contain missing values in the dataset?
samples.constraints: This question requires a straightforward identification of columns with missing values in the dataset. Only count the missing values in columns where the data type is 'object' (i.e., strings). Do not include columns of other data types and consider a "missing value" as one that is recorded as 'NaN', 'na', 'null', or an empty string in the dataset.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(author) < COUNT(*) THEN 'author' 
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS missing_columns_in_object_type_author,
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(urlToImage) < COUNT(*) THEN 'urlToImage' 
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS missing_columns_in_object_type_urlToImage
FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
WHERE author IS NULL OR author = '' OR urlToImage IS NULL OR urlToImage = '';

WITH missing_values AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(CASE WHEN author IS NULL OR author = '' OR author = 'NaN' OR author = 'na' THEN 1 END) AS author_missing,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN urlToImage IS NULL OR urlToImage = '' OR urlToImage = 'NaN' OR urlToImage = 'na' THEN 1 END) AS urlToImage_missing
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
)
SELECT 
    CASE WHEN author_missing > 0 THEN 'author' END AS missing_author,
    CASE WHEN urlToImage_missing > 0 THEN 'urlToImage' END AS missing_urlToImage
FROM missing_values
WHERE author_missing > 0 OR urlToImage_missing > 0;

SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(column_name) AS missing_columns_in_object_type
FROM (
    SELECT 'author' AS column_name
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE author IS NULL OR author = '' OR author = 'NaN' OR author = 'na'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'description' AS column_name
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE description IS NULL OR description = '' OR description = 'NaN' OR description = 'na'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'source' AS column_name
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE source IS NULL OR source = '' OR source = 'NaN' OR source = 'na'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'title' AS column_name
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE title IS NULL OR title = '' OR title = 'NaN' OR title = 'na'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'url' AS column_name
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE url IS NULL OR url = '' OR url = 'NaN' OR url = 'na'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'urlToImage' AS column_name
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE urlToImage IS NULL OR urlToImage = '' OR urlToImage = 'NaN' OR urlToImage = 'na'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'text' AS column_name
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE text IS NULL OR text = '' OR text = 'NaN' OR text = 'na'
) AS missing_columns
WHERE column_name IS NOT NULL;

WITH missing_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(CASE WHEN author IS NULL OR author = '' OR author = 'NaN' OR author = 'null' THEN 1 END) AS author_missing,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN urlToImage IS NULL OR urlToImage = '' OR urlToImage = 'NaN' OR urlToImage = 'null' THEN 1 END) AS urlToImage_missing
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
)
SELECT 
    CASE WHEN author_missing > 0 THEN 'author' END AS author_missing_column,
    CASE WHEN urlToImage_missing > 0 THEN 'urlToImage' END AS urlToImage_missing_column
FROM missing_counts;

WITH missing_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(CASE WHEN author IS NULL OR author = '' OR author IN ('NaN', 'na', 'null') THEN 1 END) AS author_missing,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN urlToImage IS NULL OR urlToImage = '' OR urlToImage IN ('NaN', 'na', 'null') THEN 1 END) AS urlToImage_missing
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
)
SELECT 
    CASE WHEN author_missing > 0 THEN 'author' END AS author_missing_column,
    CASE WHEN urlToImage_missing > 0 THEN 'urlToImage' END AS urlToImage_missing_column
FROM missing_counts
WHERE author_missing > 0 OR urlToImage_missing > 0;

samples.question: 2. Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the "compound" sentiment score column.
samples.constraints: Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the 'compound' sentiment score using standard statistical methods. Please use a standard approach and do not use any approximations or assumptions. Note that the 'compound' column contains no missing values according to the scenario information.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(compound), 2) AS compound_mean, ROUND(STDEV(compound), 2) AS compound_std FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(compound) AS compound_mean,
        STDDEV(compound) AS compound_std
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(compound_mean, 2) AS compound_mean,
    ROUND(compound_std, 2) AS compound_std
FROM 
    stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(compound), 3) AS compound_mean, 
    ROUND(SQRT(AVG((compound - (SELECT AVG(compound) FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df)) * (compound - (SELECT AVG(compound) FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df)))), 2) AS compound_std 
FROM 
    fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(compound), 2) AS compound_mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV(compound), 2) AS compound_std
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
)
SELECT compound_mean, compound_std FROM stats;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(compound, 0)), 2) AS compound_mean,
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(COALESCE(compound, 0) - ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(compound, 0)), 2), 2)), 1), 2) AS compound_std
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE compound IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT compound_mean, compound_std 
FROM stats;

samples.question: 3. Is there any correlation between the "neg" and "pos" sentiment score columns? If so, what is the correlation coefficient?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between 'neg' and 'pos' sentiment scores. If the Pearson correlation coefficient (absolute value) is close to 1, it means that there exists a strong correlation. If it is close to 0, it means that there exists a weak or no correlation. If the coefficient is positive, the correlation is positive; if negative, the correlation is negative.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND((SUM(neg * pos) - (SUM(neg) * SUM(pos) / COUNT(*))) / 
              (SQRT((SUM(neg * neg) - (SUM(neg) * SUM(neg) / COUNT(*))) * 
              (SUM(pos * pos) - (SUM(pos) * SUM(pos) / COUNT(*)))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(neg) AS sum_neg,
        SUM(pos) AS sum_pos,
        SUM(neg * neg) AS sum_neg_sq,
        SUM(pos * pos) AS sum_pos_sq,
        SUM(neg * pos) AS sum_neg_pos
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_neg_pos - sum_neg * sum_pos) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_neg_sq - sum_neg * sum_neg) * (n * sum_pos_sq - sum_pos * sum_pos))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        neg,
        pos,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS n,
        SUM(neg) OVER () AS sum_neg,
        SUM(pos) OVER () AS sum_pos,
        SUM(neg * neg) OVER () AS sum_neg_sq,
        SUM(pos * pos) OVER () AS sum_pos_sq,
        SUM(neg * pos) OVER () AS sum_neg_pos
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
),
correlation_coefficients AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_neg,
        sum_pos,
        sum_neg_sq,
        sum_pos_sq,
        sum_neg_pos,
        (n * sum_neg_pos - sum_neg * sum_pos) / 
        SQRT((n * sum_neg_sq - sum_neg * sum_neg) * (n * sum_pos_sq - sum_pos * sum_pos)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM correlation_data
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation_coefficients;

WITH valid_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(neg, 0) AS neg_score, 
        COALESCE(pos, 0) AS pos_score 
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df 
    WHERE 
        neg IS NOT NULL AND 
        pos IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(neg_score) AS sum_neg,
        SUM(pos_score) AS sum_pos,
        SUM(neg_score * pos_score) AS sum_neg_pos,
        SUM(neg_score * neg_score) AS sum_neg_sq,
        SUM(pos_score * pos_score) AS sum_pos_sq
    FROM 
        valid_scores
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((n * sum_neg_pos - sum_neg * sum_pos) / 
    NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_neg_sq - sum_neg * sum_neg) * (n * sum_pos_sq - sum_pos * sum_pos)), 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    stats;

samples.question: 1. Identify and remove any outliers in the "neg" sentiment score column using the Z-score method, where Z is defined as (value - mean) / standard deviation. Assume a data point to be an outlier if its Z-score is greater than 3 or less than -3. After removing outliers, calculate the new mean and standard deviation for the "neg" sentiment score column.
samples.constraints: Z-score is calculated with its general mathematical formula (value - mean) / standard deviation. Consider a data point as an outlier if its Z-score is greater than 3 or less than -3. Do this for the "neg" sentiment score column only.

variants.sql:
WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(neg) AS mean_neg,
        STDDEV(neg) AS std_dev_neg
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
),
filtered AS (
    SELECT 
        neg
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df,
        stats
    WHERE 
        (neg - mean_neg) / std_dev_neg BETWEEN -3 AND 3
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(neg), 2) AS mean_neg,
    ROUND(STDDEV(neg), 2) AS std_dev_neg
FROM 
    filtered;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(neg), 2) AS mean_neg, 
    ROUND(STDDEV(neg), 2) AS std_dev_neg 
FROM 
    fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df 
WHERE 
    neg NOT IN (
        SELECT neg 
        FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df 
        WHERE 
            (neg - (SELECT AVG(neg) FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df)) / 
            (SELECT STDDEV(neg) FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df) > 3 
            OR 
            (neg - (SELECT AVG(neg) FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df)) / 
            (SELECT STDDEV(neg) FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df) < -3
    );

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(neg) AS mean_neg, 
        STDDEV(neg) AS std_dev_neg
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
),
filtered AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        (neg - (SELECT mean_neg FROM stats)) / (SELECT std_dev_neg FROM stats) AS z_score
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(neg), 2) AS mean_neg, 
    ROUND(STDDEV(neg), 2) AS std_dev_neg
FROM 
    filtered
WHERE 
    z_score BETWEEN -3 AND 3;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(neg) AS mean_neg, 
        STDDEV(neg) AS std_dev_neg
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE 
        neg IS NOT NULL
),
filtered_data AS (
    SELECT 
        neg,
        (neg - COALESCE((SELECT mean_neg FROM stats), 0)) / NULLIF(COALESCE((SELECT std_dev_neg FROM stats), 1), 0) AS z_score
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE 
        neg IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(neg), 2) AS mean_neg, 
    ROUND(STDDEV(neg), 2) AS std_dev_neg
FROM 
    filtered_data
WHERE 
    z_score <= 3 AND z_score >= -3;

samples.question: 2. Perform a correlation analysis between the sentiment scores ("neg", "neu", "pos") and the article length ("text" column non-space character count) for articles published by the source "ABC News". Identify any significant correlations between the variables and provide a brief explanation of the findings.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(LENGTH(text) - LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', ''))), 2) AS article_length,
    ROUND(AVG(neg), 2) AS avg_neg,
    ROUND(AVG(neu), 2) AS avg_neu,
    ROUND(AVG(pos), 2) AS avg_pos,
    ROUND(CORR(neg, LENGTH(text) - LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', ''))), 2) AS neg_length_corr,
    ROUND(CORR(neu, LENGTH(text) - LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', ''))), 2) AS neu_length_corr,
    ROUND(CORR(pos, LENGTH(text) - LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', ''))), 2) AS pos_length_corr
FROM 
    fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
WHERE 
    source = 'abc-news';

WITH article_lengths AS (
    SELECT 
        author,
        LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', '')) AS text_length
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE 
        source = 'abc-news'
),
sentiment_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        neg,
        neu,
        pos
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE 
        source = 'abc-news'
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(pos, text_length), 2) AS pos_length_corr,
    ROUND(CORR(neu, text_length), 2) AS neu_length_corr
FROM 
    sentiment_scores, article_lengths;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(neg_length_corr), 2) AS neg_length_corr,
    ROUND(AVG(neu_length_corr), 2) AS neu_length_corr,
    ROUND(AVG(pos_length_corr), 2) AS pos_length_corr
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (LENGTH(text) - LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', ''))) AS length,
        neg,
        neu,
        pos,
        (neg * (LENGTH(text) - LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', '')))) AS neg_length_corr,
        (neu * (LENGTH(text) - LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', '')))) AS neu_length_corr,
        (pos * (LENGTH(text) - LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', '')))) AS pos_length_corr
    FROM fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE source = 'abc-news'
) AS correlations;

WITH article_length AS (
    SELECT 
        author,
        source,
        LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', '')) AS length_non_space,
        neg,
        neu,
        pos
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE 
        source = 'ABC News'
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(length_non_space, neg), 2) AS neg_length_corr,
        ROUND(CORR(length_non_space, neu), 2) AS neu_length_corr,
        ROUND(CORR(length_non_space, pos), 2) AS pos_length_corr
    FROM 
        article_length
)
SELECT 
    pos_length_corr,
    neu_length_corr
FROM 
    correlation;

WITH article_lengths AS (
    SELECT 
        author,
        source,
        LENGTH(REPLACE(text, ' ', '')) AS text_length,
        COALESCE(neg, 0) AS neg,
        COALESCE(neu, 0) AS neu,
        COALESCE(pos, 0) AS pos
    FROM 
        fb_articles_20180822_20180829_df
    WHERE 
        source = 'abc-news' AND 
        text IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT
        ROUND(
            (SUM((text_length - AVG(text_length)) * (neg - AVG(neg))) / 
            NULLIF(SQRT(SUM(POWER(text_length - AVG(text_length), 2)) * SUM(POWER(neg - AVG(neg), 2))), 0)), 2) AS neg_length_corr,
        ROUND(
            (SUM((text_length - AVG(text_length)) * (neu - AVG(neu))) / 
            NULLIF(SQRT(SUM(POWER(text_length - AVG(text_length), 2)) * SUM(POWER(neu - AVG(neu), 2))), 0)), 2) AS neu_length_corr,
        ROUND(
            (SUM((text_length - AVG(text_length)) * (pos - AVG(pos))) / 
            NULLIF(SQRT(SUM(POWER(text_length - AVG(text_length), 2)) * SUM(POWER(pos - AVG(pos), 2))), 0)), 2) AS pos_length_corr
    FROM 
        article_lengths
)
SELECT 
    neg_length_corr, 
    neu_length_corr, 
    pos_length_corr 
FROM 
    correlation;

samples.question: Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the abs_diffsel column.

samples.question: Find the site identifier(s) with the highest positive_diffsel value.

WITH MaxPositiveDiff AS (

TopSites AS (

samples.question: Calculate the correlation coefficient between the positive_diffsel and negative_diffsel columns.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(CORR(positive_diffsel, negative_diffsel), 2) AS correlation_coefficient FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(positive_diffsel) AS sum_x,
        SUM(negative_diffsel) AS sum_y,
        SUM(positive_diffsel * negative_diffsel) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(positive_diffsel * positive_diffsel) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(negative_diffsel * negative_diffsel) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

SELECT ROUND((SELECT COUNT(*) * SUM(positive_diffsel * negative_diffsel) - SUM(positive_diffsel) * SUM(negative_diffsel) 
               FROM (SELECT positive_diffsel, negative_diffsel FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel) 
               AS subquery) / 
              (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(positive_diffsel * positive_diffsel) - SUM(positive_diffsel) * SUM(positive_diffsel)) * 
                     (COUNT(*) * SUM(negative_diffsel * negative_diffsel) - SUM(negative_diffsel) * SUM(negative_diffsel))), 2) 
              AS correlation_coefficient;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(positive_diffsel) AS sum_x,
        SUM(negative_diffsel) AS sum_y,
        SUM(positive_diffsel * negative_diffsel) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(positive_diffsel * positive_diffsel) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(negative_diffsel * negative_diffsel) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_x,
        sum_y,
        sum_xy,
        sum_xx,
        sum_yy,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(COALESCE(positive_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_pos,
        SUM(COALESCE(negative_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_neg,
        SUM(COALESCE(positive_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(negative_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_prod,
        SUM(COALESCE(positive_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(positive_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_pos_sq,
        SUM(COALESCE(negative_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(negative_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_neg_sq
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
    WHERE 
        positive_diffsel IS NOT NULL AND 
        negative_diffsel IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_pos,
        sum_neg,
        sum_prod,
        sum_pos_sq,
        sum_neg_sq,
        (n * sum_prod - sum_pos * sum_neg) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_pos_sq - sum_pos * sum_pos) * (n * sum_neg_sq - sum_neg * sum_neg)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

samples.question: Identify the site(s) with outliers in the abs_diffsel column using the interquartile range (IQR) method. An outlier is defined as a value that is below Q1 - 1.5*IQR or above Q3 + 1.5*IQR. Provide the site identifier(s) and the corresponding absolute difference in selection values for the outliers.
samples.constraints: Use the interquartile range (IQR) method for outlier detection. Calculate the IQR as the difference between the first quartile (Q1) and the third quartile (Q3) of the abs_diffsel column. Consider a value as an outlier if it is below Q1 - 1.5*IQR or above Q3 + 1.5*IQR.

variants.sql:
SELECT site, abs_diffsel 
FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel 
WHERE abs_diffsel < (SELECT ROUND(quantile(abs_diffsel, 0.25), 2) - 1.5 * (ROUND(quantile(abs_diffsel, 0.75), 2) - ROUND(quantile(abs_diffsel, 0.25), 2)) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel) 
   OR abs_diffsel > (SELECT ROUND(quantile(abs_diffsel, 0.75), 2) + 1.5 * (ROUND(quantile(abs_diffsel, 0.75), 2) - ROUND(quantile(abs_diffsel, 0.25), 2)) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel);

WITH quartiles AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) AS Q3
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM 
        quartiles
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        site,
        abs_diffsel
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel,
        iqr
    WHERE 
        abs_diffsel < Q1 - 1.5 * IQR OR abs_diffsel > Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
)
SELECT 
    GROUP_CONCAT(site) AS site_identifiers,
    ROUND(GROUP_CONCAT(abs_diffsel), 2) AS outlier_values
FROM 
    outliers;

SELECT site, ROUND(abs_diffsel, 2) AS outlier_value
FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
WHERE abs_diffsel < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR 
                      FROM (SELECT 
                              percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) AS Q1, 
                              percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) AS Q3, 
                              (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) - 
                               percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel)) AS IQR 
                            FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel)) 
   OR abs_diffsel > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR 
                      FROM (SELECT 
                              percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) AS Q1, 
                              percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) AS Q3, 
                              (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) - 
                               percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel)) AS IQR 
                            FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel));

WITH quartiles AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel) AS Q3
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM 
        quartiles
)
SELECT 
    site,
    ROUND(abs_diffsel, 2) AS outlier_value
FROM 
    ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel,
    iqr
WHERE 
    abs_diffsel < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR abs_diffsel > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR);

WITH quartiles AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(COALESCE(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel), 0), 2) AS Q1,
        ROUND(COALESCE(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY abs_diffsel), 0), 2) AS Q3
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM 
        quartiles
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        site,
        ROUND(COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0), 2) AS abs_diffsel
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel,
        iqr
    WHERE 
        abs_diffsel IS NOT NULL 
        AND (abs_diffsel < Q1 - 1.5 * IQR OR abs_diffsel > Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
)
SELECT 
    GROUP_CONCAT(site) AS site_identifiers,
    GROUP_CONCAT(abs_diffsel) AS outlier_values
FROM 
    outliers;

samples.question: Perform comprehensive data preprocessing for the given dataset. This should include data cleaning, handling missing values, and feature engineering. Provide the cleaned dataset, and if any missing values were found, explain the strategy used to handle them. Additionally, generate a new feature called "diff_range" that represents the range of difference in selection (max_diffsel - min_diffsel) for each site.
samples.constraints: For data cleaning, ensure that there are no duplicated records or inconsistencies in the dataset. If missing values are found in any of the columns, use mean imputation to fill these missing values. For feature engineering, create a new column "diff_range" calculated as the difference between max_diffsel column and min_diffsel column.

variants.sql:
SELECT site,

       ROUND(min_diffsel, 2) AS min_diffsel, 
       ROUND(max_diffsel - min_diffsel, 2) AS diff_range
FROM (SELECT DISTINCT site,

             COALESCE(max_diffsel, (SELECT AVG(max_diffsel) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel)) AS max_diffsel, 
             COALESCE(min_diffsel, (SELECT AVG(min_diffsel) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel)) AS min_diffsel
      FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel);

WITH cleaned_data AS (

        COALESCE(positive_diffsel, (SELECT AVG(positive_diffsel) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel)) AS positive_diffsel,

    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel

final_data AS (

SELECT * FROM final_data;

SELECT site, 
       ROUND(abs_diffsel, 2) AS abs_diffsel, 
       ROUND(positive_diffsel, 2) AS positive_diffsel, 
       ROUND(negative_diffsel, 2) AS negative_diffsel, 
       ROUND(max_diffsel, 2) AS max_diffsel,

FROM (SELECT DISTINCT site,

                     COALESCE(negative_diffsel, (SELECT AVG(negative_diffsel) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel)) AS negative_diffsel,

samples.question: Explore the distribution of the abs_diffsel column and determine if it adheres to a normal distribution by calculating skewness and kurtosis. The skewness and kurtosis values should be calculated using Fisher’s method. If the skewness value is between -0.5 and 0.5, the data is fairly symmetrical. If the kurtosis value is around 0, then a normal distribution is often assumed.
samples.constraints: Perform the calculations using non-parametric methods, specifically the skew and kurtosis functions provided in the scipy.stats module of Python. All numeric values should be rounded to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND((SUM((abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel)) * (abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel)) * (abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel))) / COUNT(*) / (SUM((abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel)) * (abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel))) / COUNT(*))^(3/2)), 2) AS skewness_value,
    ROUND((SUM((abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel)) * (abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel)) * (abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel)) * (abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel))) / COUNT(*) / (SUM((abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel)) * (abs_diffsel - AVG(abs_diffsel))) / COUNT(*))^2) - 3, 2) AS kurtosis_value
FROM 
    ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(abs_diffsel) AS sum_x,
        SUM(abs_diffsel * abs_diffsel) AS sum_x2,
        SUM(abs_diffsel * abs_diffsel * abs_diffsel) AS sum_x3,
        SUM(abs_diffsel * abs_diffsel * abs_diffsel * abs_diffsel) AS sum_x4
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
calculations AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_x,
        sum_x2,
        sum_x3,
        sum_x4,
        ROUND((n * sum_x3 - sum_x * sum_x2) / (n * (n - 1) * (n - 2) * (sum_x2 / n - (sum_x / n) * (sum_x / n))), 2) AS skewness,
        ROUND((n * (n + 1) * sum_x4 - 4 * (n - 1) * (n - 1) * sum_x2 * sum_x2 / (n * n) + 6 * (n - 1) * sum_x2 * sum_x / (n * n) - 3 * (n - 1) * (n - 1) * (n - 2) / (n * n)) / (n * (n - 1) * (n - 2) * (n - 3)), 2) AS kurtosis
    FROM stats
)
SELECT skewness, kurtosis FROM calculations;

SELECT 
    ROUND((SUM((abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel) * (abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel) * (abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel)) / count(abs_diffsel)) / 
    (SUM((abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel) * (abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel)) / count(abs_diffsel))^(3/2), 2) AS skewness_value,
    ROUND((SUM((abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel) * (abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel) * (abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel) * (abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel)) / 
    count(abs_diffsel)) / (SUM((abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel) * (abs_diffsel - avg_abs_diffsel)) / count(abs_diffsel))^2 - 3, 2) AS kurtosis_value
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        abs_diffsel, 
        (SELECT AVG(abs_diffsel) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel) AS avg_abs_diffsel 
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel);

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(abs_diffsel), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV(abs_diffsel), 2) AS stddev,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SUM(POWER((abs_diffsel - mean) / stddev, 3)) * (n / ((n - 1) * (n - 2))), 2) AS skewness_value
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel, stats
),
kurtosis AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SUM(POWER((abs_diffsel - mean) / stddev, 4)) * (n * (n + 1) / ((n - 1) * (n - 2) * (n - 3))) - (3 * POWER(n - 1, 2) / ((n - 2) * (n - 3))), 2) AS kurtosis_value
    FROM 
        ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel, stats
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT skewness_value FROM skewness) AS skewness_value,
    (SELECT kurtosis_value FROM kurtosis) AS kurtosis_value;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_x,
        SUM(COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_x2,
        SUM(COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_x3,
        SUM(COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0) * COALESCE(abs_diffsel, 0)) AS sum_x4
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
    WHERE abs_diffsel IS NOT NULL
),
calc AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        ROUND((n * sum_x3 - sum_x * sum_x2 / n) / NULLIF(n * (n - 1) * (n - 2), 0), 2) AS skewness,
        ROUND((n * sum_x4 - sum_x2 * sum_x2 / n) / NULLIF(n * (n - 1) * (n - 2) * (n - 3), 0) - 3, 2) AS kurtosis
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    skewness, 
    kurtosis 
FROM calc;

samples.question: Utilize machine learning techniques to classify the sites into two categories based on their positive_diffsel values, with values less than or equal to the mean defined as 'low' selection, and the rest as 'high'. Split the dataset into training and testing sets with an 80:20 ratio using a specified random state of 42. Train a logistic regression model on the training set, and evaluate its performance on the testing set using accuracy as a metric.
samples.constraints: Use scikit-learn's Logistic Regression for your classifier model, 'liblinear' solver for the Logistic Regression, and a random state of 42 when splitting the data and building the model. All numeric values should be rounded to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN positive_diffsel <= (SELECT AVG(positive_diffsel) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 2) AS accuracy_score
FROM (
    SELECT site, positive_diffsel
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
    ORDER BY RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel) * 0.2
) AS test_set;

WITH mean_positive_diffsel AS (
    SELECT ROUND(AVG(positive_diffsel), 2) AS mean_value
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
classified_sites AS (
    SELECT site,
           positive_diffsel,
           CASE 
               WHEN positive_diffsel <= (SELECT mean_value FROM mean_positive_diffsel) THEN 'low'
               ELSE 'high'
           END AS selection_category
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
training_set AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM classified_sites
),
split_sets AS (
    SELECT *,
           CASE 
               WHEN rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM training_set) THEN 'train'
               ELSE 'test'
           END AS set_type
    FROM training_set
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN selection_category = 'high' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 2) AS accuracy_score
FROM split_sets
WHERE set_type = 'test';

SELECT ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN positive_diffsel <= (SELECT AVG(positive_diffsel) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 2) AS accuracy_score
FROM (
    SELECT site, positive_diffsel, 
           CASE WHEN positive_diffsel <= (SELECT AVG(positive_diffsel) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel) THEN 'low' ELSE 'high' END AS selection_category
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
    ORDER BY RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel) * 0.8
) AS training_set;

WITH mean_positive_diffsel AS (
    SELECT ROUND(AVG(positive_diffsel), 2) AS mean_value
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
classified_sites AS (
    SELECT site,
           positive_diffsel,
           CASE 
               WHEN positive_diffsel <= (SELECT mean_value FROM mean_positive_diffsel) THEN 'low'
               ELSE 'high'
           END AS selection_category
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
),
site_with_row_number AS (
    SELECT site, selection_category,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM classified_sites
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_sites,
       SUM(CASE WHEN selection_category = 'high' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN selection_category = 'low' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS low_count,
       ROUND(1.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN selection_category = 'high' AND rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM site_with_row_number) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM site_with_row_number) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 2) AS accuracy_score
FROM site_with_row_number;

WITH mean_positive_diffsel AS (
    SELECT ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(positive_diffsel, 0)), 2) AS mean_value
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
    WHERE positive_diffsel IS NOT NULL
),
classified_sites AS (
    SELECT site,
           ROUND(positive_diffsel, 2) AS positive_diffsel,
           CASE 
               WHEN COALESCE(positive_diffsel, 0) <= (SELECT mean_value FROM mean_positive_diffsel) THEN 'low'
               ELSE 'high'
           END AS selection_category
    FROM ferret-Pitt-2-preinf-lib2-100_sitediffsel
    WHERE positive_diffsel IS NOT NULL
),
training_set AS (
    SELECT site, selection_category
    FROM classified_sites
    ORDER BY RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM classified_sites) * 0.8
),
testing_set AS (
    SELECT site, selection_category
    FROM classified_sites
    WHERE site NOT IN (SELECT site FROM training_set)
)
SELECT ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN training_set.selection_category = testing_set.selection_category THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(testing_set.site), 0), 2) AS accuracy_score
FROM testing_set
JOIN training_set ON testing_set.site = training_set.site;

samples.question: What is the average duration of a budget year for all departments?
samples.constraints: Calculate the duration of each department’s budget year in days, by subtracting the budget_year_start from budget_year_end. Afterwards, calculate the mean of these durations using a built-in Python function.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(julianday(budget_year_end) - julianday(budget_year_start)), 2) AS average_duration
FROM city_departments_in_current_budget;

WITH budget_durations AS (
    SELECT 
        coa_dept_id,
        julianday(budget_year_end) - julianday(budget_year_start) AS duration_days
    FROM 
        city_departments_in_current_budget
),
average_duration AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(duration_days), 2) AS average_duration
    FROM 
        budget_durations
)
SELECT 
    average_duration 
FROM 
    average_duration;

SELECT ROUND(AVG(j.duration), 2) AS average_duration
FROM (
    SELECT 
        julianday(budget_year_end) - julianday(budget_year_start) AS duration
    FROM 
        city_departments_in_current_budget
) AS j;

WITH budget_durations AS (
    SELECT 
        Department_Name,
        coa_dept_id,
        JULIANDAY(budget_year_end) - JULIANDAY(budget_year_start) AS duration_days
    FROM 
        city_departments_in_current_budget
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(duration_days), 2) AS average_duration
FROM 
    budget_durations;

WITH budget_durations AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(DATEDIFF(
            DATE(NULLIF(budget_year_end, '')),
            DATE(NULLIF(budget_year_start, ''))
        ), 0) AS duration_days
    FROM city_departments_in_current_budget
    WHERE budget_year_start IS NOT NULL AND budget_year_end IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(duration_days, 0)), 2) AS average_duration
FROM budget_durations;

samples.question: What is the mean batting average of the players in the dataset?
samples.constraints: Consider only the players who have a recorded batting average. Do not include the one player with a missing batting average into your calculation. Report your answer rounded off to three decimal places.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(batting_average), 3) AS mean_batting_average FROM baseball_data WHERE batting_average IS NOT NULL;

WITH valid_players AS (
    SELECT batting_average
    FROM baseball_data
    WHERE batting_average IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(batting_average), 3) AS mean_batting_average
FROM valid_players;

SELECT ROUND(AVG(batting_average), 3) AS mean_batting_average
FROM (SELECT batting_average FROM baseball_data WHERE batting_average IS NOT NULL);

WITH batting_averages AS (
    SELECT batting_average
    FROM baseball_data
    WHERE batting_average IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(batting_average), 3) AS mean_batting_average
FROM batting_averages;

samples.question: Are the number of home runs hit by the players normally distributed?
samples.constraints:
Assess whether the data is normally distributed using the Shapiro-Wilk test for normality with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05. Exclude the player with a missing value of home runs in your calculations. 
If the p-value is less than 0.05, report that the distribution is not normal. If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that the distribution is normal.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(*) < 3 THEN 'not_normal' 
        ELSE 
            CASE 
                WHEN ROUND(AVG(number_of_home_runs), 2) < 0.05 THEN 'not_normal' 
                ELSE 'normal' 
            END 
    END AS normality_test
FROM baseball_data
WHERE number_of_home_runs IS NOT NULL;

WITH home_runs_data AS (
    SELECT number_of_home_runs
    FROM baseball_data
    WHERE number_of_home_runs IS NOT NULL
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(number_of_home_runs) AS mean,
        ROUND(STDEV(number_of_home_runs), 2) AS stddev
    FROM home_runs_data
),
normality_result AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN (n < 3) THEN 'not_normal'
            ELSE 
                CASE 
                    WHEN (ROUND((SUM((number_of_home_runs - mean) * (number_of_home_runs - mean)) / (n - 1)), 2) / (stddev * stddev)) < 0.05 THEN 'not_normal'
                    ELSE 'normal'
                END
        END AS normality_test
    FROM home_runs_data, shapiro_test
)
SELECT normality_test FROM normality_result;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM baseball_data WHERE number_of_home_runs IS NOT NULL) < 3 THEN 'not_normal'
        ELSE 
            CASE 
                WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM baseball_data WHERE number_of_home_runs IS NOT NULL) = 0 THEN 'not_normal'
                ELSE 
                    CASE 
                        WHEN ROUND((SELECT AVG(number_of_home_runs) FROM baseball_data WHERE number_of_home_runs IS NOT NULL), 2) < 0.05 THEN 'not_normal'
                        ELSE 'normal'
                    END 
            END 
    END AS normality_test;

WITH home_runs_data AS (
    SELECT number_of_home_runs
    FROM baseball_data
    WHERE number_of_home_runs IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(number_of_home_runs) AS mean,
        STDDEV(number_of_home_runs) AS stddev
    FROM home_runs_data
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        mean,
        stddev,
        ROUND((SUM(POWER((number_of_home_runs - mean), 4)) / n) / POWER(stddev, 4), 2) AS statistic
    FROM home_runs_data, stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN statistic < 0.05 THEN 'not_normal' 
        ELSE 'normal' 
    END AS normality_test
FROM shapiro_test;

WITH valid_home_runs AS (
    SELECT 
        number_of_home_runs
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    WHERE 
        number_of_home_runs IS NOT NULL
),
home_run_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(number_of_home_runs) AS mean,
        STDDEV(number_of_home_runs) AS stddev
    FROM 
        valid_home_runs
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        mean,
        stddev,
        (n - 1) * (1 - (SUM(POWER((number_of_home_runs - mean) / stddev, 4)) / (n * POWER(stddev, 4)))) AS W,
        (POWER(n, 2) * (n - 1)) / (n * (n - 2)) AS alpha
    FROM 
        valid_home_runs, home_run_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (W / alpha) < 0.05 THEN 'not_normal' 
        ELSE 'normal' 
    END AS normality_test
FROM 
    shapiro_test;

samples.question: What is the average number of runs scored by players who are eligible for free agency compared to players who are not eligible for free agency?
samples.constraints: Use the 'groupby' function on the 'indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility' column to group the data by whether a player is eligible for free agency or not. Then use the 'mean' function on the 'number_of_runs' column to find the average number of runs scored by these two groups of players.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility = 0 THEN number_of_runs END), 2) AS average_runs_by_not_eligible_for_free_agency,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility = 1 THEN number_of_runs END), 2) AS average_runs_by_eligible_for_free_agency
FROM baseball_data;

WITH free_agency_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility,
        AVG(number_of_runs) AS average_runs
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    GROUP BY 
        indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(CASE WHEN indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility = 0 THEN average_runs END, 2) AS average_runs_by_not_eligible_for_free_agency,
    ROUND(CASE WHEN indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility = 1 THEN average_runs END, 2) AS average_runs_by_eligible_for_free_agency
FROM 
    free_agency_stats;

WITH avg_runs AS (
    SELECT 
        indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility,
        ROUND(AVG(number_of_runs), 2) AS average_runs
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    GROUP BY 
        indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility
)
SELECT 
    MAX(CASE WHEN indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility = 0 THEN average_runs END) AS average_runs_by_not_eligible_for_free_agency,
    MAX(CASE WHEN indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility = 1 THEN average_runs END) AS average_runs_by_eligible_for_free_agency
FROM 
    avg_runs;

WITH free_agency_runs AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility, 0) AS free_agency_status,
        COALESCE(number_of_runs, 0) AS runs
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    WHERE 
        indicator_of_free_agency_eligibility IS NOT NULL
        AND number_of_runs IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN free_agency_status = 1 THEN runs END), 2) AS average_runs_by_eligible_for_free_agency,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN free_agency_status = 0 THEN runs END), 2) AS average_runs_by_not_eligible_for_free_agency
FROM 
    free_agency_runs;

samples.question: Is there a significant correlation between the number of doubles hit by a player and their salary? If so, what is the correlation coefficient and p-value?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between the number of doubles hit and player's salary. Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05. Report the p-value associated with the correlation test. Consider the relationship to be significant if the p-value is less than 0.05. If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(number_of_doubles, salary_in_thousands_of_dollars), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(*) > 2 THEN 
            ROUND(2 * (1 - ABS(CORR(number_of_doubles, salary_in_thousands_of_dollars))) / SQRT(COUNT(*) - 2), 4) 
        ELSE 
            NULL 
    END AS p_value
FROM 
    baseball_data;

WITH salary_doubles AS (
    SELECT 
        salary_in_thousands_of_dollars,
        number_of_doubles
    FROM 
        baseball_data
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(salary_in_thousands_of_dollars) AS sum_x,
        SUM(number_of_doubles) AS sum_y,
        SUM(salary_in_thousands_of_dollars * number_of_doubles) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(salary_in_thousands_of_dollars * salary_in_thousands_of_dollars) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(number_of_doubles * number_of_doubles) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        salary_doubles
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN p_value 
        ELSE 'No significant correlation' 
    END AS significance
FROM 
    correlation, p_value;

SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT (SUM(doubles.salary * doubles.salary) * COUNT(*) - SUM(doubles.salary) * SUM(doubles.doubles)) / 
           (SQRT((SUM(doubles.salary * doubles.salary) - (SUM(doubles.salary) * SUM(doubles.salary)) / COUNT(*)) * 
           (SUM(doubles.doubles * doubles.doubles) - (SUM(doubles.doubles) * SUM(doubles.doubles)) / COUNT(*)))) 
           FROM (SELECT salary_in_thousands_of_dollars AS salary, number_of_doubles AS doubles 
                 FROM baseball_data 
                 WHERE number_of_doubles IS NOT NULL AND salary_in_thousands_of_dollars IS NOT NULL) AS doubles), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    (SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN COUNT(*) > 2 THEN 
                ROUND(2 * (1 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT salary_in_thousands_of_dollars, number_of_doubles FROM baseball_data WHERE number_of_doubles IS NOT NULL AND salary_in_thousands_of_dollars IS NOT NULL) AS sub) - 2) / 
                (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT salary_in_thousands_of_dollars, number_of_doubles FROM baseball_data WHERE number_of_doubles IS NOT NULL AND salary_in_thousands_of_dollars IS NOT NULL) AS sub) * 
                (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT salary_in_thousands_of_dollars, number_of_doubles FROM baseball_data WHERE number_of_doubles IS NOT NULL AND salary_in_thousands_of_dollars IS NOT NULL) AS sub) / 
                (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT salary_in_thousands_of_dollars, number_of_doubles FROM baseball_data WHERE number_of_doubles IS NOT NULL AND salary_in_thousands_of_dollars IS NOT NULL) AS sub) 
                , 2) 
            ELSE 1 
        END 
    ) AS p_value;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        salary_in_thousands_of_dollars,
        number_of_doubles,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS n,
        AVG(salary_in_thousands_of_dollars) OVER () AS avg_salary,
        AVG(number_of_doubles) OVER () AS avg_doubles,
        SUM(salary_in_thousands_of_dollars) OVER () AS sum_salary,
        SUM(number_of_doubles) OVER () AS sum_doubles,
        SUM(salary_in_thousands_of_dollars * number_of_doubles) OVER () AS sum_product,
        SUM(salary_in_thousands_of_dollars * salary_in_thousands_of_dollars) OVER () AS sum_salary_sq,
        SUM(number_of_doubles * number_of_doubles) OVER () AS sum_doubles_sq
    FROM 
        baseball_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * sum_product - sum_salary * sum_doubles) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_salary_sq - sum_salary * sum_salary) * (n * sum_doubles_sq - sum_doubles * sum_doubles))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - CDF_NORMAL(ABS(correlation_coefficient) * SQRT((n - 2) / (1 - correlation_coefficient * correlation_coefficient)))) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation, (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM baseball_data)
)
SELECT 
    correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'Significant correlation'
        ELSE 'No significant correlation'
    END AS correlation_significance
FROM 
    correlation, p_value;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(NULLIF(salary_in_thousands_of_dollars, 0), 0) AS salary,
        COALESCE(NULLIF(number_of_doubles, 0), 0) AS doubles
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    WHERE 
        salary_in_thousands_of_dollars IS NOT NULL 
        AND number_of_doubles IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(salary, doubles), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(salary) AS sum_salary,
        SUM(doubles) AS sum_doubles,
        SUM(salary * doubles) AS sum_salary_doubles,
        SUM(salary * salary) AS sum_salary_sq,
        SUM(doubles * doubles) AS sum_doubles_sq
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        n,
        (n * sum_salary_doubles - sum_salary * sum_doubles) / 
        SQRT((n * sum_salary_sq - sum_salary * sum_salary) * (n * sum_doubles_sq - sum_doubles * sum_doubles)) AS r,
        1 - POWER(ABS(r), 2) AS r_squared
    FROM 
        correlation
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN n > 2 THEN ROUND(2 * (1 - T_DIST(ABS(r), n - 2)), 4) 
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS p_value
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'Significant correlation'
        ELSE 'No significant correlation'
    END AS correlation_significance
FROM 
    stats, p_value;

samples.question: Create a new feature called "batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage" which represents the difference between a player's batting average and their on-base percentage. Calculate the mean and standard deviation of this new feature.
samples.constraints: To calculate the new feature, subtract each player's on-base percentage from their batting average. Ignore the missing values and areas with null values for batting average or on-base percentage. Calculate both the mean and standard deviation using these new feature values.

variants.sql:
WITH batting_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        batting_average,
        on_base_percentage,
        (batting_average - on_base_percentage) AS batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    WHERE 
        batting_average IS NOT NULL 
        AND on_base_percentage IS NOT NULL
),
summary_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV(batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage), 2) AS std_dev
    FROM 
        batting_stats
)
SELECT 
    mean, 
    std_dev 
FROM 
    summary_stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage), 2) AS mean,
    ROUND(STDDEV(batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage), 2) AS std_dev
FROM (
    SELECT 
        batting_average - on_base_percentage AS batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    WHERE 
        batting_average IS NOT NULL AND on_base_percentage IS NOT NULL
) AS subquery;

WITH feature AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(batting_average - on_base_percentage, 2) AS batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    WHERE 
        batting_average IS NOT NULL 
        AND on_base_percentage IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage), 2) AS mean,
    ROUND(STDDEV(batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage), 2) AS std_dev
FROM 
    feature;

WITH batting_difference AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(COALESCE(batting_average, 0) - COALESCE(on_base_percentage, 0), 2) AS batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage
    FROM 
        baseball_data
    WHERE 
        batting_average IS NOT NULL 
        AND on_base_percentage IS NOT NULL
)

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage), 2) AS mean,
    ROUND(STDDEV(batting_average_minus_on_base_percentage), 2) AS std_dev
FROM 
    batting_difference;

samples.question: Determine which country's gross domestic product per capita in the year 1992 had the highest skewness among all countries in the dataset.
samples.constraints: Use Python's SciPy library to calculate the skewness of each country's gross domestic product per capita in 1992. Skewness should be calculated with Fisher’s definition, i.e. the one that's adjusted for the normal distribution.

variants.sql:
SELECT country 
FROM gapminder_gdp_asia 
ORDER BY gdpPercap_1992 
LIMIT 1;

WITH gdp_data AS (
    SELECT country, 
           gdpPercap_1952, 
           gdpPercap_1957, 
           gdpPercap_1962, 
           gdpPercap_1967, 
           gdpPercap_1972, 
           gdpPercap_1977, 
           gdpPercap_1982, 
           gdpPercap_1987, 
           gdpPercap_1992, 
           gdpPercap_1997, 
           gdpPercap_2002, 
           gdpPercap_2007
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia
),
gdp_1992 AS (
    SELECT country, gdpPercap_1992
    FROM gdp_data
),
gdp_stats AS (
    SELECT country,
           COUNT(*) AS n,
           AVG(gdpPercap_1992) AS mean,
           SUM(POWER(gdpPercap_1992 - AVG(gdpPercap_1992), 3)) AS sum_cubed_deviation,
           SUM(POWER(gdpPercap_1992 - AVG(gdpPercap_1992), 2)) AS sum_squared_deviation
    FROM gdp_1992
    GROUP BY country
)
SELECT country
FROM gdp_stats
ORDER BY (n * sum_cubed_deviation) / POWER(sum_squared_deviation, 1.5) DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT country 
FROM gapminder_gdp_asia 
WHERE gdpPercap_1992 = (
    SELECT gdpPercap_1992 
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia 
    ORDER BY (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM gapminder_gdp_asia AS inner_table WHERE inner_table.gdpPercap_1992 < gapminder_gdp_asia.gdpPercap_1992) - 
              (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM gapminder_gdp_asia AS inner_table WHERE inner_table.gdpPercap_1992 > gapminder_gdp_asia.gdpPercap_1992) 
    LIMIT 1
) 
LIMIT 1;

WITH gdp_data AS (
    SELECT country, gdpPercap_1992,
           AVG(gdpPercap_1992) OVER () AS avg_gdp,
           COUNT(gdpPercap_1992) OVER () AS count_gdp,
           SUM(POW(gdpPercap_1992 - AVG(gdpPercap_1992) OVER (), 3)) OVER () AS sum_cubed_diff,
           SUM(POW(gdpPercap_1992 - AVG(gdpPercap_1992) OVER (), 2)) OVER () AS sum_squared_diff
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia
),
skewness_data AS (
    SELECT country,
           ROUND((count_gdp * sum_cubed_diff) / (count_gdp - 1) / POW((sum_squared_diff / (count_gdp - 1)), 1.5), 2) AS skewness
    FROM gdp_data
)
SELECT country AS highest_skewness_country
FROM skewness_data
ORDER BY skewness DESC
LIMIT 1;

WITH gdp_1992 AS (
    SELECT 
        country,
        gdpPercap_1992
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia
    WHERE gdpPercap_1992 IS NOT NULL
),
gdp_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        country,
        ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1992), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(STDEV(gdpPercap_1992), 2) AS stddev,
        COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM gdp_1992
    GROUP BY country
    HAVING count > 1
),
gdp_skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        country,
        ROUND(SUM(POWER(gdpPercap_1992 - mean, 3)) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) / POWER(NULLIF(stddev, 0), 3), 2) AS skewness
    FROM gdp_1992
    JOIN gdp_stats ON gdp_1992.country = gdp_stats.country
    GROUP BY gdp_1992.country
)
SELECT country 
FROM gdp_skewness 
ORDER BY skewness DESC 
LIMIT 1;

samples.question: Identify any outliers in the gross domestic product per capita data for the year 1982 for all countries. Define an outlier as any data point that falls more than 1.5 times the interquartile range (IQR) below the first quartile or above the third quartile. Report the country or countries which their gdpPercap_1982 values are identified as outliers.
samples.constraints: Use the interquartile range (IQR) rule for outlier detection: a data point is considered an outlier if it falls more than 1.5*IQR below the first quartile (Q1) or above the third quartile (Q3). Don't use any other outlier detection methods or parameters.

variants.sql:
SELECT country 
FROM gapminder_gdp_asia 
WHERE gdpPercap_1982 < (SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1982), 2) - 1.5 * (SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1982), 2) - (SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1982), 2) FROM gapminder_gdp_asia WHERE gdpPercap_1982 < (SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1982), 2)) LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) / 4 FROM gapminder_gdp_asia)) LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) / 4 FROM gapminder_gdp_asia)) 
FROM gapminder_gdp_asia) 
OR gdpPercap_1982 > (SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1982), 2) + 1.5 * (SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1982), 2) - (SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1982), 2) FROM gapminder_gdp_asia WHERE gdpPercap_1982 < (SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_1982), 2)) LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) / 4 FROM gapminder_gdp_asia)) LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) / 4 FROM gapminder_gdp_asia));

WITH quartiles AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982) AS Q3
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM quartiles
),
outlier_bounds AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1 - 1.5 * IQR AS lower_bound,
        Q3 + 1.5 * IQR AS upper_bound
    FROM iqr
)
SELECT 
    country
FROM gapminder_gdp_asia, outlier_bounds
WHERE gdpPercap_1982 < lower_bound OR gdpPercap_1982 > upper_bound;

SELECT country 
FROM gapminder_gdp_asia 
WHERE gdpPercap_1982 < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR 
                         FROM (SELECT 
                                   percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982) AS Q1, 
                                   percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982) AS Q3, 
                                   (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982) - percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982)) AS IQR 
                               FROM gapminder_gdp_asia)) 
   OR gdpPercap_1982 > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR 
                        FROM (SELECT 
                                  percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982) AS Q1, 
                                  percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982) AS Q3, 
                                  (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982) - percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982)) AS IQR 
                              FROM gapminder_gdp_asia));

WITH gdp_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        gdpPercap_1982,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) OVER () AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) OVER () AS Q3
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM gdp_stats
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT country
FROM gapminder_gdp_asia, iqr
WHERE gdpPercap_1982 < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR gdpPercap_1982 > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR);

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(COALESCE(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982), 0), 2) AS Q1,
        ROUND(COALESCE(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gdpPercap_1982), 0), 2) AS Q3
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia
    WHERE gdpPercap_1982 IS NOT NULL
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        ROUND(COALESCE(Q3 - Q1, 0), 2) AS IQR
    FROM stats
),
outlier_limits AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1 - 1.5 * IQR AS lower_bound,
        Q3 + 1.5 * IQR AS upper_bound
    FROM iqr
)
SELECT country 
FROM gapminder_gdp_asia, outlier_limits
WHERE gdpPercap_1982 IS NOT NULL 
AND (gdpPercap_1982 < lower_bound OR gdpPercap_1982 > upper_bound);

samples.question: Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the gross domestic product per capita in the year 2007 for all countries in the dataset. Round your answers to 2 decimal places.
samples.constraints: Perform arithmetic mean and standard deviation calculations on the 'gdpPercap_2007' column of the dataset. Round your answer to two decimal places. Do not use modes, medians, or any other form of average.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_2007), 2) AS mean_gdp2007, ROUND(STDDEV(gdpPercap_2007), 2) AS standard_deviation_gdp2007 FROM gapminder_gdp_asia;

WITH gdp_data AS (
    SELECT gdpPercap_2007
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_2007), 2) AS mean_gdp2007,
        ROUND(STDDEV(gdpPercap_2007), 2) AS standard_deviation_gdp2007
    FROM gdp_data
)
SELECT mean_gdp2007, standard_deviation_gdp2007
FROM stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_2007), 2) AS mean_gdp2007, 
    ROUND(SQRT(AVG((gdpPercap_2007 - (SELECT AVG(gdpPercap_2007) FROM gapminder_gdp_asia)) * (gdpPercap_2007 - (SELECT AVG(gdpPercap_2007) FROM gapminder_gdp_asia)))), 2) AS standard_deviation_gdp2007 
FROM 
    gapminder_gdp_asia;

WITH gdp_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(gdpPercap_2007), 2) AS mean_gdp2007,
        ROUND(STDDEV(gdpPercap_2007), 2) AS standard_deviation_gdp2007
    FROM 
        gapminder_gdp_asia
)
SELECT 
    mean_gdp2007, 
    standard_deviation_gdp2007
FROM 
    gdp_stats;

WITH gdp_2007 AS (
    SELECT COALESCE(gdpPercap_2007, 0) AS gdp_2007
    FROM gapminder_gdp_asia
    WHERE gdpPercap_2007 IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(gdp_2007), 2) AS mean_gdp2007,
    ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(gdp_2007 - (SELECT AVG(gdp_2007) FROM gdp_2007), 2)), 2), 2) AS standard_deviation_gdp2007
FROM gdp_2007;

samples.question: Are the MEANPOT values normally distributed in the dataset?
samples.constraints: Determine the normality of the values in the MEANPOT column using the Shapiro-Wilk test for normality. Consider the values to be normally distributed if the p-value is greater than 0.05. Report your findings as "Normal" if the p-value is greater than 0.05 and "Not Normal" otherwise. Report the p-value as well.

variants.sql:
WITH meanpot_values AS (
    SELECT MEANPOT
    FROM 3901
),
meanpot_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(MEANPOT) AS mean,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM((MEANPOT - AVG(MEANPOT)) * (MEANPOT - AVG(MEANPOT))) AS variance_sum
    FROM meanpot_values
),
shapiro_wilk AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((SUM((MEANPOT - (SELECT mean FROM meanpot_stats)) * (MEANPOT - (SELECT mean FROM meanpot_stats)))) / (SELECT variance_sum FROM meanpot_stats)), 2) AS W,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM meanpot_values
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN (SELECT W FROM shapiro_wilk) > 0.05 THEN 'Normal' 
            ELSE 'Not Normal' 
        END AS normality_test_result,
        ROUND(1 - (SELECT W FROM shapiro_wilk), 4) AS p_value
)
SELECT normality_test_result, p_value
FROM p_value;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'Normal' 
        ELSE 'Not Normal' 
    END AS normality_test_result,
    ROUND(p_value, 2) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(MEANPOT) AS sum_x,
        SUM(MEANPOT * MEANPOT) AS sum_x2,
        SUM(MEANPOT * MEANPOT * MEANPOT) AS sum_x3,
        SUM(MEANPOT * MEANPOT * MEANPOT * MEANPOT) AS sum_x4
    FROM 3901
) AS stats,
(
    SELECT 
        1 - (0.5 * (1 + (SUM((MEANPOT - avg) * (MEANPOT - avg)) / (n - 1)) / (stddev * sqrt(n)))) AS p_value
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            MEANPOT,
            AVG(MEANPOT) OVER() AS avg,
            COUNT(MEANPOT) OVER() AS n,
            STDEV(MEANPOT) OVER() AS stddev
        FROM 3901
    )
) AS p_value_calc;

WITH meanpot_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        MEANPOT,
        AVG(MEANPOT) OVER () AS mean,
        COUNT(MEANPOT) OVER () AS n,
        SUM((MEANPOT - AVG(MEANPOT) OVER ()) * (MEANPOT - AVG(MEANPOT) OVER ())) OVER () AS sum_squared_diff
    FROM 
        3901
),
shapiro_wilk AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((SUM((MEANPOT - mean) * (MEANPOT - mean)) / (n - 1)), 2) AS variance,
        ROUND((SUM(POWER(MEANPOT - mean, 4)) / n) / POWER(ROUND((SUM((MEANPOT - mean) * (MEANPOT - mean)) / (n - 1)), 2), 2), 2) AS W,
        ROUND((1 - W) * (n - 1) / (1 + (n - 1) * (1 - W)), 2) AS p_value
    FROM 
        meanpot_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'Normal' 
        ELSE 'Not Normal' 
    END AS normality_test_result,
    p_value
FROM 
    shapiro_wilk;

WITH meanpot_values AS (
    SELECT COALESCE(MEANPOT, 0) AS MEANPOT
    FROM 3901
    WHERE MEANPOT IS NOT NULL
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((COUNT(*) - 1) * (SUM((MEANPOT - AVG(MEANPOT)) * (MEANPOT - AVG(MEANPOT))) / (COUNT(*) * VARIANCE(MEANPOT))), 2) AS shapiro_stat,
        ROUND(1 - EXP(-0.5 * (COUNT(*) * (shapiro_stat - AVG(MEANPOT)))), 2) AS p_value
    FROM meanpot_values
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'Normal' 
        ELSE 'Not Normal' 
    END AS normality_test_result,
    p_value
FROM shapiro_test;

samples.question: Is there any correlation between the TOTUSJH and TOTUSJZ columns in the dataset?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient between the TOTUSJH and TOTUSJZ columns. Use a two-tailed test with a significance level of 0.05 to determine the statistical significance. If the p-value is less than 0.05, report the relationship as either "Positive Correlation", "Negative Correlation" or "No Correlation", based on the sign and magnitude of the correlation coefficient. If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report "No Significant Correlation".

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN CORR(TOTUSJH, TOTUSJZ) > 0 AND P_VALUE < 0.05 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
        WHEN CORR(TOTUSJH, TOTUSJZ) < 0 AND P_VALUE < 0.05 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
