        ELSE 'No Significant Correlation'
    END AS correlation_type,
    ROUND(CORR(TOTUSJH, TOTUSJZ), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(P_VALUE, 3) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        TOTUSJH, 
        TOTUSJZ, 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(TOTUSJH * TOTUSJZ) AS sum_product,
        SUM(TOTUSJH) AS sum_x,
        SUM(TOTUSJZ) AS sum_y,
        SUM(TOTUSJH * TOTUSJH) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(TOTUSJZ * TOTUSJZ) AS sum_yy
    FROM 3901
) AS stats,
(
    SELECT 
        1.96 / SQRT(n - 2) AS critical_value,
        (1 - (CORR(TOTUSJH, TOTUSJZ) * CORR(TOTUSJH, TOTUSJZ))) / (n - 2) AS p_value
    FROM (
        SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM 3901
    )
) AS p_values;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        TOTUSJH,
        TOTUSJZ
    FROM 
        3901
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(TOTUSJH) AS sum_x,
        SUM(TOTUSJZ) AS sum_y,
        SUM(TOTUSJH * TOTUSJZ) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(TOTUSJH * TOTUSJH) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(TOTUSJZ * TOTUSJZ) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS correlation_coefficient,
        n
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        n,
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
        WHEN p_value >= 0.05 THEN 'No Significant Correlation'
        ELSE 'No Correlation'
    END AS correlation_type,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(p_value, 3) AS p_value
FROM 
    p_value_calculation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN corr_coeff > 0 AND p_value < 0.05 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
        WHEN corr_coeff < 0 AND p_value < 0.05 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
        ELSE 'No Significant Correlation'
    END AS correlation_type,
    ROUND(corr_coeff, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(p_value, 3) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(TOTUSJH * TOTUSJZ) - SUM(TOTUSJH) * SUM(TOTUSJZ)) / 
        (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(TOTUSJH * TOTUSJH) - SUM(TOTUSJH) * SUM(TOTUSJH)) * 
               (COUNT(*) * SUM(TOTUSJZ * TOTUSJZ) - SUM(TOTUSJZ) * SUM(TOTUSJZ)))) AS corr_coeff,
        2 * (1 - ABS(corr_coeff)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        (SELECT 
            TOTUSJH, 
            TOTUSJZ 
         FROM 3901) AS subquery
) AS correlation_results;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        TOTUSJH,
        TOTUSJZ,
        AVG(TOTUSJH) OVER () AS avg_TOTUSJH,
        AVG(TOTUSJZ) OVER () AS avg_TOTUSJZ,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS n
    FROM 
        3901
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM((TOTUSJH - avg_TOTUSJH) * (TOTUSJZ - avg_TOTUSJZ)) AS cov_xy,
        SUM((TOTUSJH - avg_TOTUSJH) * (TOTUSJH - avg_TOTUSJH)) AS var_x,
        SUM((TOTUSJZ - avg_TOTUSJZ) * (TOTUSJZ - avg_TOTUSJZ)) AS var_y,
        n
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(cov_xy / SQRT(var_x * var_y), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
        n
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 
            CASE 
                WHEN correlation_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
                WHEN correlation_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
            END
        ELSE 'No Significant Correlation'
    END AS correlation_type,
    correlation_coefficient,
    p_value
FROM 
    p_value;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(TOTUSJH, 0) AS TOTUSJH,
        COALESCE(TOTUSJZ, 0) AS TOTUSJZ
    FROM 
        3901
    WHERE 
        TOTUSJH IS NOT NULL AND 
        TOTUSJZ IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(TOTUSJH) AS sum_x,
        SUM(TOTUSJZ) AS sum_y,
        SUM(TOTUSJH * TOTUSJZ) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(TOTUSJH * TOTUSJH) AS sum_x2,
        SUM(TOTUSJZ * TOTUSJZ) AS sum_y2
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_x2 - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_y2 - sum_y * sum_y)), 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.1 THEN 1
            WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.3 THEN 0.1
            WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 0.05
            ELSE 0.01
        END AS significance
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN significance < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
        WHEN significance < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
        WHEN significance >= 0.05 THEN 'No Significant Correlation'
    END AS correlation_type,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(significance, 3) AS p_value
FROM 
    correlation, p_value;

samples.question:
Perform comprehensive data preprocessing for the dataset by:
1. Removing any duplicate entries.
2. Filling in missing values in the USFLUX column with the mean value of the column.
3. Transforming the MEANJZH column by applying the logarithm function (base 10).
4. Normalizing the TOTUSJZ column using Min-Max normalization.
samples.constraints: When applying the logarithm function, add a small constant (1e-10) to the MEANJZH column to avoid infinity. The Min-Max normalization needs to transform the TOTUSJZ values to the range 0 to 1.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND((TOTUSJZ - MIN(TOTUSJZ) OVER()) / (MAX(TOTUSJZ) OVER() - MIN(TOTUSJZ) OVER()), 3) AS norm_TOTUSJZ,
    ROUND(LOG10(MEANJZH + 1e-10), 3) AS log_MEANJZH,
    COUNT(DISTINCT *) AS clean_entries
FROM 
    (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901) AS unique_entries
WHERE 
    USFLUX IS NOT NULL;

WITH CleanedData AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT *
    FROM `3901`
),
FilledUSFLUX AS (
    SELECT *,
           COALESCE(USFLUX, (SELECT AVG(USFLUX) FROM CleanedData)) AS FilledUSFLUX
    FROM CleanedData
),
TransformedMEANJZH AS (
    SELECT *,
           LOG10(MEANJZH + 1e-10) AS log_MEANJZH
    FROM FilledUSFLUX
),
MinMaxNormalized AS (
    SELECT *,
           (TOTUSJZ - MIN(TOTUSJZ) OVER ()) / (MAX(TOTUSJZ) OVER () - MIN(TOTUSJZ) OVER ()) AS norm_TOTUSJZ
    FROM TransformedMEANJZH
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(norm_TOTUSJZ), 3) AS norm_TOTUSJZ,
       ROUND(AVG(log_MEANJZH), 3) AS log_MEANJZH,
       COUNT(*) AS clean_entries
FROM MinMaxNormalized;

SELECT 
    ROUND((TOTUSJZ - (SELECT MIN(TOTUSJZ) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901))) / 
           ((SELECT MAX(TOTUSJZ) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901)) - 
            (SELECT MIN(TOTUSJZ) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901))), 2) AS norm_TOTUSJZ,
    ROUND(LOG10(MEANJZH + 1e-10), 2) AS log_MEANJZH,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901)) AS clean_entries
FROM 
    (SELECT DISTINCT 
        TRUE_TIME, 
        TIME, 
        COALESCE(USFLUX, (SELECT AVG(USFLUX) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901))) AS USFLUX, 
        MEANGAM, 
        MEANGBT, 
        MEANGBZ, 
        MEANGBH, 
        MEANJZD, 
        TOTUSJZ, 
        MEANJZH, 
        TOTUSJH, 
        ABSNJZH, 
        SAVNCPP, 
        MEANPOT, 
        TOTPOT, 
        MEANSHR, 
        SHRGT45, 
        R_VALUE, 
        AREA_ACR 
    FROM 3901) AS cleaned_data
LIMIT 1;

WITH CleanedData AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        TRUE_TIME, 
        TIME, 
        COALESCE(USFLUX, AVG(USFLUX) OVER()) AS USFLUX,
        MEANGAM, 
        MEANGBT, 
        MEANGBZ, 
        MEANGBH, 
        MEANJZD, 
        TOTUSJZ, 
        MEANJZH + 1e-10 AS Adjusted_MEANJZH,
        TOTUSJH, 
        ABSNJZH, 
        SAVNCPP, 
        MEANPOT, 
        TOTPOT, 
        MEANSHR, 
        SHRGT45, 
        R_VALUE, 
        AREA_ACR
    FROM 
        3901
), LogTransformed AS (
    SELECT 
        TRUE_TIME, 
        TIME, 
        USFLUX, 
        MEANGAM, 
        MEANGBT, 
        MEANGBZ, 
        MEANGBH, 
        MEANJZD, 
        TOTUSJZ, 
        ROUND(LOG10(Adjusted_MEANJZH), 3) AS log_MEANJZH,
        TOTUSJH, 
        ABSNJZH, 
        SAVNCPP, 
        MEANPOT, 
        TOTPOT, 
        MEANSHR, 
        SHRGT45, 
        R_VALUE, 
        AREA_ACR
    FROM 
        CleanedData
), NormalizedData AS (
    SELECT 
        TRUE_TIME, 
        TIME, 
        USFLUX, 
        MEANGAM, 
        MEANGBT, 
        MEANGBZ, 
        MEANGBH, 
        MEANJZD, 
        TOTUSJZ,
        log_MEANJZH,
        TOTUSJH, 
        ABSNJZH, 
        SAVNCPP, 
        MEANPOT, 
        TOTPOT, 
        MEANSHR, 
        SHRGT45, 
        R_VALUE, 
        AREA_ACR,
        (TOTUSJZ - MIN(TOTUSJZ) OVER()) / (MAX(TOTUSJZ) OVER() - MIN(TOTUSJZ) OVER()) AS norm_TOTUSJZ
    FROM 
        LogTransformed
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(norm_TOTUSJZ), 3) AS norm_TOTUSJZ,
    ROUND(AVG(log_MEANJZH), 3) AS log_MEANJZH,
    COUNT(*) AS clean_entries
FROM 
    NormalizedData;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        TRUE_TIME, 
        TIME, 
        COALESCE(USFLUX, (SELECT AVG(USFLUX) FROM `3901` WHERE USFLUX IS NOT NULL)) AS USFLUX,
        MEANGAM,
        MEANGBT,
        MEANGBZ,
        MEANGBH,
        MEANJZD,
        TOTUSJZ,
        ROUND(LOG10(NULLIF(MEANJZH + 1e-10, 0)), 2) AS log_MEANJZH,
        TOTUSJH,
        ABSNJZH,
        SAVNCPP,
        MEANPOT,
        TOTPOT,
        MEANSHR,
        SHRGT45,
        R_VALUE,
        AREA_ACR
    FROM `3901`
    WHERE USFLUX IS NOT NULL
),
min_max AS (
    SELECT 
        *, 
        (TOTUSJZ - MIN(TOTUSJZ) OVER ()) / NULLIF((MAX(TOTUSJZ) OVER () - MIN(TOTUSJZ) OVER ()), 0) AS norm_TOTUSJZ
    FROM cleaned_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(norm_TOTUSJZ), 3) AS norm_TOTUSJZ,
    ROUND(AVG(log_MEANJZH), 3) AS log_MEANJZH,
    COUNT(*) AS clean_entries
FROM min_max;

samples.question: Create a new feature named "TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO" by dividing the TOTUSJZ column by the TOTUSJH column. Calculate the mean and standard deviation of this new feature.
samples.constraints: Add a small constant (1e-10) to the denominator (TOTUSJH column) to avoid dividing by zero.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(TOTUSJZ / (TOTUSJH + 1e-10)), 2) AS mean_ratio, ROUND(STDDEV(TOTUSJZ / (TOTUSJH + 1e-10)), 2) AS stddev_ratio FROM 3901;

WITH RatioCTE AS (
    SELECT 
        TOTUSJZ / (TOTUSJH + 1e-10) AS TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO
    FROM 
        3901
),
StatsCTE AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO), 2) AS mean_ratio,
        ROUND(STDDEV(TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO), 2) AS stddev_ratio
    FROM 
        RatioCTE
)
SELECT 
    mean_ratio, 
    stddev_ratio 
FROM 
    StatsCTE;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO), 2) AS mean_ratio, 
    ROUND(STDDEV(TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO), 2) AS stddev_ratio 
FROM (
    SELECT 
        TOTUSJZ / (TOTUSJH + 1e-10) AS TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO 
    FROM 
        3901
) AS subquery;

WITH ratio_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        TOTUSJZ / (TOTUSJH + 1e-10) AS TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO
    FROM 
        3901
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO), 2) AS mean_ratio,
    ROUND(STDDEV(TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO), 2) AS stddev_ratio
FROM 
    ratio_calculation;

WITH ratio_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(TOTUSJZ, 0) / NULLIF(TOTUSJH, 0) + 1e-10 AS TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO
    FROM 
        3901
    WHERE 
        TOTUSJZ IS NOT NULL AND TOTUSJH IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO), 2) AS mean_ratio,
    ROUND(STDDEV(TOTUSJZ_TOTUSJH_RATIO), 2) AS stddev_ratio
FROM 
    ratio_calculation;

samples.question: Perform a correlation analysis between the MEANGAM and MEANGBT columns. Additionally, for the correlated variables, identify any outliers in the MEANGAM column using the Z-score method and a threshold of 3 for the absolute Z-score.
samples.constraints:
1. Use the Pearson correlation coefficient to assess the correlation between MEANGAM and MEANGBT columns.
2. Define outliers as those data points in the MEANGAM column where the absolute Z-score exceeds 3.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(MEANGAM, MEANGBT), 3) AS correlation_coefficient,
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count,
    GROUP_CONCAT(MEANGAM) AS outlier_list
FROM 
    3901
WHERE 
    ABS((MEANGAM - (SELECT AVG(MEANGAM) FROM 3901)) / (SELECT STDEV(MEANGAM) FROM 3901)) > 3;

WITH correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(MEANGAM, MEANGBT), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        `3901`
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        MEANGAM,
        AVG(MEANGAM) OVER () AS mean_meangam,
        STDDEV(MEANGAM) OVER () AS stddev_meangam
    FROM 
        `3901`
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS outlier_count,
        ARRAY_AGG(MEANGAM) AS outlier_list
    FROM 
        stats
    WHERE 
        ABS((MEANGAM - mean_meangam) / stddev_meangam) > 3
)
SELECT 
    c.correlation_coefficient,
    o.outlier_count,
    o.outlier_list
FROM 
    correlation c, outliers o;

SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT (SUM((MEANGAM - avg_meangam) * (MEANGBT - avg_meangbt)) / 
                    (COUNT(*) * stddev_meangam * stddev_meangbt) 
            FROM (SELECT MEANGAM, MEANGBT, 
                         (SELECT AVG(MEANGAM) FROM 3901) AS avg_meangam, 
                         (SELECT AVG(MEANGBT) FROM 3901) AS avg_meangbt, 
                         (SELECT STDDEV(MEANGAM) FROM 3901) AS stddev_meangam, 
                         (SELECT STDDEV(MEANGBT) FROM 3901) AS stddev_meangbt 
                  FROM 3901)) 
            , 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) 
     FROM (SELECT MEANGAM, 
                  (MEANGAM - (SELECT AVG(MEANGAM) FROM 3901)) / (SELECT STDDEV(MEANGAM) FROM 3901) AS z_score 
           FROM 3901) 
     WHERE ABS(z_score) > 3) AS outlier_count,
    (SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(MEANGAM) 
     FROM (SELECT MEANGAM, 
                  (MEANGAM - (SELECT AVG(MEANGAM) FROM 3901)) / (SELECT STDDEV(MEANGAM) FROM 3901) AS z_score 
           FROM 3901) 
     WHERE ABS(z_score) > 3) AS outlier_list;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(MEANGAM) AS mean_meangam,
        AVG(MEANGBT) AS mean_meangbt,
        STDDEV(MEANGAM) AS stddev_meangam,
        STDDEV(MEANGBT) AS stddev_meangbt
    FROM 
        3901
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(MEANGAM, MEANGBT), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        3901
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        MEANGAM,
        (MEANGAM - s.mean_meangam) / s.stddev_meangam AS z_score
    FROM 
        3901, stats s
)
SELECT 
    c.correlation_coefficient,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN ABS(o.z_score) > 3 THEN 1 END) AS outlier_count,
    GROUP_CONCAT(CASE WHEN ABS(o.z_score) > 3 THEN o.MEANGAM END) AS outlier_list
FROM 
    correlation c, outliers o;

WITH correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(COALESCE(MEANGAM, 0), COALESCE(MEANGBT, 0)), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        3901
    WHERE 
        MEANGAM IS NOT NULL AND MEANGBT IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(COALESCE(MEANGAM, 0)) AS mean_meangam,
        STDDEV(COALESCE(MEANGAM, 0)) AS stddev_meangam
    FROM 
        3901
    WHERE 
        MEANGAM IS NOT NULL
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS outlier_count,
        ARRAY_AGG(TRUE_TIME) AS outlier_list
    FROM 
        3901
    JOIN 
        stats ON TRUE
    WHERE 
        MEANGAM IS NOT NULL AND 
        ABS((COALESCE(MEANGAM, 0) - stats.mean_meangam) / NULLIF(stats.stddev_meangam, 0)) > 3
)
SELECT 
    correlation.correlation_coefficient,
    outliers.outlier_count,
    outliers.outlier_list
FROM 
    correlation, outliers;

samples.question:
Perform a comprehensive analysis of the dataset by:
1. Removing any duplicate entries.
2. Filling in missing values in the USFLUX column with the mean value of the column.
3. Creating a new feature named "MEANGAM_MEANGBZ_DIFF" by subtracting the MEANGBZ column from the MEANGAM column.
4. Applying machine learning techniques to predict the values in the TOTUSJH column using the MEANJZH, TOTUSJZ, and MEANGBT columns. You will need to use a Random Forest Regressor with 100 trees for this task.
samples.constraints:
1. Remove duplicates based on the entire row.
2. Missing values in the USFLUX column should be replaced with the mean of the same column.
3. Use the Random Forest Regressor as the machine learning model.
4. The Random Forest Regressor should have 100 trees.
5. The independent variables for the prediction should be the MEANJZH, TOTUSJZ, and MEANGBT columns.
6. The dependent or target variable for the prediction should be the TOTUSJH column.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS duplicate_count,
    ROUND(AVG(MEANGAM - MEANGBZ), 2) AS new_feature_mean
FROM 
    (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901) AS unique_rows
WHERE 
    USFLUX IS NOT NULL;

WITH Deduplicated AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT *
    FROM `3901`
),
FilledUSFLUX AS (
    SELECT *,
           COALESCE(USFLUX, (SELECT AVG(USFLUX) FROM Deduplicated)) AS USFLUX_Filled
    FROM Deduplicated
),
FeatureEngineering AS (
    SELECT *,
           MEANGAM - MEANGBZ AS MEANGAM_MEANGBZ_DIFF
    FROM FilledUSFLUX
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS duplicate_count,
       ROUND(AVG(MEANGAM_MEANGBZ_DIFF), 2) AS new_feature_mean
FROM FeatureEngineering;

SELECT 
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901)) AS duplicate_count,
    ROUND(AVG(MEANGAM - MEANGBZ), 2) AS new_feature_mean
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        COALESCE(USFLUX, (SELECT AVG(USFLUX) FROM 3901)) AS USFLUX, 
        MEANGAM, 
        MEANGBZ 
     FROM 
        (SELECT DISTINCT * FROM 3901));

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT *
    FROM `3901`
),
filled_data AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        COALESCE(USFLUX, AVG(USFLUX) OVER ()) AS USFLUX
    FROM cleaned_data
),
feature_data AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        ROUND(MEANGAM - MEANGBZ, 2) AS MEANGAM_MEANGBZ_DIFF
    FROM filled_data
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) - COUNT(DISTINCT *) AS duplicate_count,
    ROUND(AVG(MEANGAM_MEANGBZ_DIFF), 2) AS new_feature_mean
FROM feature_data;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        TRUE_TIME,
        TIME,
        COALESCE(USFLUX, (SELECT AVG(USFLUX) FROM 3901 WHERE USFLUX IS NOT NULL)) AS USFLUX,
        MEANGAM,
        MEANGBT,
        MEANGBZ,
        MEANGBH,
        MEANJZD,
        TOTUSJZ,
        MEANJZH,
        TOTUSJH,
        ABSNJZH,
        SAVNCPP,
        MEANPOT,
        TOTPOT,
        MEANSHR,
        SHRGT45,
        R_VALUE,
        AREA_ACR
    FROM 3901
    WHERE TRUE_TIME IS NOT NULL AND TIME IS NOT NULL AND MEANGAM IS NOT NULL AND MEANGBT IS NOT NULL AND MEANGBZ IS NOT NULL
),
feature_data AS (
    SELECT 
        TRUE_TIME,
        TIME,
        USFLUX,
        MEANGAM,
        MEANGBT,
        MEANGBZ,
        MEANGBH,
        MEANJZD,
        TOTUSJZ,
        MEANJZH,
        TOTUSJH,
        ABSNJZH,
        SAVNCPP,
        MEANPOT,
        TOTPOT,
        MEANSHR,
        SHRGT45,
        R_VALUE,
        AREA_ACR,
        ROUND(MEANGAM - MEANGBZ, 2) AS MEANGAM_MEANGBZ_DIFF
    FROM cleaned_data
),
mean_feature AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(MEANGAM_MEANGBZ_DIFF), 2) AS new_feature_mean
    FROM feature_data
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cleaned_data) AS duplicate_count,
    (SELECT new_feature_mean FROM mean_feature) AS new_feature_mean;

samples.question: Is there any correlation between the MedInd and LarInd columns in the given dataset? If yes, what is the correlation coefficient?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson's correlation coefficient (r), a statistical measure that calculates the strength of the relationship between the relative movements of two variables, between the MedInd and LarInd columns. The Pearson's correlation coefficient should be rounded to 4 decimal places.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(CORR(MedInd, LarInd), 4) AS correlation_coefficient FROM `veracruz 2016`;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(MedInd) AS sum_x,
        SUM(LarInd) AS sum_y,
        SUM(MedInd * LarInd) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(MedInd * MedInd) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(LarInd * LarInd) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS r
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(r, 4) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

SELECT ROUND((SELECT SUM((MedInd - med_mean) * (LarInd - lar_mean)) / 
               (SQRT(SUM((MedInd - med_mean) * (MedInd - med_mean)) * 
               SUM((LarInd - lar_mean) * (LarInd - lar_mean)))) 
               FROM (SELECT MedInd, LarInd, 
                            (SELECT AVG(MedInd) FROM `veracruz 2016`) AS med_mean, 
                            (SELECT AVG(LarInd) FROM `veracruz 2016`) AS lar_mean 
                     FROM `veracruz 2016`)), 4) AS correlation_coefficient;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(MedInd) AS sum_x,
        SUM(LarInd) AS sum_y,
        SUM(MedInd * LarInd) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(MedInd * MedInd) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(LarInd * LarInd) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 4) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        MedInd,
        LarInd
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
    WHERE 
        MedInd IS NOT NULL 
        AND LarInd IS NOT NULL
),
count_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(MedInd) AS sum_x,
        SUM(LarInd) AS sum_y,
        SUM(MedInd * LarInd) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(MedInd * MedInd) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(LarInd * LarInd) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_x,
        sum_y,
        sum_xy,
        sum_xx,
        sum_yy,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        count_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0), 4) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

samples.question: Are there any outliers in the Agri column of the dataset? If yes, how would you detect them using Z-scores?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Z-scores for the Agri column values. Any data point that has a Z-score greater than 3 or less than -3 should be considered as an outlier.

variants.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM (
    SELECT Agri, 
           (Agri - AVG(Agri) OVER ()) / NULLIF(STDDEV(Agri) OVER (), 0) AS z_score
    FROM "veracruz 2016"
) AS subquery
WHERE z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3;

WITH AgriStats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Agri) AS mean_agri,
        STDDEV(Agri) AS stddev_agri
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
),
AgriZScores AS (
    SELECT 
        Agri,
        (Agri - (SELECT mean_agri FROM AgriStats)) / (SELECT stddev_agri FROM AgriStats) AS z_score
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM 
    AgriZScores
WHERE 
    z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM (
    SELECT Agri, 
           (Agri - (SELECT AVG(Agri) FROM `veracruz 2016`)) / 
           (SELECT STDEV(Agri) FROM `veracruz 2016`) AS Z_score
    FROM `veracruz 2016`
) AS subquery
WHERE Z_score > 3 OR Z_score < -3;

WITH AgriStats AS (
    SELECT 
        Agri,
        AVG(Agri) OVER () AS mean_agri,
        STDDEV(Agri) OVER () AS stddev_agri
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
),
ZScores AS (
    SELECT 
        Agri,
        (Agri - mean_agri) / stddev_agri AS z_score
    FROM 
        AgriStats
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM 
    ZScores
WHERE 
    z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3;

WITH AgriStats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Agri, 0)), 2) AS avg_agri,
        ROUND(STDDEV(COALESCE(Agri, 0)), 2) AS stddev_agri
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
    WHERE 
        Agri IS NOT NULL
), ZScores AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((COALESCE(Agri, 0) - a.avg_agri) / NULLIF(a.stddev_agri, 0), 2) AS z_score
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016", AgriStats a
    WHERE 
        Agri IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM 
    ZScores
WHERE 
    z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3;

samples.question: Perform correlation analysis on the given dataset to determine if there is any relationship between the Agri and Residential columns. Additionally, explore the distribution of the Agri column and identify any outliers using z-score as the outlier detection method. Treat any value which has z-score above 3 as an outlier.
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient to assess the linear relationship between Agri and Residential columns. Treat a value as an outlier if the z-score is above 3. Do not consider any other method for outlier detection. Use scipy's pearsonr method for correlation calculation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN (Agri - AVG(Agri) OVER()) / NULLIF(STDEV(Agri) OVER(), 0) > 3 THEN 1 END), 0) AS number_of_outliers,
    ROUND(CORR(Agri, Residential), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    "veracruz 2016";

WITH Agri_Residential AS (
    SELECT Agri, Residential
    FROM "veracruz 2016"
),
Correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Agri * Residential) - SUM(Agri) * SUM(Residential)) / 
        (SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(Agri * Agri) - SUM(Agri) * SUM(Agri)) * 
         SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(Residential * Residential) - SUM(Residential) * SUM(Residential))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM Agri_Residential
),
Z_Scores AS (
    SELECT 
        Agri,
        (Agri - AVG(Agri) OVER ()) / NULLIF(STDEV(Agri) OVER (), 0) AS z_score
    FROM "veracruz 2016"
),
Outliers AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
    FROM Z_Scores
    WHERE z_score > 3
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT number_of_outliers FROM Outliers) AS number_of_outliers,
    (SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM Correlation) AS correlation_coefficient;

SELECT 
    (SELECT COUNT(*) 
     FROM (SELECT Agri, 
                  (Agri - AVG(Agri)) / NULLIF(STDEV(Agri), 0) AS z_score 
           FROM `veracruz 2016`) AS subquery 
     WHERE z_score > 3) AS number_of_outliers,
    (SELECT ROUND(CORR(Agri, Residential), 2) 
     FROM `veracruz 2016`) AS correlation_coefficient;

WITH z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        Agri,
        Residential,
        (Agri - AVG(Agri) OVER ()) / NULLIF(STDEV(Agri) OVER (), 0) AS z_score
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(Agri, Residential), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
    FROM 
        z_scores
    WHERE 
        z_score > 3
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT number_of_outliers FROM outliers) AS number_of_outliers,
    (SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation) AS correlation_coefficient;

WITH Agri_Residential AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Agri, 0) AS Agri,
        COALESCE(Residential, 0) AS Residential
    FROM 
        "veracruz 2016"
    WHERE 
        Agri IS NOT NULL AND Residential IS NOT NULL
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Agri) AS mean_agri,
        STDDEV(Agri) AS stddev_agri
    FROM 
        Agri_Residential
),
Z_Scores AS (
    SELECT 
        Agri,
        Residential,
        (Agri - mean_agri) / NULLIF(stddev_agri, 0) AS z_score
    FROM 
        Agri_Residential, Stats
),
Outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
    FROM 
        Z_Scores
    WHERE 
        z_score > 3
),
Correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(Agri, Residential), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        Agri_Residential
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT number_of_outliers FROM Outliers) AS number_of_outliers,
    (SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM Correlation) AS correlation_coefficient;

samples.question: 1. Is there a significant difference in the mean value of the "nsnps" column between the rows with null values in the "tree" column and the rows without null values in the "tree" column? If yes, what is the p-value of the statistical test?
samples.constraints:
Calculate the mean value for the rows where "tree" is null and the rows where "tree" is not null separately.
Perform an independent two-sample t-test to compare these two groups. Use a significance level (alpha) of 0.05.
Report the p-value associated with the t-test. 
Consider there is a significant difference if the p-value is less than 0.05.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN tree IS NULL THEN nsnps END), 2) AS mean_with_tree_null,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN tree IS NOT NULL THEN nsnps END), 2) AS mean_with_tree_notnull
FROM 
    `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`;

WITH mean_values AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(nsnps) AS mean_nsnps,
        COUNT(*) AS count,
        CASE 
            WHEN tree IS NULL THEN 'null'
            ELSE 'not_null'
        END AS tree_status
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
    GROUP BY 
        tree_status
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        mean_nsnps,
        count,
        tree_status,
        SUM(mean_nsnps * count) OVER () AS total_mean_nsnps,
        SUM(count) OVER () AS total_count
    FROM 
        mean_values
),
t_test AS (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT mean_nsnps FROM stats WHERE tree_status = 'null') AS mean_with_tree_null,
        (SELECT mean_nsnps FROM stats WHERE tree_status = 'not_null') AS mean_with_tree_notnull,
        (SELECT count FROM stats WHERE tree_status = 'null') AS n1,
        (SELECT count FROM stats WHERE tree_status = 'not_null') AS n2,
        (SELECT mean_nsnps FROM stats WHERE tree_status = 'null') * (SELECT mean_nsnps FROM stats WHERE tree_status = 'null') AS sum_sq_null,
        (SELECT mean_nsnps FROM stats WHERE tree_status = 'not_null') * (SELECT mean_nsnps FROM stats WHERE tree_status = 'not_null') AS sum_sq_not_null
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(mean_with_tree_notnull, 2) AS mean_with_tree_notnull,
    ROUND(mean_with_tree_null, 2) AS mean_with_tree_null,
    ROUND((n1 + n2 - 2) * (SUM(sum_sq_null) / n1 + SUM(sum_sq_not_null) / n2) / (n1 + n2 - 2), 2) AS p_value
FROM 
    t_test;

SELECT 
    ROUND(mean_with_tree_notnull, 2) AS mean_with_tree_notnull,
    ROUND(mean_with_tree_null, 2) AS mean_with_tree_null,
    ROUND((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT nsnps FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table WHERE tree IS NOT NULL) AS t1) * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT nsnps FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table WHERE tree IS NULL) AS t2) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table) AS total_count) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT AVG(nsnps) FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table WHERE tree IS NOT NULL) AS mean_with_tree_notnull,
        (SELECT AVG(nsnps) FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table WHERE tree IS NULL) AS mean_with_tree_null
) AS means;

WITH mean_values AS (
    SELECT
        AVG(nsnps) AS mean_nsnps,
        COUNT(*) AS count_nsnps,
        CASE WHEN tree IS NULL THEN 'null' ELSE 'not_null' END AS tree_status
    FROM
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
    GROUP BY
        tree_status
),
t_test_stats AS (
    SELECT
        (SELECT mean_nsnps FROM mean_values WHERE tree_status = 'null') AS mean_with_tree_null,
        (SELECT mean_nsnps FROM mean_values WHERE tree_status = 'not_null') AS mean_with_tree_notnull,
        (SELECT count_nsnps FROM mean_values WHERE tree_status = 'null') AS count_null,
        (SELECT count_nsnps FROM mean_values WHERE tree_status = 'not_null') AS count_not_null
)
SELECT
    ROUND(mean_with_tree_notnull, 2) AS mean_with_tree_notnull,
    ROUND(mean_with_tree_null, 2) AS mean_with_tree_null,
    ROUND(ABS(mean_with_tree_notnull - mean_with_tree_null) / SQRT((count_null + count_not_null) / (count_null * count_not_null)), 4) AS t_statistic,
    2 * (1 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table` WHERE (nsnps - mean_with_tree_null) * (nsnps - mean_with_tree_notnull) < 0) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`)) AS p_value
FROM
    t_test_stats;

WITH mean_values AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(COALESCE(nsnps, 0)) AS mean_nsnps,
        CASE 
            WHEN tree IS NULL THEN 'null' 
            ELSE 'not_null' 
        END AS tree_status
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
    GROUP BY 
        tree_status
),
count_values AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(CASE WHEN tree IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS count_null,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN tree IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS count_not_null
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
),
stddev_values AS (
    SELECT 
        STDEV(nsnps) AS stddev_nsnps,
        CASE 
            WHEN tree IS NULL THEN 'null' 
            ELSE 'not_null' 
        END AS tree_status
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
    GROUP BY 
        tree_status
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(mean_with_tree_notnull.mean_nsnps, 2) AS mean_with_tree_notnull,
    ROUND(mean_with_tree_null.mean_nsnps, 2) AS mean_with_tree_null,
    (SELECT 
        (mean_with_tree_null.mean_nsnps - mean_with_tree_notnull.mean_nsnps) / 
        SQRT((stddev_null.stddev_nsnps * stddev_null.stddev_nsnps) / count_null.count_null + 
             (stddev_not_null.stddev_nsnps * stddev_not_null.stddev_nsnps) / count_not_null.count_not_null) 
    FROM 
        mean_values AS mean_null
    JOIN 
        stddev_values AS stddev_null ON stddev_null.tree_status = 'null'
    JOIN 
        count_values AS count_null ON count_null.count_null > 0
    JOIN 
        stddev_values AS stddev_not_null ON stddev_not_null.tree_status = 'not_null'
    JOIN 
        count_values AS count_not_null ON count_not_null.count_not_null > 0
    WHERE 
        mean_null.tree_status = 'null') AS p_value
FROM 
    mean_values AS mean_with_tree_null
JOIN 
    mean_values AS mean_with_tree_notnull ON mean_with_tree_notnull.tree_status = 'not_null';

samples.question: 2. Perform a distribution analysis on the "nsamplecov" column. Determine whether the distribution adheres to a normal distribution and calculate the skewness and kurtosis values.
samples.constraints:
Test the normality of the data using Shapiro-Wilk Test. Use a significance level (alpha) of 0.05.
Report the p-value associated with the normality test. 
Consider the distribution to be normal if the p-value is larger than 0.05.
Calculate the skewness and kurtosis values.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table) < 3 THEN 'not enough data'
        ELSE 
            CASE 
                WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table) > 0 
                THEN 
                    CASE 
                        WHEN ROUND((SUM((nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov)) * (nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov))) / COUNT(nsamplecov)), 2) > 0 
                        THEN 'yes' 
                        ELSE 'no' 
                    END 
                ELSE 'no' 
            END 
    END AS is_normal
FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(nsamplecov), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(STDEV(nsamplecov), 2) AS stddev,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
),
skew_kurtosis AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SUM(POWER(nsamplecov - (SELECT mean FROM stats), 3)) / (n * POWER((SELECT stddev FROM stats), 3)), 2) AS skewness,
        ROUND(SUM(POWER(nsamplecov - (SELECT mean FROM stats), 4)) / (n * POWER((SELECT stddev FROM stats), 4)) - 3, 2) AS kurtosis
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`,
        stats
),
normality_test AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'yes' 
            ELSE 'no' 
        END AS is_normal,
        p_value
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            ROUND(1 - (1 / (1 + (0.5 * (SELECT skewness FROM skew_kurtosis)) * (SELECT skewness FROM skew_kurtosis) / (SELECT stddev FROM stats)))), 4) AS p_value
    )
)
SELECT 
    is_normal 
FROM 
    normality_test;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'yes' 
        ELSE 'no' 
    END AS is_normal
FROM (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SKEWNESS(nsamplecov), 2) AS skewness,
        ROUND(KURTOSIS(nsamplecov), 2) AS kurtosis,
        (SELECT 
            COUNT(*) AS n,
            SUM(nsamplecov) AS sum,
            SUM(nsamplecov * nsamplecov) AS sum_sq,
            (SUM(nsamplecov) * SUM(nsamplecov) / COUNT(*)) AS mean_sq
         FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table) AS stats,
        (1 - (1 / (1 + (stats.sum_sq - stats.mean_sq) / (stats.n - 1)))) AS p_value
    FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table
) AS normality_test;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(nsamplecov), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(STDEV(nsamplecov), 2) AS stddev,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM((nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER()) * (nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER())) AS sum_sq,
        SUM((nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER()) * (nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER()) * (nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER())) AS sum_cubed,
        SUM((nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER()) * (nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER()) * (nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER()) * (nsamplecov - AVG(nsamplecov) OVER())) AS sum_fourth
    FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table
),
normality_test AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * (n - 1) * SUM((nsamplecov - mean) * (nsamplecov - mean)) / SUM((nsamplecov - mean) * (nsamplecov - mean) * (nsamplecov - mean) * (nsamplecov - mean))), 2) AS W,
        ROUND(1 - (1 / (1 + (0.5 * (W - 0.5) / sqrt(n)))), 2) AS p_value
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'yes' 
        ELSE 'no' 
    END AS is_normal
FROM normality_test;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0)) AS mean,
        SUM(COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0) * COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0)) AS sum_sq,
        SUM(COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0) * COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0) * COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0)) AS sum_cub,
        SUM(COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0) * COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0) * COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0) * COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0)) AS sum_quad
    FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table
    WHERE nsamplecov IS NOT NULL
),
calc AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        mean,
        ROUND((sum_sq / n) - (mean * mean), 2) AS variance,
        ROUND(SQRT(variance), 2) AS stddev,
        ROUND((sum_cub / n - 3 * mean * variance - mean * mean * mean) / POW(NULLIF(stddev, 0), 3), 2) AS skewness,
        ROUND((sum_quad / n - 4 * mean * (sum_cub / n) + 6 * mean * mean * variance - 3 * variance * variance) / POW(NULLIF(variance, 0), 2), 2) AS kurtosis
    FROM stats
),
normality_test AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN n < 3 THEN NULL 
            ELSE ROUND((n * (n + 1) * SUM(POWER(nsamplecov - mean, 4)) / (pow(SUM(POWER(nsamplecov - mean, 2)), 2) * (n - 1) * (n - 1))) - 3, 2) AS kurtosis_test
        END AS kurtosis_test_result,
        CASE 
            WHEN n < 3 THEN NULL 
            ELSE ROUND((1 - (1 / (1 + 0.27 * (n - 1))) * (SUM(POWER(nsamplecov - mean, 2)) / n) / (SUM(POWER(nsamplecov - mean, 4)) / (n - 1))), 2) AS shapiro_wilk_test
        END AS shapiro_wilk_p_value
    FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table
    JOIN calc ON TRUE
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN shapiro_wilk_p_value IS NULL THEN 'no_data' 
        WHEN shapiro_wilk_p_value > 0.05 THEN 'yes' 
        ELSE 'no' 
    END AS is_normal  
FROM normality_test;

samples.question: 1. Is there a correlation between the "nsnps" and "nsamplecov" columns? Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength of the correlation. Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05. Report the p-value associated with the correlation test. If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between "nsnps" and "nsamplecov". Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05. Report the p-value associated with the correlation test. Consider the relationship to be correlated if the p-value is less than 0.05. If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND((SUM(nsnps * nsamplecov) - SUM(nsnps) * SUM(nsamplecov) / COUNT(*)) / 
              (SQRT((SUM(nsnps * nsnps) - SUM(nsnps) * SUM(nsnps) / COUNT(*)) * 
                     (SUM(nsamplecov * nsamplecov) - SUM(nsamplecov) * SUM(nsamplecov) / COUNT(*)))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
              CASE WHEN (COUNT(*) - 2) = 0 THEN 'no significant correlation' 
                   ELSE CASE WHEN (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient) * SQRT((COUNT(*) - 2) / (1 - correlation_coefficient * correlation_coefficient))) < 0.05 
                             THEN 'correlated' 
                             ELSE 'no significant correlation' END 
              END AS correlation
FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(nsnps) AS sum_nsnps,
        SUM(nsamplecov) AS sum_nsamplecov,
        SUM(nsnps * nsamplecov) AS sum_nsnps_nsamplecov,
        SUM(nsnps * nsnps) AS sum_nsnps_squared,
        SUM(nsamplecov * nsamplecov) AS sum_nsamplecov_squared
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_nsnps_nsamplecov - sum_nsnps * sum_nsamplecov) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_nsnps_squared - sum_nsnps * sum_nsnps) * (n * sum_nsamplecov_squared - sum_nsamplecov * sum_nsamplecov))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'correlated' 
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS correlation
FROM 
    correlation, p_value;

SELECT 
    ROUND(corr.numerator / (sqrt(corr.numerator_sq) * sqrt(corr.denominator_sq)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'correlated' 
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS correlation
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(nsnps) AS sum_x,
        SUM(nsamplecov) AS sum_y,
        SUM(nsnps * nsamplecov) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(nsnps * nsnps) AS sum_x_sq,
        SUM(nsamplecov * nsamplecov) AS sum_y_sq
    FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table
) AS corr,
(
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table) - 2) / 
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table) AS p_value
) AS p
WHERE 
    corr.n > 1;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        nsnps,
        nsamplecov,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS n,
        AVG(nsnps) OVER () AS avg_nsnps,
        AVG(nsamplecov) OVER () AS avg_nsamplecov,
        SUM(nsnps * nsamplecov) OVER () AS sum_product,
        SUM(nsnps * nsnps) OVER () AS sum_nsnps_squared,
        SUM(nsamplecov * nsamplecov) OVER () AS sum_nsamplecov_squared
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
),
correlation_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        (sum_product - n * avg_nsnps * avg_nsamplecov) / 
        (SQRT(sum_nsnps_squared - n * avg_nsnps * avg_nsnps) * SQRT(sum_nsamplecov_squared - n * avg_nsamplecov * avg_nsamplecov)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
t_statistic AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        (correlation_coefficient * SQRT(n - 2)) / SQRT(1 - correlation_coefficient * correlation_coefficient) AS t_value,
        n - 2 AS df
    FROM 
        correlation_stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        2 * (1 - T_DIST(ABS(t_value), df)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        t_statistic
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'correlated'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS correlation
FROM 
    p_value;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(nsnps, 0) AS nsnps,
        COALESCE(nsamplecov, 0) AS nsamplecov
    FROM 
        `ts-sc4-wi100000-sl25000-Qrob_Chr05.tree_table`
    WHERE 
        nsnps IS NOT NULL AND 
        nsamplecov IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(nsnps) AS sum_x,
        SUM(nsamplecov) AS sum_y,
        SUM(nsnps * nsamplecov) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(nsnps * nsnps) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(nsamplecov * nsamplecov) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)), 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(1 - (ABS(correlation_coefficient) * SQRT((n - 2) / (1 - correlation_coefficient * correlation_coefficient))), 4) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)

SELECT 
    correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'correlated' 
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS correlation 
FROM 
    correlation, p_value;

samples.question: Check if the fare variable follows a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Use the Shapiro-Wilk test to check for normality. The null hypothesis for this test is that the data is normally distributed. If the p-value is less than 0.05, reject the null hypothesis and conclude that the data is not normally distributed. If the p-value is greater than 0.05, fail to reject the null hypothesis and conclude that the data is normally distributed.

variants.sql:
SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN FALSE ELSE TRUE END AS normality_test_result
FROM (
    SELECT Fare, 
           COUNT(*) AS count, 
           AVG(Fare) AS mean, 
           SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) AS variance_sum
    FROM titanic
    GROUP BY Fare
) AS fare_stats
HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 AND (COUNT(*) - 1) * (SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) / COUNT(*)) < 0.05;

WITH fare_data AS (
    SELECT Fare
    FROM titanic
),
fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Fare) AS mean,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev
    FROM fare_data
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        mean,
        stddev,
        ROUND((SUM((Fare - mean) * (Fare - mean)) / (n - 1)), 2) AS variance,
        ROUND((SUM((Fare - mean) * (Fare - mean)) / (n - 1)) / (stddev * stddev), 2) AS test_statistic
    FROM fare_data, fare_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN test_statistic < 0.05 THEN FALSE 
        ELSE TRUE 
    END AS normality_test_result
FROM shapiro_test;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic) < 3 THEN 'normality_test_result=False'
        ELSE 
            CASE 
                WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL) > 0 THEN 
                    CASE 
                        WHEN (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) FROM titanic) IS NOT NULL THEN 'normality_test_result=True'
                        ELSE 'normality_test_result=False'
                    END
                ELSE 'normality_test_result=False'
            END
    END AS normality_test_result;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        AVG(Fare) OVER () AS avg_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) OVER () AS stddev_fare,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS n
    FROM titanic
),
normality_test AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(POWER((Fare - avg_fare) / stddev_fare, 2)) AS W,
        n
    FROM fare_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (1 - (W / (n - 1))) < 0.05 THEN FALSE 
        ELSE TRUE 
    END AS normality_test_result
FROM normality_test;

WITH fare_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare
    FROM 
        titanic
    WHERE 
        Fare IS NOT NULL
),
fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Fare) AS mean,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev
    FROM 
        fare_data
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        mean,
        stddev,
        ROUND((SUM(POWER(Fare - mean, 2)) / NULLIF(n, 0)), 2) AS variance,
        ROUND((SUM(POWER(Fare - mean, 2)) / NULLIF(n, 0)) / (stddev * stddev), 2) AS test_statistic
    FROM 
        fare_data, fare_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND(test_statistic, 2) < 0.05 THEN FALSE 
        ELSE TRUE 
    END AS normality_test_result
FROM 
    shapiro_test;

samples.question: Use feature engineering techniques to create a new variable "Title" by extracting the title from the Name column (e.g., "Mr.", "Mrs.", "Miss"). Only consider the following titles: 'Mr.', 'Mrs.', 'Miss.' and 'Master.' (titles followed by a dot). Then, calculate the average fare for each unique title to two decimal places.
samples.constraints: Only the titles 'Mr.', 'Mrs.', 'Miss.' and 'Master.' should be considered. Titles that do not fall within these four categories should be eliminated.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    SUBSTR(Name, INSTR(Name, ' ') + 1, INSTR(SUBSTR(Name, INSTR(Name, ' ') + 1), '.') - 1) AS Title,
    ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS average_fare
FROM 
    titanic
WHERE 
    SUBSTR(Name, INSTR(Name, ' ') + 1, INSTR(SUBSTR(Name, INSTR(Name, ' ') + 1), '.') - 1) IN ('Mr', 'Mrs', 'Miss', 'Master')
GROUP BY 
    Title;

WITH ExtractedTitles AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr.%' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs.%' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss.%' THEN 'Miss.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Master.%' THEN 'Master.'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS Title,
        Fare
    FROM titanic
),
FilteredTitles AS (
    SELECT Title, Fare
    FROM ExtractedTitles
    WHERE Title IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    Title,
    ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS average_fare
FROM FilteredTitles
GROUP BY Title;

SELECT Title, ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS average_fare
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr.%' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs.%' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss.%' THEN 'Miss.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Master.%' THEN 'Master.'
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS Title,
        Fare
    FROM titanic
) AS Titles
WHERE Title IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY Title;

WITH extracted_titles AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr.%' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs.%' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss.%' THEN 'Miss.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Master.%' THEN 'Master.'
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS Title,
        Fare
    FROM titanic
)
SELECT 
    Title,
    ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS average_fare
FROM extracted_titles
WHERE Title IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY Title;

WITH extracted_titles AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr.%' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs.%' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss.%' THEN 'Miss.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Master.%' THEN 'Master.'
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS Title,
        Fare
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Name IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    Title,
    ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2) AS average_fare
FROM extracted_titles
WHERE Title IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY Title;

samples.question: Perform distribution analysis on the age and fare variables separately, then calculate and compare the skewness and kurtosis values for each. Additionally, count the number of values within one standard deviation from the mean, for both age and fare.
samples.constraints: Use Python's scipy library for your analyses. Calculate skewness and kurtosis values using the scipy.stats.skew() and scipy.stats.kurtosis() functions, respectively, with the default settings. Count the number of values within one standard deviation from the mean by applying standard formula: mean - stdev <= x <= mean + stdev.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS fare_mean,
    ROUND(STDEV(Fare), 2) AS fare_stdev,
    ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) AS age_mean,
    ROUND(STDEV(Age), 2) AS age_stdev,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Fare BETWEEN (AVG(Fare) - STDEV(Fare)) AND (AVG(Fare) + STDEV(Fare)) THEN 1 END) AS fare_values_within_one_stdev,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Age BETWEEN (AVG(Age) - STDEV(Age)) AND (AVG(Age) + STDEV(Age)) THEN 1 END) AS age_values_within_one_stdev,
    ROUND((SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) / COUNT(Fare)) / (STDEV(Fare) * STDEV(Fare)), 2) AS fare_kurtosis,
    ROUND((SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age))) / COUNT(Age)) / (STDEV(Age) * STDEV(Age)), 2) AS age_kurtosis,
    ROUND((SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare)) / STDEV(Fare) * (Fare - AVG(Fare)) / STDEV(Fare) * (Fare - AVG(Fare)) / STDEV(Fare)) / COUNT(Fare)), 2) AS fare_skewness,
    ROUND((SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) / STDEV(Age) * (Age - AVG(Age)) / STDEV(Age) * (Age - AVG(Age)) / STDEV(Age)) / COUNT(Age)), 2) AS age_skewness
FROM titanic;

WITH age_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Age) AS mean_age,
        STDDEV(Age) AS stdev_age,
        COUNT(*) AS total_age
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL
),
fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Fare) AS mean_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stdev_fare,
        COUNT(*) AS total_fare
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
),
age_distribution AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SKEWNESS(Age), 2) AS age_skewness,
        ROUND(KURTOSIS(Age), 2) AS age_kurtosis,
        COUNT(*) AS age_values_within_one_stdev
    FROM titanic, age_stats
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL 
    AND Age BETWEEN (mean_age - stdev_age) AND (mean_age + stdev_age)
),
fare_distribution AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SKEWNESS(Fare), 2) AS fare_skewness,
        ROUND(KURTOSIS(Fare), 2) AS fare_kurtosis,
        COUNT(*) AS fare_values_within_one_stdev
    FROM titanic, fare_stats
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL 
    AND Fare BETWEEN (mean_fare - stdev_fare) AND (mean_fare + stdev_fare)
)
SELECT 
    fare_kurtosis, 
    age_values_within_one_stdev, 
    fare_skewness, 
    fare_values_within_one_stdev, 
    age_skewness, 
    age_kurtosis
FROM age_distribution, fare_distribution;

SELECT 
    (SELECT ROUND((SUM((Fare - fare_mean) * (Fare - fare_mean) * (Fare - fare_mean)) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM((Fare - fare_mean) * (Fare - fare_mean)) / COUNT(*)) * COUNT(*), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT Fare, AVG(Fare) AS fare_mean, STDEV(Fare) AS fare_stdev FROM titanic) AS fare_stats 
     WHERE Fare BETWEEN fare_mean - fare_stdev AND fare_mean + fare_stdev) AS fare_values_within_one_stdev,
    (SELECT ROUND((SUM((Age - age_mean) * (Age - age_mean) * (Age - age_mean)) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM((Age - age_mean) * (Age - age_mean)) / COUNT(*)) * COUNT(*), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT Age, AVG(Age) AS age_mean, STDEV(Age) AS age_stdev FROM titanic) AS age_stats 
     WHERE Age BETWEEN age_mean - age_stdev AND age_mean + age_stdev) AS age_values_within_one_stdev,
    (SELECT ROUND((SUM((Fare - fare_mean) / (STDEV(Fare) / COUNT(*))) / COUNT(*)), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT Fare, AVG(Fare) AS fare_mean FROM titanic) AS fare_stats) AS fare_skewness,
    (SELECT ROUND((SUM((Age - age_mean) / (STDEV(Age) / COUNT(*))) / COUNT(*)), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT Age, AVG(Age) AS age_mean FROM titanic) AS age_stats) AS age_skewness,
    (SELECT ROUND((SUM((Fare - fare_mean) * (Fare - fare_mean) * (Fare - fare_mean)) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM((Fare - fare_mean) * (Fare - fare_mean)) / COUNT(*)), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT Fare, AVG(Fare) AS fare_mean FROM titanic) AS fare_stats) AS fare_kurtosis,
    (SELECT ROUND((SUM((Age - age_mean) * (Age - age_mean) * (Age - age_mean)) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM((Age - age_mean) * (Age - age_mean)) / COUNT(*)), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT Age, AVG(Age) AS age_mean FROM titanic) AS age_stats) AS age_kurtosis;

WITH age_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Age) AS mean_age,
        STDDEV(Age) AS stdev_age,
        COUNT(*) AS total_age
    FROM titanic
),
fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Fare) AS mean_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stdev_fare,
        COUNT(*) AS total_fare
    FROM titanic
),
age_distribution AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS age_values_within_one_stdev
    FROM titanic, age_stats
    WHERE Age BETWEEN (mean_age - stdev_age) AND (mean_age + stdev_age)
),
fare_distribution AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS fare_values_within_one_stdev
    FROM titanic, fare_stats
    WHERE Fare BETWEEN (mean_fare - stdev_fare) AND (mean_fare + stdev_fare)
),
skew_kurtosis AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SKEW(Age) OVER (), 2) AS age_skewness,
        ROUND(KURT(Age) OVER (), 2) AS age_kurtosis,
        ROUND(SKEW(Fare) OVER (), 2) AS fare_skewness,
        ROUND(KURT(Fare) OVER (), 2) AS fare_kurtosis
    FROM titanic
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    age_skewness,
    age_kurtosis,
    age_values_within_one_stdev,
    fare_skewness,
    fare_kurtosis,
    fare_values_within_one_stdev
FROM skew_kurtosis, age_distribution, fare_distribution;

WITH age_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Age, 0)), 2) AS mean_age,
        ROUND(STDEV(COALESCE(Age, 0)), 2) AS stdev_age,
        COUNT(*) AS count_age,
        ROUND(SUM(POW(COALESCE(Age, 0) - ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Age, 0)), 2), 3)) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS skewness_age,
        ROUND(SUM(POW(COALESCE(Age, 0) - ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Age, 0)), 2), 4)) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) - 3, 2) AS kurtosis_age
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL
),
fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2) AS mean_fare,
        ROUND(STDEV(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2) AS stdev_fare,
        COUNT(*) AS count_fare,
        ROUND(SUM(POW(COALESCE(Fare, 0) - ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2), 3)) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS skewness_fare,
        ROUND(SUM(POW(COALESCE(Fare, 0) - ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2), 4)) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) - 3, 2) AS kurtosis_fare
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
),
age_within_stdev AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS values_within_one_stdev
    FROM titanic
    JOIN age_stats ON COALESCE(Age, 0) BETWEEN mean_age - stdev_age AND mean_age + stdev_age
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL
),
fare_within_stdev AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS values_within_one_stdev
    FROM titanic
    JOIN fare_stats ON COALESCE(Fare, 0) BETWEEN mean_fare - stdev_fare AND mean_fare + stdev_fare
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(fare_stats.kurtosis_fare, 2) AS fare_kurtosis,
    age_within_stdev.values_within_one_stdev AS age_values_within_one_stdev,
    ROUND(fare_stats.skewness_fare, 2) AS fare_skewness,
    fare_within_stdev.values_within_one_stdev AS fare_values_within_one_stdev,
    ROUND(age_stats.skewness_age, 2) AS age_skewness,
    ROUND(age_stats.kurtosis_age, 2) AS age_kurtosis
FROM age_stats, fare_stats, age_within_stdev, fare_within_stdev;

samples.question: Perform a correlation analysis on the numerical variables (age, fare, SibSp, Parch) to identify any significant relationships. Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficients between all pairs of these variables and identify the pair with the strongest positive correlation.
samples.constraints: Use Python's pandas library for correlation analysis. Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficients using the 'pandas.DataFrame.corr()' function with the default method (Pearson). The pair should not compare a variable with itself.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) AS Avg_Age,
    ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS Avg_Fare,
    ROUND(AVG(SibSp), 2) AS Avg_SibSp,
    ROUND(AVG(Parch), 2) AS Avg_Parch,
    ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) AS Correlation_Age_Fare,
    ROUND(CORR(Age, SibSp), 2) AS Correlation_Age_SibSp,
    ROUND(CORR(Age, Parch), 2) AS Correlation_Age_Parch,
    ROUND(CORR(Fare, SibSp), 2) AS Correlation_Fare_SibSp,
    ROUND(CORR(Fare, Parch), 2) AS Correlation_Fare_Parch,
    ROUND(CORR(SibSp, Parch), 2) AS Correlation_SibSp_Parch
FROM titanic;

WITH correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG((Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic)) * (Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic))), 2) / 
        (ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic), 2))), 2) * 
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic), 2))), 2), 2) AS Age_Fare,
        
        ROUND(AVG((Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic)) * (SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic))), 2) / 
        (ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic), 2))), 2) * 
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic), 2))), 2), 2) AS Age_SibSp,
        
        ROUND(AVG((Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic)) * (Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic))), 2) / 
        (ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic), 2))), 2) * 
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic), 2))), 2), 2) AS Age_Parch,
        
        ROUND(AVG((Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic)) * (SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic))), 2) / 
        (ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic), 2))), 2) * 
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic), 2))), 2), 2) AS Fare_SibSp,
        
        ROUND(AVG((Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic)) * (Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic))), 2) / 
        (ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic), 2))), 2) * 
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic), 2))), 2), 2) AS Fare_Parch,
        
        ROUND(AVG((SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic)) * (Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic))), 2) / 
        (ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic), 2))), 2) * 
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG(POWER(Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic), 2))), 2), 2) AS SibSp_Parch
)

SELECT MAX(ROUND(value, 2)) AS strongest_correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT Age_Fare AS value FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT Age_SibSp FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT Age_Parch FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT Fare_SibSp FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT Fare_Parch FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT SibSp_Parch FROM correlation
) AS correlations;

SELECT ROUND(MAX(corr_value), 2) AS strongest_correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic) AS avg_age,
        (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic) AS avg_fare,
        (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic) AS avg_sibsp,
        (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic) AS avg_parch,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic) AS n,
        (SUM((Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic)) * (Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic))) / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / 
         (SELECT STDEV(Age) FROM titanic) * (SELECT STDEV(Fare) FROM titanic)) AS corr_age_fare,
        (SUM((Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic)) * (SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic))) / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / 
         (SELECT STDEV(Age) FROM titanic) * (SELECT STDEV(SibSp) FROM titanic)) AS corr_age_sibsp,
        (SUM((Age - (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic)) * (Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic))) / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / 
         (SELECT STDEV(Age) FROM titanic) * (SELECT STDEV(Parch) FROM titanic)) AS corr_age_parch,
        (SUM((Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic)) * (SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic))) / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / 
         (SELECT STDEV(Fare) FROM titanic) * (SELECT STDEV(SibSp) FROM titanic)) AS corr_fare_sibsp,
        (SUM((Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic)) * (Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic))) / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / 
         (SELECT STDEV(Fare) FROM titanic) * (SELECT STDEV(Parch) FROM titanic)) AS corr_fare_parch,
        (SUM((SibSp - (SELECT AVG(SibSp) FROM titanic)) * (Parch - (SELECT AVG(Parch) FROM titanic))) / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / 
         (SELECT STDEV(SibSp) FROM titanic) * (SELECT STDEV(Parch) FROM titanic)) AS corr_sibsp_parch
    FROM titanic
) AS correlations
WHERE corr_value IS NOT NULL;

WITH correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Age * Fare), 2) AS age_fare,
        ROUND(AVG(Age * SibSp), 2) AS age_sibsp,
        ROUND(AVG(Age * Parch), 2) AS age_parch,
        ROUND(AVG(Fare * SibSp), 2) AS fare_sibsp,
        ROUND(AVG(Fare * Parch), 2) AS fare_parch,
        ROUND(AVG(SibSp * Parch), 2) AS sibsp_parch
    FROM titanic
),
correlation_values AS (
    SELECT 
        'Age and Fare' AS variable_pair, age_fare AS correlation_coefficient FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'Age and SibSp', age_sibsp FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'Age and Parch', age_parch FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'Fare and SibSp', fare_sibsp FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'Fare and Parch', fare_parch FROM correlation
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'SibSp and Parch', sibsp_parch FROM correlation
)
SELECT 
    MAX(correlation_coefficient) AS strongest_correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation_values;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Age, 0)), 2) AS avg_age,
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2) AS avg_fare,
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(SibSp, 0)), 2) AS avg_sibsp,
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(Parch, 0)), 2) AS avg_parch,
        COUNT(*) AS total_count
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL AND SibSp IS NOT NULL AND Parch IS NOT NULL
),
covariances AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(COVAR_POP(COALESCE(Age, 0), COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2) AS cov_age_fare,
        ROUND(COVAR_POP(COALESCE(Age, 0), COALESCE(SibSp, 0)), 2) AS cov_age_sibsp,
        ROUND(COVAR_POP(COALESCE(Age, 0), COALESCE(Parch, 0)), 2) AS cov_age_parch,
        ROUND(COVAR_POP(COALESCE(Fare, 0), COALESCE(SibSp, 0)), 2) AS cov_fare_sibsp,
        ROUND(COVAR_POP(COALESCE(Fare, 0), COALESCE(Parch, 0)), 2) AS cov_fare_parch,
        ROUND(COVAR_POP(COALESCE(SibSp, 0), COALESCE(Parch, 0)), 2) AS cov_sibsp_parch
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL AND SibSp IS NOT NULL AND Parch IS NOT NULL
),
correlations AS (
    SELECT
        ROUND(COALESCE(cov_age_fare, 0) / NULLIF(ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Age, 0)), 2) * ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2), 0), 2) , 2) AS corr_age_fare,
        ROUND(COALESCE(cov_age_sibsp, 0) / NULLIF(ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Age, 0)), 2) * ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(SibSp, 0)), 2), 0), 2) , 2) AS corr_age_sibsp,
        ROUND(COALESCE(cov_age_parch, 0) / NULLIF(ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Age, 0)), 2) * ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Parch, 0)), 2), 0), 2) , 2) AS corr_age_parch,
        ROUND(COALESCE(cov_fare_sibsp, 0) / NULLIF(ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2) * ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(SibSp, 0)), 2), 0), 2) , 2) AS corr_fare_sibsp,
        ROUND(COALESCE(cov_fare_parch, 0) / NULLIF(ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2) * ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Parch, 0)), 2), 0), 2) , 2) AS corr_fare_parch,
        ROUND(COALESCE(cov_sibsp_parch, 0) / NULLIF(ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(SibSp, 0)), 2) * ROUND(STDDEV_POP(COALESCE(Parch, 0)), 2), 0), 2) , 2) AS corr_sibsp_parch
    FROM covariances
)
SELECT 
    MAX(ROUND(LEAST(corr_age_fare, corr_age_sibsp, corr_age_parch, corr_fare_sibsp, corr_fare_parch, corr_sibsp_parch), 2)) AS strongest_correlation_coefficient
FROM correlations;

samples.question: What is the mean of the EVENTMSGTYPE column?

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(EVENTMSGTYPE), 2) AS mean_eventmsgtype FROM `0020200722` WHERE EVENTMSGTYPE IS NOT NULL;

SELECT ROUND(AVG(EVENTMSGTYPE), 2) AS mean_eventmsgtype

samples.question: Are there any outliers in the SCOREMARGIN column? If so, how many?
samples.constraints: You should use the IQR method to define an outlier. An observation is considered an outlier if it lies 1.5 IQR below the first quartile or 1.5 IQR above the third quartile. Since SCOREMARGIN contains string values, first convert the SCOREMARGIN data into integer or float and then clean the data by ignoring any non-numeric characters or punctuation marks.

variants.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM (
    SELECT SCOREMARGIN,
           PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN) AS Q1,
           PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN) AS Q3,
           (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN)) AS IQR
    FROM (
        SELECT CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(SCOREMARGIN, ' ', ''), 'NaN', '') AS FLOAT) AS SCOREMARGIN
        FROM `0020200722`
        WHERE SCOREMARGIN IS NOT NULL
    )
) AS stats
WHERE SCOREMARGIN < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR SCOREMARGIN > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR);

WITH CleanedData AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(SCOREMARGIN, ' ', ''), '(', ''), ')', '') AS FLOAT) AS SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        SCOREMARGIN IS NOT NULL
),
Quartiles AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN) AS Q3
    FROM 
        CleanedData
),
IQR AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM 
        Quartiles
),
OutlierBounds AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1 - 1.5 * IQR AS LowerBound,
        Q3 + 1.5 * IQR AS UpperBound
    FROM 
        IQR
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    CleanedData, OutlierBounds
WHERE 
    SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN < LowerBound OR SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN > UpperBound;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM (
    SELECT SCOREMARGIN,
           (SELECT ROUND(QUANTILE(SCOREMARGIN, 0.25), 2) FROM (SELECT CAST(SCOREMARGIN AS FLOAT) AS SCOREMARGIN FROM 0020200722 WHERE SCOREMARGIN IS NOT NULL)) AS Q1,
           (SELECT ROUND(QUANTILE(SCOREMARGIN, 0.75), 2) FROM (SELECT CAST(SCOREMARGIN AS FLOAT) AS SCOREMARGIN FROM 0020200722 WHERE SCOREMARGIN IS NOT NULL)) AS Q3
    FROM (SELECT CAST(SCOREMARGIN AS FLOAT) AS SCOREMARGIN FROM 0020200722 WHERE SCOREMARGIN IS NOT NULL)
) AS subquery
WHERE SCOREMARGIN < (Q1 - 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1)) OR SCOREMARGIN > (Q3 + 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1));

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT
        CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(SCOREMARGIN, ' ', ''), ',', '.') AS FLOAT) AS SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN
    FROM
        `0020200722`
    WHERE
        SCOREMARGIN IS NOT NULL
),
quartiles AS (
    SELECT
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN) AS Q3
    FROM
        cleaned_data
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM
        quartiles
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
    FROM
        cleaned_data, iqr
    WHERE
        SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
)
SELECT
    outlier_count
FROM
    outliers;

WITH CleanedData AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(NULLIF(REGEXP_REPLACE(SCOREMARGIN, '[^0-9.-]', ''), '') AS FLOAT) AS SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        SCOREMARGIN IS NOT NULL
),
Statistics AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN) AS Q3
    FROM 
        CleanedData
),
IQR AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM 
        Statistics
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    CleanedData, IQR
WHERE 
    SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN IS NOT NULL 
    AND (SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR SCOREMARGIN_CLEAN > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR));

samples.question: Are there any missing values in the dataset? If so, which column has the highest number of missing values?
samples.constraints: Columns with missing values: HOMEDESCRIPTION, NEUTRALDESCRIPTION, VISITORDESCRIPTION, SCORE, SCOREMARGIN.

variants.sql:
SELECT column_name, COUNT(*) AS missing_count
FROM (
    SELECT 'HOMEDESCRIPTION' AS column_name, COUNT(*) AS missing_count FROM `0020200722` WHERE HOMEDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'NEUTRALDESCRIPTION', COUNT(*) FROM `0020200722` WHERE NEUTRALDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'VISITORDESCRIPTION', COUNT(*) FROM `0020200722` WHERE VISITORDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'SCORE', COUNT(*) FROM `0020200722` WHERE SCORE IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'SCOREMARGIN', COUNT(*) FROM `0020200722` WHERE SCOREMARGIN IS NULL
) AS missing_counts
ORDER BY missing_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

WITH MissingCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(CASE WHEN HOMEDESCRIPTION IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS HOMEDESCRIPTION_MISSING,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN NEUTRALDESCRIPTION IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS NEUTRALDESCRIPTION_MISSING,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN VISITORDESCRIPTION IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS VISITORDESCRIPTION_MISSING,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN SCORE IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS SCORE_MISSING,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN SCOREMARGIN IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS SCOREMARGIN_MISSING
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
),
MaxMissing AS (
    SELECT 
        'HOMEDESCRIPTION' AS COLUMN_NAME, HOMEDESCRIPTION_MISSING AS MISSING_COUNT FROM MissingCounts
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'NEUTRALDESCRIPTION', NEUTRALDESCRIPTION_MISSING FROM MissingCounts
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'VISITORDESCRIPTION', VISITORDESCRIPTION_MISSING FROM MissingCounts
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'SCORE', SCORE_MISSING FROM MissingCounts
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'SCOREMARGIN', SCOREMARGIN_MISSING FROM MissingCounts
)
SELECT 
    COLUMN_NAME
FROM 
    MaxMissing
WHERE 
    MISSING_COUNT = (SELECT MAX(MISSING_COUNT) FROM MaxMissing);

SELECT column_name
FROM (
    SELECT 'HOMEDESCRIPTION' AS column_name, COUNT(*) AS missing_count
    FROM 0020200722
    WHERE HOMEDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'NEUTRALDESCRIPTION', COUNT(*)
    FROM 0020200722
    WHERE NEUTRALDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'VISITORDESCRIPTION', COUNT(*)
    FROM 0020200722
    WHERE VISITORDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'SCORE', COUNT(*)
    FROM 0020200722
    WHERE SCORE IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'SCOREMARGIN', COUNT(*)
    FROM 0020200722
    WHERE SCOREMARGIN IS NULL
) AS missing_counts
WHERE missing_count = (
    SELECT MAX(missing_count)
    FROM (
        SELECT COUNT(*) AS missing_count
        FROM 0020200722
        WHERE HOMEDESCRIPTION IS NULL
        UNION ALL
        SELECT COUNT(*)
        FROM 0020200722
        WHERE NEUTRALDESCRIPTION IS NULL
        UNION ALL
        SELECT COUNT(*)
        FROM 0020200722
        WHERE VISITORDESCRIPTION IS NULL
        UNION ALL
        SELECT COUNT(*)
        FROM 0020200722
        WHERE SCORE IS NULL
        UNION ALL
        SELECT COUNT(*)
        FROM 0020200722
        WHERE SCOREMARGIN IS NULL
    )
);

WITH missing_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        'HOMEDESCRIPTION' AS column_name, COUNT(*) AS missing_count 
    FROM 
        0020200722 
    WHERE 
        HOMEDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'NEUTRALDESCRIPTION' AS column_name, COUNT(*) AS missing_count 
    FROM 
        0020200722 
    WHERE 
        NEUTRALDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'VISITORDESCRIPTION' AS column_name, COUNT(*) AS missing_count 
    FROM 
        0020200722 
    WHERE 
        VISITORDESCRIPTION IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'SCORE' AS column_name, COUNT(*) AS missing_count 
    FROM 
        0020200722 
    WHERE 
        SCORE IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'SCOREMARGIN' AS column_name, COUNT(*) AS missing_count 
    FROM 
        0020200722 
    WHERE 
        SCOREMARGIN IS NULL
),
ranked_missing AS (
    SELECT 
        column_name, 
        missing_count, 
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY missing_count DESC) AS rank
    FROM 
        missing_counts
)
SELECT 
    column_name AS max_missing_values 
FROM 
    ranked_missing 
WHERE 
    rank = 1;

WITH missing_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        'HOMEDESCRIPTION' AS column_name,
        COUNT(*) - COUNT(HOMEDESCRIPTION) AS missing_count
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        HOMEDESCRIPTION IS NULL

    UNION ALL

    SELECT 
        'NEUTRALDESCRIPTION' AS column_name,
        COUNT(*) - COUNT(NEUTRALDESCRIPTION) AS missing_count
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        NEUTRALDESCRIPTION IS NULL

    UNION ALL

    SELECT 
        'VISITORDESCRIPTION' AS column_name,
        COUNT(*) - COUNT(VISITORDESCRIPTION) AS missing_count
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        VISITORDESCRIPTION IS NULL

    UNION ALL

    SELECT 
        'SCORE' AS column_name,
        COUNT(*) - COUNT(SCORE) AS missing_count
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        SCORE IS NULL

    UNION ALL

    SELECT 
        'SCOREMARGIN' AS column_name,
        COUNT(*) - COUNT(SCOREMARGIN) AS missing_count
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        SCOREMARGIN IS NULL
)

SELECT 
    column_name 
FROM 
    missing_counts 
WHERE 
    missing_count = (SELECT MAX(missing_count) FROM missing_counts)
LIMIT 1;

samples.question: Create a new feature named "event_hour" that represents the hour of the day (in 24-hour format) when each event occurred. Perform a correlation analysis to determine if there is a relationship between the event hour and the event type (EVENTMSGTYPE).
samples.constraints:
{
Extract the hour from the WCTIMESTRING column using string manipulation functions.
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between event_hour and EVENTMSGTYPE.
Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05.
Report the p-value associated with the correlation test.
Consider the relationship to be positive if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the correlation coefficient is greater than or equal to 0.5.
Consider the relationship to be negative if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the correlation coefficient is less than or equal to -0.5.
If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.
}

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER), EVENTMSGTYPE), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    2 * (1 - ABS(CORR(CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER), EVENTMSGTYPE))) AS p_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN 2 * (1 - ABS(CORR(CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER), EVENTMSGTYPE))) < 0.05 AND CORR(CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER), EVENTMSGTYPE) >= 0.5 THEN 'positive'
        WHEN 2 * (1 - ABS(CORR(CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER), EVENTMSGTYPE))) < 0.05 AND CORR(CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER), EVENTMSGTYPE) <= -0.5 THEN 'negative'
        ELSE 'none'
    END AS relationship
FROM 
    `0020200722`;

WITH event_data AS (
    SELECT 
        EVENTMSGTYPE,
        CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) AS event_hour
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        WCTIMESTRING IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(event_hour) AS sum_event_hour,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_event_type,
        SUM(event_hour * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_product,
        SUM(event_hour * event_hour) AS sum_event_hour_sq,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_event_type_sq
    FROM 
        event_data
),
correlation_results AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        (n * sum_product - sum_event_hour * sum_event_type) AS numerator,
        (n * sum_event_hour_sq - sum_event_hour * sum_event_hour) * (n * sum_event_type_sq - sum_event_type * sum_event_type) AS denominator,
