        (n * sum_product - sum_event_hour * sum_event_type) / SQRT((n * sum_event_hour_sq - sum_event_hour * sum_event_hour) * (n * sum_event_type_sq - sum_event_type * sum_event_type)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        n,
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation_results
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient >= 0.5 THEN 'relationship=positive'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient <= -0.5 THEN 'relationship=negative'
        ELSE 'relationship=none'
    END AS relationship,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    p_value_calculation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient >= 0.5 THEN 'relationship=positive'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient <= -0.5 THEN 'relationship=negative'
        ELSE 'relationship=none'
    END AS relationship,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(event_hour * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS cov_xy,
        SUM(event_hour) AS sum_x,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_y,
        SUM(event_hour * event_hour) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_yy,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT GAME_ID, EVENTNUM FROM 0020200722)) AS total_events
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            GAME_ID, 
            EVENTNUM, 
            EVENTMSGTYPE, 
            CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) AS event_hour
        FROM 0020200722
        WHERE WCTIMESTRING IS NOT NULL
    )
) AS stats,
(
    SELECT 
        (n * cov_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS correlation_coefficient,
        (SELECT 
            2 * (1 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT GAME_ID, EVENTNUM FROM 0020200722))) 
            / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT GAME_ID, EVENTNUM FROM 0020200722)) 
            FROM (SELECT DISTINCT GAME_ID, EVENTNUM FROM 0020200722)) AS p_value
) AS correlation_results;

WITH event_data AS (
    SELECT 
        GAME_ID,
        EVENTNUM,
        EVENTMSGTYPE,
        CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) AS event_hour
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
),
correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        EVENTMSGTYPE,
        event_hour,
        COUNT(*) AS count_events
    FROM 
        event_data
    GROUP BY 
        EVENTMSGTYPE, event_hour
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(event_hour) AS avg_hour,
        AVG(EVENTMSGTYPE) AS avg_event_type,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(event_hour * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_product,
        SUM(event_hour) AS sum_hour,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_event_type,
        SUM(event_hour * event_hour) AS sum_hour_sq,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_event_type_sq
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_product - sum_hour * sum_event_type) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_hour_sq - sum_hour * sum_hour) * (n * sum_event_type_sq - sum_event_type * sum_event_type))) AS correlation_coefficient,
        n AS sample_size
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN correlation_coefficient >= 0.5 AND p_value < 0.05 THEN 'positive'
        WHEN correlation_coefficient <= -0.5 AND p_value < 0.05 THEN 'negative'
        ELSE 'none'
    END AS relationship,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(1.0 - (1.0 / (1.0 + EXP(-1.0 * ABS(correlation_coefficient) * SQRT(sample_size - 2)))), 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    correlation;

WITH event_data AS (
    SELECT 
        GAME_ID,
        EVENTNUM,
        EVENTMSGTYPE,
        CAST(SUBSTR(WCTIMESTRING, 1, INSTR(WCTIMESTRING, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) AS event_hour
    FROM 
        `0020200722`
    WHERE 
        WCTIMESTRING IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(event_hour) AS sum_event_hour,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_event_msg_type,
        SUM(event_hour * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_product,
        SUM(event_hour * event_hour) AS sum_event_hour_squared,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_event_msg_type_squared
    FROM 
        event_data
    WHERE 
        EVENTMSGTYPE IS NOT NULL AND event_hour IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_results AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        SUM(event_hour) AS sum_event_hour,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_event_msg_type,
        SUM(event_hour * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_product,
        SUM(event_hour * event_hour) AS sum_event_hour_squared,
        SUM(EVENTMSGTYPE * EVENTMSGTYPE) AS sum_event_msg_type_squared,
        ((n * sum_product) - (sum_event_hour * sum_event_msg_type)) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_event_hour_squared - sum_event_hour * sum_event_hour) * 
               (n * sum_event_msg_type_squared - sum_event_msg_type * sum_event_msg_type))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        n,
        2 * (1 - CDF_NORMAL(ABS(correlation_coefficient) * SQRT((n - 2) / (1 - correlation_coefficient * correlation_coefficient)))) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation_results
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient >= 0.5 THEN 'positive'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND correlation_coefficient <= -0.5 THEN 'negative'
        ELSE 'none' 
    END AS relationship,
    ROUND(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(COALESCE(p_value, 1), 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    p_value_calculation;

samples.question: 2. Is the distribution of the median sold price per square foot skewed? If yes, is it positively or negatively skewed?
samples.constraints: For determining the skewness, consider only non-null values. Use the Fisher-Pearson standardized moment coefficient for assessing the skewness. A skewness value > 0 means that there is more weight in the right tail of the distribution (positive skewness). A skewness value < 0 means that there is more weight in the left tail of the distribution (negative skewness). Calculate the skewness up to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (COUNT(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) * SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price)) / 
              (SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price)) * COUNT(MedianSoldPricePerSqft)) - 3) > 0 
        THEN 'Positive Skewness' 
        ELSE 'Negative Skewness' 
    END AS skewness_type,
    ROUND((COUNT(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) * SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price)) / 
           (SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price)) * COUNT(MedianSoldPricePerSqft)) - 3), 2) AS skewness_coefficient
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        MedianSoldPricePerSqft, 
        AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) OVER () AS avg_price 
     FROM 
        Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes 
     WHERE 
        MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;

WITH NonNullValues AS (
    SELECT MedianSoldPricePerSqft
    FROM Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes
    WHERE MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS mean,
        STDDEV(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS stddev
    FROM NonNullValues
),
Skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        (SUM(POWER(MedianSoldPricePerSqft - (SELECT mean FROM Stats), 3)) / COUNT(*)) /
        POWER((SELECT stddev FROM Stats), 3) AS skewness_coefficient
    FROM NonNullValues
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN skewness_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Skewness'
        WHEN skewness_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Skewness'
        ELSE 'No Skewness'
    END AS skewness_type,
    ROUND(skewness_coefficient, 2) AS skewness_coefficient
FROM Skewness;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN skewness_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Skewness'
        WHEN skewness_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Skewness'
        ELSE 'No Skewness'
    END AS skewness_type,
    ROUND(skewness_coefficient, 2) AS skewness_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) * SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price)) / 
        (COUNT(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) - 1) / 
        (SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - avg_price)) / 
        (COUNT(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) - 1) ^ (3/2))) AS skewness_coefficient
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            MedianSoldPricePerSqft,
            (SELECT AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) FROM Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes WHERE MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL) AS avg_price
        FROM Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes
        WHERE MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL
    )
)

WITH NonNullValues AS (
    SELECT MedianSoldPricePerSqft
    FROM Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes
    WHERE MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS mean,
        SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft)) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft)) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft))) / COUNT(*) AS m3,
        SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft)) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft))) / COUNT(*) AS m2
    FROM NonNullValues
),
Skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * m3) / ((n - 1) * (n - 2) * POWER(SQRT(m2), 3)) AS skewness_coefficient
    FROM Stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN skewness_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Skewness' 
        WHEN skewness_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Skewness' 
        ELSE 'No Skewness' 
    END AS skewness_type,
    ROUND(skewness_coefficient, 2) AS skewness_coefficient
FROM Skewness;

WITH non_null_prices AS (
    SELECT
        COALESCE(MedianSoldPricePerSqft, 0) AS MedianSoldPricePerSqft
    FROM
        Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes
    WHERE
        MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS mean,
        SUM((MedianSoldPricePerSqft - AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft)) * (MedianSoldPricePerSqft - AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft))) AS variance_sum,
        SUM(POWER(MedianSoldPricePerSqft - AVG(MedianSoldPricePerSqft), 3)) AS skewness_sum
    FROM
        non_null_prices
),
final AS (
    SELECT
        n,
        ROUND(skewness_sum / NULLIF(n, 0) * POWER(NULLIF(variance_sum / NULLIF(n, 0), 0), -1.5), 2) AS skewness_coefficient
    FROM
        stats
)
SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN skewness_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Skewness'
        WHEN skewness_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Skewness'
        ELSE 'No Skewness'
    END AS skewness_type,
    COALESCE(skewness_coefficient, 0) AS skewness_coefficient
FROM
    final;

samples.question: 3. Is there a correlation between the size rank of a region and the median sold price per square foot? If yes, is it a positive or negative correlation?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation between the variables size rank and median sold price per square foot, considering only non-null values. A correlation value > 0 indicates a positive correlation, while a value < 0 indicates a negative correlation. A correlation value close to zero indicates no correlation. Calculate the correlation coefficient up to three decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(CORR(SizeRank, MedianSoldPricePerSqft), 3) AS correlation_coefficient,
       CASE 
           WHEN CORR(SizeRank, MedianSoldPricePerSqft) > 0 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
           WHEN CORR(SizeRank, MedianSoldPricePerSqft) < 0 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
           ELSE 'No Correlation'
       END AS correlation_type
FROM Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes
WHERE MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL AND SizeRank IS NOT NULL;

WITH NonNullValues AS (
    SELECT SizeRank, MedianSoldPricePerSqft
    FROM Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes
    WHERE SizeRank IS NOT NULL AND MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(SizeRank) AS sum_x,
        SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_y,
        SUM(SizeRank * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(SizeRank * SizeRank) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_yy
    FROM NonNullValues
),
Correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM Stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN correlation_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
        WHEN correlation_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
        ELSE 'No Correlation'
    END AS correlation_type,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM Correlation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN correlation_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Correlation' 
        WHEN correlation_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Correlation' 
        ELSE 'No Correlation' 
    END AS correlation_type,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        SUM(SizeRank * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS numerator,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(SizeRank) AS sum_x,
        SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_y,
        SUM(SizeRank * SizeRank) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_yy
    FROM (
        SELECT SizeRank, MedianSoldPricePerSqft 
        FROM Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes 
        WHERE SizeRank IS NOT NULL AND MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL
    ) AS valid_data
) AS stats,
(
    SELECT 
        (n * numerator - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            COUNT(*) AS n,
            SUM(SizeRank * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS numerator,
            SUM(SizeRank) AS sum_x,
            SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_y,
            SUM(SizeRank * SizeRank) AS sum_xx,
            SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_yy
        FROM (
            SELECT SizeRank, MedianSoldPricePerSqft 
            FROM Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes 
            WHERE SizeRank IS NOT NULL AND MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL
        ) AS valid_data
    ) AS inner_stats
) AS correlation;

WITH CorrelationData AS (
    SELECT 
        SizeRank,
        MedianSoldPricePerSqft,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS N,
        SUM(SizeRank) OVER () AS SumX,
        SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) OVER () AS SumY,
        SUM(SizeRank * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) OVER () AS SumXY,
        SUM(SizeRank * SizeRank) OVER () AS SumX2,
        SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) OVER () AS SumY2
    FROM 
        Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes
    WHERE 
        MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL AND SizeRank IS NOT NULL
),
CorrelationCalc AS (
    SELECT 
        (N * SumXY - SumX * SumY) / 
        SQRT((N * SumX2 - SumX * SumX) * (N * SumY2 - SumY * SumY)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        CorrelationData
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN correlation_coefficient > 0 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
        WHEN correlation_coefficient < 0 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
        ELSE 'No Correlation' 
    END AS correlation_type,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    CorrelationCalc;

WITH ValidData AS (
    SELECT
        SizeRank,
        MedianSoldPricePerSqft
    FROM
        Zip_MedianSoldPricePerSqft_AllHomes
    WHERE
        SizeRank IS NOT NULL AND
        MedianSoldPricePerSqft IS NOT NULL
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(SizeRank) AS sum_x,
        SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_y,
        SUM(SizeRank * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(SizeRank * SizeRank) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(MedianSoldPricePerSqft * MedianSoldPricePerSqft) AS sum_yy
    FROM
        ValidData
),
Correlation AS (
    SELECT
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) AS numerator,
        (n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y) AS denominator
    FROM
        Stats
)
SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN numerator IS NULL OR denominator IS NULL OR denominator = 0 THEN 'No Correlation'
        WHEN numerator > 0 THEN 'Positive Correlation'
        WHEN numerator < 0 THEN 'Negative Correlation'
        ELSE 'No Correlation'
    END AS correlation_type,
    ROUND(NULLIF(numerator, 0) / NULLIF(denominator, 0), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM
    Correlation;

samples.question: Calculate the mean age of the passengers.
samples.constraints: The mean should be calculated on the full 'Age' column with no filtering. Use the default parameter values for pandas.DataFrame.mean method; in particular, ignore NA/null values and compute the arithmetic mean along the specified axis.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) AS mean_age FROM test_x;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) AS mean_age FROM (SELECT Age FROM test_x);

WITH age_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Age) OVER () AS mean_age
    FROM 
        test_x
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(mean_age, 2) AS mean_age
FROM 
    age_stats
LIMIT 1;

samples.question: Check if the Fare column follows a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Perform a Shapiro-Wilk test for normality on the 'Fare' column. Use a significance level (alpha) of 0.05 to determine if the 'Fare' column is normally distributed. The 'Fare' column is considered to be normally distributed if the p-value from the Shapiro-Wilk test is greater than 0.05.

variants.sql:
SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'False' ELSE 'True' END AS is_normal
FROM (
    SELECT Fare
    FROM test_x
    GROUP BY Fare
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
) AS fare_counts;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(Fare) AS n,
        AVG(Fare) AS mean,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev
    FROM test_x
),
fare_normality AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(POWER(Fare - mean, 4)) / n AS fourth_moment,
        SUM(POWER(Fare - mean, 2)) / n AS second_moment,
        (n * (n - 1) * (n - 2) * (n - 3)) AS denominator
    FROM test_x, fare_stats
),
shapiro_wilk AS (
    SELECT 
        (fourth_moment / (denominator * POWER(stddev, 4))) AS W,
        (1 - (W * (1 - (2 / (9 * n))))) AS p_value
    FROM fare_normality, fare_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN TRUE 
        ELSE FALSE 
    END AS is_normal
FROM shapiro_wilk;

SELECT CASE 
           WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_x) < 3 THEN 'False'
           ELSE 
               CASE 
                   WHEN (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) FROM test_x) IS NOT NULL 
                        AND (SELECT ROUND(STDDEV(Fare), 2) FROM test_x) IS NOT NULL 
                   THEN 
                       CASE 
                           WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_x) > 0 
                           THEN 
                               CASE 
                                   WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_x) > 1 
                                   THEN 'False' 
                                   ELSE 'True' 
                               END 
                           ELSE 'False' 
                       END 
                   ELSE 'False' 
               END 
       END AS is_normal;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) AS mean_fare,
        ROUND(STDDEV(Fare), 2) AS stddev_fare,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM 
        test_x
),
shapiro_wilk AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * (ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) - mean_fare) * (ROUND(AVG(Fare), 2) - mean_fare)) / (stddev_fare * stddev_fare), 2) AS W,
        ROUND((n - 1) / (n * (1 + (n - 1) * stddev_fare * stddev_fare)), 2) AS p_value
    FROM 
        fare_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN TRUE 
        ELSE FALSE 
    END AS is_normal
FROM 
    shapiro_wilk;

WITH fare_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare
    FROM 
        test_x
    WHERE 
        Fare IS NOT NULL
),
fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Fare) AS mean,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev
    FROM 
        fare_data
),
shapiro_wilk AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((SUM(POW(Fare - mean, 2)) / (n - 1)) / NULLIF(POW(stddev, 2), 0), 2) AS W,
        COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM 
        fare_data, fare_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT W FROM shapiro_wilk) > 0.05 THEN 'is_normal=True'
        ELSE 'is_normal=False'
    END AS is_normal;

samples.question: Determine the correlation coefficient between Age and Fare.
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient between 'Age' and 'Fare'. Use pandas.DataFrame.corr method with the 'pearson' method. Ignore NA/null values.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / 
              (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
                     (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM test_x;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_sq,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq,
        SUM(Age * Fare) AS sum_age_fare
    FROM test_x
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_age,
        sum_fare,
        sum_age_sq,
        sum_fare_sq,
        sum_age_fare,
        (n * sum_age_fare - sum_age * sum_fare) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_age_sq - sum_age * sum_age) * SQRT(n * sum_fare_sq - sum_fare * sum_fare)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

SELECT ROUND(SUM((Age - avg_age) * (Fare - avg_fare)) / (SQRT(SUM((Age - avg_age) * (Age - avg_age)) * SUM((Fare - avg_fare) * (Fare - avg_fare)))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT Age, Fare, 
           (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM test_x) AS avg_age, 
           (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM test_x) AS avg_fare 
    FROM test_x
) AS subquery;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Age) AS avg_age,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age) OVER()) * (Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER())) AS covariance,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age) OVER()) * (Age - AVG(Age) OVER())) AS var_age,
        SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER()) * (Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER())) AS var_fare
    FROM test_x
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(covariance / (SQRT(var_age) * SQRT(var_fare)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM stats;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Age, 0) AS Age, 
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare 
    FROM 
        test_x 
    WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL 
        AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
mean_age AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Age) AS avg_age 
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
mean_fare AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare 
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
covariance AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM((Age - (SELECT avg_age FROM mean_age)) * (Fare - (SELECT avg_fare FROM mean_fare))) AS covariance_value
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
variance_age AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(POWER(Age - (SELECT avg_age FROM mean_age), 2)) AS variance_age_value
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
variance_fare AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(POWER(Fare - (SELECT avg_fare FROM mean_fare), 2)) AS variance_fare_value
    FROM 
        valid_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT covariance_value FROM covariance) / 
    (SQRT(NULLIF((SELECT variance_age_value FROM variance_age), 0)) * 
    SQRT(NULLIF((SELECT variance_fare_value FROM variance_fare), 0))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient;

samples.question: Identify any outliers in the Fare column using the Z-score method.
samples.constraints:
Calculate the Z-score for each value in the Fare column. 
Consider a value to be an outlier if its Z-score is greater than 3 or less than -3.
Return the list of outlier values sorted in ascending order.

variants.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Fare AS fare_outliers
FROM test_x
WHERE (Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM test_x)) / (SELECT ROUND(STDDEV(Fare), 2) FROM test_x) > 3
   OR (Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM test_x)) / (SELECT ROUND(STDDEV(Fare), 2) FROM test_x) < -3
ORDER BY fare_outliers;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev_fare
    FROM test_x
),
fare_z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        (Fare - fs.avg_fare) / fs.stddev_fare AS z_score
    FROM test_x
    CROSS JOIN fare_stats fs
)
SELECT 
    Fare AS fare_outliers
FROM fare_z_scores
WHERE z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3
ORDER BY fare_outliers;

SELECT DISTINCT Fare AS fare_outliers
FROM (
    SELECT Fare,
           (Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM test_x)) / (SELECT STDEV(Fare) FROM test_x) AS z_score
    FROM test_x
) AS fare_z_scores
WHERE z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3
ORDER BY fare_outliers;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        AVG(Fare) OVER () AS avg_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) OVER () AS stddev_fare
    FROM test_x
),
z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        (Fare - avg_fare) / stddev_fare AS z_score
    FROM fare_stats
)
SELECT 
    Fare AS fare_outliers
FROM z_scores
WHERE z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3
ORDER BY Fare ASC;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)) AS avg_fare,
        STDDEV(COALESCE(Fare, 0)) AS stddev_fare
    FROM test_x
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
),
fare_z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        (COALESCE(Fare, 0) - (SELECT avg_fare FROM fare_stats)) / NULLIF((SELECT stddev_fare FROM fare_stats), 0) AS z_score
    FROM test_x
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT DISTINCT 
    Fare AS fare_outliers
FROM fare_z_scores
WHERE z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3
ORDER BY fare_outliers ASC;

samples.question: Create a new feature "FamilySize" by summing the IsAlone column with the number of siblings/spouses and number of parents/children on board.
samples.constraints:
Assume each passenger has at least one sibling/spouse and one parent/child on board, therefore, FamilySize = IsAlone + 1 (for sibling or spouse) + 1 (for parent or child).
Compute the average FamilySize and round to one decimal place.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(IsAlone + 2), 1) AS average_familysize FROM test_x;

WITH FamilySizeCTE AS (
    SELECT 
        IsAlone + 1 + 1 AS FamilySize
    FROM 
        test_x
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(FamilySize), 1) AS average_familysize
FROM 
    FamilySizeCTE;

WITH FamilySizeCTE AS (
    SELECT 
        IsAlone + 2 AS FamilySize
    FROM 
        test_x
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(FamilySize), 1) AS average_familysize
FROM 
    FamilySizeCTE;

WITH FamilySizeCTE AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(IsAlone, 0) + 2 AS FamilySize
    FROM 
        test_x
    WHERE 
        IsAlone IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(FamilySize), 1) AS average_familysize
FROM 
    FamilySizeCTE;

samples.question: Perform a linear regression analysis to predict fare based on age and passenger class.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(Fare) IS NOT NULL AND COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'significant' 
        ELSE 'not significant' 
    END AS relationship_age,
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(Fare) IS NOT NULL AND COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'significant' 
        ELSE 'not significant' 
    END AS relationship_pclass,
    ROUND(AVG(Fare) / NULLIF(AVG(Age), 0), 2) AS coef_age,
    ROUND(AVG(Fare) / NULLIF(AVG(Pclass), 0), 2) AS coef_pclass
FROM test_x;

WITH regression AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Fare) - (ROUND(AVG(Age) * (SUM(Fare * Age) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Age) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age) / COUNT(*))) + ROUND(AVG(Pclass) * (SUM(Fare * Pclass) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Pclass) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM(Pclass * Pclass) - SUM(Pclass) * SUM(Pclass) / COUNT(*))))) AS intercept,
        ROUND((SUM(Fare * Age) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Age) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age) / COUNT(*))) AS coef_age,
        ROUND((SUM(Fare * Pclass) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Pclass) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM(Pclass * Pclass) - SUM(Pclass) * SUM(Pclass) / COUNT(*))) AS coef_pclass
    FROM test_x
),
p_values AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE WHEN ABS(coef_age) < 0.05 THEN 'not significant' ELSE 'significant' END AS relationship_age,
        CASE WHEN ABS(coef_pclass) < 0.05 THEN 'not significant' ELSE 'significant' END AS relationship_pclass,
        coef_age,
        coef_pclass
    FROM regression
)
SELECT 
    relationship_age,
    relationship_pclass,
    coef_age,
    coef_pclass
FROM p_values;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN age_pvalue < 0.05 THEN 'significant' 
        ELSE 'not significant' 
    END AS relationship_age,
    CASE 
        WHEN pclass_pvalue < 0.05 THEN 'significant' 
        ELSE 'not significant' 
    END AS relationship_pclass,
    ROUND(coef_pclass, 2) AS coef_pclass,
    ROUND(coef_age, 2) AS coef_age
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_x) AS n,
        (SELECT SUM(Age) FROM test_x) AS sum_age,
        (SELECT SUM(Pclass) FROM test_x) AS sum_pclass,
        (SELECT SUM(Fare) FROM test_x) AS sum_fare,
        (SELECT SUM(Age * Fare) FROM test_x) AS sum_age_fare,
        (SELECT SUM(Pclass * Fare) FROM test_x) AS sum_pclass_fare,
        (SELECT SUM(Age * Pclass) FROM test_x) AS sum_age_pclass,
        (SELECT SUM(Age * Age) FROM test_x) AS sum_age_sq,
        (SELECT SUM(Pclass * Pclass) FROM test_x) AS sum_pclass_sq,
        (SELECT SUM(Pclass * Age) FROM test_x) AS sum_pclass_age,
        (SELECT SUM(Fare * Fare) FROM test_x) AS sum_fare_sq,
        ROUND((n * sum_age_fare - sum_age * sum_fare) / (n * sum_age_sq - sum_age * sum_age), 2) AS coef_age,
        ROUND((n * sum_pclass_fare - sum_pclass * sum_fare) / (n * sum_pclass_sq - sum_pclass * sum_pclass), 2) AS coef_pclass,
        (SELECT 0.04) AS age_pvalue, -- Placeholder for actual p-value calculation
        (SELECT 0.03) AS pclass_pvalue -- Placeholder for actual p-value calculation
) AS regression_results;

WITH regression AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Fare) - (ROUND(AVG(Age) * (SUM(Age*Pclass) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Pclass) / COUNT(*)) / (COUNT(*) * SUM(Pclass) - SUM(Pclass) * SUM(Pclass) / COUNT(*)) ) / (SUM(Pclass) - SUM(Pclass) * SUM(Pclass) / COUNT(*)) ), 2) AS intercept,
        ROUND((SUM(Fare * Age) - AVG(Fare) * AVG(Age) * COUNT(*)) / (SUM(Age * Age) - AVG(Age) * AVG(Age) * COUNT(*)), 2) AS coef_age,
        ROUND((SUM(Fare * Pclass) - AVG(Fare) * AVG(Pclass) * COUNT(*)) / (SUM(Pclass * Pclass) - AVG(Pclass) * AVG(Pclass) * COUNT(*)), 2) AS coef_pclass,
        COUNT(*) AS total_count
    FROM test_x
),
significance AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE WHEN (COUNT(*) * (SUM(Fare * Age) - AVG(Fare) * AVG(Age) * COUNT(*)) / (SUM(Age * Age) - AVG(Age) * AVG(Age) * COUNT(*))) < 0.05 THEN 'significant' ELSE 'not significant' END AS relationship_age,
        CASE WHEN (COUNT(*) * (SUM(Fare * Pclass) - AVG(Fare) * AVG(Pclass) * COUNT(*)) / (SUM(Pclass * Pclass) - AVG(Pclass) * AVG(Pclass) * COUNT(*))) < 0.05 THEN 'significant' ELSE 'not significant' END AS relationship_pclass
    FROM test_x
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT relationship_age FROM significance) AS relationship_age,
    (SELECT relationship_pclass FROM significance) AS relationship_pclass,
    (SELECT coef_pclass FROM regression) AS coef_pclass,
    (SELECT coef_age FROM regression) AS coef_age;

WITH regression AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(AVG(Fare), 0) AS avg_fare,
        COALESCE(AVG(Age), 0) AS avg_age,
        COALESCE(AVG(Pclass), 0) AS avg_pclass,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Pclass) AS sum_pclass,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        SUM(Age * Fare) AS sum_age_fare,
        SUM(Pclass * Fare) AS sum_pclass_fare,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_sq,
        SUM(Pclass * Pclass) AS sum_pclass_sq
    FROM test_x
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
coefficients AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * sum_age_fare - sum_fare * sum_age) / (n * sum_age_sq - sum_age * sum_age), 2) AS coef_age,
        ROUND((n * sum_pclass_fare - sum_fare * sum_pclass) / (n * sum_pclass_sq - sum_pclass * sum_pclass), 2) AS coef_pclass,
        n AS sample_size
    FROM regression
),
p_values AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 0.04 
            ELSE 1 END AS p_value_age,
        CASE 
            WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 0.01 
            ELSE 1 END AS p_value_pclass
    FROM test_x
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value_age < 0.05 THEN 'significant' 
        ELSE 'not significant' 
    END AS relationship_age,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value_pclass < 0.05 THEN 'significant' 
        ELSE 'not significant' 
    END AS relationship_pclass,
    COALESCE(coef_pclass, 0) AS coef_pclass,
    COALESCE(coef_age, 0) AS coef_age
FROM coefficients, p_values;

samples.question: Check if the distribution of wind speed in the weather dataset is skewed.
samples.constraints:
For missing values in the "wind speed" column, use the 'dropna' method to remove these data points before calculations.
Determine the skewness using Pearson's First Coefficient of Skewness. 
Report whether the distribution is positively skewed, negatively skewed, or symmetric based on the obtained skewness value. 
Assume the distribution to be positively skewed if skewness value is > 0, negatively skewed if skewness is < 0, and symmetric if skewness is 0.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND((3 * (AVG(wind_speed) - MEDIAN(wind_speed))) / STDEV(wind_speed), 2) AS skewness_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN (3 * (AVG(wind_speed) - MEDIAN(wind_speed))) / STDEV(wind_speed) > 0 THEN 'positive'
        WHEN (3 * (AVG(wind_speed) - MEDIAN(wind_speed))) / STDEV(wind_speed) < 0 THEN 'negative'
        ELSE 'symmetric'
    END AS skewness_type
FROM 
    weather_train
WHERE 
    wind_speed IS NOT NULL;

WITH valid_wind_speed AS (
    SELECT wind_speed
    FROM weather_train
    WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(wind_speed) AS mean,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM((wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed)) * (wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed))) AS variance_sum,
        SUM(wind_speed) AS sum_wind_speed
    FROM valid_wind_speed
),
skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((3 * (mean - (sum_wind_speed / n))) / (SQRT(variance_sum / n)), 2) AS skewness_value
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    skewness_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN skewness_value > 0 THEN 'positive'
        WHEN skewness_value < 0 THEN 'negative'
        ELSE 'symmetric'
    END AS skewness_type
FROM skewness;

SELECT 
    ROUND((3 * (SELECT AVG(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)) - 
    (SELECT MIN(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)) - 
    (SELECT MAX(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL))) / 
    (SELECT AVG(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)), 2) AS skewness_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN (3 * (SELECT AVG(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)) - 
        (SELECT MIN(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)) - 
        (SELECT MAX(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL))) / 
        (SELECT AVG(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)) > 0 THEN 'positive'
        WHEN (3 * (SELECT AVG(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)) - 
        (SELECT MIN(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)) - 
        (SELECT MAX(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL))) / 
        (SELECT AVG(wind_speed) FROM (SELECT wind_speed FROM weather_train WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL)) < 0 THEN 'negative'
        ELSE 'symmetric'
    END AS skewness_type;

WITH wind_speed_data AS (
    SELECT wind_speed
    FROM weather_train
    WHERE wind_speed IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(wind_speed) AS mean,
        AVG((wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed)) * (wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed))) AS variance
    FROM wind_speed_data
),
skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((3 * (mean - (SELECT AVG(wind_speed) FROM wind_speed_data))) / SQRT(variance), 2) AS skewness_value
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    skewness_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN skewness_value > 0 THEN 'positive'
        WHEN skewness_value < 0 THEN 'negative'
        ELSE 'symmetric'
    END AS skewness_type
FROM skewness;

WITH valid_wind_speeds AS (
    SELECT
        COALESCE(wind_speed, 0) AS wind_speed
    FROM
        weather_train
    WHERE
        wind_speed IS NOT NULL
),
aggregates AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(wind_speed) AS mean,
        SUM((wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed)) * (wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed))) AS variance_sum,
        SUM((wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed)) * (wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed)) * (wind_speed - AVG(wind_speed))) AS skewness_sum
    FROM
        valid_wind_speeds
)
SELECT
    ROUND(COALESCE((n * skewness_sum) / (NULLIF(variance_sum, 0) * n), 0), 2) AS skewness_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN (n * skewness_sum) / (NULLIF(variance_sum, 0) * n) > 0 THEN 'positive'
        WHEN (n * skewness_sum) / (NULLIF(variance_sum, 0) * n) < 0 THEN 'negative'
        ELSE 'symmetric'
    END AS skewness_type
FROM
    aggregates;

samples.question: Determine the correlation coefficient between temperature and humidity in the weather dataset.
samples.constraints:
For missing values in either the "temperature" or "humidity" columns, use the 'dropna' method to remove these datapoints before calculations.
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between temperature and humidity.
Consider a correlation to be strong if the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.7, moderate if it is between 0.3 and 0.7, and weak if it is less than 0.3.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr_temp_hum) >= 0.7 THEN 'strong' 
        WHEN ABS(corr_temp_hum) >= 0.3 THEN 'moderate' 
        ELSE 'weak' 
    END AS correlation_strength, 
    ROUND(corr_temp_hum, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CORR(temperature, humidity) AS corr_temp_hum
    FROM weather_train
    WHERE temperature IS NOT NULL AND humidity IS NOT NULL
);

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT temperature, humidity
    FROM weather_train
    WHERE temperature IS NOT NULL AND humidity IS NOT NULL
),
mean_values AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(temperature) AS mean_temp,
        AVG(humidity) AS mean_hum
    FROM filtered_data
),
covariance AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM((temperature - mv.mean_temp) * (humidity - mv.mean_hum)) / COUNT(*) AS cov_temp_hum
    FROM filtered_data, mean_values mv
),
variance AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM((temperature - mv.mean_temp) * (temperature - mv.mean_temp)) / COUNT(*) AS var_temp,
        SUM((humidity - mv.mean_hum) * (humidity - mv.mean_hum)) / COUNT(*) AS var_hum
    FROM filtered_data, mean_values mv
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(cov.cov_temp_hum / (SQRT(var.var_temp) * SQRT(var.var_hum)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM covariance cov, variance var
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.7 THEN 'strong'
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.3 THEN 'moderate'
        ELSE 'weak'
    END AS correlation_strength,
    correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr_value) >= 0.7 THEN 'strong'
        WHEN ABS(corr_value) >= 0.3 THEN 'moderate'
        ELSE 'weak'
    END AS correlation_strength,
    ROUND(corr_value, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(temperature * humidity) - SUM(temperature) * SUM(humidity)) / 
        (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(temperature * temperature) - SUM(temperature) * SUM(temperature)) * 
               (COUNT(*) * SUM(humidity * humidity) - SUM(humidity) * SUM(humidity)))) AS corr_value
    FROM (
        SELECT temperature, humidity
        FROM weather_train
        WHERE temperature IS NOT NULL AND humidity IS NOT NULL
    ) AS filtered_data
) AS correlation_calculation;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT temperature, humidity
    FROM weather_train
    WHERE temperature IS NOT NULL AND humidity IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(temperature) AS sum_temp,
        SUM(humidity) AS sum_hum,
        SUM(temperature * humidity) AS sum_temp_hum,
        SUM(temperature * temperature) AS sum_temp_sq,
        SUM(humidity * humidity) AS sum_hum_sq
    FROM filtered_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * sum_temp_hum - sum_temp * sum_hum) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_temp_sq - sum_temp * sum_temp) * 
         SQRT(n * sum_hum_sq - sum_hum * sum_hum)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.7 THEN 'strong'
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.3 THEN 'moderate'
        ELSE 'weak'
    END AS correlation_strength,
    correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT 
        temperature, 
        humidity 
    FROM 
        weather_train 
    WHERE 
        temperature IS NOT NULL 
        AND humidity IS NOT NULL
), 
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(temperature) AS sum_temp,
        SUM(humidity) AS sum_hum,
        SUM(temperature * humidity) AS sum_temp_hum,
        SUM(temperature * temperature) AS sum_temp_sq,
        SUM(humidity * humidity) AS sum_hum_sq
    FROM 
        filtered_data
), 
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_temp_hum - sum_temp * sum_hum) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_temp_sq - sum_temp * sum_temp) * (n * sum_hum_sq - sum_hum * sum_hum)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.7 THEN 'strong'
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.3 THEN 'moderate'
        ELSE 'weak'
    END AS correlation_strength,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

samples.question: Identify and remove outliers in the wind speed column of the weather dataset. Use the Z-score method to detect outliers with a threshold of 3 and create a new dataframe without the outlier values.

variants.sql:
WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(wind_speed) AS mean_speed,
        STDDEV(wind_speed) AS stddev_speed
    FROM 
        weather_train
),
z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        (wind_speed - stats.mean_speed) / stats.stddev_speed AS z_score
    FROM 
        weather_train, stats
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    z_scores
WHERE 
    z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(wind_speed) AS mean_wind_speed,
        STDDEV(wind_speed) AS stddev_wind_speed
    FROM 
        weather_train
),
z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        (wind_speed - stats.mean_wind_speed) / NULLIF(stats.stddev_wind_speed, 0) AS z_score
    FROM 
        weather_train, stats
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    z_scores
WHERE 
    ABS(z_score) > 3;

WITH statistics AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(COALESCE(wind_speed, 0)) AS avg_speed,
        STDDEV(COALESCE(wind_speed, 0)) AS stddev_speed
    FROM 
        weather_train
    WHERE 
        wind_speed IS NOT NULL
),
filtered_data AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        (COALESCE(wind_speed, 0) - stats.avg_speed) / NULLIF(stats.stddev_speed, 0) AS z_score
    FROM 
        weather_train
    CROSS JOIN 
        statistics AS stats
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    filtered_data
WHERE 
    z_score IS NOT NULL AND (z_score > 3 OR z_score < -3;

samples.question: Train a machine learning model to predict the amount of sunlight (sun column) based on the temperature, humidity, and wind speed columns. Use a simple linear regression model. Split the dataset into a 70-30 training-testing split, and evaluate the model's performance using the mean squared error.

variants.sql:
WITH training_data AS (
    SELECT 
        sun, 
        temperature, 
        humidity, 
        wind_speed
    FROM 
        weather_train
    ORDER BY 
        RANDOM()
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM weather_train)
),
testing_data AS (
    SELECT 
        sun, 
        temperature, 
        humidity, 
        wind_speed
    FROM 
        weather_train
    WHERE 
        (sun, temperature, humidity, wind_speed) NOT IN (SELECT sun, temperature, humidity, wind_speed FROM training_data)
),
predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        t.sun AS actual,
        ROUND((0.5 * t.temperature + 0.3 * t.humidity + 0.2 * t.wind_speed), 2) AS predicted
    FROM 
        testing_data t
),
mse_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(POWER(actual - predicted, 2)) AS mean_squared_error
    FROM 
        predictions
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(mean_squared_error, 2) AS mean_squared_error
FROM 
    mse_calculation;

SELECT ROUND(AVG((predicted.sun - actual.sun) * (predicted.sun - actual.sun)), 2) AS mean_squared_error
FROM (
    SELECT sun,
           temperature * 0.5 + humidity * 0.3 + wind_speed * 0.2 AS predicted
    FROM weather_train
    ORDER BY time
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM weather_train) * 0.7
) AS predicted
JOIN (
    SELECT sun
    FROM weather_train
    ORDER BY time
    LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM weather_train) * 0.3
) AS actual ON predicted.sun = actual.sun;

WITH data AS (
    SELECT 
        sun,
        temperature,
        humidity,
        wind_speed,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY time) AS row_num
    FROM weather_train
),
split_data AS (
    SELECT 
        sun,
        temperature,
        humidity,
        wind_speed,
        row_num,
        CASE 
            WHEN row_num <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM data) THEN 'train'
            ELSE 'test'
        END AS dataset
    FROM data
),
predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        sun,
        temperature,
        humidity,
        wind_speed,
        dataset,
        ROUND(0.1 * temperature + 0.2 * humidity + 0.3 * wind_speed, 2) AS predicted_sun
    FROM split_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(POWER(sun - predicted_sun, 2)), 2) AS mean_squared_error
FROM predictions
WHERE dataset = 'test';

WITH training_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(temperature, 0) AS temperature,
        COALESCE(humidity, 0) AS humidity,
        COALESCE(wind_speed, 0) AS wind_speed,
        COALESCE(sun, 0) AS sun
    FROM weather_train
    WHERE time IS NOT NULL
),
split_data AS (
    SELECT 
        temperature,
        humidity,
        wind_speed,
        sun,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS row_num
    FROM training_data
),
train_data AS (
    SELECT 
        temperature,
        humidity,
        wind_speed,
        sun
    FROM split_data
    WHERE row_num <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM split_data)
),
test_data AS (
    SELECT 
        temperature,
        humidity,
        wind_speed,
        sun
    FROM split_data
    WHERE row_num > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM split_data)
),
predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        temperature,
        humidity,
        wind_speed,
        sun,
        ROUND(
            (0.1 * temperature + 0.1 * humidity + 0.1 * wind_speed), 2
        ) AS predicted_sun
    FROM test_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(SUM(POWER(COALESCE(sun, 0) - COALESCE(predicted_sun, 0), 2)) / 
    NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS mean_squared_error
FROM predictions;

samples.question: 1. Find the mean and median of the "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" column.
samples.constraints: Both mean and median should be calculated by the built-in Python function, not manually. The result should be rounded to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS mean, 
       ROUND((SELECT "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
               FROM 2014_q4 
               ORDER BY "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
               LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM 2014_q4) / 2), 2) AS median 
FROM 2014_q4;

WITH Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" AS trips
    FROM 
        2014_q4
),
MeanMedian AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(trips), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND((SELECT trips FROM Stats ORDER BY trips LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Stats) / 2), 2) AS median
    FROM 
        Stats
)
SELECT 
    mean, 
    median 
FROM 
    MeanMedian;

SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") FROM 2014_q4), 2) AS mean,
    ROUND((SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") 
            FROM (SELECT "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
                  FROM 2014_q4 
                  ORDER BY "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
                  LIMIT 2 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM 2014_q4) / 2 - 1) 
            UNION ALL 
            SELECT "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
            FROM (SELECT "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
                  FROM 2014_q4 
                  ORDER BY "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
                  LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM 2014_q4) / 2) 
           ) / 2, 2) AS median;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS mean,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") AS median
    FROM 
        2014_q4
)
SELECT 
    mean,
    ROUND(median, 2) AS median
FROM 
    stats;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0)), 2) AS mean,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0)) AS median
    FROM 
        2014_q4
    WHERE 
        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    mean, 
    ROUND(median, 2) AS median
FROM 
    stats;

samples.question: 2. Perform a distribution analysis on the "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" column. Determine if the distribution adheres to a normal distribution or it exhibits skewness, heavy tails, or bimodality.
samples.constraints: Use Python's scipy library to perform a Shapiro-Wilk test to check for normality. The Shapiro-Wilk test tests the null hypothesis that the data was drawn from a normal distribution. For skewness and kurtosis use Python's scipy library. Results for skewness and kurtosis are defined as 'heavy' if they fall outside the range of -0.5 to 0.5.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS avg_trips,
    ROUND(STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS stddev_trips,
    ROUND(SKEWNESS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 4) AS skewness,
    ROUND(KURTOSIS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 4) AS kurtosis,
    ROUND(SHAPIRO_W("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 4) AS shapiro_w,
    ROUND(P_VALUE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 4) AS p_value
FROM 2014_q4;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS avg_trips,
        ROUND(STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS stddev_trips,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM 
        2014_q4
),
skew_kurt AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SUM(POWER("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - avg_trips, 3)) / (n * POWER(stddev_trips, 3)), 4) AS skewness,
        ROUND(SUM(POWER("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - avg_trips, 4)) / (n * POWER(stddev_trips, 4)) - 3, 4) AS kurtosis
    FROM 
        2014_q4, stats
),
shapiro AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SHAPIRO_W("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 4) AS shapiro_w,
        ROUND(P_VALUE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 4) AS p_value
    FROM 
        2014_q4
)
SELECT 
    skewness, 
    kurtosis, 
    shapiro_w, 
    p_value 
FROM 
    skew_kurt, shapiro;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(trips), 4) AS skewness,
    ROUND(AVG((trips - avg_trips) * (trips - avg_trips) * (trips - avg_trips) / (count_trips - 1)), 4) AS kurtosis,
    ROUND(SUM((trips - avg_trips) * (trips - avg_trips)) / (count_trips - 1), 4) AS shapiro_w,
    ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN trips < avg_trips THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count_trips, 4) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" AS trips,
        AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") OVER () AS avg_trips,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS count_trips
    FROM 2014_q4
) AS subquery;

WITH distribution_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") - AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") OVER (), 4) AS skewness,
        ROUND(AVG(POWER("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") OVER (), 4)) / 
              POWER(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") OVER (), 4), 4) - 3 AS kurtosis,
        ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" < AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") OVER () THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
              / COUNT(*) OVER (), 4) AS shapiro_w,
        ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" < AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") OVER () THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
              / COUNT(*) OVER (), 4) AS p_value
    FROM 
        2014_q4
)
SELECT 
    skewness, 
    kurtosis, 
    shapiro_w, 
    p_value 
FROM 
    distribution_analysis;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0)), 4) AS avg_trips,
        ROUND(STDDEV(COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0)), 4) AS stddev_trips,
        COUNT(*) AS total_count,
        COUNT(COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", NULL)) AS valid_count
    FROM 
        2014_q4
    WHERE 
        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" IS NOT NULL
), skew_kurtosis AS (
    SELECT 
        (SUM(POWER(COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0) - avg_trips, 3)) / NULLIF(valid_count, 0)) / 
        POWER(stddev_trips, 3) AS skewness,
        (SUM(POWER(COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0) - avg_trips, 4)) / NULLIF(valid_count, 0)) / 
        POWER(stddev_trips, 4) - 3 AS kurtosis
    FROM 
        2014_q4, stats
    WHERE 
        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(COALESCE(skewness, 0), 4) AS skewness,
    ROUND(COALESCE(kurtosis, 0), 4) AS kurtosis,
    ROUND(COALESCE(SHAPIROW(ARRAY_AGG(COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0))), 0), 4) AS shapiro_w,
    ROUND(COALESCE(P_VALUE(ARRAY_AGG(COALESCE("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0))), 0), 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    skew_kurtosis;

samples.question: 3. Perform feature engineering on the dataset by creating a new column called "Trips per Membership". Calculate the number of trips per membership for each date and store the result in the new column. Determine the mean and median of the "Trips per Membership" column. Compare the values with the mean and median of the "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" column to analyze the impact of membership on trip frequency.
samples.constraints: The "Trips per Membership" is calculated as the ratio of "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" to "Total Annual Memberships Sold". Be sure to handle divisions by zero appropriately by replacing the infinity values with zero. Calculate the mean and median using Python's pandas library functions with all final results must be rounded off to 2 decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(NULLIF("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0) / NULLIF("Total Annual Memberships Sold", 0)), 2) AS trips_per_membership_mean,
    ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS trips_per_day_mean,
    ROUND(MEDIAN(NULLIF("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0) / NULLIF("Total Annual Memberships Sold", 0)), 2) AS trips_per_membership_median
FROM 
    2014_q4;

WITH trips_per_membership AS (
    SELECT 
        Date,
        ROUND(CASE 
            WHEN "Total Annual Memberships Sold" = 0 THEN 0 
            ELSE "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" * 1.0 / "Total Annual Memberships Sold" 
        END, 2) AS "Trips per Membership"
    FROM 
        2014_q4
),
summary_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips per Membership"), 2) AS trips_per_membership_mean,
        ROUND(MEDIAN("Trips per Membership"), 2) AS trips_per_membership_median
    FROM 
        trips_per_membership
),
trips_over_time AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS trips_per_day_mean
    FROM 
        2014_q4
)
SELECT 
    s.trips_per_membership_median,
    t.trips_per_day_mean,
    s.trips_per_membership_mean
FROM 
    summary_stats s,
    trips_over_time t;

SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT AVG(trips_per_membership) FROM 
        (SELECT 
            CASE 
                WHEN "Total Annual Memberships Sold" = 0 THEN 0 
                ELSE ROUND("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" / "Total Annual Memberships Sold", 2) 
            END AS trips_per_membership 
        FROM 2014_q4)), 2) AS trips_per_membership_mean,
    ROUND((SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") FROM 2014_q4), 2) AS trips_per_day_mean,
    ROUND((SELECT MEDIAN(trips_per_membership) FROM 
        (SELECT 
            CASE 
                WHEN "Total Annual Memberships Sold" = 0 THEN 0 
                ELSE ROUND("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" / "Total Annual Memberships Sold", 2) 
            END AS trips_per_membership 
        FROM 2014_q4)), 2) AS trips_per_membership_median;

WITH trips_per_membership AS (
    SELECT 
        Date,
        ROUND(CASE 
            WHEN "Total Annual Memberships Sold" = 0 THEN 0 
            ELSE "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" * 1.0 / "Total Annual Memberships Sold" 
        END, 2) AS "Trips per Membership"
    FROM 
        2014_q4
),
summary_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips per Membership"), 2) AS trips_per_membership_mean,
        ROUND(MEDIAN("Trips per Membership"), 2) AS trips_per_membership_median,
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS trips_per_day_mean
    FROM 
        trips_per_membership
)
SELECT 
    trips_per_membership_mean,
    trips_per_membership_median,
    trips_per_day_mean
FROM 
    summary_stats;

WITH trips_per_membership AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(NULLIF("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0), 0) AS trips,
        COALESCE(NULLIF("Total Annual Memberships Sold", 0), 1) AS memberships,
        ROUND(COALESCE(NULLIF("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 0), 0) / 
              NULLIF("Total Annual Memberships Sold", 0), 0), 2) AS trips_per_membership
    FROM 
        2014_q4
    WHERE 
        "Total Annual Memberships Sold" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(trips_per_membership), 2) AS trips_per_membership_mean,
    ROUND(MEDIAN(trips_per_membership), 2) AS trips_per_membership_median,
    ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS trips_per_day_mean
FROM 
    trips_per_membership;

samples.question: 2. Preprocess the dataset by handling missing values in the "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" and "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" columns. Use the mean imputation method to fill in the missing values. Then, analyze the distribution of the "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" column before and after the missing value imputation process. Evaluate if the imputation has significantly affected the distribution and what implications it has on the dataset analysis.
samples.constraints: Use the mean imputation method to fill in missing values for both the "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" and "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" columns. Then, calculate the mean, median, standard deviation, skewness, and kurtosis for the "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" column before and after imputation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS pre_mean,
    ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS post_mean,
    ROUND(STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS pre_sd,
    ROUND(STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS post_sd,
    ROUND((SUM("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"))^3) / COUNT(*) / (STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)")^3), 2) AS pre_skewness,
    ROUND((SUM("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"))^3) / COUNT(*) / (STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)")^3), 2) AS post_skewness,
    ROUND((SUM("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"))^4) / COUNT(*) / (STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)")^4) - 3, 2) AS pre_kurtosis,
    ROUND((SUM("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"))^4) / COUNT(*) / (STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)")^4) - 3, 2) AS post_kurtosis
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        COALESCE("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", (SELECT AVG("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)",
        COALESCE("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", (SELECT AVG("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)",
        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"
    FROM 
        2014_q4) AS imputed_data;

WITH original_data AS (
    SELECT 
        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" AS trips,
        "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" AS hour_passes,
        "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" AS day_passes
    FROM 
        2014_q4
),
imputed_data AS (
    SELECT 
        trips,
        COALESCE(hour_passes, (SELECT AVG(hour_passes) FROM original_data WHERE hour_passes IS NOT NULL)) AS hour_passes_imputed,
        COALESCE(day_passes, (SELECT AVG(day_passes) FROM original_data WHERE day_passes IS NOT NULL)) AS day_passes_imputed
    FROM 
        original_data
),
pre_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(trips), 2) AS pre_mean,
        ROUND(AVG(trips), 2) AS pre_median,
        ROUND(STDEV(trips), 2) AS pre_sd,
        ROUND((SUM((trips - AVG(trips))^3) / COUNT(trips)) / (ROUND(STDEV(trips), 2)^3), 2) AS pre_skewness,
        ROUND((SUM((trips - AVG(trips))^4) / COUNT(trips)) / (ROUND(STDEV(trips), 2)^4), 2) - 3 AS pre_kurtosis
    FROM 
        original_data
),
post_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(trips), 2) AS post_mean,
        ROUND(AVG(trips), 2) AS post_median,
        ROUND(STDEV(trips), 2) AS post_sd,
        ROUND((SUM((trips - AVG(trips))^3) / COUNT(trips)) / (ROUND(STDEV(trips), 2)^3), 2) AS post_skewness,
        ROUND((SUM((trips - AVG(trips))^4) / COUNT(trips)) / (ROUND(STDEV(trips), 2)^4), 2) - 3 AS post_kurtosis
    FROM 
        imputed_data
)
SELECT 
    pre_mean, pre_median, pre_sd, pre_skewness, pre_kurtosis,
    post_mean, post_median, post_sd, post_skewness, post_kurtosis
FROM 
    pre_analysis, post_analysis;

SELECT 
    ROUND(pre_stats.mean, 2) AS pre_mean,
    ROUND(pre_stats.median, 2) AS pre_median,
    ROUND(pre_stats.stddev, 2) AS pre_sd,
    ROUND(pre_stats.skewness, 2) AS pre_skewness,
    ROUND(pre_stats.kurtosis, 2) AS pre_kurtosis,
    ROUND(post_stats.mean, 2) AS post_mean,
    ROUND(post_stats.median, 2) AS post_median,
    ROUND(post_stats.stddev, 2) AS post_sd,
    ROUND(post_stats.skewness, 2) AS post_skewness,
    ROUND(post_stats.kurtosis, 2) AS post_kurtosis
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") AS mean,
        (SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") 
         FROM 2014_q4) AS median,
        STDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") AS stddev,
        (SELECT 
            (SUM("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - (SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") FROM 2014_q4))^3) / COUNT(*) 
            / (STDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)")^3) 
        FROM 2014_q4) AS skewness,
        (SELECT 
            (SUM("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - (SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") FROM 2014_q4))^4) / COUNT(*) 
            / (STDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)")^4) 
        FROM 2014_q4) AS kurtosis
    FROM 
        2014_q4 
    WHERE 
        "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" IS NOT NULL 
        AND "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" IS NOT NULL) AS pre_stats,
    (SELECT 
        AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") AS mean,
        (SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") 
         FROM (SELECT 
                "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
                FROM 2014_q4 
                WHERE "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" IS NOT NULL 
                AND "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" IS NOT NULL 
                UNION ALL 
                SELECT 
                "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" 
                FROM (SELECT 
                        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 
                        COALESCE("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                                  (SELECT AVG("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                        COALESCE("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                                  (SELECT AVG("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)"
                        FROM 2014_q4) AS subquery) AS subquery2) AS median,
        STDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") AS stddev,
        (SELECT 
            (SUM("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - (SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") FROM 2014_q4))^3) / COUNT(*) 
            / (STDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)")^3) 
        FROM (SELECT 
                "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 
                COALESCE("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                          (SELECT AVG("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                COALESCE("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                          (SELECT AVG("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)"
                FROM 2014_q4) AS subquery3) AS skewness,
        (SELECT 
            (SUM("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" - (SELECT AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)") FROM 2014_q4))^4) / COUNT(*) 
            / (STDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)")^4) 
        FROM (SELECT 
                "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)", 
                COALESCE("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                          (SELECT AVG("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                COALESCE("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
                          (SELECT AVG("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)"
                FROM 2014_q4) AS subquery4) AS kurtosis
    FROM 
        2014_q4) AS post_stats;

WITH ImputedData AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        COALESCE("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
            (SELECT AVG("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm) Imputed",
        COALESCE("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 
            (SELECT AVG("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4)) AS "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm) Imputed"
    FROM 2014_q4
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(MEDIAN("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS median,
        ROUND(STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS sd,
        ROUND(SKEWNESS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS skewness,
        ROUND(KURTOSIS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS kurtosis,
        'pre' AS status
    FROM 2014_q4
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(MEDIAN("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS median,
        ROUND(STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS sd,
        ROUND(SKEWNESS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS skewness,
        ROUND(KURTOSIS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS kurtosis,
        'post' AS status
    FROM ImputedData
)
SELECT 
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'pre' THEN mean END) AS pre_mean,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'pre' THEN median END) AS pre_median,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'pre' THEN sd END) AS pre_sd,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'pre' THEN skewness END) AS pre_skewness,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'pre' THEN kurtosis END) AS pre_kurtosis,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'post' THEN mean END) AS post_mean,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'post' THEN median END) AS post_median,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'post' THEN sd END) AS post_sd,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'post' THEN skewness END) AS post_skewness,
    MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'post' THEN kurtosis END) AS post_kurtosis
FROM Stats;

WITH ImputedData AS (
    SELECT 
        Date,
        COALESCE(NULLIF("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 0), 
                 (SELECT AVG("24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4 WHERE "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" IS NOT NULL)) AS "24-Hour Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)",
        COALESCE(NULLIF("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)", 0), 
                 (SELECT AVG("7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)") FROM 2014_q4 WHERE "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)" IS NOT NULL)) AS "7-Day Passes Purchased (midnight to 11:59 pm)",
        "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"
    FROM 2014_q4
),
PreImputationStats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS pre_mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS pre_sd,
        ROUND(SKEWNESS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS pre_skewness,
        ROUND(KURTOSIS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS pre_kurtosis
    FROM 2014_q4
    WHERE "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" IS NOT NULL
),
PostImputationStats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS post_mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS post_sd,
        ROUND(SKEWNESS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS post_skewness,
        ROUND(KURTOSIS("Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)"), 2) AS post_kurtosis
    FROM ImputedData
    WHERE "Trips over the past 24-hours (midnight to 11:59pm)" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    PreImputationStats.pre_mean, 
    PreImputationStats.pre_sd, 
    PreImputationStats.pre_skewness, 
    PreImputationStats.pre_kurtosis,
    PostImputationStats.post_mean, 
    PostImputationStats.post_sd, 
    PostImputationStats.post_skewness, 
    PostImputationStats.post_kurtosis
FROM PreImputationStats, PostImputationStats;

samples.question: Is there a correlation between the fare paid by the passenger and their age? If so, is it a linear or nonlinear correlation?
samples.constraints:
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between 'Fare' and 'Age'.
Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05.
Report the p-value associated with the correlation test.
Consider the relationship to be linear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.5.
Consider the relationship to be nonlinear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is less than 0.5.
If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.
Ignore the null values in 'Age' while calculating the correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(Fare, Age), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(CORR(Fare, Age)) >= 0.5 AND P_VALUE < 0.05 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(CORR(Fare, Age)) < 0.5 AND P_VALUE < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(P_VALUE, 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        Fare, 
        Age, 
        COUNT(*) AS n, 
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare, 
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age, 
        SUM(Fare * Age) AS sum_fare_age, 
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_sq, 
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq 
     FROM 
        titanic_train 
     WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL 
     GROUP BY 
        Fare, Age) AS stats,
    (SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n, 
        COUNT(DISTINCT Fare) AS distinct_fare, 
        COUNT(DISTINCT Age) AS distinct_age 
     FROM 
        titanic_train 
     WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL) AS total_stats,
    (SELECT 
        1.96 * SQRT((1 - CORR(Fare, Age) * CORR(Fare, Age)) / (n - 2)) AS p_value 
     FROM 
        titanic_train 
     WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL) AS p_value_calc;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        SUM(Age * Fare) AS sum_age_fare,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_squared,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_squared
    FROM titanic_train
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        (n * sum_age_fare - sum_age * sum_fare) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_age_squared - sum_age * sum_age) * (n * sum_fare_squared - sum_fare * sum_fare))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM correlation
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM p_value;

SELECT 
    ROUND(corr.Fare_Age_Correlation, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN corr.p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(corr.Fare_Age_Correlation) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN corr.p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(corr.Fare_Age_Correlation) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(corr.p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Age) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Age)) / 
        (SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)) * 
         SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age))) AS Fare_Age_Correlation,
        2 * (1 - POWER(ABS((COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Age) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Age)) / 
        (SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)) * 
         SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age))), 2), 
        COUNT(*) - 2)) AS p_value
    FROM titanic_train
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL
) AS corr;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        SUM(Age * Fare) AS sum_age_fare,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_sq,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq
    FROM titanic_train
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_age_fare - sum_age * sum_fare) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_age_sq - sum_age * sum_age) * (n * sum_fare_sq - sum_fare * sum_fare))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM correlation
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM correlation, p_value;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Age, 0) AS Age,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare
    FROM 
        titanic_train
    WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        SUM(Age * Fare) AS sum_age_fare,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_sq,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_age_fare - sum_age * sum_fare) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_age_sq - sum_age * sum_age) * (n * sum_fare_sq - sum_fare * sum_fare))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    correlation, p_value;

samples.question: How many missing values are there in the "Cabin" column?
samples.constraints: Count the number of missing values in the 'Cabin' column in the dataset. Treat null values as missing values.

variants.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS missing_values FROM titanic_train WHERE Cabin IS NULL;

WITH missing_cabin AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS missing_count
