samples.question: 1. What is the average damage in USD caused by storms in each year from 2000 to 2010? Are there any significant differences in the average damage between years?
samples.constraints:
{
Calculate the mean damage in USD for each year.
Perform a one-way Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) to test whether there are significant differences in the average damage between years.
The significance level (alpha) for the ANOVA test should be 0.05.
Report the p-value associated with the ANOVA test.
If the p-value is less than 0.05, infer that there are significant differences.
If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, infer that there are no significant differences.
}

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(damage_USD), 2) AS average_damage, year
FROM cost_data_with_errors
WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010
GROUP BY year;

WITH yearly_damage AS (
    SELECT year, ROUND(AVG(damage_USD), 2) AS avg_damage
    FROM cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010
    GROUP BY year
),
anova_results AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(avg_damage) AS overall_mean,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(POWER(avg_damage - (SELECT AVG(avg_damage) FROM yearly_damage), 2)) AS ss_between,
        SUM(POWER(avg_damage - (SELECT AVG(avg_damage) FROM yearly_damage), 2)) AS ss_within
    FROM yearly_damage
),
anova_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        ss_between / (COUNT(*) - 1) AS ms_between,
        ss_within / (SUM(n) - COUNT(*)) AS ms_within
    FROM anova_results
),
final_result AS (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT ms_between FROM anova_calculation) / (SELECT ms_within FROM anova_calculation) AS f_statistic,
        1 - (SELECT f_statistic FROM anova_calculation) AS p_value
)
SELECT 
    p_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'significant'
        ELSE 'none'
    END AS difference_type
FROM final_result;

SELECT 
    p_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'significant'
        ELSE 'none'
    END AS difference_type
FROM (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(damage_USD), 2) AS avg_damage,
        year
    FROM 
        cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE 
        year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010
    GROUP BY 
        year
) AS yearly_damage,
(
    SELECT 
        (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT year) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010) AS k,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010) AS n,
        (SELECT SUM((damage_USD - (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010)) * (damage_USD - (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010))) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010) AS ss_total,
        (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT year) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010) AS k
    ) AS stats,
    (
        SELECT 
            SUM((avg_damage - (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010)) * (avg_damage - (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010))) / (k - 1) AS ss_between,
            SUM(ss_total) / (n - k) AS ms_within,
            ss_between / (k - 1) / (SUM(ss_total) / (n - k)) AS f_statistic,
            1 - (F_DIST(f_statistic, k - 1, n - k)) AS p_value
        FROM 
            (SELECT 
                ROUND(AVG(damage_USD), 2) AS avg_damage,
                year
            FROM 
                cost_data_with_errors
            WHERE 
                year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010
            GROUP BY 
                year) AS yearly_damage
    ) AS anova
) AS final_result;

WITH yearly_damage AS (
    SELECT year, ROUND(AVG(damage_USD), 2) AS avg_damage
    FROM cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010
    GROUP BY year
),
anova_results AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(avg_damage) AS overall_mean,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(POWER(avg_damage - (SELECT AVG(avg_damage) FROM yearly_damage), 2)) AS ss_between,
        SUM(POWER(avg_damage - (SELECT AVG(avg_damage) FROM yearly_damage), 2)) AS ss_within
    FROM yearly_damage
)
SELECT 
    (ss_between / (COUNT(*) - 1)) / (ss_within / (SUM(n) - COUNT(*))) AS F_statistic,
    1 - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY F_statistic) AS p_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'significant'
        ELSE 'none'
    END AS difference_type
FROM anova_results;

WITH yearly_damage AS (
    SELECT 
        year,
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(damage_USD, 0)), 2) AS avg_damage
    FROM 
        cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE 
        year BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010 
        AND damage_USD IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        year
),
anova_results AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(avg_damage) AS total_damage,
        SUM(avg_damage * avg_damage) AS total_damage_squared
    FROM 
        yearly_damage
),
anova_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        total_damage,
        total_damage_squared,
        (total_damage_squared / n) - (CAST(total_damage AS FLOAT) / n * CAST(total_damage AS FLOAT) / n) AS variance_between,
        (total_damage_squared - (total_damage_squared / n) * n) / (COUNT(year) - 1) AS variance_within
    FROM 
        anova_results
)

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (variance_between / variance_within) > 0 THEN 
            ROUND((variance_between / variance_within), 4) 
        ELSE 
            NULL 
    END AS p_value,
    CASE 
        WHEN (variance_between / variance_within) < 0.05 THEN 
            'none' 
        ELSE 
            'none' 
    END AS difference_type
FROM 
    anova_calculation;

samples.question: 2. Is there a correlation between the maximum storm category achieved by a storm and the recorded damage in USD? If so, what is the strength and direction of the correlation?
samples.constraints:
{
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between maximum storm category and damage in USD.
Use a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05 to assess the significance of the correlation. 
Report the p-value associated with the correlation test.
If the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.5, infer the relationship to be linear.
If the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is less than 0.5, infer the relationship to be nonlinear.
If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.
}

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(max_storm_cat, damage_USD), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(ROUND(CORR(max_storm_cat, damage_USD), 2)) >= 0.5 AND COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(ROUND(CORR(max_storm_cat, damage_USD), 2)) < 0.5 AND COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(PVALUE(CORR(max_storm_cat, damage_USD)), 2) AS p_value
FROM cost_data_with_errors;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        max_storm_cat,
        damage_USD
    FROM 
        cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE 
        damage_USD IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(max_storm_cat) AS avg_storm_cat,
        AVG(damage_USD) AS avg_damage,
        SUM(max_storm_cat * damage_USD) AS sum_product,
        SUM(max_storm_cat * max_storm_cat) AS sum_storm_cat_sq,
        SUM(damage_USD * damage_USD) AS sum_damage_sq
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_product - COUNT(max_storm_cat) * avg_storm_cat * avg_damage) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_storm_cat_sq - COUNT(max_storm_cat) * avg_storm_cat * avg_storm_cat) * 
         SQRT(n * sum_damage_sq - COUNT(damage_USD) * avg_damage * avg_damage)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 0.0 
            ELSE 1.0 
        END AS significance
    FROM 
        correlation
),
final_result AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        CASE 
            WHEN significance = 0.0 THEN 'linear'
            ELSE 'nonlinear'
        END AS relationship_type,
        CASE 
            WHEN significance = 0.0 THEN 0.0
            ELSE 1.0
        END AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation, p_value
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    relationship_type,
    p_value
FROM 
    final_result;

SELECT 
    ROUND(corr(max_storm_cat, damage_USD), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(corr(max_storm_cat, damage_USD)) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(corr(max_storm_cat, damage_USD)) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        max_storm_cat, 
        damage_USD, 
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors) AS n,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE max_storm_cat IS NOT NULL AND damage_USD IS NOT NULL) AS valid_count,
        (SELECT 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(max_storm_cat * damage_USD) - SUM(max_storm_cat) * SUM(damage_USD)) / 
            (SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(max_storm_cat * max_storm_cat) - SUM(max_storm_cat) * SUM(max_storm_cat)) * 
            SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(damage_USD * damage_USD) - SUM(damage_USD) * SUM(damage_USD))) 
         FROM cost_data_with_errors 
         WHERE max_storm_cat IS NOT NULL AND damage_USD IS NOT NULL) AS r,
        (SELECT 
            2 * (1 - TDIST(ABS(r * SQRT(valid_count - 2) / SQRT(1 - r * r)), valid_count - 2)) 
         FROM cost_data_with_errors 
         WHERE max_storm_cat IS NOT NULL AND damage_USD IS NOT NULL) AS p_value
    FROM cost_data_with_errors 
    WHERE max_storm_cat IS NOT NULL AND damage_USD IS NOT NULL
) AS correlation_data;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        max_storm_cat,
        damage_USD,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_count
    FROM 
        cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE 
        damage_USD IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(max_storm_cat) AS sum_x,
        SUM(damage_USD) AS sum_y,
        SUM(max_storm_cat * max_storm_cat) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(damage_USD * damage_USD) AS sum_yy,
        SUM(max_storm_cat * damage_USD) AS sum_xy
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_x,
        sum_y,
        sum_xx,
        sum_yy,
        sum_xy,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS r,
        (1 - ( (n - 2) * (1 - ((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))^2)) )) AS p_value
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(r, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 2) AS p_value
FROM 
    correlation;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(max_storm_cat, 0) AS storm_category,
        COALESCE(damage_USD, 0) AS damage
    FROM 
        cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE 
        max_storm_cat IS NOT NULL 
        AND damage_USD IS NOT NULL
), 
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(storm_category) AS sum_x,
        SUM(damage) AS sum_y,
        SUM(storm_category * damage) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(storm_category * storm_category) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(damage * damage) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        correlation_data
), 
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient,
        n
    FROM 
        stats
), 
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 2) AS p_value
FROM 
    correlation, 
    p_value;

samples.question: 1. Is there a relationship between the maximum storm category achieved by a storm and the duration of its activity? How does this relationship differ between storms causing high and low damage?
samples.constraints:
{
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between maximum storm category and the duration of activity for storms that caused damage above and below the median damage level.
Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05.
Report the p-value associated with the correlation test.
Consider the relationship to be linear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.4.
Consider the relationship to be nonlinear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is less than 0.4.
If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.
}

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(max_storm_cat) >= 0.4 AND COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 'linear' 
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS high_damage_relationship_type,
    ROUND(CORR(max_storm_cat, LENGTH(dates_active) - LENGTH(REPLACE(dates_active, '–', '')) + 1), 2) AS high_damage_correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(PVAL(CORR(max_storm_cat, LENGTH(dates_active) - LENGTH(REPLACE(dates_active, '–', '')) + 1)), 4) AS high_damage_p_value
FROM cost_data_with_errors
WHERE damage_USD > (SELECT MEDIAN(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors);

WITH damage_levels AS (
    SELECT 
        damage_USD,
        CASE 
            WHEN damage_USD >= (SELECT MEDIAN(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors) THEN 'high'
            ELSE 'low'
        END AS damage_category,
        max_storm_cat,
        LENGTH(dates_active) AS duration
    FROM cost_data_with_errors
),
correlation_results AS (
    SELECT 
        damage_category,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(max_storm_cat) AS sum_x,
        SUM(duration) AS sum_y,
        SUM(max_storm_cat * duration) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(max_storm_cat * max_storm_cat) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(duration * duration) AS sum_yy
    FROM damage_levels
    GROUP BY damage_category
),
correlation_coefficient AS (
    SELECT 
        damage_category,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS r,
        n
    FROM correlation_results
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        damage_category,
        r,
        n,
        2 * (1 - ABS(r)) AS p_value
    FROM correlation_coefficient
)
SELECT 
    damage_category,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) >= 0.4 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) < 0.4 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(r, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM p_value_calculation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(high_damage_corr) >= 0.4 AND high_damage_p_value < 0.05 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(high_damage_corr) < 0.4 AND high_damage_p_value < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS high_damage_relationship_type,
    ROUND(high_damage_corr, 2) AS high_damage_correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(high_damage_p_value, 4) AS high_damage_p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE damage_USD > (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS high_damage_count,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE damage_USD <= (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS low_damage_count,
        (SELECT 
            (SUM(max_storm_cat * duration) - (SUM(max_storm_cat) * SUM(duration) / high_damage_count)) / (high_damage_count - 1) 
            / (SQRT((SUM(max_storm_cat * max_storm_cat) - (SUM(max_storm_cat) * SUM(max_storm_cat) / high_damage_count)) / (high_damage_count - 1) * 
            (SUM(duration * duration) - (SUM(duration) * SUM(duration) / high_damage_count)) / (high_damage_count - 1))) 
            AS high_damage_corr
        FROM (
            SELECT 
                max_storm_cat, 
                (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE damage_USD > (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS duration
            FROM cost_data_with_errors
            WHERE damage_USD > (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)
        )) AS high_damage_corr_subquery,
        (SELECT 
            COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE damage_USD > (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)
        ) AS high_damage_count,
        (SELECT 
            COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE damage_USD <= (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)
        ) AS low_damage_count,
        (SELECT 
            (1 - (high_damage_count - 2) * (1 - high_damage_corr * high_damage_corr) / (high_damage_count - 2)) AS high_damage_p_value
        FROM (
            SELECT 
                max_storm_cat, 
                (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE damage_USD > (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS duration
            FROM cost_data_with_errors
            WHERE damage_USD > (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)
        )) AS high_damage_p_value_subquery
    ) AS final_result;

WITH damage_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        damage_USD,
        MAX(max_storm_cat) AS max_storm_cat,
        LENGTH(dates_active) AS duration,
        CASE 
            WHEN damage_USD >= (SELECT MEDIAN(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors) THEN 'high' 
            ELSE 'low' 
        END AS damage_type
    FROM 
        cost_data_with_errors
    GROUP BY 
        damage_USD
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        damage_type,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(max_storm_cat) AS sum_x,
        SUM(duration) AS sum_y,
        SUM(max_storm_cat * duration) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(max_storm_cat * max_storm_cat) AS sum_x2,
        SUM(duration * duration) AS sum_y2
    FROM 
        damage_stats
    GROUP BY 
        damage_type
),
correlation_results AS (
    SELECT 
        damage_type,
        n,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (SQRT((n * sum_x2 - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_y2 - sum_y * sum_y))) AS r,
        2 * (1 - T_DIST(ABS((n - 2) * r), n - 2)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    damage_type AS high_damage_relationship_type,
    ROUND(r, 2) AS high_damage_correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS high_damage_p_value
FROM 
    correlation_results
WHERE 
    damage_type = 'high';

WITH activity_duration AS (
    SELECT 
        name,
        COALESCE(NULLIF(dates_active, ''), 'Unknown') AS dates_active,
        max_storm_cat,
        LENGTH(dates_active) - LENGTH(REPLACE(dates_active, '–', '')) + 1 AS duration,
        damage_USD
    FROM 
        cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE 
        dates_active IS NOT NULL AND 
        max_storm_cat IS NOT NULL AND 
        damage_USD IS NOT NULL
),
median_damage AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY damage_USD) AS median_value
    FROM 
        activity_duration
),
correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        max_storm_cat,
        duration,
        CASE 
            WHEN damage_USD >= (SELECT median_value FROM median_damage) THEN 'high' 
            ELSE 'low' 
        END AS damage_category
    FROM 
        activity_duration
)
SELECT 
    damage_category || '_damage_relationship_type' AS high_damage_relationship_type,
    ROUND(CORR(max_storm_cat, duration), 2) AS high_damage_correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(*) > 2 THEN 
            ROUND((1 - (ABS(ROUND(CORR(max_storm_cat, duration), 2)) - 0.4) * 10), 4) 
        ELSE 
            1.0 
    END AS high_damage_p_value
FROM 
    correlation_data
GROUP BY 
    damage_category;

samples.question: 2. Can we predict the maximum sustained wind speed based on the recorded damage in USD and the minimum recorded pressure? What is the performance of the prediction model?

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG((max_sust_wind - (0.5 * damage_USD + 0.5 * min_p)) * (max_sust_wind - (0.5 * damage_USD + 0.5 * min_p))), 4) AS mean_squared_error
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(max_sust_wind, (SELECT AVG(max_sust_wind) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS max_sust_wind,
        COALESCE(damage_USD, (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS damage_USD,
        COALESCE(min_p, (SELECT AVG(min_p) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS min_p
    FROM cost_data_with_errors
) AS imputed_data;

WITH imputed_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(max_sust_wind, (SELECT AVG(max_sust_wind) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS max_sust_wind,
        COALESCE(damage_USD, (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS damage_USD,
        COALESCE(min_p, (SELECT AVG(min_p) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS min_p
    FROM cost_data_with_errors
),
train_data AS (
    SELECT *, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM imputed_data
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT max_sust_wind, damage_USD, min_p
    FROM train_data
    WHERE rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_data)
),
test_set AS (
    SELECT max_sust_wind, damage_USD, min_p
    FROM train_data
    WHERE rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_data)
),
predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        max_sust_wind,
        damage_USD,
        min_p,
        (0.1 * damage_USD + 0.2 * min_p) AS predicted_max_sust_wind
    FROM test_set
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG((max_sust_wind - predicted_max_sust_wind) * (max_sust_wind - predicted_max_sust_wind)), 4) AS mean_squared_error
FROM predictions;

SELECT ROUND(AVG((predicted.max_sust_wind - actual.max_sust_wind) * (predicted.max_sust_wind - actual.max_sust_wind)), 4) AS mean_squared_error
FROM (
    SELECT 
        AVG(max_sust_wind) AS max_sust_wind,
        AVG(damage_USD) AS damage_USD,
        AVG(min_p) AS min_p
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            COALESCE(max_sust_wind, (SELECT AVG(max_sust_wind) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS max_sust_wind,
            COALESCE(damage_USD, (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS damage_USD,
            COALESCE(min_p, (SELECT AVG(min_p) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS min_p
        FROM cost_data_with_errors
    ) AS imputed_data
    WHERE year IN (SELECT year FROM cost_data_with_errors ORDER BY RANDOM() LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors) * 0.8)
) AS predicted
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        max_sust_wind
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            COALESCE(max_sust_wind, (SELECT AVG(max_sust_wind) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS max_sust_wind,
            COALESCE(damage_USD, (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS damage_USD,
            COALESCE(min_p, (SELECT AVG(min_p) FROM cost_data_with_errors)) AS min_p
        FROM cost_data_with_errors
    ) AS imputed_data
    WHERE year NOT IN (SELECT year FROM cost_data_with_errors ORDER BY RANDOM() LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_data_with_errors) * 0.8)
) AS actual
ON 1=1;

WITH imputed_data AS (
    SELECT 
        name,
        COALESCE(max_sust_wind, AVG(max_sust_wind) OVER ()) AS max_sust_wind,
        COALESCE(damage_USD, AVG(damage_USD) OVER ()) AS damage_USD,
        COALESCE(min_p, AVG(min_p) OVER ()) AS min_p,
        year
    FROM cost_data_with_errors
),
train_data AS (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM imputed_data
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT max_sust_wind, damage_USD, min_p
    FROM train_data
    WHERE rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_data)
),
test_set AS (
    SELECT max_sust_wind, damage_USD, min_p
    FROM train_data
    WHERE rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_data)
),
mse_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(POWER(max_sust_wind - predicted_max_sust_wind, 2)) AS mse
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            max_sust_wind,
            damage_USD * 0.1 + min_p * 0.2 AS predicted_max_sust_wind
        FROM test_set
    )
)
SELECT ROUND(mse, 4) AS mean_squared_error
FROM mse_calculation;

WITH imputed_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(max_sust_wind, (SELECT AVG(max_sust_wind) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE max_sust_wind IS NOT NULL)) AS max_sust_wind,
        COALESCE(damage_USD, (SELECT AVG(damage_USD) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE damage_USD IS NOT NULL)) AS damage_USD,
        COALESCE(min_p, (SELECT AVG(min_p) FROM cost_data_with_errors WHERE min_p IS NOT NULL)) AS min_p
    FROM 
        cost_data_with_errors
    WHERE 
        max_sust_wind IS NOT NULL AND damage_USD IS NOT NULL AND min_p IS NOT NULL
),
train_data AS (
    SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM imputed_data
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT max_sust_wind, damage_USD, min_p 
    FROM train_data 
    WHERE rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_data)
),
test_set AS (
    SELECT max_sust_wind, damage_USD, min_p 
    FROM train_data 
    WHERE rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.8 FROM train_data)
),
predictions AS (
    SELECT 
        max_sust_wind,
        damage_USD,
        min_p,
        ROUND((damage_USD * 0.1 + min_p * 0.2), 2) AS predicted_wind_speed
    FROM 
        test_set
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(POWER(predicted_wind_speed - max_sust_wind, 2)), 4) AS mean_squared_error
FROM 
    predictions;

samples.question: 1. What is the mean wind speed in the dataset?
samples.constraints: Calculate the arithmetic mean of WINDSPEED, excluding any null values. The mean must be calculated to three decimal places.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 3) AS mean_windspeed FROM baro_2015 WHERE WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL;

WITH mean_calculation AS (
    SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) AS mean_windspeed
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT ROUND(mean_windspeed, 3) AS mean_windspeed
FROM mean_calculation;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 3) AS mean_windspeed FROM (SELECT WINDSPEED FROM baro_2015 WHERE WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL);

WITH windspeed_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        WINDSPEED,
        AVG(WINDSPEED) OVER () AS mean_windspeed
    FROM 
        baro_2015
    WHERE 
        WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(DISTINCT mean_windspeed, 3) AS mean_windspeed
FROM 
    windspeed_stats;

WITH valid_windspeed AS (
    SELECT WINDSPEED
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(WINDSPEED, 0)), 3) AS mean_windspeed
FROM valid_windspeed;

samples.question: 2. Are there any outliers in the atmospheric pressure column (BARO)? If yes, how many outliers are there?
samples.constraints: An outlier is any value that is more than 1.5 times the interquartile range above the third quartile or below the first quartile. Ignore null values.

variants.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
FROM baro_2015
WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL AND (BARO < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR FROM (SELECT 
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q1,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q3,
    (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO)) AS IQR
    FROM baro_2015 WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL)) OR 
    (BARO > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR FROM (SELECT 
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q1,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q3,
    (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO)) AS IQR
    FROM baro_2015 WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL)));

WITH quartiles AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q3
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM quartiles
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
    FROM baro_2015, iqr
    WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL 
    AND (BARO < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR BARO > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))
)
SELECT number_of_outliers FROM outliers;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers FROM (
    SELECT BARO
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL
) AS filtered_data
WHERE BARO < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR FROM (
                    SELECT 
                        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q1,
                        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q3,
                        (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO)) AS IQR
                    FROM baro_2015
                    WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL
                ) AS IQR_values)
OR BARO > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR FROM (
                    SELECT 
                        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q1,
                        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q3,
                        (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO)) AS IQR
                    FROM baro_2015
                    WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL
                ) AS IQR_values);

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        BARO,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) OVER () AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) OVER () AS Q3
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM stats
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
FROM baro_2015 b
JOIN iqr i ON 1=1
WHERE b.BARO IS NOT NULL 
AND (b.BARO < (i.Q1 - 1.5 * i.IQR) OR b.BARO > (i.Q3 + 1.5 * i.IQR));

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY BARO) AS Q3
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE BARO IS NOT NULL
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1, 
        Q3, 
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM stats
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
    FROM baro_2015
    JOIN iqr ON BARO IS NOT NULL
    WHERE 
        BARO < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR 
        BARO > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(number_of_outliers, 0) AS number_of_outliers
FROM outliers;

samples.question: 1. What is the distribution of wind speeds (WINDSPEED) in the dataset? Is it normally distributed?
samples.constraints: Use the Shapiro-Wilk test to determine if the distribution is normal. Accept the null hypothesis that the data is normally distributed if the p-value is greater than 0.05, and reject it otherwise.

variants.sql:
WITH windspeed_data AS (
    SELECT WINDSPEED
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(WINDSPEED) AS mean,
        STDDEV(WINDSPEED) AS stddev
    FROM windspeed_data
),
normality_check AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN (n < 3) THEN 'insufficient data'
            ELSE ROUND((SUM((WINDSPEED - mean) * (WINDSPEED - mean)) / (n - 1)), 2) AS variance,
            ROUND((1 - (variance / (variance + stddev * stddev))) * n, 2) AS W,
            ROUND((1 - (W / n)), 2) AS p_value
        END AS result
    FROM windspeed_data, shapiro_test
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'normal_distribution=yes'
        ELSE 'normal_distribution=no'
    END AS normal_distribution
FROM normality_check;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'normal_distribution=yes' 
        ELSE 'normal_distribution=no' 
    END AS normal_distribution
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(WINDSPEED) AS sum_x,
        SUM(WINDSPEED * WINDSPEED) AS sum_x2,
        (SUM(WINDSPEED) / COUNT(*)) AS mean,
        (SUM(WINDSPEED * WINDSPEED) / COUNT(*)) - (SUM(WINDSPEED) / COUNT(*)) * (SUM(WINDSPEED) / COUNT(*)) AS variance,
        (SELECT 
            (1 - (SUM((WINDSPEED - mean) * (WINDSPEED - mean)) / (n * variance))) AS p_value
         FROM 
            (SELECT WINDSPEED, 
                    (SUM(WINDSPEED) / COUNT(*)) AS mean,
                    (SUM(WINDSPEED * WINDSPEED) / COUNT(*)) - (SUM(WINDSPEED) / COUNT(*)) * (SUM(WINDSPEED) / COUNT(*)) AS variance
             FROM baro_2015) 
        ) 
    ) AS stats
);

WITH windspeed_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        WINDSPEED,
        AVG(WINDSPEED) OVER () AS avg_windspeed,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_count,
        SUM((WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED) OVER ()) * (WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED) OVER ())) OVER () AS sum_squared_diff,
        SUM((WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED) OVER ()) * (WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED) OVER ()) * (WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED) OVER ())) OVER () AS sum_cubed_diff
    FROM baro_2015
),
shapiro_wilk AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((total_count * sum_squared_diff) / (total_count - 1), 2) AS W,
        ROUND((sum_cubed_diff / (total_count - 1)) / (sum_squared_diff / (total_count - 1)), 2) AS statistic
    FROM windspeed_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN statistic > 0.05 THEN 'normal_distribution=yes'
        ELSE 'normal_distribution=no'
    END AS normal_distribution
FROM shapiro_wilk;

WITH wind_speed_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(WINDSPEED, 0) AS WINDSPEED
    FROM 
        baro_2015
    WHERE 
        WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 2) AS avg_windspeed,
        ROUND(STDEV(WINDSPEED), 2) AS stddev_windspeed,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        ROUND(1.0 - (SUM(POWER(WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED), 2)) / NULLIF(SUM(POWER(WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED), 2)), 0)), 2) AS p_value
    FROM 
        wind_speed_data
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value > 0.05 THEN 'normal_distribution=yes'
        ELSE 'normal_distribution=no'
    END AS normal_distribution
FROM 
    shapiro_test;

samples.question: 2. Calculate the average wind speed (WINDSPEED) for each month in the dataset.
samples.constraints: Assume that the "DATE TIME" column is in the format "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS". Extract the month from each date and calculate the mean wind speed for each respective month. Keep only two decimal places. The data is in chronological order so the answer should also be in order.

variants.sql:
SELECT strftime('%m', "DATE TIME") AS month, ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 2) AS avg_windspeed
FROM baro_2015
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

WITH monthly_data AS (
    SELECT 
        strftime('%m', "DATE TIME") AS month,
        WINDSPEED
    FROM 
        baro_2015
),
monthly_avg AS (
    SELECT 
        month,
        ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 2) AS avg_windspeed
    FROM 
        monthly_data
    GROUP BY 
        month
)
SELECT 
    month,
    avg_windspeed
FROM 
    monthly_avg
ORDER BY 
    month;

SELECT 
    strftime('%m', "DATE TIME") AS month,
    ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 2) AS avg_windspeed
FROM 
    baro_2015
GROUP BY 
    month
ORDER BY 
    month;

WITH monthly_data AS (
    SELECT 
        strftime('%m', "DATE TIME") AS month,
        WINDSPEED
    FROM baro_2015
)
SELECT 
    month,
    ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 2) AS avg_windspeed
FROM monthly_data
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

WITH monthly_wind_data AS (
    SELECT 
        strftime('%m', "DATE TIME") AS month,
        COALESCE(WINDSPEED, 0) AS windspeed
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE "DATE TIME" IS NOT NULL AND WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    month,
    ROUND(AVG(windspeed), 2) AS avg_windspeed
FROM monthly_wind_data
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

    COUNT(CASE WHEN GUSTS IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS GUSTS,

SELECT

WITH missing_counts AS (

)
SELECT

WITH missing_counts AS (

samples.question: 1. Is there a relationship between wind speed (WINDSPEED) and atmospheric pressure (BARO) for wind direction (DIR) equal to 180 degrees? Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient for this specific wind direction.
samples.constraints:
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between wind speed and atmospheric pressure for wind direction equal to 180 degrees.
Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05.
Report the p-value associated with the correlation test.
Consider the relationship to be linear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.5.
Consider the relationship to be nonlinear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is less than 0.5.
If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(WINDSPEED, BARO), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND(CORR(WINDSPEED, BARO), 2) IS NOT NULL THEN 
            (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM baro_2015 WHERE DIR = 180) 
        ELSE 0 
    END AS n,
    ROUND(1 - POWER(1 - (ROUND(CORR(WINDSPEED, BARO), 2) * ROUND(CORR(WINDSPEED, BARO), 2)), (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM baro_2015 WHERE DIR = 180) - 2), 4) AS p_value
FROM baro_2015
WHERE DIR = 180;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT WINDSPEED, BARO
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE DIR = 180
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(WINDSPEED) AS avg_wind,
        AVG(BARO) AS avg_baro,
        SUM((WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED)) * (BARO - AVG(BARO))) AS cov,
        SUM((WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED)) * (WINDSPEED - AVG(WINDSPEED))) AS var_wind,
        SUM((BARO - AVG(BARO)) * (BARO - AVG(BARO))) AS var_baro
    FROM filtered_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(cov / SQRT(var_wind * var_baro), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
        n
    FROM stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM correlation
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'none'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value,
    correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation, p_value;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'none' 
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(WINDSPEED) AS sum_x,
        SUM(BARO) AS sum_y,
        SUM(WINDSPEED * WINDSPEED) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(BARO * BARO) AS sum_yy,
        SUM(WINDSPEED * BARO) AS sum_xy,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS correlation_coefficient,
        2 * (1 - (ABS(correlation_coefficient) * SQRT(n - 2) / SQRT(1 - correlation_coefficient * correlation_coefficient))) AS p_value
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            WINDSPEED, 
            BARO 
        FROM baro_2015 
        WHERE DIR = 180
    )
) AS correlation_results;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT WINDSPEED, BARO
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE DIR = 180
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(WINDSPEED) AS sum_x,
        SUM(BARO) AS sum_y,
        SUM(WINDSPEED * WINDSPEED) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(BARO * BARO) AS sum_yy,
        SUM(WINDSPEED * BARO) AS sum_xy
    FROM filtered_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS r,
        n
    FROM stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(r)) AS p_value
    FROM correlation
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'none' 
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value,
    ROUND(r, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation, p_value;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT
        COALESCE(WINDSPEED, 0) AS WINDSPEED,
        COALESCE(BARO, 0) AS BARO
    FROM
        baro_2015
    WHERE
        DIR = 180 AND
        WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL AND
        BARO IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(WINDSPEED) AS sum_x,
        SUM(BARO) AS sum_y,
        SUM(WINDSPEED * WINDSPEED) AS sum_x2,
        SUM(BARO * BARO) AS sum_y2,
        SUM(WINDSPEED * BARO) AS sum_xy
    FROM
        filtered_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT
        CAST(n AS FLOAT) AS n,
        CAST(sum_x AS FLOAT) AS sum_x,
        CAST(sum_y AS FLOAT) AS sum_y,
        CAST(sum_x2 AS FLOAT) AS sum_x2,
        CAST(sum_y2 AS FLOAT) AS sum_y2,
        CAST(sum_xy AS FLOAT) AS sum_xy,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (SQRT(n * sum_x2 - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_y2 - sum_y * sum_y)) AS r
    FROM
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT
        2 * (1 - ABS(r)) AS p_value
    FROM
        correlation
)
SELECT
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value >= 0.05 THEN 'none'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(COALESCE(p_value, 1), 4) AS p_value,
    ROUND(COALESCE(r, 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM
    correlation, p_value;

samples.question: 2. Perform data preprocessing on the dataset, which includes removing outliers in the wind speed (WINDSPEED) column using the Z-score method (outliers are values that have a Z-score greater than 3 or lesser than -3) and handling missing values in the atmospheric temperature (AT) column by replacing them with the mean temperature. After preprocessing, calculate the mean wind speed and average atmospheric temperature.
samples.constraints:
Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the wind speed (WINDSPEED) column before preprocessing.
Replace any wind speed value that is more than three standard deviations away from the mean, with the mean wind speed.
Calculate the mean atmosphere temperature (AT), and fill missing values in the atmospheric temperature (AT) column with this mean.
Calculate the mean values after preprocessing.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 2) AS mean_wind_pre,
    ROUND(AVG(AT), 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_pre,
    ROUND(AVG(CASE 
        WHEN WINDSPEED > (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) + 3 * STDDEV(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015) THEN (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015)
        WHEN WINDSPEED < (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) - 3 * STDDEV(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015) THEN (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015)
        ELSE WINDSPEED END), 2) AS mean_wind_post,
    ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(AT, (SELECT AVG(AT) FROM baro_2015))), 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_post
FROM baro_2015;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(WINDSPEED) AS mean_wind,
        STDDEV(WINDSPEED) AS stddev_wind,
        AVG(AT) AS mean_temp
    FROM baro_2015
),
preprocessed AS (
    SELECT 
        DATE TIME,
        CASE 
            WHEN WINDSPEED > (mean_wind + 3 * stddev_wind) OR WINDSPEED < (mean_wind - 3 * stddev_wind) THEN mean_wind
            ELSE WINDSPEED 
        END AS adjusted_windspeed,
        COALESCE(AT, mean_temp) AS adjusted_temp
    FROM baro_2015, stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(adjusted_windspeed), 2) AS mean_wind_post,
    ROUND(AVG(adjusted_temp), 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_post,
    ROUND((SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015), 2) AS mean_wind_pre,
    ROUND((SELECT AVG(AT) FROM baro_2015), 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_pre
FROM preprocessed;

SELECT 
    ROUND(mean_wind_pre, 2) AS mean_wind_pre,
    ROUND(mean_atmos_temp_pre, 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_pre,
    ROUND(mean_atmos_temp_post, 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_post,
    ROUND(mean_wind_post, 2) AS mean_wind_post
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015) AS mean_wind_pre,
        (SELECT AVG(AT) FROM baro_2015 WHERE AT IS NOT NULL) AS mean_atmos_temp_pre,
        (SELECT AVG(CASE 
            WHEN WINDSPEED > (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015) + 3 * (SELECT STDEV(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015)
            THEN (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015)
            WHEN WINDSPEED < (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015) - 3 * (SELECT STDEV(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015)
            THEN (SELECT AVG(WINDSPEED) FROM baro_2015)
            ELSE WINDSPEED END) 
        FROM baro_2015) AS mean_wind_post,
        (SELECT AVG(COALESCE(AT, (SELECT AVG(AT) FROM baro_2015 WHERE AT IS NOT NULL))) FROM baro_2015) AS mean_atmos_temp_post
);

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(WINDSPEED) AS mean_wind,
        STDDEV(WINDSPEED) AS stddev_wind,
        AVG(AT) AS mean_atmos_temp
    FROM baro_2015
),
preprocessed AS (
    SELECT 
        DATE TIME,
        CASE 
            WHEN WINDSPEED > (mean_wind + 3 * stddev_wind) THEN mean_wind
            WHEN WINDSPEED < (mean_wind - 3 * stddev_wind) THEN mean_wind
            ELSE WINDSPEED 
        END AS adjusted_windspeed,
        COALESCE(AT, mean_atmos_temp) AS adjusted_atmos_temp
    FROM baro_2015, stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(mean_wind, 2) AS mean_wind_pre,
    ROUND(mean_atmos_temp, 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_pre,
    ROUND(AVG(adjusted_atmos_temp), 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_post,
    ROUND(AVG(adjusted_windspeed), 2) AS mean_wind_post
FROM stats, preprocessed;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(WINDSPEED), 2) AS mean_wind_pre,
        ROUND(STDDEV(WINDSPEED), 2) AS stddev_wind_pre,
        ROUND(AVG(AT), 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_pre
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL AND AT IS NOT NULL
),
preprocessed AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(NULLIF(WINDSPEED, 0), ROUND((SELECT mean_wind_pre FROM stats), 2)) AS adjusted_windspeed,
        COALESCE(AT, (SELECT mean_atmos_temp_pre FROM stats)) AS adjusted_at
    FROM baro_2015
    WHERE WINDSPEED IS NOT NULL
),
final_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN adjusted_windspeed < (SELECT mean_wind_pre FROM stats) + 3 * (SELECT stddev_wind_pre FROM stats) 
                       AND adjusted_windspeed > (SELECT mean_wind_pre FROM stats) - 3 * (SELECT stddev_wind_pre FROM stats) 
                       THEN adjusted_windspeed ELSE (SELECT mean_wind_pre FROM stats) END), 2) AS mean_wind_post,
        ROUND(AVG(adjusted_at), 2) AS mean_atmos_temp_post
    FROM preprocessed
)

SELECT 
    (SELECT mean_wind_pre FROM stats) AS mean_wind_pre,
    (SELECT mean_atmos_temp_pre FROM stats) AS mean_atmos_temp_pre,
    (SELECT mean_wind_post FROM final_stats) AS mean_wind_post,
    (SELECT mean_atmos_temp_post FROM final_stats) AS mean_atmos_temp_post;

samples.question: 2. Is the distribution of offender ages normally distributed or skewed?
samples.constraints: Calculate skewness of the 'Age' column using the skew function from the 'scipy.stats'. A skewness value between -0.5 to +0.5 indicates that the distribution is approximately symmetric, a skewness value greater than +0.5 indicates a distribution skewed to the right and a skewness value less than -0.5 indicates a distribution skewed to the left.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age))) / COUNT(Age)) = 0 THEN 'distribution_symmetric'
        WHEN (SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age))) / COUNT(Age)) / 
             POWER(SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age))) / COUNT(Age), 1.5) > 0.5 THEN 'distribution_skewed_right'
        WHEN (SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age))) / COUNT(Age)) / 
             POWER(SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age))) / COUNT(Age), 1.5) < -0.5 THEN 'distribution_skewed_left'
        ELSE 'distribution_symmetric'
    END AS distribution_skew
FROM arrest_expungibility;

WITH age_distribution AS (
    SELECT Age, COUNT(*) AS age_count
    FROM arrest_expungibility
    GROUP BY Age
),
age_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Age) AS avg_age,
        COUNT(*) AS total_count,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age)) * (Age - AVG(Age))) AS variance_sum
    FROM age_distribution
),
skewness_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(POWER(Age - avg_age, 3) * age_count) / total_count AS skewness_numerator,
        SUM(POWER(Age - avg_age, 2) * age_count) / total_count AS variance
    FROM age_distribution, age_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN skewness_numerator / POWER(variance, 1.5) BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 THEN 'approximately_symmetric'
        WHEN skewness_numerator / POWER(variance, 1.5) > 0.5 THEN 'skewed_right'
        ELSE 'skewed_left'
    END AS distribution_skew
FROM skewness_calculation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM arrest_expungibility) - 
             (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM arrest_expungibility WHERE Age < (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM arrest_expungibility)) > 0 THEN 'skewed_left'
        WHEN (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM arrest_expungibility) - 
             (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM arrest_expungibility WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM arrest_expungibility)) < 0 THEN 'skewed_right'
        ELSE 'approximately_symmetric' 
    END AS distribution_skew;

WITH age_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Age,
        COUNT(*) AS count,
        AVG(Age) OVER () AS avg_age,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age) OVER ()) * (Age - AVG(Age) OVER ())) OVER () AS variance_sum,
        SUM((Age - AVG(Age) OVER ()) * (Age - AVG(Age) OVER ()) * (Age - AVG(Age) OVER ())) OVER () AS skew_sum
    FROM 
        arrest_expungibility
),
final_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(count) AS total_count,
        ROUND(SQRT(variance_sum / (total_count - 1)), 2) AS std_dev,
        ROUND(skew_sum / (n * POWER(std_dev, 3)), 2) AS skewness
    FROM 
        age_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN skewness BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 THEN 'approximately_symmetric'
        WHEN skewness > 0.5 THEN 'skewed_right'
        ELSE 'skewed_left' 
    END AS distribution_skew
FROM 
    final_stats;

WITH age_distribution AS (
    SELECT 
        Age,
        COUNT(*) AS age_count
    FROM 
        arrest_expungibility
    WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        Age
),
age_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS total_count,
        SUM(age_count) AS sum_count,
        SUM(age_count * Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(age_count * Age * Age) AS sum_age_squared
    FROM 
        age_distribution
),
skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(
            (total_count * SUM(age_count * (Age - (sum_age / NULLIF(total_count, 0))) * (Age - (sum_age / NULLIF(total_count, 0))))) / 
            (SUM(age_count) * SUM(age_count * Age * Age) - (SUM(age_count * Age) * SUM(age_count * Age) / NULLIF(total_count, 0)))
        ), 2) AS skew_value
    FROM 
        age_distribution, age_stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN skew_value BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 THEN 'approximately_symmetric'
        WHEN skew_value > 0.5 THEN 'skewed_right'
        WHEN skew_value < -0.5 THEN 'skewed_left'
    END AS distribution_skew
FROM 
    skewness;

samples.question: 3. Is there a correlation between the count of offenses and the age of the offender?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between count and age. Ignore the null values in the 'Count' column for this analysis. A value of r below -0.6 or above +0.6 indicates a strong correlation, between -0.6 and -0.3 or between +0.3 and +0.6 indicates a moderate correlation, -0.3 and +0.3 indicates a weak correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(CORR(Age, Count)) > 0.6 THEN 'strong'
        WHEN ABS(CORR(Age, Count)) BETWEEN 0.3 AND 0.6 THEN 'moderate'
        ELSE 'weak'
    END AS correlation_strength
FROM arrest_expungibility
WHERE Count IS NOT NULL;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT Age, Count
    FROM arrest_expungibility
    WHERE Count IS NOT NULL
),
mean_values AS (
    SELECT AVG(Age) AS avg_age, AVG(Count) AS avg_count
    FROM valid_data
),
covariance AS (
    SELECT SUM((Age - mv.avg_age) * (Count - mv.avg_count)) / COUNT(*) AS cov
    FROM valid_data, mean_values mv
),
variance_age AS (
    SELECT SUM((Age - mv.avg_age) * (Age - mv.avg_age)) / COUNT(*) AS var_age
    FROM valid_data, mean_values mv
),
variance_count AS (
    SELECT SUM((Count - mv.avg_count) * (Count - mv.avg_count)) / COUNT(*) AS var_count
    FROM valid_data, mean_values mv
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT ROUND(cov.cov / (SQRT(var.var_age) * SQRT(var.var_count)), 2) AS r
    FROM covariance cov, variance_age var_age, variance_count var_count
)
SELECT CASE 
           WHEN r < -0.6 OR r > 0.6 THEN 'strong'
           WHEN r BETWEEN -0.6 AND -0.3 OR r BETWEEN 0.3 AND 0.6 THEN 'moderate'
           ELSE 'weak'
       END AS correlation_strength
FROM correlation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr) > 0.6 THEN 'strong' 
        WHEN ABS(corr) BETWEEN 0.3 AND 0.6 THEN 'moderate' 
        ELSE 'weak' 
    END AS correlation_strength
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (SUM((Age - avg_age) * (Count - avg_count)) / 
        (SQRT(SUM(POWER(Age - avg_age, 2))) * SQRT(SUM(POWER(Count - avg_count, 2))))) AS corr
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            Age, 
            Count, 
            (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM arrest_expungibility WHERE Count IS NOT NULL) AS avg_age,
            (SELECT AVG(Count) FROM arrest_expungibility WHERE Count IS NOT NULL) AS avg_count
        FROM arrest_expungibility
        WHERE Count IS NOT NULL
    )
)

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(Age) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Count) AS sum_count,
        SUM(Age * Count) AS sum_age_count,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_squared,
        SUM(Count * Count) AS sum_count_squared
    FROM arrest_expungibility
    WHERE Count IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_age_count - sum_age * sum_count) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_age_squared - sum_age * sum_age) * (n * sum_count_squared - sum_count * sum_count))) AS r
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(r) > 0.6 THEN 'strong'
        WHEN ABS(r) BETWEEN 0.3 AND 0.6 THEN 'moderate'
        ELSE 'weak' 
    END AS correlation_strength
FROM correlation;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        Age, 
        Count 
    FROM 
        arrest_expungibility 
    WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL 
        AND Count IS NOT NULL
), 
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Count) AS sum_count,
        SUM(Age * Count) AS sum_age_count,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_squared,
        SUM(Count * Count) AS sum_count_squared
    FROM 
        valid_data
), 
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_age_count - sum_age * sum_count) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_age_squared - sum_age * sum_age) * (n * sum_count_squared - sum_count * sum_count)), 0) AS r
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(r) > 0.6 THEN 'strong' 
        WHEN ABS(r) BETWEEN 0.3 AND 0.6 THEN 'moderate' 
        ELSE 'weak' 
    END AS correlation_strength
FROM 
    correlation;

samples.question: 2. Are there any outliers in the age distribution of offenders in 'Assault' category, according to the IQR method? If yes, report the number of outliers.
samples.constraints: You are to use the Interquartile Range (IQR) method for outlier detection. Calculate the IQR as Q3 (75th percentile) - Q1 (25th percentile) for the 'Assault' category. Outliers are considered as values lying below Q1 - 1.5 * IQR or above Q3 + 1.5 * IQR.

variants.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
FROM arrest_expungibility
WHERE Age < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR FROM (
    SELECT 
        percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) AS Q1,
        percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) AS Q3,
        (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) - percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age)) AS IQR
    FROM arrest_expungibility
    WHERE Category = 'Assault'
)) OR Age > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR FROM (
    SELECT 
        percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) AS Q1,
        percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) AS Q3,
        (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) - percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age)) AS IQR
    FROM arrest_expungibility
    WHERE Category = 'Assault'
));

WITH age_distribution AS (
    SELECT Age
    FROM arrest_expungibility
    WHERE Category = 'Assault'
),
quartiles AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) AS Q3
    FROM age_distribution
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM quartiles
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
    FROM age_distribution, iqr
    WHERE Age < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Age > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
)
SELECT number_of_outliers FROM outliers;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
FROM (
    SELECT Age,
           (SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) FROM arrest_expungibility WHERE Category = 'Assault') AS Q1,
           (SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Age) FROM arrest_expungibility WHERE Category = 'Assault') AS Q3
    FROM arrest_expungibility
    WHERE Category = 'Assault'
) AS subquery
WHERE Age < (Q1 - 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1)) OR Age > (Q3 + 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1));

WITH age_distribution AS (
    SELECT 
        Age,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) OVER() AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) OVER() AS Q3
    FROM 
        arrest_expungibility
    WHERE 
        Category = 'Assault'
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM 
        age_distribution
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
    FROM 
        arrest_expungibility,
        iqr
    WHERE 
        Category = 'Assault' AND 
        (Age < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Age > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))
)
SELECT 
    number_of_outliers 
FROM 
    outliers;

WITH age_distribution AS (
    SELECT
        Age
    FROM
        arrest_expungibility
    WHERE
        Category = 'Assault' AND Age IS NOT NULL
),
quartiles AS (
    SELECT
        ROUND(COALESCE((SELECT AVG(Age) FROM (
            SELECT Age
            FROM age_distribution
            ORDER BY Age
            LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM age_distribution) / 2 OFFSET (SELECT (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2 FROM age_distribution)
        )), 0), 2) AS Q1,
        ROUND(COALESCE((SELECT AVG(Age) FROM (
            SELECT Age
            FROM age_distribution
            ORDER BY Age
            LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM age_distribution) / 2 OFFSET (SELECT COUNT(*) / 2 FROM age_distribution)
        )), 0), 2) AS Q3
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM
        quartiles
)
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS number_of_outliers
FROM
    age_distribution,
    iqr
WHERE
    Age < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Age > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR);

samples.question: What is the mean value of the "Value" column?
samples.constraints:

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(Value), 2) AS mean_value FROM oecd_education_spending WHERE Value IS NOT NULL;

WITH non_null_values AS (

)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(Value), 2) AS mean_value

SELECT ROUND(AVG(Value), 2) AS mean_value

    FROM oecd_education_spending

samples.question: Are there any outliers in the "Value" column? If yes, how many and what are their locations (row numbers)?
samples.constraints:
Use the IQR method to detect outliers. Define an outlier as a data point that falls below Q1 - 1.5*IQR or above Q3 + 1.5*IQR.
Return the list of row numbers (starting from 0) for those outliers in ascending order. If there are no outliers, return an empty list.
Ignore the null values in the "Value" column.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () - 1 AS row_number
FROM oecd_education_spending
WHERE Value IS NOT NULL AND (Value < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR FROM (
    SELECT 
        percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) AS Q1,
        percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) AS Q3,
        (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) - percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value)) AS IQR
    FROM oecd_education_spending
    WHERE Value IS NOT NULL
)) OR Value > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR FROM (
    SELECT 
        percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) AS Q1,
        percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) AS Q3,
        (percentile_cont(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) - percentile_cont(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value)) AS IQR
    FROM oecd_education_spending
    WHERE Value IS NOT NULL
)));

WITH ValueStats AS (
    SELECT 
        MIN(Value) AS min_value,
        MAX(Value) AS max_value,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) AS Q3
    FROM oecd_education_spending
    WHERE Value IS NOT NULL
),
IQR_Calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM ValueStats
),
Outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER () - 1 AS row_number,
        LOCATION,
        Value
    FROM oecd_education_spending
    CROSS JOIN IQR_Calculation
    WHERE Value IS NOT NULL
    AND (Value < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Value > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))
)
SELECT row_number
FROM Outliers
ORDER BY row_number;

SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () - 1 AS row_number
FROM (
    SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS rn, Value
    FROM oecd_education_spending
    WHERE Value IS NOT NULL
) AS subquery,
(
    SELECT 
        MIN(Value) AS Q1,
        MAX(Value) AS Q3,
        (MAX(Value) - MIN(Value)) AS IQR
    FROM (
        SELECT Value
        FROM oecd_education_spending
        WHERE Value IS NOT NULL
        ORDER BY Value
        LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM oecd_education_spending WHERE Value IS NOT NULL) / 4
    ) AS quartiles
) AS iqr_values
WHERE Value < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Value > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
ORDER BY row_number;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Value,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY TIME) - 1 AS row_num,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) OVER () AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) OVER () AS Q3
    FROM 
        oecd_education_spending
    WHERE 
        Value IS NOT NULL
),
IQR AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM 
        stats
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    row_num
FROM 
    stats, IQR
WHERE 
    Value < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Value > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
ORDER BY 
    row_num;

WITH ValueStats AS (
    SELECT 
        MIN(Value) AS min_value,
        MAX(Value) AS max_value,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Value) AS Q3
    FROM oecd_education_spending
    WHERE Value IS NOT NULL
),
IQR_Calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR,
        (Q1 - 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1)) AS lower_bound,
        (Q3 + 1.5 * (Q3 - Q1)) AS upper_bound
    FROM ValueStats
)
SELECT 
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER () - 1 AS row_number
FROM oecd_education_spending, IQR_Calculation
WHERE Value IS NOT NULL
AND (Value < lower_bound OR Value > upper_bound
AND LOCATION IS NOT NULL)
ORDER BY row_number;

samples.question: Is there a correlation between the "Value" column and the "TIME" column? If yes, what is the correlation coefficient?
samples.constraints:
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient.
Ignore all the pairs that either "Value" or "TIME" is null.
Round your final answer to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT TIME, Value
    FROM oecd_education_spending
    WHERE Value IS NOT NULL AND TIME IS NOT NULL
),
mean_values AS (
    SELECT AVG(TIME) AS avg_time, AVG(Value) AS avg_value
    FROM filtered_data
),
covariance AS (
    SELECT SUM((TIME - mv.avg_time) * (Value - mv.avg_value)) / COUNT(*) AS cov
    FROM filtered_data
    CROSS JOIN mean_values mv
),
variance_time AS (
    SELECT SUM((TIME - mv.avg_time) * (TIME - mv.avg_time)) / COUNT(*) AS var_time
    FROM filtered_data
    CROSS JOIN mean_values mv
),
variance_value AS (
    SELECT SUM((Value - mv.avg_value) * (Value - mv.avg_value)) / COUNT(*) AS var_value
    FROM filtered_data
    CROSS JOIN mean_values mv
)
SELECT ROUND(cov.cov / (SQRT(var.var_time) * SQRT(var_value.var_value)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM covariance cov, variance_time var, variance_value var_value;

SELECT ROUND(CORR(Value, TIME), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (SELECT Value, TIME 
      FROM oecd_education_spending 
      WHERE Value IS NOT NULL AND TIME IS NOT NULL);

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT TIME, Value
    FROM oecd_education_spending
    WHERE Value IS NOT NULL AND TIME IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(TIME) AS sum_x,
        SUM(Value) AS sum_y,
        SUM(TIME * Value) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(TIME * TIME) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(Value * Value) AS sum_yy
    FROM valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        TIME,
        Value
    FROM 
        oecd_education_spending
    WHERE 
        TIME IS NOT NULL 
        AND Value IS NOT NULL
),
n AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS count,
        SUM(TIME) AS sum_time,
        SUM(Value) AS sum_value,
        SUM(TIME * Value) AS sum_time_value,
        SUM(TIME * TIME) AS sum_time_sq,
        SUM(Value * Value) AS sum_value_sq
    FROM 
        valid_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(
        (count * sum_time_value - sum_time * sum_value) /
        NULLIF(SQRT((count * sum_time_sq - sum_time * sum_time) * (count * sum_value_sq - sum_value * sum_value)), 0), 
        2
    ) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    n;

samples.question: Apply feature engineering techniques to the dataset. Create a new feature by subtracting the mean value of the "Value" column from each value in that column. Calculate and report the standard deviation of this new feature.

samples.question: Perform outlier detection on the percentage of graduates in the field of Architecture over the years using the Z-score method with a threshold of 3. Identify all years with outliers, then calculate the mean and standard deviation for the years without these outliers.
samples.constraints:
Use the Z-score method with a threshold of 3 for outlier detection.
Include all years in the dataset for the calculation.
After identifying the outliers, remove them and then calculate the mean percentage and the standard deviation of the remaining data. Round to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Architecture), 2) AS mean_without_outliers,
    ROUND(STDEV(Architecture), 2) AS std_without_outliers
FROM 
    percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa
WHERE 
    ABS(Architecture - (SELECT AVG(Architecture) FROM percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa)) / (SELECT STDEV(Architecture) FROM percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa) < 3;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Architecture) AS mean_architecture,
        STDDEV(Architecture) AS stddev_architecture
    FROM 
        `percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa`
),
z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        Year,
        Architecture,
        (Architecture - (SELECT mean_architecture FROM stats)) / (SELECT stddev_architecture FROM stats) AS z_score
    FROM 
        `percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa`
),
filtered AS (
    SELECT 
        Architecture
    FROM 
        z_scores
    WHERE 
        ABS(z_score) < 3
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Architecture), 2) AS mean_without_outliers,
    ROUND(STDDEV(Architecture), 2) AS std_without_outliers
FROM 
    filtered;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Architecture), 2) AS mean_without_outliers,
    ROUND(STDDEV(Architecture), 2) AS std_without_outliers
FROM 
    percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa
WHERE 
    Architecture NOT IN (
        SELECT Architecture
        FROM (
            SELECT 
                Architecture,
                (Architecture - AVG(Architecture) OVER ()) / NULLIF(STDDEV(Architecture) OVER (), 0) AS z_score
            FROM 
                percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa
        ) AS z_scores
        WHERE ABS(z_score) > 3
    );

WITH ArchitectureStats AS (
    SELECT 
        Year,
        Architecture,
        AVG(Architecture) OVER () AS mean_arch,
        STDDEV(Architecture) OVER () AS stddev_arch
    FROM 
        `percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa`
),
ZScores AS (
    SELECT 
        Year,
        Architecture,
        (Architecture - mean_arch) / stddev_arch AS z_score
    FROM 
        ArchitectureStats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Architecture), 2) AS mean_without_outliers,
    ROUND(STDDEV(Architecture), 2) AS std_without_outliers
FROM 
    ZScores
WHERE 
    ABS(z_score) < 3;

WITH ArchitectureStats AS (
    SELECT 
        Year,
        COALESCE(Architecture, 0) AS Architecture
    FROM 
        `percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa`
    WHERE 
        Architecture IS NOT NULL
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Architecture) AS Mean,
        STDDEV(Architecture) AS StdDev
    FROM 
        ArchitectureStats
),
ZScores AS (
    SELECT 
        Year,
        Architecture,
        (Architecture - (SELECT Mean FROM Stats)) / NULLIF((SELECT StdDev FROM Stats), 0) AS ZScore
    FROM 
        ArchitectureStats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT Mean FROM Stats), 2) AS mean_without_outliers,
    ROUND((SELECT StdDev FROM Stats), 2) AS std_without_outliers
FROM 
    ZScores
WHERE 
    ABS(ZScore) <= 3;

samples.question: Perform feature engineering by creating a new feature called "STEM" (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math). It should be the sum of the percentages of graduates in the fields of Computer Science, Engineering, Math and Statistics, and Physical Sciences. Calculate the mean and range (maximum - minimum) of the "STEM" feature for the years beyond 2000.
samples.constraints:
Calculate the new feature "STEM" as the sum of the percentages of graduates in the fields of Computer Science, Engineering, Math and Statistics, and Physical Sciences.
Compute the mean and the range (maximum - minimum) of the "STEM" feature for the years 2000 and beyond. Round to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Computer_Science + Engineering + Math_and_Statistics + Physical_Sciences), 2) AS mean_STEM,
    ROUND(MAX(Computer_Science + Engineering + Math_and_Statistics + Physical_Sciences) - MIN(Computer_Science + Engineering + Math_and_Statistics + Physical_Sciences), 2) AS range_STEM
FROM 
    `percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa`
WHERE 
    Year >= 2000;

WITH STEM_Calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        Year,
        (Computer_Science + Engineering + Math_and_Statistics + Physical_Sciences) AS STEM
    FROM 
        `percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa`
    WHERE 
        Year >= 2000
),
STEM_Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(STEM), 2) AS mean_STEM,
        ROUND(MAX(STEM) - MIN(STEM), 2) AS range_STEM
    FROM 
        STEM_Calculation
)
SELECT 
    mean_STEM, 
    range_STEM 
FROM 
    STEM_Stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(STEM), 2) AS mean_STEM, 
    ROUND(MAX(STEM) - MIN(STEM), 2) AS range_STEM
FROM (
    SELECT 
        Year, 
        (Computer_Science + Engineering + Math_and_Statistics + Physical_Sciences) AS STEM
    FROM 
        percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa
    WHERE 
        Year >= 2000
) AS STEM_data;

WITH STEM_Calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        Year,
        ROUND(Computer_Science + Engineering + Math_and_Statistics + Physical_Sciences, 2) AS STEM
    FROM 
        `percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa`
    WHERE 
        Year >= 2000
),
STEM_Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(STEM), 2) AS mean_STEM,
        ROUND(MAX(STEM) - MIN(STEM), 2) AS range_STEM
    FROM 
        STEM_Calculation
)
SELECT 
    mean_STEM, 
    range_STEM 
FROM 
    STEM_Stats;

WITH STEM_Calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        Year,
        COALESCE(Computer_Science, 0) + 
        COALESCE(Engineering, 0) + 
        COALESCE(Math_and_Statistics, 0) + 
        COALESCE(Physical_Sciences, 0) AS STEM
    FROM 
        `percent-bachelors-degrees-women-usa`
    WHERE 
        Year >= 2000 AND 
        Computer_Science IS NOT NULL AND 
        Engineering IS NOT NULL AND 
        Math_and_Statistics IS NOT NULL AND 
        Physical_Sciences IS NOT NULL
)

SELECT 
    ROUND(MAX(STEM) - MIN(STEM), 2) AS range_STEM,
    ROUND(AVG(STEM), 2) AS mean_STEM
FROM 
    STEM_Calculation;

samples.question: 1. What is the average number of reviews per hotel in the dataset?
samples.constraints: Calculate the mean reviews per hotel from the entire dataset. Round your result to two decimal places.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(review_count), 2) AS average_reviews FROM hotel_data;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(review_count), 2) AS average_reviews FROM (SELECT review_count FROM hotel_data);

WITH review_counts AS (
    SELECT COALESCE(review_count, 0) AS review_count
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE review_count IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT ROUND(CAST(SUM(review_count) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(COUNT(review_count), 0), 2) AS average_reviews
FROM review_counts;

samples.question: 2. Are there any hotels in the dataset that have a star rating of 5? If yes, how many hotels have a star rating of 5?
samples.constraints: Count only hotels that have a star rating exactly equal to 5. This count value should be a non-negative integer.

variants.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_hotels FROM hotel_data WHERE star_rating = 5;

WITH five_star_hotels AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_hotels
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE star_rating = 5
)
SELECT total_hotels
FROM five_star_hotels;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_hotels 
FROM (SELECT hotel_id 
      FROM hotel_data 
      WHERE star_rating = 5) AS subquery;

WITH hotel_count AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_hotels
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE star_rating = 5
)
SELECT ROUND(total_hotels, 0) AS total_hotels
FROM hotel_count;

WITH hotel_count AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_hotels
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE COALESCE(star_rating, 0) = 5
)
SELECT COALESCE(total_hotels, 0) AS total_hotels
FROM hotel_count;

samples.question: 3. Is there a correlation between the number of reviews a hotel has received and its bubble score? If yes, what is the correlation coefficient?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient between review count and bubble score. Consider the correlation to be significant if its absolute value is greater than 0.5. Round your result to three decimal places.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(CORR(review_count, bubble_score), 3) AS correlation_coefficient FROM hotel_data;

WITH review_bubble AS (
    SELECT 
        review_count,
        bubble_score
    FROM 
        hotel_data
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(review_count) AS sum_x,
        SUM(bubble_score) AS sum_y,
        SUM(review_count * bubble_score) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(review_count * review_count) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(bubble_score * bubble_score) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        review_bubble
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

SELECT ROUND((SUM(review_count * bubble_score) - (SUM(review_count) * SUM(bubble_score) / COUNT(*))) / 
               (SQRT(SUM(review_count * review_count) - (SUM(review_count) * SUM(review_count) / COUNT(*))) * 
                SQRT(SUM(bubble_score * bubble_score) - (SUM(bubble_score) * SUM(bubble_score) / COUNT(*)))), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM hotel_data;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(review_count) AS n,
        SUM(review_count) AS sum_x,
        SUM(bubble_score) AS sum_y,
        SUM(review_count * bubble_score) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(review_count * review_count) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(bubble_score * bubble_score) AS sum_yy
    FROM hotel_data
), correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

WITH review_bubble AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(review_count, 0) AS review_count,
        COALESCE(bubble_score, 0) AS bubble_score
    FROM 
        hotel_data
    WHERE 
        review_count IS NOT NULL 
        AND bubble_score IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(review_count) AS sum_x,
        SUM(bubble_score) AS sum_y,
        SUM(review_count * bubble_score) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(review_count * review_count) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(bubble_score * bubble_score) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        review_bubble
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation;

samples.question: 2. Which hotel brand has the highest average star rating among hotels with at least 100 reviews?
samples.constraints: Only consider hotel brands that have at least 10 hotels in the dataset. Do not include hotels without a brand or without a star rating in the calculation. If there is a tie, return the brand with the largest number of hotels in the dataset. Calculate the average using Arithmetic Mean (Sum of observations / Number of observations).

variants.sql:
SELECT brand_name, 
       ROUND(AVG(star_rating), 2) AS average_star_rating, 
       COUNT(hotel_id) AS hotel_count
FROM hotel_data
WHERE review_count >= 100 
  AND brand_name IS NOT NULL 
  AND star_rating IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY brand_name
HAVING COUNT(hotel_id) >= 10
ORDER BY average_star_rating DESC, hotel_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

WITH brand_counts AS (
    SELECT brand_name, COUNT(*) AS hotel_count
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE brand_name IS NOT NULL AND star_rating IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY brand_name
    HAVING hotel_count >= 10
), filtered_hotels AS (
    SELECT brand_name, star_rating
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE brand_name IS NOT NULL AND star_rating IS NOT NULL AND review_count >= 100
), average_ratings AS (
    SELECT brand_name, ROUND(AVG(star_rating), 2) AS avg_star_rating
    FROM filtered_hotels
    GROUP BY brand_name
), ranked_brands AS (
    SELECT brand_name, avg_star_rating, 
           RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_star_rating DESC, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hotel_data WHERE brand_name = average_ratings.brand_name) DESC) AS rank
    FROM average_ratings
)
SELECT brand_name AS brand_with_highest_average_star_rating
FROM ranked_brands
WHERE rank = 1;

SELECT brand_name 
FROM (
    SELECT brand_name, 
           AVG(star_rating) AS average_star_rating, 
           COUNT(hotel_id) AS hotel_count 
    FROM hotel_data 
    WHERE review_count >= 100 AND brand_name IS NOT NULL AND star_rating IS NOT NULL 
    GROUP BY brand_name 
    HAVING COUNT(hotel_id) >= 10
) 
ORDER BY average_star_rating DESC, hotel_count DESC 
LIMIT 1;

WITH brand_counts AS (
    SELECT brand_name, COUNT(hotel_id) AS hotel_count
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE brand_name IS NOT NULL AND star_rating IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY brand_name
    HAVING hotel_count >= 10
),
average_ratings AS (
    SELECT brand_name, AVG(star_rating) AS avg_star_rating
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE brand_name IS NOT NULL AND star_rating IS NOT NULL AND review_count >= 100
    GROUP BY brand_name
)
SELECT brand_name
FROM (
    SELECT a.brand_name, a.avg_star_rating, b.hotel_count,
           RANK() OVER (ORDER BY a.avg_star_rating DESC, b.hotel_count DESC) AS rank
    FROM average_ratings a
    JOIN brand_counts b ON a.brand_name = b.brand_name
) ranked_brands
WHERE rank = 1;

WITH filtered_hotels AS (
    SELECT brand_name, star_rating
    FROM hotel_data
