    WHERE star_rating IS NOT NULL AND brand_name IS NOT NULL AND review_count >= 100
),
brand_counts AS (
    SELECT brand_name, COUNT(*) AS hotel_count
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE brand_name IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY brand_name
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10
),
average_ratings AS (
    SELECT f.brand_name, AVG(f.star_rating) AS avg_star_rating
    FROM filtered_hotels f
    JOIN brand_counts b ON f.brand_name = b.brand_name
    GROUP BY f.brand_name
)
SELECT brand_name
FROM average_ratings
ORDER BY avg_star_rating DESC, (SELECT hotel_count FROM brand_counts WHERE brand_counts.brand_name = average_ratings.brand_name) DESC
LIMIT 1;

samples.question: 2. Among the hotels with a star rating, what is the correlation between the number of reviews a hotel has received and its bubble score? Do hotels with higher star ratings tend to have higher bubble scores and more reviews?
samples.constraints: Calculate the correlation coefficient using the Pearson method. Consider only non-null values. Report the correlation separately for hotels with star ratings below 3, between 3 and 4, and above 4.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(review_count, bubble_score) FILTER (WHERE star_rating > 4), 2) AS above4_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(review_count, bubble_score) FILTER (WHERE star_rating < 3), 2) AS below3_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(review_count, bubble_score) FILTER (WHERE star_rating BETWEEN 3 AND 4), 2) AS between3and4_correlation
FROM hotel_data
WHERE star_rating IS NOT NULL AND review_count IS NOT NULL AND bubble_score IS NOT NULL;

WITH filtered_hotels AS (
    SELECT 
        star_rating,
        bubble_score,
        review_count
    FROM 
        hotel_data
    WHERE 
        star_rating IS NOT NULL AND 
        bubble_score IS NOT NULL AND 
        review_count IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN star_rating < 3 THEN 'below3'
            WHEN star_rating >= 3 AND star_rating <= 4 THEN 'between3and4'
            ELSE 'above4'
        END AS rating_category,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(review_count) AS sum_x,
        SUM(bubble_score) AS sum_y,
        SUM(review_count * bubble_score) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(review_count * review_count) AS sum_x2,
        SUM(bubble_score * bubble_score) AS sum_y2
    FROM 
        filtered_hotels
    GROUP BY 
        rating_category
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
    (SQRT((n * sum_x2 - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_y2 - sum_y * sum_y))), 2) AS correlation
FROM 
    correlation_calculation
GROUP BY 
    rating_category;

SELECT 
    (SELECT ROUND(CORR(review_count, bubble_score), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT review_count, bubble_score 
           FROM hotel_data 
           WHERE star_rating > 4 AND review_count IS NOT NULL AND bubble_score IS NOT NULL)) AS above4_correlation,
    (SELECT ROUND(CORR(review_count, bubble_score), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT review_count, bubble_score 
           FROM hotel_data 
           WHERE star_rating < 3 AND review_count IS NOT NULL AND bubble_score IS NOT NULL)) AS below3_correlation,
    (SELECT ROUND(CORR(review_count, bubble_score), 2) 
     FROM (SELECT review_count, bubble_score 
           FROM hotel_data 
           WHERE star_rating BETWEEN 3 AND 4 AND review_count IS NOT NULL AND bubble_score IS NOT NULL)) AS between3and4_correlation;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        review_count,
        bubble_score,
        CASE 
            WHEN star_rating < 3 THEN 'below3'
            WHEN star_rating BETWEEN 3 AND 4 THEN 'between3and4'
            WHEN star_rating > 4 THEN 'above4'
        END AS rating_group
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE star_rating IS NOT NULL AND review_count IS NOT NULL AND bubble_score IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_results AS (
    SELECT 
        rating_group,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(review_count) AS sum_x,
        SUM(bubble_score) AS sum_y,
        SUM(review_count * bubble_score) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(review_count * review_count) AS sum_x2,
        SUM(bubble_score * bubble_score) AS sum_y2
    FROM correlation_data
    GROUP BY rating_group
)
SELECT 
    rating_group,
    ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
          (SQRT(n * sum_x2 - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_y2 - sum_y * sum_y)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation_results;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT
        COALESCE(review_count, 0) AS review_count,
        COALESCE(bubble_score, 0) AS bubble_score,
        CASE 
            WHEN star_rating < 3 THEN 'below3'
            WHEN star_rating BETWEEN 3 AND 4 THEN 'between3and4'
            WHEN star_rating > 4 THEN 'above4'
        END AS rating_group
    FROM hotel_data
    WHERE star_rating IS NOT NULL
      AND review_count IS NOT NULL
      AND bubble_score IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT
        rating_group,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(review_count) AS sum_x,
        SUM(bubble_score) AS sum_y,
        SUM(review_count * bubble_score) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(review_count * review_count) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(bubble_score * bubble_score) AS sum_yy
    FROM filtered_data
    GROUP BY rating_group
)
SELECT
    ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)), 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    rating_group
FROM correlation
ORDER BY rating_group;

samples.question: 3. What is the average review count for hotels in each city? Are there any cities where the average review count is significantly higher or lower compared to the overall average review count of all hotels?
samples.constraints: Calculate the average review count for each city. Compare the results to the overall average review count. Report cities where the average review count is more or less than twice the overall average.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN avg_review_count < overall_avg * 0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lower_city_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN avg_review_count > overall_avg * 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS higher_city_count
FROM (
    SELECT 
        city_name, 
        AVG(review_count) AS avg_review_count,
        (SELECT AVG(review_count) FROM hotel_data) AS overall_avg
    FROM hotel_data
    GROUP BY city_name
) AS city_avg;

WITH city_avg AS (
    SELECT city_name, AVG(review_count) AS avg_review_count
    FROM hotel_data
    GROUP BY city_name
), overall_avg AS (
    SELECT AVG(review_count) AS overall_avg_review_count
    FROM hotel_data
), comparison AS (
    SELECT city_name, avg_review_count,
           CASE 
               WHEN avg_review_count > 2 * overall_avg_review_count THEN 'higher'
               WHEN avg_review_count < overall_avg_review_count / 2 THEN 'lower'
               ELSE 'normal'
           END AS category
    FROM city_avg, overall_avg
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(CASE WHEN category = 'lower' THEN 1 END) AS lower_city_count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN category = 'higher' THEN 1 END) AS higher_city_count
FROM comparison;

WITH city_avg AS (
    SELECT city_name, AVG(review_count) AS avg_review_count
    FROM hotel_data
    GROUP BY city_name
), overall_avg AS (
    SELECT AVG(review_count) AS overall_avg_review_count
    FROM hotel_data
)
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN city_avg.avg_review_count < 2 * overall_avg.overall_avg_review_count THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lower_city_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN city_avg.avg_review_count > 2 * overall_avg.overall_avg_review_count THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS higher_city_count
FROM city_avg, overall_avg;

WITH city_avg AS (
    SELECT 
        city_name, 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(review_count, 0)), 2) AS avg_review_count
    FROM 
        hotel_data
    WHERE 
        city_name IS NOT NULL AND 
        review_count IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        city_name
), overall_avg AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(review_count, 0)), 2) AS overall_avg_review_count
    FROM 
        hotel_data
    WHERE 
        review_count IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN ca.avg_review_count < (2 * oa.overall_avg_review_count) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lower_city_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN ca.avg_review_count > (2 * oa.overall_avg_review_count) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS higher_city_count
FROM 
    city_avg ca, overall_avg oa;

samples.question: Check if the fare distribution is skewed.
samples.constraints: Calculate the skewness of the fare column using Pearson's moment coefficient of skewness. Ignore null values. Round the final output to 2 decimal places.

variants.sql:
WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(Fare) AS n,
        AVG(Fare) AS mean,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
),
fare_skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(POWER(Fare - mean, 3)) / n AS numerator,
        POWER(stddev, 3) * n AS denominator
    FROM titanic, fare_stats
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(numerator / denominator, 2) AS skewness_fare
FROM fare_skewness;

SELECT ROUND((COUNT(Fare) * SUM(POWER(Fare - avg_fare, 3)) / (COUNT(Fare) * SUM(POWER(Fare - avg_fare, 2)) * POWER((COUNT(Fare) - 1), 1.5))), 2) AS skewness_fare
FROM (SELECT Fare, AVG(Fare) OVER () AS avg_fare FROM titanic WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL) AS fare_data;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(Fare) AS n,
        AVG(Fare) AS mean,
        SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) AS m3,
        SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) AS m2
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
),
skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * m3) / (POWER(m2, 1.5)), 2) AS skewness_fare
    FROM fare_stats
)
SELECT skewness_fare FROM skewness;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(COALESCE(Fare, 0)) AS n,
        AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)) AS mean,
        AVG(POWER(COALESCE(Fare, 0) - AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 3)) AS third_moment,
        AVG(POWER(COALESCE(Fare, 0) - AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)), 2)) AS second_moment
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
),
skewness AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN NULLIF(second_moment, 0) IS NOT NULL THEN 
                ROUND((third_moment / POWER(second_moment, 1.5)), 2)
            ELSE 
                NULL 
        END AS skewness_fare
    FROM fare_stats
)
SELECT COALESCE(skewness_fare, 0) AS skewness_fare FROM skewness;

samples.question: Find the correlation coefficient between the passenger class and the fare.
samples.constraints: Calculate the correlation using the Pearson method. Do not include the rows with null values in either Pclass or Fare in the calculation. Round the final output to 2 decimal places.

variants.sql:
WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT Pclass, Fare
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Pclass) AS avg_pclass,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        SUM((Pclass - AVG(Pclass)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) AS cov,
        SUM((Pclass - AVG(Pclass)) * (Pclass - AVG(Pclass))) AS var_pclass,
        SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare)) * (Fare - AVG(Fare))) AS var_fare
    FROM valid_data
)
SELECT ROUND(cov / SQRT(var_pclass * var_fare), 2) AS correlation_pclass_fare
FROM stats;

SELECT ROUND(CORR(Pclass, Fare), 2) AS correlation_pclass_fare
FROM (SELECT Pclass, Fare FROM titanic WHERE Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL);

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Pclass) AS sum_x,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_y,
        SUM(Pclass * Fare) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(Pclass * Pclass) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_yy
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        sum_x,
        sum_y,
        sum_xy,
        sum_xx,
        sum_yy,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_pclass_fare
FROM correlation;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(Pclass AS FLOAT) AS pclass,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS fare
    FROM 
        titanic
    WHERE 
        Pclass IS NOT NULL 
        AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
mean_values AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(pclass) AS mean_pclass,
        AVG(fare) AS mean_fare
    FROM 
        valid_data
),
covariance AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM((pclass - mv.mean_pclass) * (fare - mv.mean_fare)) / COUNT(*) AS cov
    FROM 
        valid_data vd,
        mean_values mv
),
variance AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(POWER(pclass - mv.mean_pclass, 2)) / COUNT(*) AS var_pclass,
        SUM(POWER(fare - mv.mean_fare, 2)) / COUNT(*) AS var_fare
    FROM 
        valid_data vd,
        mean_values mv
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(cov.cov / NULLIF(sq.var_pclass * sq.var_fare, 0), 2) AS correlation_pclass_fare
FROM 
    covariance cov, 
    variance sq;

samples.question: Identify and remove any outliers in the fare column using the Z-score method.
samples.constraints: Calculate the Z-scores for the 'Fare' column values and classify a value as an outlier if its Z-score is greater than 3. After removal of outliers, calculate the number of entries left in the dataset.

variants.sql:
WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Fare) AS mean_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) AS stddev_fare
    FROM titanic
),
fare_z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Fare,
        (Fare - (SELECT mean_fare FROM fare_stats)) / (SELECT stddev_fare FROM fare_stats) AS z_score
    FROM titanic
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS number_of_entries_left
FROM fare_z_scores
WHERE ABS(z_score) <= 3;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS number_of_entries_left
FROM (
    SELECT Fare,
           (Fare - (SELECT AVG(Fare) FROM titanic)) / (SELECT STDEV(Fare) FROM titanic) AS z_score
    FROM titanic
) AS fare_z_scores
WHERE ABS(z_score) <= 3;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        AVG(Fare) OVER () AS avg_fare,
        STDDEV(Fare) OVER () AS stddev_fare
    FROM 
        titanic
),
fare_z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        (Fare - avg_fare) / stddev_fare AS z_score
    FROM 
        fare_stats
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS number_of_entries_left
FROM 
    fare_z_scores
WHERE 
    ABS(z_score) <= 3;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(COALESCE(Fare, 0)) AS mean_fare,
        STDDEV(COALESCE(Fare, 0)) AS stddev_fare
    FROM 
        titanic
    WHERE 
        Fare IS NOT NULL
), fare_z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Fare,
        (COALESCE(Fare, 0) - mean_fare) / NULLIF(stddev_fare, 0) AS z_score
    FROM 
        titanic, fare_stats
    WHERE 
        Fare IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS number_of_entries_left
FROM 
    fare_z_scores
WHERE 
    z_score IS NOT NULL AND ABS(z_score) <= 3;

samples.question: Create a new feature called 'FamilySize' by combining the 'SibSp' and 'Parch' columns, which represents the total number of family members a passenger had aboard the Titanic. Then, find the correlation coefficient between 'FamilySize' and 'Survived'.
samples.constraints: Create 'FamilySize' by adding up 'SibSp' and 'Parch', then calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient between 'FamilySize' and 'Survived'.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(CORR(SibSp + Parch, Survived), 2) AS correlation_coefficient FROM titanic;

WITH FamilyData AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        (SibSp + Parch) AS FamilySize
    FROM 
        titanic
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(FamilySize) AS avg_family_size,
        AVG(Survived) AS avg_survived,
        SUM((FamilySize - AVG(FamilySize)) * (Survived - AVG(Survived))) AS covariance,
        SUM((FamilySize - AVG(FamilySize)) * (FamilySize - AVG(FamilySize))) AS variance_family_size,
        SUM((Survived - AVG(Survived)) * (Survived - AVG(Survived))) AS variance_survived
    FROM 
        FamilyData
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(covariance / SQRT(variance_family_size * variance_survived), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    Stats;

SELECT ROUND(AVG((FamilySize - avg_family_size) * (Survived - avg_survived)) / (SQRT(AVG(POWER(FamilySize - avg_family_size, 2)) * AVG(POWER(Survived - avg_survived, 2)))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (SibSp + Parch) AS FamilySize,
        Survived,
        (SELECT AVG(SibSp + Parch) FROM titanic) AS avg_family_size,
        (SELECT AVG(Survived) FROM titanic) AS avg_survived
    FROM titanic
) AS subquery;

WITH FamilyData AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        (SibSp + Parch) AS FamilySize
    FROM 
        titanic
),
Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(FamilySize) AS avg_family_size,
        AVG(Survived) AS avg_survived,
        SUM((FamilySize - AVG(FamilySize) OVER()) * (Survived - AVG(Survived) OVER())) AS covariance,
        SUM((FamilySize - AVG(FamilySize) OVER()) * (FamilySize - AVG(FamilySize) OVER())) AS variance_family_size,
        SUM((Survived - AVG(Survived) OVER()) * (Survived - AVG(Survived) OVER())) AS variance_survived
    FROM 
        FamilyData
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(covariance / SQRT(variance_family_size * variance_survived), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    Stats;

WITH FamilyData AS (
    SELECT
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        COALESCE(SibSp, 0) + COALESCE(Parch, 0) AS FamilySize
    FROM
        titanic
    WHERE
        Survived IS NOT NULL
        AND SibSp IS NOT NULL
        AND Parch IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
    ROUND(CORR(CAST(FamilySize AS FLOAT), CAST(Survived AS FLOAT)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM
    FamilyData
WHERE
    FamilySize IS NOT NULL;

samples.question: Using machine learning algorithms, build a classification model to predict survival (0 = No, 1 = Yes) based on the passenger's age, gender, and fare. Train a logistic regression model with default parameters provided by the sklearn library. Evaluate the model's performance using accuracy as the evaluation metric.
samples.constraints: Split the dataset into a training set and a test set with a ratio of 70:30 using sklearn's train_test_split function with a random_state of 42. Don't balance the classes or perform any other preprocessing that isn't mentioned.

variants.sql:
WITH survival_data AS (
    SELECT 
        Survived,
        Age,
        Sex,
        Fare
    FROM 
        titanic
    WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
encoded_data AS (
    SELECT 
        Survived,
        Age,
        CASE 
            WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS Sex_encoded,
        Fare
    FROM 
        survival_data
),
train_test_split AS (
    SELECT 
        *,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM 
        encoded_data
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT 
        Survived, Age, Sex_encoded, Fare
    FROM 
        train_test_split
    WHERE 
        rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM train_test_split)
),
test_set AS (
    SELECT 
        Survived, Age, Sex_encoded, Fare
    FROM 
        train_test_split
    WHERE 
        rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM train_test_split)
),
accuracy_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN t.Survived = p.Survived THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 2) AS classifier_accuracy
    FROM 
        test_set t
    JOIN 
        (SELECT 
            Survived,
            CASE 
                WHEN Age < 30 AND Sex_encoded = 0 AND Fare < 20 THEN 0
                ELSE 1 
            END AS Survived
         FROM 
            train_set) p ON t.Age = p.Age AND t.Sex_encoded = p.Sex_encoded AND t.Fare = p.Fare
)
SELECT 
    classifier_accuracy 
FROM 
    accuracy_calculation;

SELECT ROUND(AVG(Survived), 2) AS classifier_accuracy
FROM (
    SELECT Survived
    FROM titanic
    WHERE PassengerId IN (
        SELECT PassengerId
        FROM titanic
        ORDER BY RANDOM()
        LIMIT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic) * 0.7
    )
) AS training_set;

WITH model_data AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE WHEN Survived = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS survived,
        Age,
        Fare,
        Sex,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
train_data AS (
    SELECT survived, Age, Fare, Sex
    FROM model_data
    WHERE rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM model_data)
),
test_data AS (
    SELECT survived, Age, Fare, Sex
    FROM model_data
    WHERE rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 0.7 FROM model_data)
),
accuracy AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN t.survived = p.survived THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS classifier_accuracy
    FROM test_data t
    JOIN train_data p ON t.Age = p.Age AND t.Fare = p.Fare AND t.Sex = p.Sex
)
SELECT classifier_accuracy FROM accuracy;

WITH filtered_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Age, 0) AS Age,
        CASE 
            WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN 1 
            WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN 0 
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS Gender,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare,
        Survived
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND 
          Sex IS NOT NULL AND 
          Fare IS NOT NULL
),
train_set AS (
    SELECT *, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY RANDOM()) AS rn
    FROM filtered_data
),
train AS (
    SELECT Age, Gender, Fare, Survived
    FROM train_set
    WHERE rn <= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM filtered_data) * 0.7
),
test AS (
    SELECT Age, Gender, Fare, Survived
    FROM train_set
    WHERE rn > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM filtered_data) * 0.7
),
accuracy AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Survived = p.Survived THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS classifier_accuracy
    FROM test t
    JOIN train p ON t.Age = p.Age AND t.Gender = p.Gender AND t.Fare = p.Fare
)
SELECT classifier_accuracy FROM accuracy;

samples.question: Perform feature engineering by creating a new feature called 'Title' from the 'Name' column, which represents the title (e.g., Mr., Mrs., Miss) of each passenger. Then, analyze the distribution of the 'Title' feature and check if it is correlated with the passenger class ('Pclass') using the chi-square test.
samples.constraints: Generate 'Title' by extracting the title before the period (.) in the 'Name' column, and the title is defined as a string that contains no spaces. For others which are not 'Mr.', 'Mrs.', 'Miss.', replace them with 'Other'. The degrees of freedom for the chi-square test are calculated as (r - 1) * (c - 1), where r equals the number of rows (categories in 'Title') and c equals the number of columns (categories in 'Pclass'). Use a significance level of 0.05.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE Title = 'Mr.') * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE Pclass = 1) / COUNT(*), 4) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr.%' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs.%' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss.%' THEN 'Miss'
            ELSE 'Other'
        END AS Title,
        Pclass
    FROM titanic
) AS titles;

WITH ExtractedTitles AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr.%' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs.%' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss.%' THEN 'Miss'
            ELSE 'Other'
        END AS Title
    FROM titanic
),
TitleDistribution AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        COUNT(*) AS Count
    FROM ExtractedTitles
    GROUP BY Title
),
PclassDistribution AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        Pclass,
        COUNT(*) AS Count
    FROM ExtractedTitles
    GROUP BY Title, Pclass
),
ChiSquareTable AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        Pclass,
        Count,
        (SELECT SUM(Count) FROM PclassDistribution WHERE Title = p.Title) AS RowTotal,
        (SELECT SUM(Count) FROM PclassDistribution WHERE Pclass = p.Pclass) AS ColTotal
    FROM PclassDistribution p
),
ExpectedValues AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        Pclass,
        ROUND((RowTotal * ColTotal) / (SELECT SUM(Count) FROM TitleDistribution), 2) AS Expected
    FROM ChiSquareTable
),
ChiSquareCalc AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        Pclass,
        SUM((Count - Expected) * (Count - Expected) / Expected) AS ChiSquare
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            c.Title,
            c.Pclass,
            c.Count,
            e.Expected
        FROM ChiSquareTable c
        JOIN ExpectedValues e ON c.Title = e.Title AND c.Pclass = e.Pclass
    )
    GROUP BY Title, Pclass
),
TotalChiSquare AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(ChiSquare) AS TotalChiSquare
    FROM ChiSquareCalc
),
DegreesOfFreedom AS (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(DISTINCT Title) - 1) * (COUNT(DISTINCT Pclass) - 1) AS DoF
    FROM ExtractedTitles
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(TotalChiSquare.TotalChiSquare / (SELECT DoF FROM DegreesOfFreedom), 4) AS p_value
FROM TotalChiSquare;

SELECT ROUND(CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN Title = 'Mr.' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(*), 4) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr.%' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs.%' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss.%' THEN 'Miss'
            ELSE 'Other'
        END AS Title,
        Pclass
    FROM titanic
) AS subquery;

WITH TitleExtraction AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr.%' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs.%' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss.%' THEN 'Miss'
            ELSE 'Other'
        END AS Title
    FROM titanic
),
TitlePclassCount AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        Pclass,
        COUNT(*) AS Count
    FROM TitleExtraction
    GROUP BY Title, Pclass
),
ChiSquare AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        SUM(Count) AS TotalPerTitle,
        SUM(CASE WHEN Pclass = 1 THEN Count ELSE 0 END) AS CountPclass1,
        SUM(CASE WHEN Pclass = 2 THEN Count ELSE 0 END) AS CountPclass2,
        SUM(CASE WHEN Pclass = 3 THEN Count ELSE 0 END) AS CountPclass3
    FROM TitlePclassCount
    GROUP BY Title
),
ExpectedCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        TotalPerTitle,
        (TotalPerTitle * (SUM(CountPclass1) OVER ()) / (SUM(TotalPerTitle) OVER ())) AS ExpectedCountPclass1,
        (TotalPerTitle * (SUM(CountPclass2) OVER ()) / (SUM(TotalPerTitle) OVER ())) AS ExpectedCountPclass2,
        (TotalPerTitle * (SUM(CountPclass3) OVER ()) / (SUM(TotalPerTitle) OVER ())) AS ExpectedCountPclass3
    FROM ChiSquare
),
ChiSquareCalc AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(POWER(CountPclass1 - ExpectedCountPclass1, 2) / ExpectedCountPclass1) +
        SUM(POWER(CountPclass2 - ExpectedCountPclass2, 2) / ExpectedCountPclass2) +
        SUM(POWER(CountPclass3 - ExpectedCountPclass3, 2) / ExpectedCountPclass3 AS ChiSquareValue
    FROM ChiSquare
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(ChiSquareValue, 4) AS p_value
FROM ChiSquareCalc;

WITH Titles AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        CASE 
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mr. %' THEN 'Mr.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Mrs. %' THEN 'Mrs.'
            WHEN Name LIKE '%Miss. %' THEN 'Miss.'
            ELSE 'Other'
        END AS Title
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Name IS NOT NULL
),
TitleDistribution AS (
    SELECT Title, COUNT(*) AS Count
    FROM Titles
    GROUP BY Title
),
PclassDistribution AS (
    SELECT Pclass, COUNT(*) AS Count
    FROM Titles
    GROUP BY Pclass
),
ChiSquare AS (
    SELECT 
        t.Title,
        p.Pclass,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Mr.' AND p.Pclass = 1 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Mr_Pclass1,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Mr.' AND p.Pclass = 2 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Mr_Pclass2,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Mr.' AND p.Pclass = 3 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Mr_Pclass3,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Mrs.' AND p.Pclass = 1 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Mrs_Pclass1,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Mrs.' AND p.Pclass = 2 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Mrs_Pclass2,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Mrs.' AND p.Pclass = 3 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Mrs_Pclass3,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Miss.' AND p.Pclass = 1 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Miss_Pclass1,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Miss.' AND p.Pclass = 2 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Miss_Pclass2,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Miss.' AND p.Pclass = 3 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Miss_Pclass3,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Other' AND p.Pclass = 1 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Other_Pclass1,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Other' AND p.Pclass = 2 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Other_Pclass2,
        COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN t.Title = 'Other' AND p.Pclass = 3 THEN 1 END), 0) AS Observed_Other_Pclass3
    FROM Titles t
    CROSS JOIN PclassDistribution p
    GROUP BY t.Title, p.Pclass
),
ExpectedCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        Pclass,
        ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(Count), 0) * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Titles) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM PclassDistribution), 2) AS Expected
    FROM TitleDistribution
    CROSS JOIN PclassDistribution
    GROUP BY Title, Pclass
),
ChiSquareResults AS (
    SELECT 
        Title,
        Pclass,
        (POW((Observed_Mr_Pclass1 - Expected), 2) / NULLIF(Expected, 0)) AS ChiSquare_Mr_Pclass1,
        (POW((Observed_Mr_Pclass2 - Expected), 2) / NULLIF(Expected, 0)) AS ChiSquare_Mr_Pclass2,
        (POW((Observed_Mr_Pclass3 - Expected), 2) / NULLIF(Expected, 0)) AS ChiSquare_Mr_Pclass3
    FROM ChiSquare
    JOIN ExpectedCounts ON ChiSquare.Title = ExpectedCounts.Title AND ChiSquare.Pclass = ExpectedCounts.Pclass
),
FinalChiSquare AS (
    SELECT SUM(ChiSquare_Mr_Pclass1 + ChiSquare_Mr_Pclass2 + ChiSquare_Mr_Pclass3) AS ChiSquareStat
    FROM ChiSquareResults
)
SELECT ROUND(ChiSquareStat, 4) AS p_value
FROM FinalChiSquare;

samples.question: Preprocess the dataset by using comprehensive data preprocessing techniques, including cleaning, transformation, and handling missing values. Remove duplicate rows, normalize the 'Fare' column by scaling between 0 and 1, impute missing values in the 'Age' column using k-Nearest Neighbors algorithm with k=3, and drop the 'Cabin' column due to high missing values. Finally, create a new feature called 'AgeGroup' by binning the passengers into different age groups: 'Child' (age<=12), 'Teenager' (12<age<=18), 'Adult' (18<age<=60) and 'Senior' (age>60). Report the number of passengers in each category.
samples.constraints: Use sklearn's MinMaxScaler to normalize the 'Fare' column. For the 'Age' column, use sklearn's KNeighborsClassifier to make imputations, and learn the nearest neighbors on the 'Fare' and 'Pclass' columns. The 'AgeGroup' category should be a string of 'Child', 'Teenager', 'Adult' or 'Senior' based on the age of the passenger.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN Age <= 12 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS child_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN Age > 60 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS senior_count
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        PassengerId, 
        Survived, 
        Pclass, 
        Name, 
        Sex, 
        COALESCE(Age, ROUND(AVG(Age) OVER (PARTITION BY Fare, Pclass))) AS Age, 
        SibSp, 
        Parch, 
        Ticket, 
        (Fare - MIN(Fare) OVER ()) / (MAX(Fare) OVER () - MIN(Fare) OVER ()) AS Fare,
        Embarked
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Cabin IS NULL
) AS processed_data;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        PassengerId, 
        Survived, 
        Pclass, 
        Name, 
        Sex, 
        COALESCE(Age, AVG(Age) OVER()) AS Age, 
        SibSp, 
        Parch, 
        Ticket, 
        Fare, 
        Embarked
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Cabin IS NULL
), normalized_data AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId, 
        Survived, 
        Pclass, 
        Name, 
        Sex, 
        Age, 
        SibSp, 
        Parch, 
        Ticket, 
        ROUND((Fare - MIN(Fare) OVER()) / (MAX(Fare) OVER() - MIN(Fare) OVER()), 2) AS Fare, 
        Embarked
    FROM cleaned_data
), age_grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        PassengerId, 
        Survived, 
        Pclass, 
        Name, 
        Sex, 
        Age, 
        SibSp, 
        Parch, 
        Ticket, 
        Fare, 
        Embarked,
        CASE 
            WHEN Age <= 12 THEN 'Child'
            WHEN Age > 12 AND Age <= 18 THEN 'Teenager'
            WHEN Age > 18 AND Age <= 60 THEN 'Adult'
            ELSE 'Senior'
        END AS AgeGroup
    FROM normalized_data
)
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Child' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS child_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Senior' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS senior_count
FROM age_grouped;

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Child' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS child_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Senior' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS senior_count
FROM (
    SELECT 
        DISTINCT PassengerId, 
        Survived, 
        Pclass, 
        Name, 
        Sex, 
        COALESCE(Age, (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL AND Pclass IS NOT NULL)) AS Age,
        SibSp, 
        Parch, 
        Ticket, 
        ROUND((Fare - (SELECT MIN(Fare) FROM titanic)) / (SELECT (MAX(Fare) - MIN(Fare)) FROM titanic), 2) AS Fare,
        Embarked,
        CASE 
            WHEN COALESCE(Age, (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL AND Pclass IS NOT NULL)) <= 12 THEN 'Child'
            WHEN COALESCE(Age, (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL AND Pclass IS NOT NULL)) <= 18 THEN 'Teenager'
            WHEN COALESCE(Age, (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL AND Pclass IS NOT NULL)) <= 60 THEN 'Adult'
            ELSE 'Senior' 
        END AS AgeGroup
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Cabin IS NULL
) AS processed_data;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        Name,
        Sex,
        COALESCE(Age, AVG(Age) OVER (PARTITION BY Fare, Pclass)) AS Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Ticket,
        (Fare - MIN(Fare) OVER ()) / (MAX(Fare) OVER () - MIN(Fare) OVER ()) AS Fare,
        Embarked
    FROM titanic
    WHERE Cabin IS NULL
),
age_grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN Age <= 12 THEN 'Child'
            WHEN Age > 12 AND Age <= 18 THEN 'Teenager'
            WHEN Age > 18 AND Age <= 60 THEN 'Adult'
            ELSE 'Senior'
        END AS AgeGroup
    FROM cleaned_data
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Child' THEN 1 END) AS child_count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Senior' THEN 1 END) AS senior_count
FROM age_grouped;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        Name,
        Sex,
        COALESCE(Age, 
            (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM titanic WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL AND Pclass IS NOT NULL)) AS Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Ticket,
        Fare,
        Embarked
    FROM titanic
    WHERE PassengerId IS NOT NULL
),
normalized_data AS (
    SELECT
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        Name,
        Sex,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Ticket,
        ROUND((Fare - MIN(Fare) OVER()) / NULLIF((MAX(Fare) OVER() - MIN(Fare) OVER()), 0), 2) AS NormalizedFare,
        Embarked
    FROM cleaned_data
),
age_grouped AS (
    SELECT
        PassengerId,
        Survived,
        Pclass,
        Name,
        Sex,
        Age,
        SibSp,
        Parch,
        Ticket,
        NormalizedFare,
        Embarked,
        CASE 
            WHEN Age <= 12 THEN 'Child'
            WHEN Age > 12 AND Age <= 18 THEN 'Teenager'
            WHEN Age > 18 AND Age <= 60 THEN 'Adult'
            WHEN Age > 60 THEN 'Senior'
            ELSE 'Unknown'
        END AS AgeGroup
    FROM normalized_data
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Child' THEN 1 END) AS child_count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN AgeGroup = 'Senior' THEN 1 END) AS senior_count
FROM age_grouped
WHERE Age IS NOT NULL;

samples.question: Is there a correlation between the passenger class and the fare paid?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between 'Pclass' and 'Fare'. Ignore rows with missing values in these two columns. Round the result to two decimal places.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(CORR(Pclass, Fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient FROM titanic_test WHERE Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT Pclass, Fare
    FROM titanic_test
    WHERE Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Pclass) AS avg_pclass,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        SUM(Pclass * Fare) AS sum_pclass_fare,
        SUM(Pclass * Pclass) AS sum_pclass_sq,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq
    FROM valid_data
)
SELECT ROUND(
    (n * sum_pclass_fare - SUM(Pclass) * SUM(Fare)) /
    (SQRT(n * sum_pclass_sq - SUM(Pclass) * SUM(Pclass)) * SQRT(n * sum_fare_sq - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare))),
    2
) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM stats;

SELECT ROUND(CORR(Pclass, Fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM (SELECT Pclass, Fare 
      FROM titanic_test 
      WHERE Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL);

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        Fare,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(Pclass) OVER () AS avg_pclass,
        AVG(Fare) OVER () AS avg_fare,
        SUM((Pclass - AVG(Pclass) OVER ()) * (Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER ())) AS cov,
        SUM((Pclass - AVG(Pclass) OVER ()) * (Pclass - AVG(Pclass) OVER ())) AS var_pclass,
        SUM((Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER ()) * (Fare - AVG(Fare) OVER ())) AS var_fare
    FROM 
        titanic_test
    WHERE 
        Pclass IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(SUM(cov) / SQRT(SUM(var_pclass) * SUM(var_fare)), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    stats;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass, 
        Fare 
    FROM 
        titanic_test 
    WHERE 
        Pclass IS NOT NULL AND 
        Fare IS NOT NULL
), 
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Pclass) AS sum_pclass,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        AVG(Pclass) AS avg_pclass,
        AVG(Fare) AS avg_fare,
        SUM(Pclass * Fare) AS sum_pclass_fare,
        SUM(Pclass * Pclass) AS sum_pclass_sq,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq
    FROM 
        valid_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(
        (n * sum_pclass_fare - sum_pclass * sum_fare) /
        (SQRT(n * sum_pclass_sq - sum_pclass * sum_pclass) * SQRT(n * sum_fare_sq - sum_fare * sum_fare)),
        2
    ) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    stats
WHERE 
    n > 0 AND 
    NULLIF(n * sum_pclass_sq - sum_pclass * sum_pclass, 0) IS NOT NULL AND 
    NULLIF(n * sum_fare_sq - sum_fare * sum_fare, 0) IS NOT NULL;

samples.question: What is the average age of male passengers in each passenger class? How does it compare to the average age of female passengers in each passenger class?
samples.constraints: Determine the average age by using all the non-null age data for male and female passengers in each passenger class. Use the arithmetic mean formula for your calculation. The output should include the average age for males and females in each of passenger classes 1, 2, and 3.

variants.sql:
SELECT Pclass, 
       ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN Age END), 2) AS average_age_male, 
       ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN Age END), 2) AS average_age_female 
FROM titanic_test 
WHERE Age IS NOT NULL 
GROUP BY Pclass;

WITH male_avg AS (
    SELECT Pclass, ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) AS average_age_male
    FROM titanic_test
    WHERE Sex = 'male' AND Age IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY Pclass
),
female_avg AS (
    SELECT Pclass, ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) AS average_age_female
    FROM titanic_test
    WHERE Sex = 'female' AND Age IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY Pclass
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(male_avg.average_age_male, 0) AS average_age_male_class1,
    COALESCE(female_avg.average_age_female, 0) AS average_age_female_class1,
    COALESCE(male_avg.average_age_male, 0) AS average_age_male_class2,
    COALESCE(female_avg.average_age_female, 0) AS average_age_female_class2,
    COALESCE(male_avg.average_age_male, 0) AS average_age_male_class3,
    COALESCE(female_avg.average_age_female, 0) AS average_age_female_class3
FROM male_avg
FULL OUTER JOIN female_avg ON male_avg.Pclass = female_avg.Pclass
ORDER BY male_avg.Pclass;

SELECT 
    (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male' AND Pclass = 1) AS average_age_male_class1,
    (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female' AND Pclass = 1) AS average_age_female_class1,
    (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male' AND Pclass = 2) AS average_age_male_class2,
    (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female' AND Pclass = 2) AS average_age_female_class2,
    (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male' AND Pclass = 3) AS average_age_male_class3,
    (SELECT ROUND(AVG(Age), 2) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female' AND Pclass = 3) AS average_age_female_class3;

WITH avg_age AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        Sex,
        AVG(Age) AS average_age
    FROM 
        titanic_test
    WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass, Sex
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN average_age END), 2) AS average_age_male_class1,
    ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' AND Pclass = 1 THEN average_age END), 2) AS average_age_female_class1,
    ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' AND Pclass = 2 THEN average_age END), 2) AS average_age_male_class2,
    ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' AND Pclass = 2 THEN average_age END), 2) AS average_age_female_class2,
    ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' AND Pclass = 3 THEN average_age END), 2) AS average_age_male_class3,
    ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' AND Pclass = 3 THEN average_age END), 2) AS average_age_female_class3
FROM 
    avg_age
GROUP BY 
    Pclass;

WITH average_ages AS (
    SELECT 
        Pclass,
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN Age END, 0)), 2) AS average_age_male,
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN Age END, 0)), 2) AS average_age_female
    FROM 
        titanic_test
    WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL
        AND Sex IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        Pclass
)
SELECT 
    average_age_male AS average_age_male_class1,
    (SELECT average_age_male FROM average_ages WHERE Pclass = 1) AS average_age_male_class1,
    (SELECT average_age_male FROM average_ages WHERE Pclass = 2) AS average_age_male_class2,
    (SELECT average_age_male FROM average_ages WHERE Pclass = 3) AS average_age_male_class3,
    (SELECT average_age_female FROM average_ages WHERE Pclass = 1) AS average_age_female_class1,
    (SELECT average_age_female FROM average_ages WHERE Pclass = 2) AS average_age_female_class2,
    (SELECT average_age_female FROM average_ages WHERE Pclass = 3) AS average_age_female_class3
FROM 
    average_ages
WHERE 
    Pclass = 1;

samples.question: Are there any outliers in the fare paid by the passengers? If so, how many are there and can you identify them?
samples.constraints: Identify outliers using the IQR method where a fare is considered an outlier if it is 1.5 times the IQR above the third quartile or below the first quartile. Use all fare values for this analysis and do not consider the outlier if it's missing.

variants.sql:
SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(PassengerId) AS outlier_ids, COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM titanic_test
WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL AND (Fare < (SELECT AVG(Fare) - 1.5 * (SELECT P75 - P25 FROM (SELECT 
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS P25, 
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS P75 
    FROM titanic_test WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL)) ) 
    OR Fare > (SELECT AVG(Fare) + 1.5 * (SELECT P75 - P25 FROM (SELECT 
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS P25, 
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS P75 
    FROM titanic_test WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL)) )));

WITH fare_data AS (
    SELECT Fare
    FROM titanic_test
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
), 
quartiles AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS Q3
    FROM fare_data
), 
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM quartiles
), 
outliers AS (
    SELECT PassengerId
    FROM titanic_test
    WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL
    AND (Fare < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR FROM iqr) OR Fare > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR FROM iqr))
)
SELECT 
    GROUP_CONCAT(PassengerId) AS outlier_ids,
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM outliers;

SELECT 
    (SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(PassengerId) 
     FROM titanic_test 
     WHERE Fare < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Fare > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)) AS outlier_ids,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) 
     FROM titanic_test 
     WHERE Fare < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Fare > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    (SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS Q3,
        (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare)) AS IQR
     FROM titanic_test 
     WHERE Fare IS NOT NULL) AS stats;

WITH fare_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Fare,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) OVER () AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) OVER () AS Q3
    FROM 
        titanic_test
    WHERE 
        Fare IS NOT NULL
), 
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM 
        fare_stats
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    GROUP_CONCAT(PassengerId) AS outlier_ids,
    COUNT(PassengerId) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    titanic_test
WHERE 
    Fare IS NOT NULL AND 
    (Fare < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR FROM iqr) OR 
     Fare > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR FROM iqr));

WITH fare_data AS (
    SELECT
        PassengerId,
        COALESCE(Fare, 0) AS Fare
    FROM
        titanic_test
    WHERE
        Fare IS NOT NULL
),
quartiles AS (
    SELECT
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY Fare) AS Q3
    FROM
        fare_data
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM
        quartiles
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT
        PassengerId
    FROM
        fare_data,
        iqr
    WHERE
        Fare < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR Fare > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
)
SELECT
    GROUP_CONCAT(PassengerId) AS outlier_ids,
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM
    outliers;

samples.question: Can you identify any patterns or relationships between the number of siblings/spouses each passenger had aboard and the number of parents/children they had aboard?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between the number of siblings/spouses (SibSp) and the number of parents/children (Parch). Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05. Report the p-value associated with the correlation test. Consider the relationship to be linear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.5. Consider the relationship to be nonlinear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is less than 0.5. If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(SibSp, Parch), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(CORR(SibSp, Parch)) >= 0.5 AND PVAL < 0.05 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(CORR(SibSp, Parch)) < 0.5 AND PVAL < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(PVAL, 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    titanic_test;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        SibSp,
        Parch
    FROM 
        titanic_test
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(SibSp) AS sum_x,
        SUM(Parch) AS sum_y,
        SUM(SibSp * Parch) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(SibSp * SibSp) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(Parch * Parch) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    correlation, p_value;

SELECT 
    ROUND(corr(SibSp, Parch), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND(corr(SibSp, Parch), 2) >= 0.5 AND p_value < 0.05 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ROUND(corr(SibSp, Parch), 2) < 0.5 AND p_value < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(SibSp) AS sum_sibsp,
        SUM(Parch) AS sum_parch,
        SUM(SibSp * Parch) AS sum_sibsp_parch,
        SUM(SibSp * SibSp) AS sum_sibsp_squared,
        SUM(Parch * Parch) AS sum_parch_squared,
        (SELECT 
            COUNT(*) 
         FROM titanic_test) AS total_count
    FROM titanic_test
) AS stats,
(
    SELECT 
        1.0 * (n * sum_sibsp_parch - sum_sibsp * sum_parch) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_sibsp_squared - sum_sibsp * sum_sibsp) * 
         SQRT(n * sum_parch_squared - sum_parch * sum_parch)) AS correlation,
        2 * (1 - (1 / (1 + (n - 2) * (1 - (1.0 * (n * sum_sibsp_parch - sum_sibsp * sum_parch) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_sibsp_squared - sum_sibsp * sum_sibsp) * 
         SQRT(n * sum_parch_squared - sum_parch * sum_parch))^2))))) AS p_value
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            COUNT(*) AS n,
            SUM(SibSp) AS sum_sibsp,
            SUM(Parch) AS sum_parch,
            SUM(SibSp * Parch) AS sum_sibsp_parch,
            SUM(SibSp * SibSp) AS sum_sibsp_squared,
            SUM(Parch * Parch) AS sum_parch_squared
        FROM titanic_test
    ) AS inner_stats
) AS correlation_stats;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(SibSp) AS avg_sibsp, 
        AVG(Parch) AS avg_parch,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM 
        titanic_test
),
squared_diffs AS (
    SELECT 
        (SibSp - (SELECT avg_sibsp FROM correlation_data)) * (SibSp - (SELECT avg_sibsp FROM correlation_data)) AS sibsp_diff_sq,
        (Parch - (SELECT avg_parch FROM correlation_data)) * (Parch - (SELECT avg_parch FROM correlation_data)) AS parch_diff_sq,
        (SibSp - (SELECT avg_sibsp FROM correlation_data)) * (Parch - (SELECT avg_parch FROM correlation_data)) AS covar
    FROM 
        titanic_test
),
correlation_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(covar) / (SQRT(SUM(sibsp_diff_sq)) * SQRT(SUM(parch_diff_sq))) AS correlation_coefficient,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM 
        squared_diffs
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 AND (1 - (1 - 0.05) ^ (n - 2)) < 0.05 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 AND (1 - (1 - 0.05) ^ (n - 2)) < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(1 - (1 - 0.05) ^ (n - 2), 4) AS p_value
FROM 
    correlation_stats;

WITH correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(SibSp, 0) AS SibSp,
        COALESCE(Parch, 0) AS Parch
    FROM 
        titanic_test
    WHERE 
        SibSp IS NOT NULL AND 
        Parch IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(SibSp) AS sum_x,
        SUM(Parch) AS sum_y,
        SUM(SibSp * Parch) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(SibSp * SibSp) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(Parch * Parch) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        correlation_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)), 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
        SUM(SibSp) AS total_sibsp,
        SUM(Parch) AS total_parch
    FROM 
        stats
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        CASE 
            WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
            ELSE 'nonlinear'
        END AS relationship_type,
        CASE 
            WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 AND (SELECT n FROM stats) > 2 THEN 0.0000
            ELSE 0.0500
        END AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    relationship_type,
    p_value
FROM 
    p_value_calculation;

samples.question: Is there a correlation between the age of the passengers and the fare paid? How does this correlation differ among male and female passengers?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between age and fare for male and female passengers separately. Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05. Report the p-value associated with the correlation test. Consider the relationship to be linear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.5. Consider the relationship to be nonlinear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is less than 0.5. If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient_male,
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) >= 0.5 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male') > 0 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) < 0.5 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male') > 0 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_male,
    ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient_female,
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) >= 0.5 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female') > 0 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) < 0.5 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female') > 0 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_female,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male') AS p_value_male,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female') AS p_value_female
FROM titanic_test;

WITH male_data AS (
    SELECT Age, Fare
    FROM titanic_test
    WHERE Sex = 'male'
),
female_data AS (
    SELECT Age, Fare
    FROM titanic_test
    WHERE Sex = 'female'
),
correlation_male AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM male_data
),
correlation_female AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(Age, Fare), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM female_data
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN n > 2 THEN 
                2 * (1 - TDIST(ABS(correlation_coefficient) * SQRT((n - 2) / (1 - POWER(correlation_coefficient, 2))), n - 2))
            ELSE 
                NULL 
        END AS p_value_male,
        CASE 
            WHEN n > 2 THEN 
                2 * (1 - TDIST(ABS(correlation_coefficient) * SQRT((n - 2) / (1 - POWER(correlation_coefficient, 2))), n - 2))
            ELSE 
                NULL 
        END AS p_value_female
    FROM correlation_male, correlation_female
)
SELECT 
    correlation_male.correlation_coefficient AS correlation_coefficient_male,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value_male < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_male.correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value_male < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_male.correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_male,
    correlation_female.correlation_coefficient AS correlation_coefficient_female,
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value_female < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_female.correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value_female < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_female.correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_female,
    p_value_male AS p_value_male,
    p_value_female AS p_value_female
FROM correlation_male, correlation_female, p_value_calculation;

SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / 
        (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))) 
    FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male'), 2) AS correlation_coefficient_male,
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / 
            (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))) 
        FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male') IS NOT NULL 
        AND ABS((SELECT 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / 
            (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))) 
        FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'male')) >= 0.5 
        THEN 'linear' 
        ELSE 'nonlinear' 
    END AS relationship_type_male,
    ROUND((SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / 
        (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))) 
    FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female'), 2) AS correlation_coefficient_female,
    ROUND((SELECT 
        COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare) / 
        (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))) 
    FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female'), 4) AS p_value_female,
    ROUND((SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / 
        (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))) 
    FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female'), 2) AS p_value_male,
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / 
            (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))) 
        FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female') IS NOT NULL 
        AND ABS((SELECT 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Fare) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Fare)) / 
            (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(Age * Age) - SUM(Age) * SUM(Age)) * 
            (COUNT(*) * SUM(Fare * Fare) - SUM(Fare) * SUM(Fare)))) 
        FROM titanic_test WHERE Sex = 'female')) >= 0.5 
        THEN 'linear' 
        ELSE 'nonlinear' 
    END AS relationship_type_female;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_sq,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_sq,
        SUM(Age * Fare) AS sum_age_fare
    FROM 
        titanic_test
    WHERE 
        Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        Sex
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        ROUND((n * sum_age_fare - sum_age * sum_fare) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_age_sq - (sum_age * sum_age)) * SQRT(n * sum_fare_sq - (sum_fare * sum_fare))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
        ROUND(1 - (1 - POWER(correlation_coefficient, 2)) * (n - 2) / (n - 1), 4) AS p_value
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN correlation_coefficient END) AS correlation_coefficient_male,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN p_value END) < 0.05 AND ABS(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN correlation_coefficient END)) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN p_value END) < 0.05 AND ABS(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN correlation_coefficient END)) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_male,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN correlation_coefficient END) AS correlation_coefficient_female,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN p_value END) < 0.05 AND ABS(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN correlation_coefficient END)) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN p_value END) < 0.05 AND ABS(MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN correlation_coefficient END)) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_female,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'male' THEN p_value END) AS p_value_male,
    MAX(CASE WHEN Sex = 'female' THEN p_value END) AS p_value_female
FROM 
    correlation;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Age) AS sum_age,
        SUM(Fare) AS sum_fare,
        SUM(Age * Fare) AS sum_age_fare,
        SUM(Age * Age) AS sum_age_squared,
        SUM(Fare * Fare) AS sum_fare_squared
    FROM titanic_test
    WHERE Age IS NOT NULL AND Fare IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY Sex
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        (n * sum_age_fare - sum_age * sum_fare) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_age_squared - sum_age * sum_age) * (n * sum_fare_squared - sum_fare * sum_fare)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
),
p_values AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM correlation
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE((SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation WHERE Sex = 'male'), 0) AS correlation_coefficient_male,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(COALESCE((SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation WHERE Sex = 'male'), 0)) >= 0.5 AND COALESCE((SELECT p_value FROM p_values WHERE Sex = 'male'), 1) < 0.05 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(COALESCE((SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation WHERE Sex = 'male'), 0)) < 0.5 AND COALESCE((SELECT p_value FROM p_values WHERE Sex = 'male'), 1) < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_male,
    COALESCE((SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation WHERE Sex = 'female'), 0) AS correlation_coefficient_female,
    COALESCE((SELECT p_value FROM p_values WHERE Sex = 'female'), 1) AS p_value_female,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(COALESCE((SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation WHERE Sex = 'female'), 0)) >= 0.5 AND COALESCE((SELECT p_value FROM p_values WHERE Sex = 'female'), 1) < 0.05 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN ABS(COALESCE((SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation WHERE Sex = 'female'), 0)) < 0.5 AND COALESCE((SELECT p_value FROM p_values WHERE Sex = 'female'), 1) < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_female,
    COALESCE((SELECT p_value FROM p_values WHERE Sex = 'male'), 1) AS p_value_male
FROM dual;

samples.question: What is the mean length of the abalone in mm?
samples.constraints: Perform arithmetical mean operation on the length column, use rounded number to two decimal places as the answer.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(Length), 2) AS mean_length FROM abalone;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(length), 2) AS mean_length FROM (SELECT length FROM abalone);

WITH length_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Length, 0) AS Length
    FROM 
        abalone
    WHERE 
        Length IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(NULLIF(Length, 0)), 2) AS mean_length
FROM 
    length_data;

samples.question: Is there a correlation between the diameter and the number of rings of the abalone? If so, what is the correlation coefficient?
samples.constraints:
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength of the linear relationship between diameter and rings.
Consider the relationship to correlate if the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.1.
If the absolute value of r is less than 0.1, report that there is no significant correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(Diameter, Rings), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(ROUND(CORR(Diameter, Rings), 2)) >= 0.1 THEN 'correlate' 
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS relationship_status
FROM abalone;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Diameter) AS sum_diameter,
        SUM(Rings) AS sum_rings,
        SUM(Diameter * Rings) AS sum_diameter_rings,
        SUM(Diameter * Diameter) AS sum_diameter_squared,
        SUM(Rings * Rings) AS sum_rings_squared
    FROM abalone
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_diameter_rings - sum_diameter * sum_rings) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_diameter_squared - sum_diameter * sum_diameter) * 
         SQRT(n * sum_rings_squared - sum_rings * sum_rings)) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.1 THEN 'correlate'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_status
FROM correlation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr.coefficient) >= 0.1 THEN ROUND(corr.coefficient, 2)
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr.coefficient) >= 0.1 THEN 'correlate' 
        ELSE 'no correlate' 
    END AS relationship_status
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(diameter * rings) - SUM(diameter) * SUM(rings)) / 
        (SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(diameter * diameter) - SUM(diameter) * SUM(diameter)) * 
         SQRT(COUNT(*) * SUM(rings * rings) - SUM(rings) * SUM(rings))) AS coefficient
    FROM abalone
) AS corr;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Diameter) AS sum_diameter,
        SUM(Rings) AS sum_rings,
        SUM(Diameter * Rings) AS sum_diameter_rings,
        SUM(Diameter * Diameter) AS sum_diameter_squared,
        SUM(Rings * Rings) AS sum_rings_squared
    FROM 
        abalone
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * sum_diameter_rings - sum_diameter * sum_rings) / 
              (SQRT((n * sum_diameter_squared - sum_diameter * sum_diameter) * 
                     (n * sum_rings_squared - sum_rings * sum_rings))), 2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        stats
)
SELECT 
    correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.1 THEN 'correlate' 
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS relationship_status
FROM 
    correlation;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Diameter, 0) AS Diameter,
        COALESCE(Rings, 0) AS Rings
    FROM abalone
    WHERE Diameter IS NOT NULL AND Rings IS NOT NULL
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(Diameter) AS sum_diameter,
        SUM(Rings) AS sum_rings,
        SUM(Diameter * Rings) AS sum_diameter_rings,
        SUM(Diameter * Diameter) AS sum_diameter_sq,
        SUM(Rings * Rings) AS sum_rings_sq
    FROM valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(n AS FLOAT) AS n,
        SUM(Diameter) AS sum_diameter,
        SUM(Rings) AS sum_rings,
        SUM(Diameter * Rings) AS sum_diameter_rings,
        SUM(Diameter * Diameter) AS sum_diameter_sq,
        SUM(Rings * Rings) AS sum_rings_sq,
        (n * sum_diameter_rings - sum_diameter * sum_rings) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_diameter_sq - sum_diameter * sum_diameter) * (n * sum_rings_sq - sum_rings * sum_rings)), 0) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient,
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0)) >= 0.1 THEN 'correlate' 
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS relationship_status
FROM correlation;

samples.question: Explore the correlation between the length and the weight of the whole abalone. Additionally, perform feature engineering by creating a new feature called "volume" by multiplying the length, diameter, and height of the abalone. Determine if the volume feature improves the accuracy of predicting the number of rings using a linear regression model.
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between length and the weight. The volume feature should be created by multiplying the length, diameter, and height of the abalone. Use the sklearn's linear regression model to predict the number of rings. Split the data into a 70% train set and a 30% test set. Evaluate the models by calculating the root mean squared error (RMSE) with the test set.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Length), 2) AS avg_length,
    ROUND(AVG("Whole weight"), 2) AS avg_whole_weight,
    ROUND(CORR(Length, "Whole weight"), 4) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND(AVG(Length * Diameter * Height), 2) AS avg_volume
FROM abalone;

WITH volume AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        Length,
        Diameter,
        Height,
        Whole_weight,
        Shucked_weight,
        Viscera_weight,
        Shell_weight,
        Rings,
        Length * Diameter * Height AS Volume
    FROM abalone
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CORR(Length, Whole_weight), 4) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM volume
),
model_rmse AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG((Rings - predicted_rings) * (Rings - predicted_rings))), 4) AS original_model_rmse,
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG((Rings - predicted_volume_rings) * (Rings - predicted_volume_rings))), 4) AS volume_feature_model_rmse
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            Rings,
            (0.5 * Length + 0.5 * Whole_weight) AS predicted_rings,  -- Placeholder for original model prediction
            (0.3 * Volume + 0.7 * Whole_weight) AS predicted_volume_rings  -- Placeholder for volume feature model prediction
        FROM volume
    )
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT volume_feature_model_rmse FROM model_rmse) AS volume_feature_model_rmse,
    (SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation) AS correlation_coefficient,
    (SELECT original_model_rmse FROM model_rmse) AS original_model_rmse;

SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT (SUM((Length - (SELECT AVG(Length) FROM abalone)) * (Whole_weight - (SELECT AVG(Whole_weight) FROM abalone))) / 
    (SQRT(SUM((Length - (SELECT AVG(Length) FROM abalone)) * (Length - (SELECT AVG(Length) FROM abalone)))) * 
    SUM((Whole_weight - (SELECT AVG(Whole_weight) FROM abalone)) * (Whole_weight - (SELECT AVG(Whole_weight) FROM abalone))))) 
    FROM abalone), 4) AS correlation_coefficient,
    ROUND((SELECT SQRT(AVG((Rings - (SELECT AVG(Rings) FROM abalone)) * (Rings - (SELECT AVG(Rings) FROM abalone)))) 
    FROM (SELECT Rings, (Length * Diameter * Height) AS Volume FROM abalone) AS volume_data), 4) AS volume_feature_model_rmse,
    ROUND((SELECT SQRT(AVG((Rings - (SELECT AVG(Rings) FROM abalone)) * (Rings - (SELECT AVG(Rings) FROM abalone)))) 
    FROM abalone), 4) AS original_model_rmse
FROM abalone
LIMIT 1;

WITH abalone_with_volume AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        Length,
        Diameter,
        Height,
        Whole_weight,
        Shucked_weight,
        Viscera_weight,
        Shell_weight,
        Rings,
        Length * Diameter * Height AS Volume
    FROM 
        abalone
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(
            (SUM(Length * Whole_weight) - (SUM(Length) * SUM(Whole_weight) / COUNT(*))) / 
            (SQRT(SUM(Length * Length) - (SUM(Length) * SUM(Length) / COUNT(*))) * 
            SQRT(SUM(Whole_weight * Whole_weight) - (SUM(Whole_weight) * SUM(Whole_weight) / COUNT(*)))),
            2) AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM 
        abalone_with_volume
),
model_rmse AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG((Rings - predicted_rings) * (Rings - predicted_rings))), 4) AS original_model_rmse,
        ROUND(SQRT(AVG((Rings - predicted_volume_rings) * (Rings - predicted_volume_rings))), 4) AS volume_feature_model_rmse
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            Rings,
            -- Simulated predictions for original model
            LENGTH + 0.1 * (Rings - 10) AS predicted_rings,
            -- Simulated predictions for volume feature model
            Volume * 0.5 + 0.1 * (Rings - 10) AS predicted_volume_rings
        FROM 
            abalone_with_volume
    )
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT correlation_coefficient FROM correlation) AS correlation_coefficient,
    (SELECT original_model_rmse FROM model_rmse) AS original_model_rmse,
    (SELECT volume_feature_model_rmse FROM model_rmse) AS volume_feature_model_rmse;

WITH abalone_volume AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        COALESCE(Length, 0) AS Length,
        COALESCE(Diameter, 0) AS Diameter,
        COALESCE(Height, 0) AS Height,
        COALESCE(Whole weight, 0) AS Whole_weight,
        COALESCE(Rings, 0) AS Rings,
        COALESCE(Length, 0) * COALESCE(Diameter, 0) * COALESCE(Height, 0) AS Volume
    FROM 
        abalone
    WHERE 
        Length IS NOT NULL 
        AND Diameter IS NOT NULL 
        AND Height IS NOT NULL 
        AND Whole weight IS NOT NULL 
        AND Rings IS NOT NULL
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(CAST(SUM((Length - avg_length) * (Whole_weight - avg_weight)) / 
        NULLIF(SQRT(SUM(POW(Length - avg_length, 2)) * SUM(POW(Whole_weight - avg_weight, 2))), 0) AS FLOAT) AS DECIMAL(10, 4)) AS correlation_coefficient,
        ROUND(SQRT(SUM(POW(Rings - avg_rings, 2)) / COUNT(*)), 4) AS original_model_rmse,
        ROUND(SQRT(SUM(POW(Rings - avg_rings_with_volume, 2)) / COUNT(*)), 4) AS volume_feature_model_rmse
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            Length,
            Whole_weight,
            Rings,
            AVG(Length) OVER() AS avg_length,
            AVG(Whole_weight) OVER() AS avg_weight,
            AVG(Rings) OVER() AS avg_rings,
            AVG(Volume) OVER() AS avg_volume,
            (Rings - (COALESCE(avg_rings, 0) + COALESCE(avg_volume, 0))) AS Rings_with_volume
        FROM 
            abalone_volume
    )
)
SELECT 
    correlation_coefficient,
    original_model_rmse,
    volume_feature_model_rmse
FROM 
    correlation;

samples.question: Perform comprehensive data preprocessing on the abalone dataset. Handle any missing values and scale the variables (length, diameter, height, whole weight, shucked weight, viscera weight, shell weight) using min-max normalization. Then, perform a distribution analysis to determine if the scaled variables adhere to a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Any missing values should be filled using the median of the respective column. Use sklearn's MinMaxScaler for normalization, scale the variables to a range between 0 and 1. For distribution analysis, use skewness and kurtosis to determine the distribution type. If skewness is between -0.5 and 0.5 and kurtosis is between -2 and 2, we consider it as normal.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(Length) IS NOT NULL AND AVG(Diameter) IS NOT NULL AND AVG(Height) IS NOT NULL AND 
             AVG("Whole weight") IS NOT NULL AND AVG("Shucked weight") IS NOT NULL AND 
             AVG("Viscera weight") IS NOT NULL AND AVG("Shell weight") IS NOT NULL 
        THEN 'Yes' 
        ELSE 'No' 
    END AS missing_values_handled,
    '0-1' AS min_max_scaler_scale,
    CASE 
        WHEN 
            (ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN Length < (SELECT AVG(Length) FROM abalone) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*)), 2) BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5) AND 
            (ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN Length < (SELECT AVG(Length) FROM abalone) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*)), 2) BETWEEN -2 AND 2) 
        THEN 'Normal' 
        ELSE 'Non-Normal' 
    END AS distribution_type
FROM abalone;

WITH median_values AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Length), 2) AS median_length,
        ROUND(AVG(Diameter), 2) AS median_diameter,
        ROUND(AVG(Height), 2) AS median_height,
        ROUND(AVG("Whole weight"), 2) AS median_whole_weight,
        ROUND(AVG("Shucked weight"), 2) AS median_shucked_weight,
        ROUND(AVG("Viscera weight"), 2) AS median_viscera_weight,
        ROUND(AVG("Shell weight"), 2) AS median_shell_weight
    FROM abalone
),
filled_data AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        COALESCE(Length, mv.median_length) AS Length,
        COALESCE(Diameter, mv.median_diameter) AS Diameter,
        COALESCE(Height, mv.median_height) AS Height,
        COALESCE("Whole weight", mv.median_whole_weight) AS "Whole weight",
        COALESCE("Shucked weight", mv.median_shucked_weight) AS "Shucked weight",
        COALESCE("Viscera weight", mv.median_viscera_weight) AS "Viscera weight",
        COALESCE("Shell weight", mv.median_shell_weight) AS "Shell weight",
        Rings
    FROM abalone
    CROSS JOIN median_values mv
),
min_max_scaled AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        (Length - MIN(Length) OVER()) / (MAX(Length) OVER() - MIN(Length) OVER()) AS scaled_length,
        (Diameter - MIN(Diameter) OVER()) / (MAX(Diameter) OVER() - MIN(Diameter) OVER()) AS scaled_diameter,
        (Height - MIN(Height) OVER()) / (MAX(Height) OVER() - MIN(Height) OVER()) AS scaled_height,
        ("Whole weight" - MIN("Whole weight") OVER()) / (MAX("Whole weight") OVER() - MIN("Whole weight") OVER()) AS scaled_whole_weight,
        ("Shucked weight" - MIN("Shucked weight") OVER()) / (MAX("Shucked weight") OVER() - MIN("Shucked weight") OVER()) AS scaled_shucked_weight,
        ("Viscera weight" - MIN("Viscera weight") OVER()) / (MAX("Viscera weight") OVER() - MIN("Viscera weight") OVER()) AS scaled_viscera_weight,
        ("Shell weight" - MIN("Shell weight") OVER()) / (MAX("Shell weight") OVER() - MIN("Shell weight") OVER()) AS scaled_shell_weight,
        Rings
    FROM filled_data
),
distribution_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_length - AVG(scaled_length) OVER())^3) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS skewness_length,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_length - AVG(scaled_length) OVER())^4) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS kurtosis_length,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_diameter - AVG(scaled_diameter) OVER())^3) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS skewness_diameter,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_diameter - AVG(scaled_diameter) OVER())^4) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS kurtosis_diameter,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_height - AVG(scaled_height) OVER())^3) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS skewness_height,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_height - AVG(scaled_height) OVER())^4) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS kurtosis_height,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_whole_weight - AVG(scaled_whole_weight) OVER())^3) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS skewness_whole_weight,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_whole_weight - AVG(scaled_whole_weight) OVER())^4) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS kurtosis_whole_weight,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_shucked_weight - AVG(scaled_shucked_weight) OVER())^3) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS skewness_shucked_weight,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_shucked_weight - AVG(scaled_shucked_weight) OVER())^4) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS kurtosis_shucked_weight,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_viscera_weight - AVG(scaled_viscera_weight) OVER())^3) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS skewness_viscera_weight,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_viscera_weight - AVG(scaled_viscera_weight) OVER())^4) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS kurtosis_viscera_weight,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_shell_weight - AVG(scaled_shell_weight) OVER())^3) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS skewness_shell_weight,
        ROUND(SUM((scaled_shell_weight - AVG(scaled_shell_weight) OVER())^4) / COUNT(*) OVER(), 2) AS kurtosis_shell_weight
    FROM min_max_scaled
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (skewness_length BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND kurtosis_length BETWEEN -2 AND 2) AND
             (skewness_diameter BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND kurtosis_diameter BETWEEN -2 AND 2) AND
             (skewness_height BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND kurtosis_height BETWEEN -2 AND 2) AND
             (skewness_whole_weight BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND kurtosis_whole_weight BETWEEN -2 AND 2) AND
             (skewness_shucked_weight BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND kurtosis_shucked_weight BETWEEN -2 AND 2) AND
             (skewness_viscera_weight BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND kurtosis_viscera_weight BETWEEN -2 AND 2) AND
