             (skewness_shell_weight BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND kurtosis_shell_weight BETWEEN -2 AND 2) 
        THEN 'Normal' 
        ELSE 'Non-Normal' 
    END AS distribution_type,
    '0-1' AS min_max_scaler_scale,
    'Yes' AS missing_values_handled
FROM distribution_analysis
LIMIT 1;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(SKEWNESS) BETWEEN 0 AND 0.5 AND ABS(KURTOSIS) BETWEEN -2 AND 2 THEN 'Normal' 
        ELSE 'Non-Normal' 
    END AS distribution_type,
    '0-1' AS min_max_scaler_scale,
    'Yes' AS missing_values_handled
FROM (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((Length - (SELECT MEDIAN(Length) FROM abalone)) / 
              ((SELECT MAX(Length) FROM abalone) - (SELECT MIN(Length) FROM abalone)), 2) AS Length,
        ROUND((Diameter - (SELECT MEDIAN(Diameter) FROM abalone)) / 
              ((SELECT MAX(Diameter) FROM abalone) - (SELECT MIN(Diameter) FROM abalone)), 2) AS Diameter,
        ROUND((Height - (SELECT MEDIAN(Height) FROM abalone)) / 
              ((SELECT MAX(Height) FROM abalone) - (SELECT MIN(Height) FROM abalone)), 2) AS Height,
        ROUND((Whole_weight - (SELECT MEDIAN(Whole_weight) FROM abalone)) / 
              ((SELECT MAX(Whole_weight) FROM abalone) - (SELECT MIN(Whole_weight) FROM abalone)), 2) AS Whole_weight,
        ROUND((Shucked_weight - (SELECT MEDIAN(Shucked_weight) FROM abalone)) / 
              ((SELECT MAX(Shucked_weight) FROM abalone) - (SELECT MIN(Shucked_weight) FROM abalone)), 2) AS Shucked_weight,
        ROUND((Viscera_weight - (SELECT MEDIAN(Viscera_weight) FROM abalone)) / 
              ((SELECT MAX(Viscera_weight) FROM abalone) - (SELECT MIN(Viscera_weight) FROM abalone)), 2) AS Viscera_weight,
        ROUND((Shell_weight - (SELECT MEDIAN(Shell_weight) FROM abalone)) / 
              ((SELECT MAX(Shell_weight) FROM abalone) - (SELECT MIN(Shell_weight) FROM abalone)), 2) AS Shell_weight,
        (SELECT 
            (SUM(POWER(Length, 3)) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM(POWER(Length, 2)) / COUNT(*)) - 3 AS SKEWNESS,
            (SUM(POWER(Length, 4)) / COUNT(*)) / (SUM(POWER(Length, 2)) / COUNT(*)) - 3 AS KURTOSIS
         FROM abalone) AS stats
    FROM abalone
) AS scaled_data;

WITH filled_data AS (
    SELECT 
        Sex,
        COALESCE(Length, (SELECT MEDIAN(Length) FROM abalone)) AS Length,
        COALESCE(Diameter, (SELECT MEDIAN(Diameter) FROM abalone)) AS Diameter,
        COALESCE(Height, (SELECT MEDIAN(Height) FROM abalone)) AS Height,
        COALESCE(Whole_weight, (SELECT MEDIAN(Whole_weight) FROM abalone)) AS Whole_weight,
        COALESCE(Shucked_weight, (SELECT MEDIAN(Shucked_weight) FROM abalone)) AS Shucked_weight,
        COALESCE(Viscera_weight, (SELECT MEDIAN(Viscera_weight) FROM abalone)) AS Viscera_weight,
        COALESCE(Shell_weight, (SELECT MEDIAN(Shell_weight) FROM abalone)) AS Shell_weight,
        Rings
    FROM abalone
),
min_max AS (
    SELECT 
        Length,
        Diameter,
        Height,
        Whole_weight,
        Shucked_weight,
        Viscera_weight,
        Shell_weight,
        (Length - MIN(Length) OVER()) / (MAX(Length) OVER() - MIN(Length) OVER()) AS Scaled_Length,
        (Diameter - MIN(Diameter) OVER()) / (MAX(Diameter) OVER() - MIN(Diameter) OVER()) AS Scaled_Diameter,
        (Height - MIN(Height) OVER()) / (MAX(Height) OVER() - MIN(Height) OVER()) AS Scaled_Height,
        (Whole_weight - MIN(Whole_weight) OVER()) / (MAX(Whole_weight) OVER() - MIN(Whole_weight) OVER()) AS Scaled_Whole_weight,
        (Shucked_weight - MIN(Shucked_weight) OVER()) / (MAX(Shucked_weight) OVER() - MIN(Shucked_weight) OVER()) AS Scaled_Shucked_weight,
        (Viscera_weight - MIN(Viscera_weight) OVER()) / (MAX(Viscera_weight) OVER() - MIN(Viscera_weight) OVER()) AS Scaled_Viscera_weight,
        (Shell_weight - MIN(Shell_weight) OVER()) / (MAX(Shell_weight) OVER() - MIN(Shell_weight) OVER()) AS Scaled_Shell_weight
    FROM filled_data
),
distribution_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Length) - AVG(Scaled_Length) OVER(), 2) AS Skewness_Length,
        ROUND(AVG(POW(Scaled_Length - AVG(Scaled_Length) OVER(), 4)) / POW(STDEV(Scaled_Length) OVER(), 4) - 3, 2) AS Kurtosis_Length,
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Diameter) - AVG(Scaled_Diameter) OVER(), 2) AS Skewness_Diameter,
        ROUND(AVG(POW(Scaled_Diameter - AVG(Scaled_Diameter) OVER(), 4)) / POW(STDEV(Scaled_Diameter) OVER(), 4) - 3, 2) AS Kurtosis_Diameter,
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Height) - AVG(Scaled_Height) OVER(), 2) AS Skewness_Height,
        ROUND(AVG(POW(Scaled_Height - AVG(Scaled_Height) OVER(), 4)) / POW(STDEV(Scaled_Height) OVER(), 4) - 3, 2) AS Kurtosis_Height,
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Whole_weight) - AVG(Scaled_Whole_weight) OVER(), 2) AS Skewness_Whole_weight,
        ROUND(AVG(POW(Scaled_Whole_weight - AVG(Scaled_Whole_weight) OVER(), 4)) / POW(STDEV(Scaled_Whole_weight) OVER(), 4) - 3, 2) AS Kurtosis_Whole_weight,
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Shucked_weight) - AVG(Scaled_Shucked_weight) OVER(), 2) AS Skewness_Shucked_weight,
        ROUND(AVG(POW(Scaled_Shucked_weight - AVG(Scaled_Shucked_weight) OVER(), 4)) / POW(STDEV(Scaled_Shucked_weight) OVER(), 4) - 3, 2) AS Kurtosis_Shucked_weight,
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Viscera_weight) - AVG(Scaled_Viscera_weight) OVER(), 2) AS Skewness_Viscera_weight,
        ROUND(AVG(POW(Scaled_Viscera_weight - AVG(Scaled_Viscera_weight) OVER(), 4)) / POW(STDEV(Scaled_Viscera_weight) OVER(), 4) - 3, 2) AS Kurtosis_Viscera_weight,
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Shell_weight) - AVG(Scaled_Shell_weight) OVER(), 2) AS Skewness_Shell_weight,
        ROUND(AVG(POW(Scaled_Shell_weight - AVG(Scaled_Shell_weight) OVER(), 4)) / POW(STDEV(Scaled_Shell_weight) OVER(), 4) - 3, 2) AS Kurtosis_Shell_weight
    FROM min_max
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (MIN(Skewness_Length) BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND MIN(Kurtosis_Length) BETWEEN -2 AND 2) THEN 'Normal' 
        ELSE 'Non-Normal' 
    END AS distribution_type,
    '0-1' AS min_max_scaler_scale,
    'Yes' AS missing_values_handled
FROM distribution_analysis;

WITH filled_abalone AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Sex, 'Unknown') AS Sex,
        COALESCE(Length, (SELECT MEDIAN(Length) FROM abalone WHERE Length IS NOT NULL)) AS Length,
        COALESCE(Diameter, (SELECT MEDIAN(Diameter) FROM abalone WHERE Diameter IS NOT NULL)) AS Diameter,
        COALESCE(Height, (SELECT MEDIAN(Height) FROM abalone WHERE Height IS NOT NULL)) AS Height,
        COALESCE(Whole_weight, (SELECT MEDIAN(Whole_weight) FROM abalone WHERE Whole_weight IS NOT NULL)) AS Whole_weight,
        COALESCE(Shucked_weight, (SELECT MEDIAN(Shucked_weight) FROM abalone WHERE Shucked_weight IS NOT NULL)) AS Shucked_weight,
        COALESCE(Viscera_weight, (SELECT MEDIAN(Viscera_weight) FROM abalone WHERE Viscera_weight IS NOT NULL)) AS Viscera_weight,
        COALESCE(Shell_weight, (SELECT MEDIAN(Shell_weight) FROM abalone WHERE Shell_weight IS NOT NULL)) AS Shell_weight,
        Rings
    FROM abalone
),
min_max AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((Length - MIN(Length)) / NULLIF((MAX(Length) - MIN(Length)), 0), 2) AS Scaled_Length,
        ROUND((Diameter - MIN(Diameter)) / NULLIF((MAX(Diameter) - MIN(Diameter)), 0), 2) AS Scaled_Diameter,
        ROUND((Height - MIN(Height)) / NULLIF((MAX(Height) - MIN(Height)), 0), 2) AS Scaled_Height,
        ROUND((Whole_weight - MIN(Whole_weight)) / NULLIF((MAX(Whole_weight) - MIN(Whole_weight)), 0), 2) AS Scaled_Whole_weight,
        ROUND((Shucked_weight - MIN(Shucked_weight)) / NULLIF((MAX(Shucked_weight) - MIN(Shucked_weight)), 0), 2) AS Scaled_Shucked_weight,
        ROUND((Viscera_weight - MIN(Viscera_weight)) / NULLIF((MAX(Viscera_weight) - MIN(Viscera_weight)), 0), 2) AS Scaled_Viscera_weight,
        ROUND((Shell_weight - MIN(Shell_weight)) / NULLIF((MAX(Shell_weight) - MIN(Shell_weight)), 0), 2) AS Scaled_Shell_weight
    FROM filled_abalone
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Length) - AVG(Scaled_Length) * AVG(Scaled_Length) * AVG(Scaled_Length) * AVG(Scaled_Length), 2) AS Skewness,
        ROUND(AVG(Scaled_Length) * AVG(Scaled_Length) * AVG(Scaled_Length) * AVG(Scaled_Length) - 4 * AVG(Scaled_Length) * AVG(Scaled_Length) * AVG(Scaled_Length), 2) AS Kurtosis
    FROM min_max
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN Skewness BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 AND Kurtosis BETWEEN -2 AND 2 THEN 'Normal' 
        ELSE 'Non-Normal' 
    END AS distribution_type,
    '0-1' AS min_max_scaler_scale,
    'Yes' AS missing_values_handled
FROM stats;

samples.question: What is the mean of the DBH_CM column?
samples.constraints: Calculate the arithmetic mean of the 'DBH_CM' column. The answer should be rounded to the nearest hundredth. Do not consider missing values, outliers, or data error possibilities, as it was stated there are no missing values in this column and no further cleaning or preprocessing is needed for this problem.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(DBH_CM), 2) AS mean_dbh_cm FROM tree;
SELECT ROUND(AVG(DBH_CM), 2) AS mean_dbh_cm FROM (SELECT DBH_CM FROM tree);

WITH dbh_stats AS (
    SELECT DBH_CM,
           AVG(DBH_CM) OVER () AS mean_dbh_cm
    FROM tree
)
SELECT ROUND(DISTINCT mean_dbh_cm, 2) AS mean_dbh_cm
FROM dbh_stats;

samples.question: Are the HT_M column and the BA_M2 column correlated?
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between the 'HT_M' and 'BA_M2' columns. The answer should be rounded to the third decimal place. Consider the relationship to be linear if the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.5. Consider the relationship to be non-linear if the absolute value of r is less than 0.5.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr(HT_M, BA_M2)) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear' 
        ELSE 'non-linear' 
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(corr(HT_M, BA_M2), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM tree;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(HT_M) AS sum_ht,
        SUM(BA_M2) AS sum_ba,
        SUM(HT_M * BA_M2) AS sum_ht_ba,
        SUM(HT_M * HT_M) AS sum_ht_sq,
        SUM(BA_M2 * BA_M2) AS sum_ba_sq
    FROM tree
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_ht_ba - sum_ht * sum_ba) / 
        (SQRT((n * sum_ht_sq - sum_ht * sum_ht) * (n * sum_ba_sq - sum_ba * sum_ba))) AS r
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(r) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear' 
        ELSE 'non-linear' 
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(r, 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr_value) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear' 
        ELSE 'non-linear' 
    END AS relationship_type, 
    ROUND(corr_value, 3) AS correlation_coefficient 
FROM (
    SELECT 
        (COUNT(*) * SUM(HT_M * BA_M2) - SUM(HT_M) * SUM(BA_M2)) / 
        (SQRT((COUNT(*) * SUM(HT_M * HT_M) - SUM(HT_M) * SUM(HT_M)) * 
               (COUNT(*) * SUM(BA_M2 * BA_M2) - SUM(BA_M2) * SUM(BA_M2)))) AS corr_value 
    FROM tree
) AS correlation;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(HT_M) AS sum_ht,
        SUM(BA_M2) AS sum_ba,
        SUM(HT_M * BA_M2) AS sum_ht_ba,
        SUM(HT_M * HT_M) AS sum_ht_sq,
        SUM(BA_M2 * BA_M2) AS sum_ba_sq
    FROM tree
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_ht_ba - sum_ht * sum_ba) / 
        (SQRT(n * sum_ht_sq - sum_ht * sum_ht) * SQRT(n * sum_ba_sq - sum_ba * sum_ba)) AS r
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(r) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear' 
        ELSE 'non-linear' 
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND(r, 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

WITH valid_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(HT_M, 0) AS ht_m,
        COALESCE(BA_M2, 0) AS ba_m2
    FROM tree
    WHERE HT_M IS NOT NULL AND BA_M2 IS NOT NULL
),
n AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS count,
           SUM(ht_m) AS sum_ht_m,
           SUM(ba_m2) AS sum_ba_m2,
           SUM(ht_m * ba_m2) AS sum_ht_m_ba_m2,
           SUM(ht_m * ht_m) AS sum_ht_m_squared,
           SUM(ba_m2 * ba_m2) AS sum_ba_m_squared
    FROM valid_data
),
correlation AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(count AS FLOAT) AS n,
        CAST(sum_ht_m AS FLOAT) AS sum_ht_m,
        CAST(sum_ba_m2 AS FLOAT) AS sum_ba_m2,
        CAST(sum_ht_m_ba_m2 AS FLOAT) AS sum_ht_m_ba_m2,
        CAST(sum_ht_m_squared AS FLOAT) AS sum_ht_m_squared,
        CAST(sum_ba_m_squared AS FLOAT) AS sum_ba_m_squared
    FROM n
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND((n * sum_ht_m_ba_m2 - sum_ht_m * sum_ba_m2) / 
                   NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_ht_m_squared - sum_ht_m * sum_ht_m) * 
                                (n * sum_ba_m_squared - sum_ba_m2 * sum_ba_m2)), 0), 3) >= 0.5 
        THEN 'linear' 
        ELSE 'non-linear' 
    END AS relationship_type,
    ROUND((n * sum_ht_m_ba_m2 - sum_ht_m * sum_ba_m2) / 
          NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_ht_m_squared - sum_ht_m * sum_ht_m) * 
                       (n * sum_ba_m_squared - sum_ba_m2 * sum_ba_m2)), 0), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM correlation;

samples.question: How many outliers are there in the TPH_PLT column?
samples.constraints: Detect outliers in the 'TPH_PLT' column using the IQR method, where observations that fall below Q1 - 1.5*IQR or above Q3 + 1.5*IQR are considered outliers. Do not consider missing values, as it was stated there are no missing values in this column.

variants.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM tree
WHERE TPH_PLT < (SELECT AVG(TPH_PLT) - 1.5 * (SELECT AVG(TPH_PLT) FROM tree) - (SELECT AVG(TPH_PLT) FROM tree WHERE TPH_PLT < (SELECT AVG(TPH_PLT) FROM tree)) )
OR TPH_PLT > (SELECT AVG(TPH_PLT) + 1.5 * (SELECT AVG(TPH_PLT) FROM tree) - (SELECT AVG(TPH_PLT) FROM tree WHERE TPH_PLT > (SELECT AVG(TPH_PLT) FROM tree)) );

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) AS Q3
    FROM tree
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM stats
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
    FROM tree, iqr
    WHERE TPH_PLT < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR TPH_PLT > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR)
)
SELECT outliers_count FROM outliers;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM tree
WHERE TPH_PLT < (SELECT Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
                  FROM (SELECT 
                            PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) AS Q1,
                            PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) AS Q3,
                            (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT)) AS IQR
                        FROM tree))
   OR TPH_PLT > (SELECT Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
                  FROM (SELECT 
                            PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) AS Q1,
                            PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) AS Q3,
                            (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) - PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT)) AS IQR
                        FROM tree));

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) AS Q1,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT) AS Q3
    FROM tree
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        (Q3 - Q1) AS IQR
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM tree, iqr
WHERE TPH_PLT < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) OR TPH_PLT > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR);

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(TPH_PLT), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT), 2) AS Q1,
        ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY TPH_PLT), 2) AS Q3
    FROM tree
    WHERE TPH_PLT IS NOT NULL
),
iqr AS (
    SELECT 
        Q1,
        Q3,
        ROUND(Q3 - Q1, 2) AS IQR
    FROM stats
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS outliers_count
FROM tree
JOIN iqr ON 1=1
WHERE TPH_PLT IS NOT NULL 
AND (TPH_PLT < COALESCE(Q1, 0) - 1.5 * COALESCE(IQR, 0) OR 
     TPH_PLT > COALESCE(Q3, 0) + 1.5 * COALESCE(IQR, 0));

samples.question: What is the median HT_M value for the plant species with a CON value of 1, and a PLTID of 5?
samples.constraints: Filter the data frame first by CON value of 1, then by PLTID of 5, calculate the median HT_M value of these entries.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(HT_M), 2) AS median_ht_m
FROM (
    SELECT HT_M
    FROM tree
    WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5
    ORDER BY HT_M
    LIMIT 2 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tree WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5) % 2
    OFFSET (SELECT (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2 FROM tree WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5)
);

WITH filtered_trees AS (
    SELECT HT_M
    FROM tree
    WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5
),
ranked_trees AS (
    SELECT HT_M,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY HT_M) AS rn,
           COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_count
    FROM filtered_trees
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(HT_M), 2) AS median_ht_m
FROM ranked_trees
WHERE rn IN ((total_count + 1) / 2, (total_count + 2) / 2);

SELECT ROUND(AVG(HT_M), 2) AS median_ht_m
FROM (
    SELECT HT_M
    FROM tree
    WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5
    ORDER BY HT_M
    LIMIT 2 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tree WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5) % 2
    OFFSET (SELECT (COUNT(*) - 1) / 2 FROM tree WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5)
) AS median_values;

WITH filtered_tree AS (
    SELECT HT_M,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY HT_M) AS rn,
           COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_count
    FROM tree
    WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5
)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(HT_M), 2) AS median_ht_m
FROM filtered_tree
WHERE rn IN ((total_count + 1) / 2, (total_count + 2) / 2);

WITH filtered_tree AS (
    SELECT HT_M
    FROM tree
    WHERE CON = 1 AND PLTID = 5 AND HT_M IS NOT NULL
),
ranked_tree AS (
    SELECT HT_M,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY HT_M) AS rn,
           COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_count
    FROM filtered_tree
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(*) = 0 THEN CAST(NULL AS FLOAT)
        WHEN total_count % 2 = 1 THEN ROUND(COALESCE((SELECT HT_M FROM ranked_tree WHERE rn = (total_count + 1) / 2), 0), 2)
        ELSE ROUND(COALESCE((SELECT AVG(HT_M) FROM ranked_tree WHERE rn IN (total_count / 2, total_count / 2 + 1)), 0), 2)
    END AS median_ht_m
FROM ranked_tree;

samples.question: How many unique plant species (represented by unique SPP_SYMBOL values) are there in the dataset, where each species has at least 5 observations?
samples.constraints: Count unique SPP_SYMBOL values that appear at least 5 times.

variants.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT SPP_SYMBOL) AS unique_species_count 
FROM tree 
GROUP BY SPP_SYMBOL 
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5;

WITH species_count AS (
    SELECT SPP_SYMBOL, COUNT(*) AS observation_count
    FROM tree
    GROUP BY SPP_SYMBOL
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT SPP_SYMBOL) AS unique_species_count
FROM species_count
WHERE observation_count >= 5;

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT SPP_SYMBOL) AS unique_species_count
FROM (SELECT SPP_SYMBOL
      FROM tree
      GROUP BY SPP_SYMBOL
      HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5) AS subquery;

WITH species_counts AS (
    SELECT SPP_SYMBOL, COUNT(*) AS observation_count
    FROM tree
    GROUP BY SPP_SYMBOL
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT SPP_SYMBOL) AS unique_species_count
FROM species_counts
WHERE observation_count >= 5;

WITH species_count AS (
    SELECT SPP_SYMBOL, COUNT(*) AS observation_count
    FROM tree
    WHERE SPP_SYMBOL IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY SPP_SYMBOL
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT SPP_SYMBOL) AS unique_species_count
FROM species_count
WHERE observation_count >= 5;

samples.question: Identify the date with the highest closing value of the S&P 500 Index (.SPX). Calculate the percentage change in the stock price of Apple Inc. (AAPL) from its closing price on the previous day to its closing price on the identified date.
samples.constraints:
1. The date where the S&P 500 Index (.SPX) reached its maximum value should be identified.
2. The percentage change is calculated as: ((price on identified date / price on previous day) - 1) * 100.
3. Percentage change should be calculated only if the previous day data exists. If the identified date is the first date in the dataset, state that the previous day data doesn't exist.
4. The data for the previous day is defined as the data on the date immediately preceding the identified date when sorting the dates in ascending order. Hunting for the "previous" trading day is not required.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    MAX(CASE WHEN .SPX = (SELECT MAX(.SPX) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data) THEN Date END) AS max_SPX_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN LAG(AAPL.O) OVER (ORDER BY Date) IS NOT NULL THEN ROUND(((AAPL.O / LAG(AAPL.O) OVER (ORDER BY Date)) - 1) * 100, 2)
        ELSE 'Previous day data doesn''t exist'
    END AS AAPL_price_percentage_change
FROM 
    tr_eikon_eod_data
WHERE 
    .SPX = (SELECT MAX(.SPX) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data);

WITH MaxSPX AS (
    SELECT Date, .SPX
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    ORDER BY .SPX DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
PreviousDay AS (
    SELECT Date, "AAPL.O"
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE Date < (SELECT Date FROM MaxSPX)
    ORDER BY Date DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
AAPLPriceChange AS (
    SELECT 
        m.Date AS max_SPX_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN p."AAPL.O" IS NOT NULL THEN ROUND(((a."AAPL.O" / p."AAPL.O") - 1) * 100, 2)
            ELSE 'Previous day data does not exist'
        END AS AAPL_price_percentage_change
    FROM MaxSPX m
    LEFT JOIN tr_eikon_eod_data a ON a.Date = m.Date
    LEFT JOIN PreviousDay p ON 1=1
)
SELECT max_SPX_date, AAPL_price_percentage_change
FROM AAPLPriceChange;

SELECT 
    (SELECT Date FROM tr_eikon_eod_data 
     WHERE .SPX = (SELECT MAX(.SPX) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data)) AS max_SPX_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT Date FROM tr_eikon_eod_data 
              WHERE .SPX = (SELECT MAX(.SPX) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data)) = 
             (SELECT MIN(Date) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data) THEN 'Previous day data doesn''t exist'
        ELSE ROUND(((SELECT AAPL.O FROM tr_eikon_eod_data 
                      WHERE Date = (SELECT Date FROM tr_eikon_eod_data 
                                    WHERE .SPX = (SELECT MAX(.SPX) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data)) ) / 
                     (SELECT AAPL.O FROM tr_eikon_eod_data 
                      WHERE Date = (SELECT Date FROM tr_eikon_eod_data 
                                    WHERE .SPX = (SELECT MAX(.SPX) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data) 
                                    LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1)) - 1) * 100, 2)
    END AS AAPL_price_percentage_change;

WITH max_spx AS (
    SELECT Date, .SPX,
           RANK() OVER (ORDER BY .SPX DESC) as spx_rank
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
),
previous_day_data AS (
    SELECT a.Date, a.AAPL.O AS current_aapl_price, 
           LAG(a.AAPL.O) OVER (ORDER BY a.Date) AS previous_aapl_price
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data a
    JOIN max_spx b ON a.Date = (SELECT MAX(Date) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data WHERE Date < b.Date)
    WHERE b.spx_rank = 1
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT Date FROM max_spx WHERE spx_rank = 1) AS max_SPX_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN previous_aapl_price IS NULL THEN 'Previous day data doesn\'t exist'
        ELSE ROUND(((current_aapl_price / previous_aapl_price) - 1) * 100, 2)
    END AS AAPL_price_percentage_change
FROM previous_day_data;

WITH max_spx AS (
    SELECT Date, .SPX
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE .SPX IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY .SPX DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
previous_day AS (
    SELECT Date, COALESCE(AAPL.O, 0) AS AAPL_previous
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE Date = (
        SELECT Date
        FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
        WHERE Date < (SELECT Date FROM max_spx)
        ORDER BY Date DESC
        LIMIT 1
    )
),
current_day AS (
    SELECT Date, COALESCE(AAPL.O, 0) AS AAPL_current
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE Date = (SELECT Date FROM max_spx)
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT Date FROM max_spx) AS max_SPX_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT AAPL_previous FROM previous_day) IS NULL THEN 'Previous day data doesn''t exist'
        ELSE ROUND(((SELECT AAPL_current FROM current_day) / NULLIF((SELECT AAPL_previous FROM previous_day), 0) - 1) * 100, 2)
    END AS AAPL_price_percentage_change
;

samples.question: Perform data preprocessing on the stock prices of Microsoft Corporation (MSFT), SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY), and the CBOE Volatility Index (.VIX). This preprocessing includes removing missing values, normalizing the data, and encoding any categorical variables. Calculate the correlation matrix between the preprocessed stock prices.
samples.constraints:
1. Missing values should be removed entirely from the dataset.
2. The normalization method to be used is feature scaling (rescaling the data to range between 0 and 1).
3. For categorical variables, use one hot encoding method, though no categorical data exists in the provided price columns.
4. The correlation computation method to be used is Pearson's correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(MSFT_O, .VIX), 2) AS MSFT_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(SPY, .VIX), 2) AS SPY_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(MSFT_O, SPY), 2) AS MSFT_SPY_correlation
FROM 
    tr_eikon_eod_data
WHERE 
    MSFT_O IS NOT NULL AND SPY IS NOT NULL AND .VIX IS NOT NULL;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT 
        MSFT.O AS MSFT_price,
        SPY AS SPY_price,
        .VIX AS VIX_price
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE MSFT.O IS NOT NULL AND SPY IS NOT NULL AND .VIX IS NOT NULL
),
normalized_data AS (
    SELECT 
        (MSFT_price - MIN(MSFT_price) OVER ()) / (MAX(MSFT_price) OVER () - MIN(MSFT_price) OVER ()) AS normalized_MSFT,
        (SPY_price - MIN(SPY_price) OVER ()) / (MAX(SPY_price) OVER () - MIN(SPY_price) OVER ()) AS normalized_SPY,
        (VIX_price - MIN(VIX_price) OVER ()) / (MAX(VIX_price) OVER () - MIN(VIX_price) OVER ()) AS normalized_VIX
    FROM cleaned_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(normalized_MSFT, normalized_VIX), 2) AS MSFT_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(normalized_SPY, normalized_VIX), 2) AS SPY_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(normalized_MSFT, normalized_SPY), 2) AS MSFT_SPY_correlation
FROM normalized_data;

SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT CORR(msft, vix) FROM (SELECT MSFT_O AS msft, .VIX AS vix FROM tr_eikon_eod_data WHERE MSFT_O IS NOT NULL AND .VIX IS NOT NULL)), 2) AS MSFT_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND((SELECT CORR(spy, vix) FROM (SELECT SPY AS spy, .VIX AS vix FROM tr_eikon_eod_data WHERE SPY IS NOT NULL AND .VIX IS NOT NULL)), 2) AS SPY_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND((SELECT CORR(msft, spy) FROM (SELECT MSFT_O AS msft, SPY AS spy FROM tr_eikon_eod_data WHERE MSFT_O IS NOT NULL AND SPY IS NOT NULL)), 2) AS MSFT_SPY_correlation
FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
WHERE MSFT_O IS NOT NULL AND SPY IS NOT NULL AND .VIX IS NOT NULL;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT 
        AAPL.O AS AAPL_O, 
        MSFT.O AS MSFT_O, 
        SPY, 
        .VIX 
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data 
    WHERE MSFT.O IS NOT NULL AND SPY IS NOT NULL AND .VIX IS NOT NULL
),
normalized_data AS (
    SELECT 
        (MSFT_O - MIN(MSFT_O) OVER ()) / (MAX(MSFT_O) OVER () - MIN(MSFT_O) OVER ()) AS MSFT_O_normalized,
        (SPY - MIN(SPY) OVER ()) / (MAX(SPY) OVER () - MIN(SPY) OVER ()) AS SPY_normalized,
        (VIX - MIN(.VIX) OVER ()) / (MAX(.VIX) OVER () - MIN(.VIX) OVER ()) AS VIX_normalized
    FROM cleaned_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(MSFT_O_normalized, VIX_normalized), 2) AS MSFT_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(SPY_normalized, VIX_normalized), 2) AS SPY_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(MSFT_O_normalized, SPY_normalized), 2) AS MSFT_SPY_correlation
FROM normalized_data;

WITH cleaned_data AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(AAPL.O AS FLOAT) AS AAPL_O,
        CAST(MSFT.O AS FLOAT) AS MSFT_O,
        CAST(SPY AS FLOAT) AS SPY,
        CAST(.VIX AS FLOAT) AS VIX
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE AAPL.O IS NOT NULL 
      AND MSFT.O IS NOT NULL 
      AND SPY IS NOT NULL 
      AND .VIX IS NOT NULL
),
normalized_data AS (
    SELECT 
        AAPL_O,
        MSFT_O,
        SPY,
        VIX,
        (AAPL_O - MIN(AAPL_O) OVER()) / NULLIF((MAX(AAPL_O) OVER() - MIN(AAPL_O) OVER()), 0) AS AAPL_O_normalized,
        (MSFT_O - MIN(MSFT_O) OVER()) / NULLIF((MAX(MSFT_O) OVER() - MIN(MSFT_O) OVER()), 0) AS MSFT_O_normalized,
        (SPY - MIN(SPY) OVER()) / NULLIF((MAX(SPY) OVER() - MIN(SPY) OVER()), 0) AS SPY_normalized,
        (VIX - MIN(VIX) OVER()) / NULLIF((MAX(VIX) OVER() - MIN(VIX) OVER()), 0) AS VIX_normalized
    FROM cleaned_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(MSFT_O_normalized, VIX_normalized), 2) AS MSFT_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(SPY_normalized, VIX_normalized), 2) AS SPY_VIX_correlation,
    ROUND(CORR(MSFT_O_normalized, SPY_normalized), 2) AS MSFT_SPY_correlation
FROM normalized_data;

samples.question: Using feature engineering techniques, create a new feature that represents the average stock price of Apple Inc. (AAPL), Microsoft Corporation (MSFT), and Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) on the given dates. Calculate the correlation between this new feature and the closing value of the S&P 500 Index (.SPX).
samples.constraints:
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient (r) to assess the strength and direction of the linear relationship between the newly created average stock price feature and the closing value of the S&P 500 Index (.SPX).
Assess the significance of the correlation using a two-tailed test with a significance level (alpha) of 0.05.
Report the p-value associated with the correlation test.
Consider the relationship to be linear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is greater than or equal to 0.5.
Consider the relationship to be nonlinear if the p-value is less than 0.05 and the absolute value of r is less than 0.5.
If the p-value is greater than or equal to 0.05, report that there is no significant correlation.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(corr) >= 0.5 AND pval < 0.05 THEN 'linear' 
        WHEN ABS(corr) < 0.5 AND pval < 0.05 THEN 'nonlinear' 
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS relationship_type_relation,
    ROUND(pval, 4) AS p_value_pval,
    ROUND(corr, 2) AS correlation_coefficient_corr
FROM (
    SELECT 
        AVG((AAPL.O + MSFT.O + AMZN.O) / 3) AS avg_stock_price,
        .SPX,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        (SUM((avg_stock_price - (SELECT AVG((AAPL.O + MSFT.O + AMZN.O) / 3) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data)) * (.SPX - (SELECT AVG(.SPX) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data))) / (n - 1)) / 
        (SQRT(SUM(POWER(avg_stock_price - (SELECT AVG((AAPL.O + MSFT.O + AMZN.O) / 3), 2) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data)) * (n - 1)) * 
        SQRT(SUM(POWER(.SPX - (SELECT AVG(.SPX) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data), 2) * (n - 1)))) AS corr,
        2 * (1 - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tr_eikon_eod_data) * (1 - POWER(corr, 2))) AS pval
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE AAPL.O IS NOT NULL AND MSFT.O IS NOT NULL AND AMZN.O IS NOT NULL AND .SPX IS NOT NULL
) AS correlation_data;

WITH average_stock_prices AS (
    SELECT 
        Date,
        ROUND((AAPL.O + MSFT.O + AMZN.O) / 3, 2) AS avg_stock_price,
        .SPX
    FROM 
        tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE 
        AAPL.O IS NOT NULL AND MSFT.O IS NOT NULL AND AMZN.O IS NOT NULL AND .SPX IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(avg_stock_price) AS sum_x,
        SUM(.SPX) AS sum_y,
        SUM(avg_stock_price * .SPX) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(avg_stock_price * avg_stock_price) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(.SPX * .SPX) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        average_stock_prices
),
correlation_results AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (SQRT(n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * SQRT(n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS r
    FROM 
        correlation_calculation
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        n,
        r,
        2 * (1 - ABS(r)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation_results
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(r) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_relation,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value_pval,
    ROUND(r, 2) AS correlation_coefficient_corr
FROM 
    p_value_calculation;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_val < 0.05 AND ABS(corr) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_val < 0.05 AND ABS(corr) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS relationship_type_relation,
    ROUND(p_val, 4) AS p_value_pval,
    ROUND(corr, 2) AS correlation_coefficient_corr
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(avg_price) AS avg_price,
        AVG(spx) AS spx,
        SUM((avg_price - (SELECT AVG(avg_price) FROM (SELECT (AAPL_O + MSFT_O + AMZN_O) / 3 AS avg_price FROM tr_eikon_eod_data WHERE AAPL_O IS NOT NULL AND MSFT_O IS NOT NULL AND AMZN_O IS NOT NULL))) * (spx - (SELECT AVG(spx) FROM (SELECT .SPX AS spx FROM tr_eikon_eod_data WHERE .SPX IS NOT NULL)))) AS cov,
        SUM(POW(avg_price - (SELECT AVG(avg_price) FROM (SELECT (AAPL_O + MSFT_O + AMZN_O) / 3 AS avg_price FROM tr_eikon_eod_data WHERE AAPL_O IS NOT NULL AND MSFT_O IS NOT NULL AND AMZN_O IS NOT NULL)), 2)) AS var_avg_price,
        SUM(POW(spx - (SELECT AVG(spx) FROM (SELECT .SPX AS spx FROM tr_eikon_eod_data WHERE .SPX IS NOT NULL)), 2)) AS var_spx
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            (AAPL_O + MSFT_O + AMZN_O) / 3 AS avg_price,
            .SPX AS spx
        FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
        WHERE AAPL_O IS NOT NULL AND MSFT_O IS NOT NULL AND AMZN_O IS NOT NULL AND .SPX IS NOT NULL
    ) AS subquery
) AS correlation_data,
(
    SELECT 
        (n - 2) AS df,
        1 - POWER(1 - (POWER(cov / (SQRT(var_avg_price) * SQRT(var_spx)), 2)), df) AS p_val,
        (cov / (SQRT(var_avg_price) * SQRT(var_spx))) AS corr
) AS stats;

WITH avg_stock_prices AS (
    SELECT 
        Date,
        ROUND((AAPL.O + MSFT.O + AMZN.O) / 3, 2) AS avg_price,
        .SPX
    FROM 
        tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE 
        AAPL.O IS NOT NULL AND MSFT.O IS NOT NULL AND AMZN.O IS NOT NULL AND .SPX IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(avg_price) AS avg_avg_price,
        AVG(.SPX) AS avg_spx,
        SUM((avg_price - AVG(avg_price)) * (.SPX - AVG(.SPX))) AS cov_xy,
        SUM((avg_price - AVG(avg_price)) * (avg_price - AVG(avg_price))) AS var_x,
        SUM((.SPX - AVG(.SPX)) * (.SPX - AVG(.SPX))) AS var_y
    FROM 
        avg_stock_prices
),
correlation_results AS (
    SELECT 
        cov_xy / (SQRT(var_x) * SQRT(var_y)) AS correlation_coefficient,
        n
    FROM 
        correlation_calculation
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM 
        correlation_results
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation' 
    END AS relationship_type_relation,
    ROUND(p_value, 4) AS p_value_pval,
    ROUND(correlation_coefficient, 2) AS correlation_coefficient_corr
FROM 
    correlation_results, p_value_calculation;

WITH avg_stock_prices AS (
    SELECT 
        Date, 
        COALESCE((AAPL.O + MSFT.O + AMZN.O) / NULLIF((CASE WHEN AAPL.O IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + 
                                                         CASE WHEN MSFT.O IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + 
                                                         CASE WHEN AMZN.O IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 0), 0) AS avg_price,
        COALESCE(.SPX, 0) AS sp500
    FROM tr_eikon_eod_data
    WHERE AAPL.O IS NOT NULL OR MSFT.O IS NOT NULL OR AMZN.O IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(avg_price) AS sum_x,
        SUM(sp500) AS sum_y,
        SUM(avg_price * sp500) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(avg_price * avg_price) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(sp500 * sp500) AS sum_yy
    FROM avg_stock_prices
    WHERE avg_price IS NOT NULL AND sp500 IS NOT NULL
),
correlation_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) AS numerator,
        SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)) AS denominator
    FROM correlation_stats
),
correlation_result AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN denominator != 0 THEN ROUND(numerator / denominator, 2) 
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS correlation_coefficient
    FROM correlation_calculation
),
p_value_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        correlation_coefficient,
        2 * (1 - ABS(correlation_coefficient)) AS p_value
    FROM correlation_result
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) >= 0.5 THEN 'linear'
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 AND ABS(correlation_coefficient) < 0.5 THEN 'nonlinear'
        ELSE 'no significant correlation'
    END AS relationship_type_relation,
    ROUND(COALESCE(p_value, 1), 4) AS p_value_pval,
    ROUND(COALESCE(correlation_coefficient, 0), 2) AS correlation_coefficient_corr
FROM p_value_calculation;

samples.question: What is the average trading volume of AAPL stock?

samples.question: Examine the correlation between the average number of agents talking and the average waiting time for callers.
samples.constraints: Transform the average waiting time from 'HH:MM:SS' string format to seconds (integer type). Then use the Pearson's method to calculate the correlation coefficient between the average number of agents talking and the transformed average waiting time. The result should be rounded to three decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(CORR(avg_num_agents_talking, CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics;

WITH transformed_data AS (
    SELECT 
        "avg. num. agents talking",
        CAST(SUBSTR("avg. wait time", 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 +
        CAST(SUBSTR("avg. wait time", 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 +
        CAST(SUBSTR("avg. wait time", 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS avg_wait_time_seconds
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
),
correlation_data AS (
    SELECT 
        "avg. num. agents talking",
        avg_wait_time_seconds
    FROM 
        transformed_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR("avg. num. agents talking", avg_wait_time_seconds), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    correlation_data;

SELECT ROUND((SELECT SUM((avg_agents_talking - avg_agents_talking_mean) * (wait_time_seconds - wait_time_seconds_mean)) 
                FROM (SELECT AVG(avg_num_agents_talking) AS avg_agents_talking_mean,
                             AVG(wait_time_seconds) AS wait_time_seconds_mean,
                             avg_num_agents_talking,
                             CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
                             CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
                             CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS wait_time_seconds
                      FROM (SELECT avg_num_agents_talking, avg_wait_time 
                            FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics 
                            WHERE avg_wait_time IS NOT NULL)) AS inner_query) 
                / (SELECT SQRT(SUM(POWER(avg_agents_talking - avg_agents_talking_mean, 2)) 
                               FROM (SELECT AVG(avg_num_agents_talking) AS avg_agents_talking_mean,
                                            avg_num_agents_talking
                                     FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics 
                                     WHERE avg_wait_time IS NOT NULL) AS inner_query1)) 
                / SQRT(SUM(POWER(wait_time_seconds - wait_time_seconds_mean, 2)) 
                       FROM (SELECT AVG(wait_time_seconds) AS wait_time_seconds_mean,
                                    CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
                                    CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
                                    CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS wait_time_seconds
                             FROM (SELECT avg_wait_time 
                                   FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics 
                                   WHERE avg_wait_time IS NOT NULL) AS inner_query2))) 
                ) , 3) AS correlation_coefficient;

WITH transformed_data AS (
    SELECT 
        avg.num_agents_talking,
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS wait_time_seconds
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            avg. num. agents talking AS num_agents_talking,
            avg. wait time AS avg_wait_time
        FROM 
            20170413_000000_group_statistics
    ) AS avg_data
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        num_agents_talking,
        wait_time_seconds,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS n,
        SUM(num_agents_talking) OVER () AS sum_x,
        SUM(wait_time_seconds) OVER () AS sum_y,
        SUM(num_agents_talking * wait_time_seconds) OVER () AS sum_xy,
        SUM(num_agents_talking * num_agents_talking) OVER () AS sum_x2,
        SUM(wait_time_seconds * wait_time_seconds) OVER () AS sum_y2
    FROM 
        transformed_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
    (SQRT((n * sum_x2 - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_y2 - sum_y * sum_y))), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    stats
LIMIT 1;

WITH transformed_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(CAST(avg."num. agents talking" AS FLOAT), 0) AS avg_agents_talking,
        COALESCE(
            CAST(SUBSTR(avg."wait time", 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 +
            CAST(SUBSTR(avg."wait time", 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 +
            CAST(SUBSTR(avg."wait time", 7, 2) AS INTEGER), 0
        ) AS avg_wait_time_seconds
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
    WHERE 
        avg."num. agents talking" IS NOT NULL 
        AND avg."wait time" IS NOT NULL
)

SELECT 
    ROUND(CORR(avg_agents_talking, NULLIF(avg_wait_time_seconds, 0)), 3) AS correlation_coefficient
FROM 
    transformed_data;

samples.question: Are there any outliers in the average wait time for callers before being answered by an agent? If so, how many outliers are there?
samples.constraints: Detect the outliers using the Z-score method. Consider any data point with an absolute Z-score value greater than 3 as an outlier.

variants.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS num_of_outliers
FROM (
    SELECT ROUND((CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) - avg_wait_mean) / avg_wait_stddev, 2) AS z_score
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            AVG(CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)) AS avg_wait_mean,
            STDDEV(CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)) AS avg_wait_stddev
        FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics
    )
) 
WHERE ABS(z_score) > 3;

WITH wait_time_seconds AS (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 +
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 +
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS wait_time_sec
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(wait_time_sec) AS mean_wait_time,
        STDDEV(wait_time_sec) AS stddev_wait_time
    FROM 
        wait_time_seconds
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS num_of_outliers
    FROM 
        wait_time_seconds, stats
    WHERE 
        ABS((wait_time_sec - mean_wait_time) / stddev_wait_time) > 3
)
SELECT 
    num_of_outliers 
FROM 
    outliers;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS num_of_outliers
FROM (
    SELECT *,
           (CAST(avg_wait_time AS INTEGER) - (SELECT AVG(CAST(avg_wait_time AS INTEGER)) FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics)) / 
           (SELECT STDEV(CAST(avg_wait_time AS INTEGER)) FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics) AS z_score
    FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics
) AS subquery
WHERE ABS(z_score) > 3;

WITH wait_time_seconds AS (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS wait_time_sec
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(wait_time_sec) AS mean,
        STDDEV(wait_time_sec) AS stddev
    FROM 
        wait_time_seconds
),
z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        wait_time_sec,
        (wait_time_sec - s.mean) / s.stddev AS z_score
    FROM 
        wait_time_seconds w
    CROSS JOIN 
        stats s
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS num_of_outliers
FROM 
    z_scores
WHERE 
    ABS(z_score) > 3;

WITH wait_time_seconds AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 +
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 +
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS wait_time_sec
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
    WHERE 
        avg_wait_time IS NOT NULL
), stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(wait_time_sec), 2) AS mean,
        ROUND(STDDEV(wait_time_sec), 2) AS stddev
    FROM 
        wait_time_seconds
), outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS num_of_outliers
    FROM 
        wait_time_seconds, stats
    WHERE 
        ABS(wait_time_sec - mean) > 3 * stddev
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(num_of_outliers, 0) AS num_of_outliers
FROM 
    outliers;

samples.question: Can we generate a new feature representing the call abandonment rate? If so, what is the call abandonment rate for the timestamp "20170413_080000"?
samples.constraints: Calculate the call abandonment rate for a specific timestamp as the total number of calls abandoned divided by the total number of calls made during that time. Express the result as a percentage.

variants.sql:
WITH total_calls AS (
    SELECT 
        num_calls_answered + num_calls_abandoned + num_calls_transferred + num_calls_timed_out AS total_calls_made
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
    WHERE 
        timestamp = '2017-04-13 08:00:00'
),
abandoned_calls AS (
    SELECT 
        num_calls_abandoned
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
    WHERE 
        timestamp = '2017-04-13 08:00:00'
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((CAST(abandoned.num_calls_abandoned AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(total.total_calls_made, 0)) * 100, 2) AS abandonment_rate
FROM 
    total_calls total, 
    abandoned_calls abandoned;

SELECT ROUND((SELECT num.calls_abandoned FROM 
    (SELECT num.calls_abandoned, 
            (num.calls_answered + num.calls_abandoned + num.calls_transferred + num.calls_timed_out) AS total_calls 
     FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics 
     WHERE timestamp = '20170413_080000') AS num) * 100.0 / 
    (SELECT total_calls FROM 
     (SELECT (num.calls_answered + num.calls_abandoned + num.calls_transferred + num.calls_timed_out) AS total_calls 
      FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics 
      WHERE timestamp = '20170413_080000') AS total) 
    , 2) AS abandonment_rate;

WITH call_data AS (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        SUM(num.calls.answered) AS total_calls_answered,
        SUM(num.calls.abandoned) AS total_calls_abandoned
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
    WHERE 
        timestamp = '20170413_080000'
    GROUP BY 
        timestamp
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((total_calls_abandoned * 100.0) / NULLIF(total_calls_answered + total_calls_abandoned, 0), 2) AS abandonment_rate
FROM 
    call_data;

WITH total_calls AS (
    SELECT
        COALESCE(SUM(num. calls answered), 0) + COALESCE(SUM(num. calls abandoned), 0) + COALESCE(SUM(num. calls transferred), 0) + COALESCE(SUM(num. calls timed out), 0) AS total_calls_made,
        COALESCE(SUM(num. calls abandoned), 0) AS total_calls_abandoned
    FROM
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
    WHERE
        timestamp = '2017-04-13 08:00:00'
)

SELECT
    ROUND(COALESCE(NULLIF(total_calls_abandoned, 0), 0) * 100.0 / NULLIF(total_calls_made, 0), 2) AS abandonment_rate
FROM
    total_calls
WHERE
    total_calls_made IS NOT NULL;

samples.question: Using machine learning techniques, can we predict the number of agents needed to handle incoming calls based on the timestamp and other available information? If so, predict the number for the timestamp "20170413_120000".
samples.constraints: Use a simple linear regression model for prediction. The model should be trained with features such as the timestamp, number of calls answered, number of call abandoned, etc., and the target variable should be the average number of agents staffed. Perform prediction for the given timestamp after training the model.

variants.sql:
WITH feature_data AS (
    SELECT 
        strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', timestamp) AS timestamp,
        num_calls_answered,
        num_calls_abandoned,
        num_busy_overflows,
        avg_num_agents_staffed
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
),
training_data AS (
    SELECT 
        num_calls_answered,
        num_calls_abandoned,
        num_busy_overflows,
        avg_num_agents_staffed
    FROM 
        feature_data
    WHERE 
        timestamp < '2017-04-13 12:00:00'
),
coefficients AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(avg_num_agents_staffed) - 
              (AVG(num_calls_answered) * (SUM(num_calls_answered * avg_num_agents_staffed) / SUM(num_calls_answered * num_calls_answered)) +
               AVG(num_calls_abandoned) * (SUM(num_calls_abandoned * avg_num_agents_staffed) / SUM(num_calls_abandoned * num_calls_abandoned)) +
               AVG(num_busy_overflows) * (SUM(num_busy_overflows * avg_num_agents_staffed) / SUM(num_busy_overflows * num_busy_overflows))), 2) AS intercept,
        ROUND(SUM(num_calls_answered * avg_num_agents_staffed) / SUM(num_calls_answered * num_calls_answered), 2) AS coeff_calls_answered,
        ROUND(SUM(num_calls_abandoned * avg_num_agents_staffed) / SUM(num_calls_abandoned * num_calls_abandoned), 2) AS coeff_calls_abandoned,
        ROUND(SUM(num_busy_overflows * avg_num_agents_staffed) / SUM(num_busy_overflows * num_busy_overflows), 2) AS coeff_busy_overflows
    FROM 
        training_data
),
prediction AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(intercept + 
              coeff_calls_answered * (SELECT num_calls_answered FROM feature_data WHERE timestamp = '2017-04-13 12:00:00') + 
              coeff_calls_abandoned * (SELECT num_calls_abandoned FROM feature_data WHERE timestamp = '2017-04-13 12:00:00') + 
              coeff_busy_overflows * (SELECT num_busy_overflows FROM feature_data WHERE timestamp = '2017-04-13 12:00:00'), 2) AS predicted_agents
    FROM 
        coefficients
)
SELECT predicted_agents FROM prediction;

SELECT ROUND(AVG(avg_num_agents_staffed), 2) AS predicted_agents
FROM (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        num_calls_answered,
        num_calls_abandoned,
        avg_num_agents_staffed
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
    WHERE 
        timestamp < '2017-04-13 12:00:00'
) AS training_data
WHERE 
    timestamp = '2017-04-13 12:00:00';

WITH features AS (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        num_calls_answered,
        num_calls_abandoned,
        num_busy_overflows,
        num_calls_transferred,
        num_calls_timed_out,
        avg_num_agents_staffed,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY timestamp) AS rn
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
),
model AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(avg_num_agents_staffed) AS avg_agents,
        SUM(num_calls_answered) AS total_calls_answered,
        SUM(num_calls_abandoned) AS total_calls_abandoned,
        SUM(num_busy_overflows) AS total_busy_overflows,
        SUM(num_calls_transferred) AS total_calls_transferred,
        SUM(num_calls_timed_out) AS total_calls_timed_out
    FROM 
        features
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(avg_agents + (total_calls_answered * 0.1) - (total_calls_abandoned * 0.05) + (total_busy_overflows * 0.2) + (total_calls_transferred * 0.15) - (total_calls_timed_out * 0.1), 2) AS predicted_agents
FROM 
    model
WHERE 
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM features WHERE timestamp = '2017-04-13 12:00:00');

WITH data AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(STRFTIME('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', timestamp) AS DATETIME) AS timestamp,
        COALESCE(num. calls answered, 0) AS calls_answered,
        COALESCE(num. calls abandoned, 0) AS calls_abandoned,
        COALESCE(avg. num. agents staffed, 0) AS agents_staffed
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
    WHERE 
        timestamp IS NOT NULL
),
model AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(agents_staffed) AS avg_agents_staffed,
        AVG(calls_answered) AS avg_calls_answered,
        AVG(calls_abandoned) AS avg_calls_abandoned
    FROM 
        data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(COALESCE(avg_agents_staffed, 0) + 
          (0.5 * COALESCE(avg_calls_answered, 0)) - 
          (0.3 * COALESCE(avg_calls_abandoned, 0)), 2) AS predicted_agents
FROM 
    model
WHERE 
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM data) > 0;

samples.question: Using feature engineering techniques, create a new feature that represents the waiting time for callers before being answered by an agent as a percentage of the average abandonment time. Then, explore the distribution of this new feature and determine if it adheres to a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Create a new feature 'waiting_ratio' that is defined as the ratio of average waiting time to the average abandonment time, represented as a percentage. Convert the waiting and abandonment time from format HH:MM:SS to seconds before the calculation. After creating the feature, calculate the skewness of this new feature. Use the skewness to determine whether the data is normally distributed. For normally distributed data, skewness should be about 0.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND(AVG(CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)) / 
                     NULLIF(AVG(CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)), 0) * 100, 2) 
        BETWEEN -0.5 AND 0.5 THEN 'is_normal=True' 
        ELSE 'is_normal=False' 
    END AS is_normal
FROM 
    20170413_000000_group_statistics;

WITH time_conversion AS (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        num_busy_overflows,
        num_calls_answered,
        num_calls_abandoned,
        num_calls_transferred,
        num_calls_timed_out,
        avg_num_agents_talking,
        avg_num_agents_staffed,
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS wait_time_seconds,
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS abandonment_time_seconds
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
),
waiting_ratio_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        num_busy_overflows,
        num_calls_answered,
        num_calls_abandoned,
        num_calls_transferred,
        num_calls_timed_out,
        avg_num_agents_talking,
        avg_num_agents_staffed,
        ROUND((wait_time_seconds * 100.0 / NULLIF(abandonment_time_seconds, 0)), 2) AS waiting_ratio
    FROM 
        time_conversion
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(ROUND(AVG(waiting_ratio), 2)) < 0.1 THEN 'is_normal=True' 
        ELSE 'is_normal=False' 
    END AS is_normal
FROM 
    waiting_ratio_calculation;

SELECT CASE 
           WHEN ROUND(AVG(waiting_ratio), 2) = 0 THEN 'is_normal=False' 
           ELSE 'is_normal=True' 
       END AS is_normal
FROM (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(
            (CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
             CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
             CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)) * 100.0 /
            NULLIF(
                (CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
                 CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
                 CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER)), 0), 
            2) AS waiting_ratio
    FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics
) AS ratios;

WITH time_converted AS (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        num_busy_overflows,
        num_calls_answered,
        num_calls_abandoned,
        num_calls_transferred,
        num_calls_timed_out,
        avg_num_agents_talking,
        avg_num_agents_staffed,
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_wait_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS avg_wait_time_sec,
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(avg_abandonment_time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) AS avg_abandonment_time_sec
    FROM 
        20170413_000000_group_statistics
),
waiting_ratio AS (
    SELECT 
        timestamp,
        num_busy_overflows,
        num_calls_answered,
        num_calls_abandoned,
        num_calls_transferred,
        num_calls_timed_out,
        avg_num_agents_talking,
        avg_num_agents_staffed,
        ROUND((avg_wait_time_sec * 100.0 / NULLIF(avg_abandonment_time_sec, 0)), 2) AS waiting_ratio
    FROM 
        time_converted
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(ROUND(AVG(waiting_ratio), 2)) < 0.1 THEN 'is_normal=True' 
        ELSE 'is_normal=False' 
    END AS is_normal
FROM 
    waiting_ratio;

WITH waiting_and_abandonment AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(NULLIF(strftime('%s', avg_wait_time), 0), 0) AS avg_wait_time_seconds,
        COALESCE(NULLIF(strftime('%s', avg_abandonment_time), 0), 0) AS avg_abandonment_time_seconds
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            timestamp,
            CAST(avg. wait time AS TEXT) AS avg_wait_time,
            CAST(avg. abandonment time AS TEXT) AS avg_abandonment_time
        FROM 20170413_000000_group_statistics
        WHERE avg. wait time IS NOT NULL AND avg. abandonment time IS NOT NULL
    )
),
waiting_ratio AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(COALESCE(NULLIF(avg_wait_time_seconds, 0), 0) * 100.0 / NULLIF(avg_abandonment_time_seconds, 0), 0), 2) AS waiting_ratio_value
    FROM waiting_and_abandonment
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ABS(AVG(waiting_ratio_value) - 0) < 0.1 THEN 'is_normal=True'
        ELSE 'is_normal=False'
    END AS is_normal
FROM waiting_ratio;

samples.question: 2. Check if the RHO_OLD column follows a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Use the Shapiro-Wilk test to evaluate if the RHO_OLD column follows a normal distribution. In the test, if the p-value is less than 0.05, then it does not follow a normal distribution. If the p-value is greater than 0.05, then it follows a normal distribution.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(RHO_OLD) < 3 THEN 'Insufficient Data'
        ELSE 
            CASE 
                WHEN (SUM((RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD)) * (RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD))) / (COUNT(RHO_OLD) - 1)) < 0.05 THEN 'Not Normal'
                ELSE 'Normal'
            END 
    END AS normality_status
FROM well_2_complete;

WITH rho_old_data AS (
    SELECT RHO_OLD
    FROM well_2_complete
    WHERE RHO_OLD IS NOT NULL
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(RHO_OLD) AS mean,
        STDDEV(RHO_OLD) AS stddev
    FROM rho_old_data
),
normality_check AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN (n < 3) THEN 'Not Enough Data'
            ELSE 
                CASE 
                    WHEN (1 - (1 / (1 + (n - 1) * (stddev / mean)))) < 0.05 THEN 'Not Normal'
                    ELSE 'Normal'
                END
        END AS normality_status
    FROM shapiro_test
)
SELECT normality_status
FROM normality_check;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'Not Normal' 
        ELSE 'Normal' 
    END AS normality_status
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(RHO_OLD) AS mean,
        STDDEV_POP(RHO_OLD) AS stddev,
        SUM((RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD)) * (RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD))) AS sum_sq_diff,
        SUM((RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD))^4) AS sum_fourth_diff,
        (SUM((RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD))^4) / (n * stddev^4)) AS W,
        (1 - (1.0 / (n - 1) * (sum_sq_diff / (n * stddev^2)))) AS p_value
    FROM (
        SELECT RHO_OLD 
        FROM well_2_complete 
        WHERE RHO_OLD IS NOT NULL
    )
) AS stats;

WITH rho_old_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        RHO_OLD,
        AVG(RHO_OLD) OVER () AS mean_rho_old,
        COUNT(RHO_OLD) OVER () AS n,
        SUM((RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD) OVER ()) * (RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD) OVER ())) OVER () AS sum_sq_diff,
        SUM((RHO_OLD - AVG(RHO_OLD) OVER ())) OVER () AS sum_diff
    FROM well_2_complete
    WHERE RHO_OLD IS NOT NULL
),
shapiro_wilk AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((n * (sum_sq_diff / (n - 1))) / (sum_diff * sum_diff), 2) AS W_statistic,
        ROUND(1 - (1 / (1 + (W_statistic * (n - 1)))), 2) AS p_value
    FROM rho_old_stats
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'Not Normal' 
        ELSE 'Normal' 
    END AS normality_status
FROM shapiro_wilk;

WITH rho_old_data AS (
    SELECT COALESCE(RHO_OLD, 0) AS RHO_OLD
    FROM well_2_complete
    WHERE RHO_OLD IS NOT NULL
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(CASE WHEN RHO_OLD IS NOT NULL THEN RHO_OLD END) AS sum_rho_old,
        SUM(CASE WHEN RHO_OLD IS NOT NULL THEN RHO_OLD * RHO_OLD END) AS sum_rho_old_sq
    FROM rho_old_data
),
normality_result AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN (n > 0 AND (sum_rho_old_sq - (sum_rho_old * sum_rho_old) / n) / (n - 1) < 0.05) THEN 'Not Normal'
            ELSE 'Normal'
        END AS normality_status
    FROM shapiro_test
)

SELECT normality_status FROM normality_result;

samples.question: 1. Identify and remove any outliers in the SWX column using the Z-score method with a threshold of 3. Calculate the new mean and standard deviation of the SWX column after removing the outliers.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(SWX), 3) AS mean_after_removal, 
    ROUND(STDDEV(SWX), 3) AS std_dev_after_removal, 
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    well_2_complete
WHERE 
    SWX IS NOT NULL 
    AND ABS((SWX - (SELECT AVG(SWX) FROM well_2_complete WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL)) / (SELECT STDDEV(SWX) FROM well_2_complete WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL)) < 3;

WITH SWX_Cleaned AS (
    SELECT SWX
    FROM well_2_complete
    WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL
), SWX_Stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(SWX) AS mean_swx,
        STDDEV(SWX) AS stddev_swx
    FROM SWX_Cleaned
), SWX_With_ZScore AS (
    SELECT 
        SWX,
        (SWX - (SELECT mean_swx FROM SWX_Stats)) / (SELECT stddev_swx FROM SWX_Stats) AS z_score
    FROM SWX_Cleaned
), SWX_Final AS (
    SELECT SWX
    FROM SWX_With_ZScore
    WHERE ABS(z_score) <= 3
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT AVG(SWX) FROM SWX_Final), 3) AS mean_after_removal,
    ROUND((SELECT STDDEV(SWX) FROM SWX_Final), 3) AS std_dev_after_removal,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SWX_Cleaned) - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SWX_Final) AS outlier_count
;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(SWX), 3) AS mean_after_removal,
    ROUND(STDDEV(SWX), 3) AS std_dev_after_removal,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM well_2_complete WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL AND 
        (SWX - (SELECT AVG(SWX) FROM well_2_complete WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL)) / 
        (SELECT STDDEV(SWX) FROM well_2_complete WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL) > 3 OR 
        SWX - (SELECT AVG(SWX) FROM well_2_complete WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL) / 
        (SELECT STDDEV(SWX) FROM well_2_complete WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL) < -3) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    (SELECT SWX FROM well_2_complete WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL) AS filtered;

WITH SWX_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        SWX,
        AVG(SWX) OVER () AS mean_swx,
        STDDEV(SWX) OVER () AS stddev_swx
    FROM 
        well_2_complete
    WHERE 
        SWX IS NOT NULL
),
SWX_filtered AS (
    SELECT 
        SWX,
        mean_swx,
        stddev_swx,
        (SWX - mean_swx) / stddev_swx AS z_score
    FROM 
        SWX_stats
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(SWX), 3) AS mean_after_removal,
    ROUND(STDDEV(SWX), 3) AS std_dev_after_removal,
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM 
    SWX_filtered
WHERE 
    ABS(z_score) <= 3;

WITH swx_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(SWX, 0)), 3) AS mean_swx,
        ROUND(STDDEV(COALESCE(SWX, 0)), 3) AS stddev_swx
    FROM well_2_complete
    WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL
),
z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        DEPTH,
        VP,
        VS,
        RHO_OLD,
        GR,
        NPHI,
        RHO,
        SW,
        SWX,
        VPVS,
        IP,
        IS,
        VSH,
        RHOm,
        RHOf,
        PHI,
        (COALESCE(SWX, 0) - (SELECT mean_swx FROM swx_stats)) / NULLIF((SELECT stddev_swx FROM swx_stats), 0) AS z_score
    FROM well_2_complete
    WHERE SWX IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(SWX, 0)), 3) AS mean_after_removal,
    ROUND(STDDEV(COALESCE(SWX, 0)), 3) AS std_dev_after_removal,
    COUNT(*) AS outlier_count
FROM z_scores
WHERE z_score IS NOT NULL AND (z_score < 3 AND z_score > -3);

samples.question: 3. Find the correlation coefficient between the number of photos taken during the trajectories and the total duration spent at each point of interest. Use the Python Pandas library's corr() function for the calculation.
samples.constraints: Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient between the number of photos and the total duration spent at each point of interest using pandas' corr() function.

variants.sql: SELECT ROUND(CORR("#photo", poiDuration), 2) AS correlation FROM "traj-Osak";

WITH photo_duration AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM("#photo") AS total_photos,
        SUM(poiDuration) AS total_duration
    FROM 
        "traj-Osak"
    GROUP BY 
        poiID
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        total_photos,
        total_duration,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(total_photos) AS sum_x,
        SUM(total_duration) AS sum_y,
        SUM(total_photos * total_duration) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(total_photos * total_photos) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(total_duration * total_duration) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        photo_duration
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / 
           (SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y))), 2) AS correlation
FROM 
    stats;

SELECT ROUND((SUM(#photo * poiDuration) - SUM(#photo) * SUM(poiDuration) / COUNT(*)) / 
              (SQRT((SUM(#photo * #photo) - SUM(#photo) * SUM(#photo) / COUNT(*)) * 
                     (SUM(poiDuration * poiDuration) - SUM(poiDuration) * SUM(poiDuration) / COUNT(*)))), 2) AS correlation
FROM (SELECT userID, trajID, SUM(#photo) AS #photo, SUM(poiDuration) AS poiDuration
      FROM `traj-Osak`
      GROUP BY userID, trajID);

WITH photo_duration AS (
    SELECT 
        poiID,
        SUM(#photo) AS total_photos,
        SUM(poiDuration) AS total_duration
    FROM 
        "traj-Osak"
    GROUP BY 
        poiID
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        total_photos,
        total_duration,
        AVG(total_photos) OVER () AS avg_photos,
        AVG(total_duration) OVER () AS avg_duration,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS n
    FROM 
        photo_duration
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(SUM((total_photos - avg_photos) * (total_duration - avg_duration)) / 
    (SQRT(SUM(POWER(total_photos - avg_photos, 2)) * SUM(POWER(total_duration - avg_duration, 2)))), 2) AS correlation
FROM 
    stats;

WITH photo_duration AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(SUM("#photo"), 0) AS total_photos,
        COALESCE(SUM(poiDuration), 0) AS total_duration
    FROM 
        "traj-Osak"
    WHERE 
        "#photo" IS NOT NULL AND
        poiDuration IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        poiID
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        total_photos,
        total_duration,
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM(total_photos) AS sum_x,
        SUM(total_duration) AS sum_y,
        SUM(total_photos * total_duration) AS sum_xy,
        SUM(total_photos * total_photos) AS sum_xx,
        SUM(total_duration * total_duration) AS sum_yy
    FROM 
        photo_duration
    WHERE 
        total_photos IS NOT NULL AND
        total_duration IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND((n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / NULLIF(SQRT((n * sum_xx - sum_x * sum_x) * (n * sum_yy - sum_y * sum_y)), 0), 2) AS correlation
FROM 
    stats;

samples.question: 1. Identify and remove any outliers in the duration of the trajectories based on the Z-score method where an outlier is defined as a data point that is located outside the whiskers of the box plot (a data point is considered to be an outlier if its z-score is less than -2.5 or greater than 2.5). Calculate the new mean and standard deviation of the trajectory durations after removing the outliers.
samples.constraints: For outlier detection, use the Z-score method where an outlier is defined as a data point that is located outside the whiskers of the box plot (a data point is considered to be an outlier if its z-score is less than -2.5 or greater than 2.5). For calculating the mean and standard deviation, use the built-in Python functions from numpy. The values should be rounded off to 2 decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(poiDuration), 2) AS mean_new, ROUND(STDDEV(poiDuration), 2) AS std_dev_new
FROM traj-Osak
WHERE poiDuration BETWEEN (SELECT AVG(poiDuration) - 2.5 * STDDEV(poiDuration) FROM traj-Osak) 
                      AND (SELECT AVG(poiDuration) + 2.5 * STDDEV(poiDuration) FROM traj-Osak);

WITH traj_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(poiDuration), 2) AS mean_duration,
        ROUND(STDDEV(poiDuration), 2) AS stddev_duration
    FROM 
        `traj-Osak`
),
filtered_traj AS (
    SELECT 
        poiDuration,
        (poiDuration - (SELECT mean_duration FROM traj_stats)) / (SELECT stddev_duration FROM traj_stats) AS z_score
    FROM 
        `traj-Osak`
),
valid_traj AS (
    SELECT 
        poiDuration
    FROM 
        filtered_traj
    WHERE 
        z_score BETWEEN -2.5 AND 2.5
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(poiDuration), 2) AS mean_new,
    ROUND(STDDEV(poiDuration), 2) AS std_dev_new
FROM 
    valid_traj;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(poiDuration), 2) AS mean_new, 
    ROUND(STDEV(poiDuration), 2) AS std_dev_new 
FROM 
    (SELECT poiDuration 
     FROM traj-Osak 
     WHERE poiDuration NOT IN 
         (SELECT poiDuration 
          FROM traj-Osak 
          WHERE (poiDuration - (SELECT AVG(poiDuration) FROM traj-Osak)) / 
                (SELECT STDEV(poiDuration) FROM traj-Osak) < -2.5 
          OR (poiDuration - (SELECT AVG(poiDuration) FROM traj-Osak)) / 
                (SELECT STDEV(poiDuration) FROM traj-Osak) > 2.5));

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(poiDuration), 2) AS mean_duration,
        ROUND(STDDEV(poiDuration), 2) AS stddev_duration
    FROM `traj-Osak`
),
z_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        userID,
        trajID,
        poiID,
        startTime,
        endTime,
        `#photo`,
        trajLen,
        poiDuration,
        (poiDuration - (SELECT mean_duration FROM stats)) / (SELECT stddev_duration FROM stats) AS z_score
    FROM `traj-Osak`
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(poiDuration), 2) AS mean_new,
    ROUND(STDDEV(poiDuration), 2) AS std_dev_new
FROM z_scores
WHERE z_score BETWEEN -2.5 AND 2.5;

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(poiDuration, 0)), 2) AS mean_duration,
        ROUND(STDDEV(COALESCE(poiDuration, 0)), 2) AS stddev_duration
    FROM 
        `traj-Osak`
    WHERE 
        poiDuration IS NOT NULL
),
outliers AS (
    SELECT 
        poiDuration,
        (poiDuration - COALESCE((SELECT mean_duration FROM stats), 0)) / NULLIF(COALESCE((SELECT stddev_duration FROM stats), 1), 0) AS z_score
    FROM 
        `traj-Osak`
    WHERE 
        poiDuration IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(COALESCE(poiDuration, 0)), 2) AS mean_new,
    ROUND(STDDEV(COALESCE(poiDuration, 0)), 2) AS std_dev_new
FROM 
    `traj-Osak`
WHERE 
    poiDuration IS NOT NULL
    AND poiDuration IN (SELECT poiDuration FROM outliers WHERE z_score >= -2.5 AND z_score <= 2.5);

samples.question: Calculate the mean, standard deviation, minimum, and maximum values of the "Volume" column.
samples.constraints: Use Python's built-in statistical functions to calculate these values. Round these numbers to two decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Volume), 2) AS mean_volume, 
    ROUND(STDEV(Volume), 2) AS std_volume, 
    MIN(Volume) AS min_volume, 
    MAX(Volume) AS max_volume 
FROM random_stock_data;

WITH volume_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND(AVG(Volume), 2) AS mean_volume,
        ROUND(STDDEV(Volume), 2) AS std_volume,
        MIN(Volume) AS min_volume,
        MAX(Volume) AS max_volume
    FROM random_stock_data
)
SELECT * FROM volume_stats;

SELECT 
    ROUND(AVG(Volume), 2) AS mean_volume,
    MIN(Volume) AS min_volume,
    MAX(Volume) AS max_volume,
    ROUND(SQRT(AVG((Volume - (SELECT AVG(Volume) FROM random_stock_data)) * (Volume - (SELECT AVG(Volume) FROM random_stock_data)))), 2) AS std_volume
FROM random_stock_data;

WITH volume_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        Volume,
        AVG(Volume) OVER () AS mean_volume,
        STDDEV(Volume) OVER () AS std_volume,
        MIN(Volume) OVER () AS min_volume,
        MAX(Volume) OVER () AS max_volume
    FROM random_stock_data
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(DISTINCT mean_volume, 2) AS mean_volume,
    DISTINCT min_volume,
    DISTINCT max_volume,
    ROUND(DISTINCT std_volume, 2) AS std_volume
FROM volume_stats
LIMIT 1;

WITH volume_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(AVG(Volume), 0) AS mean_volume,
        COALESCE(MIN(Volume), 0) AS min_volume,
        COALESCE(MAX(Volume), 0) AS max_volume,
        COALESCE(STDEV(Volume), 0) AS std_volume
    FROM random_stock_data
    WHERE Volume IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ROUND(mean_volume, 2) AS mean_volume,
    min_volume,
    max_volume,
    ROUND(std_volume, 2) AS std_volume
FROM volume_stats;

samples.question: Check if the "Close" column follows a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Use the Shapiro-Wilk test to determine whether the "Close" column follows a normal distribution. The null hypothesis is that the data was drawn from a normal distribution. Use a significance level (alpha) of 0.05. If the p-value is less than 0.05, reject the null hypothesis and conclude that the data does not come from a normal distribution. Otherwise, do not reject the null hypothesis and conclude that the data does come from a normal distribution. Round the p-value to four decimal places.

variants.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND(P.VALUE, 4) < 0.05 THEN 'Not a normal distribution' 
        ELSE 'Normal distribution' 
    END AS result
FROM (
    SELECT 
        P.VALUE 
    FROM 
        random_stock_data 
    WHERE 
        Close IS NOT NULL
    ) AS P;

WITH close_data AS (
    SELECT Close
    FROM random_stock_data
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Close) AS mean,
        STDEV(Close) AS stddev,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM close_data
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((SUM((Close - mean) * (Close - mean)) / (n - 1)) / (stddev * stddev), 4) AS p_value
    FROM close_data, stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'Not a normal distribution'
        ELSE 'Normal distribution'
    END AS result
FROM shapiro_test;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*), 4) FROM (SELECT Close FROM random_stock_data)) < 0.05 
        THEN 'Not a normal distribution' 
        ELSE 'Normal distribution' 
    END AS result;

WITH close_data AS (
    SELECT Close
    FROM random_stock_data
),
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(Close) AS mean,
        STDEV(Close) AS stddev,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM close_data
),
shapiro AS (
    SELECT 
        ROUND((SUM((Close - mean) * (Close - mean)) / (n - 1)) / (stddev * stddev), 4) AS p_value
    FROM close_data, stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN p_value < 0.05 THEN 'Not a normal distribution' 
        ELSE 'Normal distribution' 
    END AS result
FROM shapiro;

WITH close_data AS (
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(Close, 0) AS CloseValue
    FROM 
        random_stock_data
    WHERE 
        Close IS NOT NULL
), 
stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        AVG(CloseValue) AS mean,
        STDDEV(CloseValue) AS stddev
    FROM 
        close_data
),
shapiro_test AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(SUM(POWER((CloseValue - mean), 4)) AS FLOAT) / (n * POWER(stddev, 4)) AS W, 
        n
    FROM 
        close_data, stats
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN ROUND((1 - W), 4) < 0.05 THEN 'Not a normal distribution' 
        ELSE 'Normal distribution' 
    END AS result
FROM 
    shapiro_test;

samples.question: Create a new feature called "Price Range" by calculating the difference between the "High" and "Low" values for each entry. Then, determine if the "Price Range" follows a normal distribution.
samples.constraints: Calculate "Price Range" for each row by subtracting the "Low" value from the "High" value. Test the normality of the resulting column using the Shapiro-Wilk test. Consider the data to follow a normal distribution if the p-value is greater than 0.05.

variants.sql:

WITH PriceRange AS (
